nodes.doc:
**C5 `detExtend` (`Bext`).** The determined form `b u v w := detExtend u (v ⊗ₜ w)`.
Built as `(psiD u) ∘ₗ gInv ∘ₗ A` where `A` is the antisymmetrizer
`(id − swap) / 2`; this makes `detExtend u` antisymmetric for **all** `(v, w)`, while on
the determined edge `D` it reads the glued value `psiD u` (`gInv` fixes `D`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.detExtend
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 →ₗ[ℝ] TensorProduct ℝ ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.doc: `detExtend` is additive in `u` (isolated, applied at a tensor `z`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.detExtend_add
nodes.signature:
∀ (u u' : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (z : TensorProduct ℝ ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
  (LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.detExtend (u + u')) z =
    (LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.detExtend u) z + (LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.detExtend u') z

nodes.doc:
**C5b `detExtend_on_edgeSlot3` [PR-4 — the determined identity].**
On the slot-3-Schwartz edge `L²_σ ⊗ 𝒮`, `detExtend u` is the genuine determined value:
for ALL `u, v` and any Schwartz `w`,
`detExtend u (v ⊗ₜ w) = convBLT_fixedTest w … u v`,
i.e. the value is the evaluation of the jointly continuous B7-bounded extension `Bw`
(C3), NOT a Hamel value.

This is the load-bearing identity for `b_cont_fixedTest`.  Both `v ⊗ₜ w ∈ edgeSlot3` and
`w ⊗ₜ v ∈ edgeSlot2` are in the determined domain `D`; `gInv` fixes `D` and the
antisymmetrizer combines the two determined edge values into the genuine
`convBLT_fixedTest w u v`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.detExtend_on_edgeSlot3
nodes.signature:
∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hw_H1 : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w)
  (hw_sigma : w ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (hw_sch : LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 ⟨w, hw_sigma⟩),
  (LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.detExtend u) (v ⊗ₜ[ℝ] ⟨w, hw_sigma⟩) =
    ((LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.convBLT_fixedTest w hw_H1 hw_sigma hw_sch) u) v

  (LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.detExtend (c • u)) z = c • (LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.detExtend u) z

**The overlap identity (consumes the proved tensor-intersection lemma).**
`(𝒮 ⊗ L²_σ) ⊓ (L²_σ ⊗ 𝒮) = 𝒮 ⊗ 𝒮`, on whose image the two edge prescriptions agree.
This is `TensorIntersection.range_map_subtype_inf_range_map_subtype` specialized to
`S = schwartzSpan`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.edge_inf_eq_schwartz_tensor

  (TensorProduct.mapIncl LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan
      LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan).range

  (z : TensorProduct ℝ ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan),
  (LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.edge2Lift✝ u)
      (LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.retr2✝
        ((TensorProduct.mapIncl LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan
            LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan)
          z)) =
    (LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.edge3Lift✝ u)
      (LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.retr3✝
        ((TensorProduct.mapIncl LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan
            LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan)
          z))

nodes.doc:
The two edge prescriptions, composed with the retractions and `mapIncl`, are **equal
as linear maps** on `𝒮 ⊗ 𝒮` (`TensorProduct.ext'`, tmul case = overlap agreement).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.edge_lift_agree_map
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
  LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.edge2Lift✝ u ∘ₗ
      LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.retr2✝ ∘ₗ
        TensorProduct.mapIncl LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan
          LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan =
    LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.edge3Lift✝ u ∘ₗ
      LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.retr3✝ ∘ₗ
        TensorProduct.mapIncl LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan
          LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.schwartzSpan

nodes.doc: Additivity of `extendOfNorm` along `eH1` (via uniqueness of the bounded extension).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.extendOfNorm_eH1_add
nodes.signature:
∀ (f f' : ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.H1Sigma' →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ) {C C' : ℝ},
  (∀ (x : ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.H1Sigma'), ‖f x‖ ≤ C * ‖LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.eH1✝ x‖) →
    (∀ (x : ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.H1Sigma'), ‖f' x‖ ≤ C' * ‖LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.eH1✝ x‖) →
      (f + f').extendOfNorm LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.eH1✝ =
        f.extendOfNorm LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.eH1✝ + f'.extendOfNorm LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.eH1✝

nodes.doc: Homogeneity of `extendOfNorm` along `eH1`.

    (c • f).extendOfNorm LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.eH1✝ =
      c • f.extendOfNorm LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.eH1✝

nodes.doc:
The inner linear functional `v ↦ convFormH1 u v w` on `H1Sigma'`, for fixed
H¹ `u` and Schwartz test `w`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin
nodes.signature:
(w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) →
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w →
    ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.H1Sigma' → ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.H1Sigma' →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin_add_1
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hw_H1 : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w) (u u' : ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.H1Sigma'),
  LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin✝ w hw_H1 (u + u') =
    LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin✝ w hw_H1 u + LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin✝ w hw_H1 u'

nodes.doc: The inner B7 bound at fixed `u`: `‖innerLin w u v‖ ≤ (C_w ‖u‖) ‖v‖`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hw_H1 : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w) (hw_sigma : w ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
  LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 ⟨w, hw_sigma⟩ →
    ∀ {C_w : ℝ},
      (∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hu : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u) (hv : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v),
          u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 →
            v ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 → |LerayHopf.convFormH1 u v w hu hv hw_H1| ≤ C_w * ‖u‖ * ‖v‖) →
        ∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.H1Sigma'),
          ‖(LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin✝ w hw_H1 u) v‖ ≤ C_w * ‖u‖ * ‖v‖

nodes.doc: The uniform B7 bound packaged in terms of `innerLin` and the constant `cwConst`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin_bound'
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hw_H1 : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w) (hw_sigma : w ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3)
  (hw_sch : LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 ⟨w, hw_sigma⟩) (u v : ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.H1Sigma'),
  ‖(LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin✝ w hw_H1 u) v‖ ≤
    LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.cwConst✝ w hw_H1 hw_sigma hw_sch * ‖u‖ * ‖LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.eH1✝ v‖

nodes.doc: The packaged uniform bound for `innerLin` in the bounded tower.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin_bound_bdd
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hw_H1 : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w) {C : ℝ},
  (∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hu : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u) (hv : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v),
      u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 → v ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 → |LerayHopf.convFormH1 u v w hu hv hw_H1| ≤ C * ‖u‖ * ‖v‖) →
    ∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.H1Sigma'),
      ‖(LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin✝ w hw_H1 u) v‖ ≤ C * ‖u‖ * ‖LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.eH1✝ v‖

nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.innerLin_smul_1
nodes.signature:

nodes.doc:
**C11 `r3ConvectionGapOp_holds` [assembled].**
`∀ 𝔊 : R3GalerkinScheme, Nonempty (ConvectionGapOp 𝔊)`.

Theorem replacing the former `r3ConvectionGapOp_exists` axiom.  The root namespace re-exports
this theorem under that compatibility name (Hard Rule #2).  Assembled from the five
determined-form fields:
- `b               := convFormL2_def` (C6)
- `b_extends       := convFormL2_extends` (C9)
- `b_multilinear   := convFormL2_multilinear` (C7)
- `b_antisymm_gap  := convFormL2_antisymm` (C8)
- `b_cont_fixedTest := convFormL2_cont_fixedTest` (C10).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.ConvectionExtension.r3ConvectionGapOp_holds

Auxiliary: if a projection `P : V →ₗ[K] V` restricts to the identity on a
submodule `S` (i.e. `P ∘ₗ S.subtype = S.subtype`), then the tensor map
`map P id` fixes every element of `range (map S.subtype id)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.TensorIntersection.map_proj_id_fix_of_mem_range

  P ∘ₗ S.subtype = S.subtype →
    ∀ {x : TensorProduct K V V},
      x ∈ (TensorProduct.map S.subtype LinearMap.id).range → (TensorProduct.map P LinearMap.id) x = x

nodes.doc:
**Main lemma (issue #56).** Inside `V ⊗ V`, the image of `S ⊗ V` and the image
of `V ⊗ S` intersect in exactly the image of `S ⊗ S`.

Stated for a general `DivisionRing K`, `K`-vector space `V`, and submodule
`S : Submodule K V`. The two outer ranges are the images of `S ⊗ V` and `V ⊗ S`;
the right-hand side `range (mapIncl S S)` is the image of `S ⊗ S`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3.TensorIntersection.range_map_subtype_inf_range_map_subtype
nodes.signature:
∀ {K : Type u_1} {V : Type u_2} [inst : Field K] [inst_1 : AddCommGroup V] [inst_2 : Module K V] (S : Submodule K V),
  (TensorProduct.map S.subtype LinearMap.id).range ⊓ (TensorProduct.map LinearMap.id S.subtype).range =
    (TensorProduct.mapIncl S S).range

nodes.doc:
The Galerkin approximation-projection family on `L²(ℝ³; ℝ³)`.

Bundles the `n`-indexed family of continuous projectors `P n : L2VF_R3 →L[ℝ] L2VF_R3`
with the five key properties needed for the Galerkin construction:

- `preserves_sigma`: each `P n` maps `L2Sigma_R3` into itself (the approximation respects
  the divergence-free constraint; follows for frequency-ball truncation or mollification);
- `tendsto_id`: `P n u → u` in `L²` as `n → ∞` **for every `u ∈ L2Sigma_R3`** (strong
  convergence of the approximation on the divergence-free subspace; Paley–Wiener
  approximation theory).  The convergence is RESTRICTED to `L2Sigma_R3`: a divergence-free
  Galerkin scheme is total only in `L²_σ(ℝ³)`, never in all of `L²(ℝ³; ℝ³)` (its prefix-span
  projections land in the closed div-free subspace, so an unrestricted `∀ u : L2VF_R3` form
  would force every `u` to be divergence-free — a latent over-strength, Codex-confirmed).
  Every consumer applies `P n` only to div-free data, so the Σ-restriction is exactly what
  the Leray–Hopf assembly needs;
- `norm_le`: `‖P n u‖ ≤ ‖u‖` (non-expansiveness; follows e.g. from the fact that `P n` is
  the L² projection onto a subspace);
- `idem`: `P n ∘ P n = P n` (idempotence; `P n` is a projection);
- `range_schwartz`: every element of the range of `P n` has Schwartz component
  representatives (excludes `P = id` since `L²(ℝ³) ⊄ Schwartz(ℝ³)`; ensures every
  `IsGalerkinTest_R3` field is Schwartz so `b_bound` and `reg_mem`/H¹ apply).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme
nodes.signature: Type

nodes.doc:
The bundle of ℝ³ Navier–Stokes forms: only the trilinear convection form `b`
(the viscous form is the concrete `stokesTestPairing_R3`, NOT axiomatized).

Mirrors `Torus3NSForms` with `L2Sigma_R3` in place of `L2Sigma`.  The `𝔊 : R3GalerkinScheme`
parameter is threaded so that the evolution `r3Evolution 𝔊 F` can reference the Galerkin
projection `𝔊.P n`.  The pin `b_galerkin` uses Schwartz component witnesses
(`L2VF_projComponent_R3 j`) directly — independent of `𝔊` — to pin `b` to
`convIntegralSchwartz` on Schwartz div-free fields.

**Non-vacuity:** `b_galerkin` pins `b` to `convIntegralSchwartz` (the genuine
`∑_{i,a} ∫ u_a (∂_a v_i) w_i` convection integral) on Schwartz div-free fields.
This excludes `b := 0` since `convIntegralSchwartz ≢ 0` on a concrete Schwartz triple.

**Antisymmetry convention:** `b u v w = -b u w v` (skew in the last two slots).

**Smooth-test convection bound:** For a **canonical Schwartz divergence-free** test `w`
(`IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 w`), `|b(u,v,w)| ≤ C(w)·‖u‖·‖v‖`.  This uses the full
(scheme-independent) test class, matching the weak NS formulation in `r3Evolution`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3NSForms
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme → Type

nodes.doc:
**b(u, u, u) = 0** follows purely from antisymmetry.

Proof: `b(u, u, u) = -b(u, u, u)` by `b_antisymm u u u`, so `b(u, u, u) = 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3NSForms.b_self_zero
nodes.signature: ∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3), F.b u u u = 0

nodes.doc:
**G2. The conditional concrete `R3NSForms` — a genuine derivation.**

Given the isolated `ConvectionGap 𝔊` (a total `b`, its extension `b_extends`, the
algebraic trilinear witness `b_multilinear`, antisymmetry `b_antisymm_gap`, fixed-test
bilinear continuity `b_cont_fixedTest`, and density `schwartz_dense`), a genuine
`R3NSForms 𝔊` exists.  Each `R3NSForms` field is obtained as follows:

- `b_add_{1,2,3}` — from `g.b_multilinear`: obtain `B`, rewrite `g.b _ _ _ = B _ _ _`, close
                  by `B`'s `map_add` in the respective slot.  **No continuity, no density**;
                  works for *arbitrary* `u v w : L²_σ` — this is exactly what the false
                  `b_cont` was (wrongly) needed for, supplied honestly by `b_multilinear`;
- `b_smul_{1,2,3}` — same, via `B`'s `map_smul`;
- `b_antisymm`  — directly from `g.b_antisymm_gap` (asserted over arbitrary `L²_σ`); it is
                  the honest residual of the missing operator, **not** derived from
                  continuity;
- `b_bound`     — from `convFormSchwartz_bound` (Tier S): take the constant `C` at the
                  *given* Schwartz `w`; `g.b_extends` turns `|g.b u v w|` into
                  `|convFormSchwartz …| ≤ C‖u‖‖v‖` on the Schwartz class, then
                  `g.b_cont_fixedTest w hw` (slots 1,2 at fixed `w`) + `g.schwartz_dense`
                  + continuity of `‖·‖` extend it to all `u v : L²_σ`;
- `b_galerkin`  — from `convFormSchwartz_eq_witness` (Tier S) via `g.b_extends`.

So `ConvectionGap` does *not* hand over the *quantitative* `R3NSForms` content ready-made:
the bound and the pin are derived from the Tier-S lemmas through `b_extends` /
`b_cont_fixedTest`; only the algebraic trilinear/antisymmetry content (`b_multilinear`,
`b_antisymm_gap`) is asserted, as the honest residual of the missing weak operator.
Discharging `ConvectionGap` later (when the weak-`(u·∇)v` extension calculus lands)
discharges `r3_NSForms_exist` for free, with no edit to `AxiomaticClosure.lean`, and never
vacuously (the `convFormSchwartz_eq_witness` pin through `b_extends` excludes `b = 0`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3NSForms_of_gap
nodes.signature: ∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (g : LerayHopf.ConvectionGap 𝔊), Nonempty (LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊)

nodes.doc:
The H¹(graph-norm) test-approximation property for an `R3GalerkinScheme`.

Every canonical Schwartz divergence-free test `w` is approximable, in the combined
`L² + viscousFormSq` graph seminorm, by Galerkin test fields of the scheme `𝔊`.
This is the hypothesis threaded into `galerkin_limit_passage_R3` (issue #4 PR-3/PR-4)
to bridge the `WeakFormNS` test extension step (W2): `𝔊.tendsto_id` is L²-only and
does not control `viscousFormSq_R3(P_N w − w)`.

The predicate lives here — upstream of `AubinLionsLimitPassage` and the capstone chain —
so it can be imported by both without introducing a cycle.  It is NOT a structure field
of `R3GalerkinScheme` (route decision R2 in the architect plan, issue #93).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.R3TestApproxH1
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme → Prop

A countable Schwartz, divergence-free basis family for `L²_σ(ℝ³)` whose finite
spans exhaust `L²_σ(ℝ³)`.  This bundles the single classical input to the Galerkin
construction (existence + density of smooth div-free fields, a Helmholtz/Weyl fact)
WITHOUT proving it: it is a hypothesis, supplied by the caller.

nodes.doc: The time axis.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Time
nodes.signature: Type

nodes.doc:
Isolated analytic frontier: UNIFORM time-equicontinuity in L² of the Galerkin curves.

For every error `ε > 0`, there is a shift bound `δ > 0` such that, uniformly in `n` and in
`s, t ∈ [0,T]` with `|s - t| < δ`, the L² distance of the Galerkin states is `< ε`:
    `‖(galSeq n).u s - (galSeq n).u t‖_{L²(ℝ³)} < ε`.

This is the L²-modulus content of the Bochner–Sobolev bound `‖uₙ‖_{W^{1,?}(0,T;X)} ≤ C`,
which is derivable IN PRINCIPLE from `GalerkinSolutionData_R3.u_hasDeriv` + the energy/
regularity bounds, but whose vector-valued-Sobolev packaging mathlib LACKS (no
`W^{1,p}(0,T;X)`, no weak time derivative, no Aubin–Lions lemma).

Honesty (no-smuggle): this field speaks ONLY about the GIVEN Galerkin sequence's
self-equicontinuity in time; it supplies NEITHER a subsequence, NOR a limit, NOR any
space-time convergence, NOR any spatial compactness (that is P3's job). It is a uniform
modulus of continuity, nothing more.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TimeCompactnessInput
nodes.signature:
(𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) →
  (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) →
    (ν : ℝ) → ℝ → (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) → ((n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) → Prop

nodes.doc:
**Pythagorean L²-norm decomposition.** `‖u‖²_{L2VF} = ∑ⱼ ‖componentC j u‖²_{L2C}`.

The L²-inner product is the integral of the pointwise Euclidean inner product; the Euclidean
norm² of a `Fin 3`-vector is the sum of its component squares; and `‖componentC j u‖` (complex
embedding) equals `‖projComponent j u‖` (real component).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.L2VF_norm_sq_eq_sum_componentC
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), ‖u‖ ^ 2 = ∑ j, ‖(LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) u‖ ^ 2

nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)) (t : ℝ),
  HasDerivAt (fun s => ↑(c s)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c t))) t →
    HasDerivAt (fun s => 1 / 2 * ‖↑(c s)‖ ^ 2) (-ν * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq 1 ↑(c t)) t

nodes.doc:
**G1 (THE core).** Forward-global existence: there is `c : ℝ → Vₙ` with
`c 0 = velocityProjection_n n u₀` and `∀ t ≥ 0, HasDerivAt (↑∘c) (G_n (c t)) t`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.forwardGlobalSolution_exists
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (n : ℕ),
      ∃ c,
        ↑(c 0) = (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑u₀ ∧
          ∀ (t : ℝ), 0 ≤ t → HasDerivAt (fun s => ↑(c s)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c t))) t

nodes.doc: H¹ regularity of any `Vₙ`-curve point: a finite-Fourier-support field is in `memH1VF`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinCurve_reg_mem
nodes.signature: ∀ (n : ℕ) (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) v = v → LerayHopf.memH1VF v

nodes.doc:
**A1.** `⟪v, G_n v⟫ ≤ 0` for `v ∈ Vₙ` (dissipation at a point), from R2 + `b_self_zero` +
`stokesTestPairing_diag`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinField_inner_self_nonpos
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ),
  0 < ν → ∀ (v : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)), inner ℝ ↑v ↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n v) ≤ 0

nodes.doc:
**G1 helper (splice-agreement).** Two local solutions agreeing at one point agree on the
overlap.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinField_solution_agree
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (α β : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)) {a b t₀ : ℝ},
  a ≤ b →
    t₀ ∈ Set.Icc a b →
      α t₀ = β t₀ →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc a b, HasDerivAt α (LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (α t)) t) →
          (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc a b, HasDerivAt β (LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (β t)) t) →
            ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc a b, α t = β t

nodes.doc: **G1 helper (uniform `δ`).** A single uniform local-existence time on the a-priori ball.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinField_uniform_local_time
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (R : ℝ),
  ∃ δ,
    0 < δ ∧
      ∀ x₀ ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R,
        ∀ (t₀ : ℝ),
          ∃ α,
            α t₀ = x₀ ∧
              ∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo (t₀ - δ) (t₀ + δ), HasDerivAt α (LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (α t)) t

nodes.doc: The bilinear part of the Galerkin field `(u, u') ↦ (toDual).symm (w ↦ -b(u,u',w))`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_bilinearPart
nodes.signature:
LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms →
  ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) →L[ℝ] ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) →L[ℝ] ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_bilinearPart_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (u u' : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
  ((LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_bilinearPart F ν n) u) u' =
    (LerayHopf.Torus.rieszSymmCLM✝ n) (LerayHopf.Torus.bInner✝ F n u u')

nodes.doc:
The continuous linear functional on `Vₙ := velocitySpan n` whose Riesz representative is the
Galerkin vector field: `φ w = - ν · stokesTestPairing(u, w) - F.b(σu, σu, σw)`.

Right-linearity in `w` comes from `stokesTestPairing_add_right`/`_smul_right` (valid on `Vₙ`
elements via `velocityP_fixes_span`) and `F.b_add_3`/`F.b_smul_3` (with `velocitySpanToSigma`
additive/homogeneous).  Continuity is automatic on the finite-dim `Vₙ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_functional
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms → ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) →L[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_functional_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (u w : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
  (LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_functional F ν n u) w =
    -ν * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing ↑u ↑w -
      F.b (LerayHopf.velocitySpanToSigma n u) (LerayHopf.velocitySpanToSigma n u) (LerayHopf.velocitySpanToSigma n w)

nodes.doc: The linear part of the Galerkin field `u ↦ (toDual).symm (w ↦ -ν·stokes(u,w))`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_linearPart
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms → ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) →L[ℝ] ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_linearPart_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (u : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
  (LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_linearPart F ν n) u =
    (LerayHopf.Torus.rieszSymmCLM✝ n) (LerayHopf.Torus.stokesInner✝ ν n u)

nodes.doc:
The finite-dim Galerkin vector field `G_n` on `Vₙ`: the Riesz representative of
`galerkinODE_functional`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms → ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)

nodes.doc: **C1 (the enabler).** The Galerkin field `G_n` is `C¹` on the finite-dim `Vₙ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField_contDiff
nodes.signature: ∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ), ContDiff ℝ 1 (LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n)

nodes.doc: The Galerkin field equals `Bil(u,u) + Lin u`, by Riesz injectivity (test against all `w`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField_eq_parts
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (u : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
  LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n u =
    ((LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_bilinearPart F ν n) u) u + (LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_linearPart F ν n) u

nodes.doc:
**R2 (the weak↔vector-field bridge).** Testing `G_n u` against any `w ∈ Vₙ` recovers the
functional `w ↦ -ν·stokes(u,w) - b(u,u,w)`.  Sign/`ν` conventions match `u_ode`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField_spec
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (u w : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
  inner ℝ ↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n u) ↑w =
    -ν * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing ↑u ↑w -
      F.b (LerayHopf.velocitySpanToSigma n u) (LerayHopf.velocitySpanToSigma n u) (LerayHopf.velocitySpanToSigma n w)

nodes.doc:
**D — the per-`n` Galerkin solver (the deliverable).**  Assemble all 8 fields of the torus
`GalerkinSolutionData` from the forward-global curve `forwardGlobalSolution_exists` (G1) + the
Riesz characterization R2, the dissipation bounds, and the H¹ regularity — sorry-free, axiom-free.

The weak Galerkin ODE field `u_ode` is DERIVED from the autonomous field equation `c' = G_n(c)`
via R2 (`galerkinODE_vectorField_spec`): an arbitrary test `w : L2Sigma` fixed by `Pₙ` is viewed in
`Vₙ` (`mem_velocitySpan_of_fixed`, using `w ∈ L2Sigma`), then the spec converts the curve derivative
into the `-ν·stokes - b` weak form.  Forward-time (`0 ≤ t`) per the corrected structure.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinSolutionData_torus
nodes.signature:
(F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) →
  (ν : ℝ) → 0 < ν → (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) → (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n

nodes.doc: **E2 — uniform energy bound.** `½‖c t‖² ≤ ½‖Pₙ u₀‖²` for the G1 curve at `t ≥ 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkin_energy_bound_curve
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (n : ℕ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
      ↑(c 0) = (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑u₀ →
        (∀ (s : ℝ), 0 ≤ s → HasDerivAt (fun r => ↑(c r)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c s))) s) →
          ∀ (t : ℝ), 0 ≤ t → 1 / 2 * ‖↑(c t)‖ ^ 2 ≤ 1 / 2 * ‖(LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑u₀‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
The energy identity along the field flow (forward time), mirroring `galerkin_energy_identity`:
`d/dt ½‖c t‖² = -ν·viscousFormSq 1 (c t)`.  Here `c : ℝ → velocitySpan n` is the G1 curve and the
ambient derivative is the field.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkin_energy_identity_curve
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)) (t : ℝ),
  0 ≤ t →
    (∀ (s : ℝ), 0 ≤ s → HasDerivAt (fun r => ↑(c r)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c s))) s) →
      HasDerivAt (fun s => 1 / 2 * ‖↑(c s)‖ ^ 2) (-ν * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq 1 ↑(c t)) t

nodes.doc:
**reg_bound (E3 torus form, planner risk #3).**
`∫₀ᵀ h1EnergySq (c t) ≤ T‖u₀‖² + ‖u₀‖²/(2ν)`.

Split `h1EnergySq = ‖·‖² + (2π)²⁻¹·viscousFormSq 1`; the L²-part integrates to `≤ T‖u₀‖²`
(pointwise `‖c t‖² ≤ ‖u₀‖²` from A3 + nonexpansive `Pₙ`), and the gradient part is
`≤ (2π)²⁻¹·‖u₀‖²/(2ν) ≤ ‖u₀‖²/(2ν)` (since `(2π)²⁻¹ ≤ 1`) via `galerkin_viscous_bound`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkin_reg_bound_curve
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (n : ℕ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
      ↑(c 0) = (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑u₀ →
        (∀ (s : ℝ), 0 ≤ s → HasDerivAt (fun r => ↑(c r)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c s))) s) →
          ∀ {T : ℝ}, 0 < T → ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T, LerayHopf.h1EnergySq ↑(c t) ≤ T * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2 + ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2 / (2 * ν)

nodes.doc:
**E3-analog — viscous dissipation bound.** `∫₀ᵀ viscousFormSq 1 (c t) dt ≤ ‖u₀‖²/(2ν)`.

From the energy identity `d/dt ½‖c t‖² = -ν·viscousFormSq 1 (c t)`, integrating the negation
`-½‖c t‖²` (whose forward derivative is `ν·viscousFormSq 1 (c t) ≥ 0`) over `[0,T]` gives
`ν∫ viscousFormSq 1 (c t) ≤ ½‖c 0‖² - ½‖c T‖² ≤ ½‖c 0‖² ≤ ½‖u₀‖²`.  Case-splits on integrability
of the viscous integrand (mirrors R3's E3); when non-integrable the integral is `0` and the bound
is vacuous (RHS `≥ 0`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.galerkin_viscous_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (n : ℕ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
      ↑(c 0) = (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑u₀ →
        (∀ (s : ℝ), 0 ≤ s → HasDerivAt (fun r => ↑(c r)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c s))) s) →
          ∀ {T : ℝ}, 0 < T → ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T, LerayHopf.viscousFormSq 1 ↑(c t) ≤ ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2 / (2 * ν)

nodes.doc:
**`h1EnergySq` split on `Vₙ`.** For `u ∈ Vₙ` (finite Fourier support):
`h1EnergySq u = ‖u‖²_{L2VF} + (2π)²⁻¹ · viscousFormSq 1 u`.

The H¹ weight `(1 + ∑ᵢkᵢ²)` splits into the L²-part (`1`) — which is `‖u‖²` by Parseval +
Pythagoras — and the gradient part (`∑ᵢkᵢ²`), which is `(2π)²⁻¹·viscousFormSq 1 u`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.h1EnergySq_eq_L2_add_viscous
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) u = u →
    LerayHopf.h1EnergySq u = ‖u‖ ^ 2 + ((2 * Real.pi) ^ 2)⁻¹ * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq 1 u

nodes.doc: `t ↦ ‖c t‖²` is continuous (the ambient curve is differentiable, hence continuous).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.normSq_curve_continuous
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
  (∀ (s : ℝ), 0 ≤ s → HasDerivAt (fun r => ↑(c r)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c s))) s) →
    ∀ {T : ℝ}, ContinuousOn (fun t => ‖↑(c t)‖ ^ 2) (Set.Icc 0 T)

nodes.doc: **A3.** Any forward local solution on `[0, T]` stays in the ball `‖c t‖ ≤ ‖c 0‖`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.norm_le_of_forwardSolution
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)) {T : ℝ},
      0 ≤ T →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, HasDerivAt (fun s => ↑(c s)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c t))) t) →
          ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖↑(c t)‖ ≤ ‖↑(c 0)‖

nodes.doc: The Riesz inverse as a `ContinuousLinearMap` `(Vₙ →L[ℝ] ℝ) →L[ℝ] Vₙ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.rieszSymmCLM
nodes.signature: (n : ℕ) → (↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) →L[ℝ] ℝ) →L[ℝ] ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.rieszSymmCLM_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (φ : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) →L[ℝ] ℝ),
  (LerayHopf.Torus.rieszSymmCLM✝ n) φ = (InnerProductSpace.toDual ℝ ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)).symm φ

nodes.doc: The ambient derivative of the assembled curve equals the field, at forward times.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.solutionCurve_deriv_eq
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
  (∀ (s : ℝ), 0 ≤ s → HasDerivAt (fun r => ↑(c r)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c s))) s) →
    ∀ (t : ℝ),
      0 ≤ t →
        deriv (fun s => ↑(LerayHopf.velocitySpanToSigma n (c s))) t =
          ↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c t))

nodes.doc: Forward-time differentiability of the assembled curve (proved; `t ≥ 0`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.solutionCurve_hasDeriv_forward
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
  (∀ (s : ℝ), 0 ≤ s → HasDerivAt (fun r => ↑(c r)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c s))) s) →
    ∀ (t : ℝ),
      0 ≤ t →
        HasDerivAt (fun s => ↑(LerayHopf.velocitySpanToSigma n (c s)))
          (deriv (fun s => ↑(LerayHopf.velocitySpanToSigma n (c s))) t) t

nodes.doc: Local copy of `ContDiffAt.exists_forall_mem_closedBall_exists_eq_forall_mem_Ioo_hasDerivAt`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.solve_c1_exists
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : NormedSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E] {f : E → E} {x₀ : E},
  ContDiffAt ℝ 1 f x₀ →
    ∀ (t₀ : ℝ),
      ∃ r > 0,
        ∃ ε > 0, ∀ x ∈ Metric.closedBall x₀ r, ∃ α, α t₀ = x ∧ ∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo (t₀ - ε) (t₀ + ε), HasDerivAt α (f (α t)) t

nodes.doc: Tiling induction: a forward solution exists on `[0, k·δ/2]` for every `k`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.solve_exists_on_step
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (n : ℕ) (x₀ : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)) {δ : ℝ},
      0 < δ →
        (∀ y ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 ‖x₀‖,
            ∀ (t₀ : ℝ),
              ∃ α,
                α t₀ = y ∧
                  ∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo (t₀ - δ) (t₀ + δ),
                    HasDerivAt α (LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (α t)) t) →
          ∀ (k : ℕ),
            ∃ c,
              c 0 = x₀ ∧
                ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 (↑k * (δ / 2)), HasDerivAt c (LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c t)) t

nodes.doc: Transport an intrinsic `HasDerivAt` in `Vₙ` to the ambient `L2VF` curve.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.solve_hasDerivAt_ambient
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)) (t : ℝ),
  HasDerivAt c (LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c t)) t →
    HasDerivAt (fun s => ↑(c s)) (↑(LerayHopf.Torus.galerkinODE_vectorField F ν n (c t))) t

nodes.doc: Two-sided uniqueness on `Icc a b` with interior initial time `t₀`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.solve_ode_unique_Icc
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : NormedSpace ℝ E] {v : ℝ → E → E} {s : ℝ → Set E} {K : NNReal}
  {f' g' : ℝ → E} {a b t₀ : ℝ},
  (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo a b, LipschitzOnWith K (v t) (s t)) →
    t₀ ∈ Set.Ioo a b →
      ContinuousOn f' (Set.Icc a b) →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo a b, HasDerivAt f' (v t (f' t)) t) →
          (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo a b, f' t ∈ s t) →
            ContinuousOn g' (Set.Icc a b) →
              (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo a b, HasDerivAt g' (v t (g' t)) t) →
                (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo a b, g' t ∈ s t) → f' t₀ = g' t₀ → Set.EqOn f' g' (Set.Icc a b)

nodes.doc: Backward uniqueness on `Icc a b` with `b` the initial time (time-reversed).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.solve_ode_unique_left
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : NormedSpace ℝ E] {v : ℝ → E → E} {s : ℝ → Set E} {K : NNReal}
  {f' g' : ℝ → E} {a b : ℝ},
  (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioc a b, LipschitzOnWith K (v t) (s t)) →
    ContinuousOn f' (Set.Icc a b) →
      (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioc a b, HasDerivWithinAt f' (v t (f' t)) (Set.Iic t) t) →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioc a b, f' t ∈ s t) →
          ContinuousOn g' (Set.Icc a b) →
            (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioc a b, HasDerivWithinAt g' (v t (g' t)) (Set.Iic t) t) →
              (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioc a b, g' t ∈ s t) → f' b = g' b → Set.EqOn f' g' (Set.Icc a b)

nodes.doc: Forward uniqueness on `Icc a b` with `a` the initial time.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.solve_ode_unique_right
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : NormedSpace ℝ E] {v : ℝ → E → E} {s : ℝ → Set E} {K : NNReal}
  {f' g' : ℝ → E} {a b : ℝ},
  (∀ t ∈ Set.Ico a b, LipschitzOnWith K (v t) (s t)) →
    ContinuousOn f' (Set.Icc a b) →
      (∀ t ∈ Set.Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt f' (v t (f' t)) (Set.Ici t) t) →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Ico a b, f' t ∈ s t) →
          ContinuousOn g' (Set.Icc a b) →
            (∀ t ∈ Set.Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt g' (v t (g' t)) (Set.Ici t) t) →
              (∀ t ∈ Set.Ico a b, g' t ∈ s t) → f' a = g' a → Set.EqOn f' g' (Set.Icc a b)

nodes.doc: Local copy of `IsPicardLindelof.exists_eq_forall_mem_Icc_hasDerivWithinAt`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.solve_pl_exists
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : NormedSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E] {f : ℝ → E → E}
  {tmin tmax : ℝ} {t₀ : ↑(Set.Icc tmin tmax)} {x₀ x : E} {a r L K : NNReal},
  IsPicardLindelof f t₀ x₀ a r L K →
    x ∈ Metric.closedBall x₀ ↑r →
      ∃ α, α ↑t₀ = x ∧ ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc tmin tmax, HasDerivWithinAt α (f t (α t)) (Set.Icc tmin tmax) t

nodes.doc: Inner functional: `w ↦ -ν·stokes(σu, σw)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.stokesInner
nodes.signature: ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) →L[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.stokesInner_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (u w : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
  (LerayHopf.Torus.stokesInner✝ ν n u) w = -ν * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing ↑u ↑w

nodes.doc: Outer map: `u ↦ stokesInner u`, linear in `u` by left-linearity of stokes on `Vₙ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.stokesOut
nodes.signature: ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) →L[ℝ] ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n) →L[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.stokesOut_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (u : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)),
  (LerayHopf.Torus.stokesOut✝ ν n) u = LerayHopf.Torus.stokesInner✝ ν n u

nodes.doc: Left-additivity on `Vₙ`, via right-additivity + symmetry.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.stokesTestPairing_add_left
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (u u' w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) w = w →
    LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing (u + u') w = LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u w + LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u' w

nodes.doc:
Right-additivity of `stokesTestPairing` on `Vₙ`: linearity in the second slot.

On `Vₙ` the coefficient of `w` (and `w'`) vanishes outside `fourierBox n`; the bilinear
integrand is additive in `ŵ`, and the per-`k` term is additive, so the `∑'` is additive.  Proved
via `tsum`-additivity using that each tsum is actually a finite sum over the box.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.stokesTestPairing_add_right
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (u w w' : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) u = u →
    LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u (w + w') = LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u w + LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u w'

nodes.doc:
**Stokes diagonal.** `stokesTestPairing u u = viscousFormSq 1 u` for all `u`.

Both are global `∑'`-tsums; on the diagonal `Re[ûⱼ(k)·conj(ûⱼ(k))] = ‖ûⱼ(k)‖²`, matching
`viscousFormSq 1`'s integrand termwise.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.stokesTestPairing_diag
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u u = LerayHopf.viscousFormSq 1 u

nodes.doc: Left-homogeneity on `Vₙ`, via right-homogeneity + symmetry.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.stokesTestPairing_smul_left
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ) (u w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) w = w → LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing (c • u) w = c * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u w

nodes.doc: Right-homogeneity of `stokesTestPairing` on `Vₙ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.stokesTestPairing_smul_right
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ) (u w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) u = u → LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u (c • w) = c * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u w

nodes.doc:
The torus stokes pairing is symmetric: `stokesTestPairing u w = stokesTestPairing w u`.

Pointwise, `Re[ûⱼ(k)·conj(ŵⱼ(k))] = Re[ŵⱼ(k)·conj(ûⱼ(k))]` (conjugate transpose), so each
summand of the `∑'`-tsum matches; no convergence is needed.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.stokesTestPairing_symm
nodes.signature: ∀ (u w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u w = LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing w u

nodes.doc: The squared Fourier coefficients of any `f : L2C` are summable (from `lp` membership).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.summable_norm_mFourierCoeff3_sq
nodes.signature: ∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C), Summable fun k => ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f k‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
`velocityProjection_n n` fixes any element of `velocitySpan n` (as a `velocitySpan`-coercion
hypothesis convenient for the stokes right-linearity helpers).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.velocityP_fixes_coe
nodes.signature: ∀ (n : ℕ) (w : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)), (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑w = ↑w

nodes.doc:
The Galerkin projection is L²-nonexpansive: `‖Pₙ u‖ ≤ ‖u‖`.

By Pythagoras + per-component Parseval, `‖Pₙ u‖² = ∑ⱼ∑_{k∈box}‖ûⱼ(k)‖² ≤ ∑ⱼ∑'ₖ‖ûⱼ(k)‖² = ‖u‖²`
(the cutoff `Pₙ` only drops Fourier modes).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus.velocityProjection_n_norm_le
nodes.signature: ∀ (n : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), ‖(LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) u‖ ≤ ‖u‖

nodes.doc:
The spatial 3-torus 𝕋³, realized as `UnitAddTorus (Fin 3)` = `Fin 3 → UnitAddCircle`,
with its product Haar (probability) measure.

`UnitAddCircle = AddCircle (1 : ℝ)` carries a `MeasureSpace` instance via
`AddCircle.measureSpace` (total mass 1), and the product measure on
`Fin 3 → UnitAddCircle` is provided by `MeasureTheory.MeasureSpace.pi`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus3
nodes.signature: Type

nodes.doc:
The bundle of T³ Navier–Stokes forms: only the trilinear convection form `b`
(the viscous form is the concrete `stokesTestPairing`, NOT axiomatized).

**Non-vacuity:** The field `b_galerkin` pins `b` to `galerkinConvection` on Galerkin
subspaces, which is generically nonzero.  This excludes the vacuous witness `b := 0`
and ensures the existence witness `torus3_NSForms_exists` (issue #22, in
`TorusConvectionForm.lean`) is genuinely about Navier–Stokes (not Stokes or heat).

**Antisymmetry convention:** `b u v w = -b u w v` (skew in the last two slots).
This is the correct form for the T³ convection form `b(u,v,w) = ∫ (u·∇v)·w`.

**Multilinearity:** `b` is trilinear (additive + ℝ-homogeneous in each argument).
Required to exclude pathological `F` where `b(u,u,0)≠0` (which would render
`galerkin_ode_solution` inconsistent when tested against `w=0`).

**Smooth-test convection bound (Fix 2):** For a Galerkin test `w`, the bound is
`|b(u,v,w)| ≤ C(w) · ‖u‖_{L²} · ‖v‖_{L²}`.  TRUE: `b(u,v,w) = -∫(u·∇)w·v`,
so `|b| ≤ ‖∇w‖_∞ ‖u‖_{L²} ‖v‖_{L²}` with `‖∇w‖_∞ < ∞` for trig polynomials.
This is the correct shape for the strong-L²(0,T) limit passage.

**Viscous form (v4 de-axiomatization):** The Stokes form is no longer a field of this
structure.  It is the concrete definition `stokesTestPairing`, which is sound for all
`u : L2VF` (tsum returns 0 off the summable set).  The diagonal recovers `viscousFormSq 1 u`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms
nodes.signature: Type

nodes.doc:
**b(u, u, u) = 0** follows purely from antisymmetry.

Proof: `b(u, u, u) = -b(u, u, u)` by `b_antisymm u u u`, so `b(u, u, u) = 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms.b_self_zero
nodes.signature: ∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma), F.b u u u = 0

nodes.doc: Subtraction in the first slot of `F.b` (from additivity + `(-1)`-homogeneity).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms.b_sub_1
nodes.signature: ∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (a b v z : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma), F.b (a - b) v z = F.b a v z - F.b b v z

nodes.doc: Subtraction in the second slot of `F.b`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms.b_sub_2
nodes.signature: ∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (u a b z : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma), F.b u (a - b) z = F.b u a z - F.b u b z

nodes.doc:
**The conditional concrete `Torus3NSForms` — a genuine derivation (issue #22).**

Given the isolated `TorusConvectionGap`, a genuine `Torus3NSForms` exists, **sorry-free**.  Each
`Torus3NSForms` field is obtained as follows (mirroring ℝ³ `R3NSForms_of_gap`):

- `b_add_{1,2,3}` — from `g.b_multilinear`: rewrite `g.b _ _ _ = B _ _ _`, close by `B`'s
                  `map_add`.  Works for *arbitrary* `u v w : L²_σ`, no continuity/density;
- `b_smul_{1,2,3}` — same, via `B`'s `map_smul`;
- `b_antisymm`  — directly from `g.b_antisymm_gap`;
- `b_bound`     — from `g.b_bound_test` (fixed constant `C(w)` on the test diagonal) +
                  `g.b_cont_fixedTest` + `g.galerkinTest_dense`, via the `le_of_tendsto_of_tendsto`
                  limiting argument (the box-truncated `galerkinConvection_bound` is *not* used
                  here: its constant is `n`-dependent, whereas `b_bound` needs a single `C(w)`);
- `b_galerkin`  — directly from `g.b_galerkin_pin`.

So `TorusConvectionGap` does **not** hand over the quantitative `Torus3NSForms` content ready-made:
the unrestricted bound is derived from the test-diagonal bound through fixed-test continuity and
density; only the algebraic trilinear/antisymmetry content is asserted, as the honest residual of
the missing weak operator.  Non-vacuity flows through `g.b_galerkin_pin` (excludes `b = 0`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms_of_gap
nodes.signature: ∀ (g : LerayHopf.TorusConvectionGap), Nonempty LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms

nodes.doc: The antisymmetrizer `A := (id − swap)/2` on `L²_σ ⊗ L²_σ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.antisymmetrizer
nodes.signature: TensorProduct ℝ ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma →ₗ[ℝ] TensorProduct ℝ ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.antisymmetrizer_tmul
nodes.signature:
∀ (v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.antisymmetrizer (v ⊗ₜ[ℝ] w) = 2⁻¹ • (v ⊗ₜ[ℝ] w - w ⊗ₜ[ℝ] v)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.antisymmetrizer_tmul_swap
nodes.signature:
∀ (v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.antisymmetrizer (w ⊗ₜ[ℝ] v) =
    -LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.antisymmetrizer (v ⊗ₜ[ℝ] w)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.coeff_proj_add
nodes.signature:
∀ (j : Fin 3) (u u' : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ),
  LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) (u + u')) k =
    LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) u) k +
      LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) u') k

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.coeff_proj_smul
nodes.signature:
∀ (j : Fin 3) (c : ℝ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ),
  LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) (c • u)) k =
    ↑c * LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) u) k

nodes.doc:
**`convBLTgalerkin`.** Jointly continuous bilinear form
`L2Sigma →L[ℝ] L2Sigma →L[ℝ] ℝ` extending `(u, v) ↦ convFormFourier u v w` for a
fixed Galerkin test `w : galerkinTestSpan`.

Constructed by extending the Galerkin-test bound through the H¹/Galerkin-test slice.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBLTgalerkin
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.galerkinTestSpan → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma →L[ℝ] ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma →L[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.doc:
**`convBLTgalerkin_span_linear`.** The map `w ↦ convBLTgalerkin w` is
ℝ-linear over `galerkinTestSpan`.

Linearity follows by dense agreement on the Galerkin-test square, using `convFormFourier`'s
linearity in the third slot.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBLTgalerkinLin
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.galerkinTestSpan →ₗ[ℝ] ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma →L[ℝ] ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma →L[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBLTgalerkin_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.galerkinTestSpan) (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  ((LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBLTgalerkin w) u) v = LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convValW ↑u ↑v ↑w

nodes.doc:
**`convBLTgalerkin_overlap`.** For `s, s' ∈ 𝒢` and any `u`,
`convBLTgalerkin s' u (s : L2Sigma) = -convBLTgalerkin s u (s' : L2Sigma)`.

This is the torus analog of `convBLTspan_overlap`, following from `convFormFourier_antisymm_galerkinTest`
extended to all `u` by density, exactly as R3.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBLTgalerkin_overlap
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (s s' : ↥LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.galerkinTestSpan),
  ((LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBLTgalerkin s') u) ↑s =
    -((LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBLTgalerkin s) u) ↑s'

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBLTw
nodes.signature: (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) → LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma →L[ℝ] ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma →L[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBLTw_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (hw : LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w) (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  ((LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBLTw w hw) u) v = LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convValW (↑u) (↑v) w

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convBilL2Sigma
nodes.signature: (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) → LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma →ₗ[ℝ] ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma →ₗ[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormFourier_eq_galerkin
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑u = ↑u →
    (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑v = ↑v →
      LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest v →
        LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w → LerayHopf.convFormFourier u v w = LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n ↑u ↑v ↑w

nodes.doc:
**`convFormL2_antisymm` [proved from `antisymmetrizer_tmul_swap`].**
`b u v w = − b u w v` for all `u v w`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_antisymm
nodes.signature:
∀ (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def u v w = -LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def u w v

nodes.doc:
**`convFormL2_bound_galerkinTest`.** For Galerkin tests `u, v, w`,
`|b u v w| ≤ C(w) · ‖u‖ · ‖v‖` for some `C(w) ≥ 0`.

This transfers the bilinear bound from `convBLTgalerkin`, built from
`galerkinConvection_bound` / `convSummand_summable`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_bound_galerkinTest
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w →
    ∃ C,
      ∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
        LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest u →
          LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest v → |LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def u v w| ≤ C * ‖↑u‖ * ‖↑v‖

nodes.doc:
**`convFormL2_cont_fixedTest` [proved].** For a Galerkin test `w`, `(u, v) ↦ b u v w` is
jointly L²-continuous: on the determined slice it equals `convBLTw w`, a `ContinuousLinearMap`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_cont_fixedTest
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w → Continuous fun p => LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def p.1 p.2 w

nodes.doc:
**`convFormL2_def` (`b`).** The determined trilinear convection
form:

`b u v w := detExtend u (v ⊗ₜ w)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma → ℝ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def_eq
nodes.signature:
∀ (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def u v w = (LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.detExtend u) (v ⊗ₜ[ℝ] w)

nodes.doc: **Bridge: on a Galerkin test `w`, the determined form equals the genuine bilinear.**
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def_eq_convValW
nodes.signature:
∀ (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w →
    LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def u v w = LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convValW (↑u) (↑v) w

nodes.doc:
**`convFormL2_galerkinTest_dense` [proved sorry-free].** Every `u : L2Sigma` is an
L²-limit of Galerkin tests.

Proof: `velocityProjection_n n u → u` by `velocityProjection_n_tendsto`; each
`velocityProjection_n n u` is a Galerkin test by definition of `IsGalerkinTest`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_galerkinTest_dense
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma), ∃ s, (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest (s n)) ∧ Filter.Tendsto s Filter.atTop (nhds u)

nodes.doc:
**`convFormL2_galerkin_pin`.** On Galerkin subspaces `Vₙ`,
`b` restricts to the finite box-truncated form `galerkinConvection n`.

This follows from `convFormFourier` matching `galerkinConvection` on the finite box plus the
determination identity on the Galerkin-test slice.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_galerkin_pin
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑u = ↑u →
    (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑v = ↑v →
      (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) ↑w = ↑w →
        LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def u v w = LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n ↑u ↑v ↑w

nodes.doc:
**`convFormL2_multilinear` [proved from `detExtend` linearity].** There exists a genuine
ℝ-trilinear map `B` with `b u v w = B u v w`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_multilinear
nodes.signature: ∃ B, ∀ (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma), LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convFormL2_def u v w = ((B u) v) w

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → Fin 3 → Fin 3 → (Fin 3 → ℤ) → (Fin 3 → ℤ) → ℂ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW_add_u
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (u u' v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF) (i a : Fin 3) (kl : (Fin 3 → ℤ) × (Fin 3 → ℤ)),
  LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW (u + u') v (↑w) i a kl.1 kl.2 =
    LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW u v (↑w) i a kl.1 kl.2 +
      LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW u' v (↑w) i a kl.1 kl.2

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW_add_v
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (u v v' : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF) (i a : Fin 3) (kl : (Fin 3 → ℤ) × (Fin 3 → ℤ)),
  LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW u (v + v') (↑w) i a kl.1 kl.2 =
    LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW u v (↑w) i a kl.1 kl.2 +
      LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW u v' (↑w) i a kl.1 kl.2

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW_add_w
nodes.signature:
∀ (u v w w' : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF) (i a : Fin 3) (kl : (Fin 3 → ℤ) × (Fin 3 → ℤ)),
  LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW u v (w + w') i a kl.1 kl.2 =
    LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW u v w i a kl.1 kl.2 +
      LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.convSummandW u v w' i a kl.1 kl.2

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.dom_summable
nodes.signature:
∀ (U V Wc : (Fin 3 → ℤ) → ℝ) (n : ℕ),
  (Summable fun k => U k ^ 2) →
    (Summable fun l => V l ^ 2) →
      (∀ (k : Fin 3 → ℤ), 0 ≤ U k) →
        (∀ (l : Fin 3 → ℤ), 0 ≤ V l) →
          (∀ (m : Fin 3 → ℤ), 0 ≤ Wc m) →
            (∀ m ∉ LerayHopf.fourierBox n, Wc m = 0) →
              Summable fun kl => Wc (-(kl.1 + kl.2)) * (U kl.1 * V (-kl.1 - -(kl.1 + kl.2)))

nodes.name: LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.dom_tsum_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (fU fV : ↥LerayHopf.L2C) (Wc : (Fin 3 → ℤ) → ℝ) (n : ℕ),
  (∀ (m : Fin 3 → ℤ), 0 ≤ Wc m) →
    (∀ m ∉ LerayHopf.fourierBox n, Wc m = 0) →
      ∑' (kl : (Fin 3 → ℤ) × (Fin 3 → ℤ)),
          Wc (-(kl.1 + kl.2)) *
            (‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 fU kl.1‖ * ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 fV (-kl.1 - -(kl.1 + kl.2))‖) ≤
        (∑ m ∈ LerayHopf.fourierBox n, Wc m) *
          (LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.l2coeff fU * LerayHopf.TorusConvectionExtension.l2coeff fV)

nodes.doc:
The **weak Navier–Stokes equation** for a curve `u : Time → E.H`.

A curve `u` satisfies the weak NS equation on `(0, T)` with viscosity `ν` iff
for every test function `ψ : Time → ℝ` that is `C¹`, has compact support contained
in the open interval `(0, T)` (so boundary terms vanish), and for every spatial
test vector `w : E.H` satisfying `E.isTest w` (e.g. a smooth/Galerkin test function),
the following integral identity holds:

  `∫ t in 0..T, (-(⟪u t, w⟫_ℝ) * ψ'(t) + ψ(t) * (ν * E.viscousForm (u t) w + E.convForm (u t) (u t) w)) = 0`

The `tsupport ψ ⊆ Set.Ioo 0 T` condition ensures the test function vanishes at the
endpoints, so the integration-by-parts boundary terms are zero (fixing defect 2).

The `E.isTest w` condition restricts spatial tests to the admissible class
(e.g. smooth/Galerkin div-free vectors), matching the Faedo–Galerkin limit-passage
argument (Fix 3 of the Codex axiom audit).

**Side-condition design note (issue #64).** `WeakFormNS` is the distributional identity
only. It deliberately does not bundle interval-integrability or measurability side conditions
for the displayed integrand, because the current capstones package those obligations in the
domain-specific full solution structures (`LerayHopfSolutionFull` and
`LerayHopfSolutionFull_R3`) through their `energy_class` fields and in the limit-passage
axioms that produce the good representative. Public statements should therefore cite the
full solution structures, not bare `WeakFormNS`, as the weak-solution contract.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.WeakFormNS
nodes.signature: ℝ → ℝ → (E : LerayHopf.DissipativeEvolution) → (LerayHopf.Time → E.H) → Prop

nodes.doc:
Generic bound: the interval integral of `R` over any subinterval `[x, y] ⊆ [a, b]` is
absolutely bounded by the integral of a nonnegative continuous dominating function `bd`
over the whole interval `[a, b]`, whenever `|R| ≤ bd` pointwise on `[a, b]`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.abs_intervalIntegral_le_of_le_abs
nodes.signature:
∀ {R bd : ℝ → ℝ} {a b x y : ℝ},
  a ≤ b →
    x ∈ Set.Icc a b →
      y ∈ Set.Icc a b →
        ContinuousOn R (Set.Icc a b) →
          ContinuousOn bd (Set.Icc a b) →
            (∀ σ ∈ Set.Icc a b, |R σ| ≤ bd σ) → |∫ (σ : ℝ) in x..y, R σ| ≤ ∫ (σ : ℝ) in a..b, bd σ

nodes.doc:
**Abstract Galerkin energy identity.**

Given a curve `u : ℝ → H` in a real inner product space satisfying the abstract
Galerkin energy law (the inner-product form of the projected ODE), with trilinear
skew-symmetry `B w w w = 0` killing the nonlinear term, the kinetic energy
`(1/2) * ‖u(t)‖²` has derivative `- D(u(t))` at every `t`.

Proof sketch for lean-prover:
1. `HasDerivAt.norm_sq` applied to `(hODE t).1` gives
   `HasDerivAt (‖u ·‖²) (2 * ⟪u t, deriv u t⟫) t`.
2. The ODE law `(hODE t).2` gives
   `⟪deriv u t, u t⟫ = - D(u t) - B(u t)(u t)(u t)`.
3. `hB` gives `B(u t)(u t)(u t) = 0`, so `⟪deriv u t, u t⟫ = - D(u t)`.
4. Inner product symmetry: `⟪u t, deriv u t⟫ = ⟪deriv u t, u t⟫`
   (via `real_inner_comm` or `inner_comm`).
5. Scale by `1/2` using `HasDerivAt.inner` with the constant function `1/2`
   (since `HasDerivAt.const_mul` is outside the import closure of this file),
   then rewrite the resulting function and derivative value with `ring`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.abstract_galerkin_energy_identity
nodes.signature:
∀ {H : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup H] [inst_1 : InnerProductSpace ℝ H] (u : ℝ → H) (D : H → ℝ)
  (B : H → H → H → ℝ),
  (∀ (w : H), B w w w = 0) →
    (∀ (t : ℝ), HasDerivAt u (deriv u t) t ∧ inner ℝ (deriv u t) (u t) + D (u t) + B (u t) (u t) (u t) = 0) →
      ∀ (t : ℝ), HasDerivAt (fun s => 1 / 2 * ‖u s‖ ^ 2) (-D (u t)) t

nodes.doc:
**Abstract Galerkin energy inequality.**

Integrating the energy identity over `[s, t]` via FTC-2 gives the energy
inequality: `(1/2) * ‖u(t)‖² + ∫_s^t D(u(τ)) dτ ≤ (1/2) * ‖u(s)‖²`.

The equality `d/dt (half * ‖u‖²) = - D(u)` from `abstract_galerkin_energy_identity`
makes the integral an equality; the `≤` form matches `EnergyInequality`.

Proof sketch for lean-prover:
1. Apply `intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt` with
   `f := fun s => (1/2) * ‖u s‖²` and `f' := fun τ => - D(u τ)`, using
   `abstract_galerkin_energy_identity` for the `HasDerivAt` hypothesis.
   The integrability follows from `hint` and `IntervalIntegrable.neg`.
2. This gives `∫ τ in s..t, - D(u τ) = (1/2) * ‖u t‖² - (1/2) * ‖u s‖²`.
3. Rewrite via `intervalIntegral.integral_neg`:
   `- ∫ τ in s..t, D(u τ) = (1/2) * ‖u t‖² - (1/2) * ‖u s‖²`.
4. Rearrange with `linarith`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.abstract_galerkin_energy_inequality
nodes.signature:
∀ {H : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup H] [inst_1 : InnerProductSpace ℝ H] (u : ℝ → H) (D : H → ℝ)
  (B : H → H → H → ℝ),
  (∀ (w : H), B w w w = 0) →
    (∀ (t : ℝ), HasDerivAt u (deriv u t) t ∧ inner ℝ (deriv u t) (u t) + D (u t) + B (u t) (u t) (u t) = 0) →
      (∀ (a b : ℝ), IntervalIntegrable (fun τ => D (u τ)) MeasureTheory.volume a b) →
        ∀ {s t : ℝ}, 0 ≤ s → s ≤ t → 1 / 2 * ‖u t‖ ^ 2 + ∫ (τ : ℝ) in s..t, D (u τ) ≤ 1 / 2 * ‖u s‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
**Abstract Galerkin approximation satisfies `EnergyInequality`.**

Under the same hypotheses as `abstract_galerkin_energy_inequality`, the explicit
`EnergyData` with

    E t    := (1/2) * ‖u t‖²
    A s t  := ∫ τ in s..t, D(u τ)
    ν      := 1

satisfies the abstract `EnergyInequality` from `LerayHopf.EnergySkeleton`.

This closes the loop: `energy_nonincreasing_from_nonnegative_dissipation` then
gives `E t ≤ E s` for `0 ≤ s ≤ t`, provided `D ≥ 0`.

Proof sketch for lean-prover:
Unfold `EnergyInequality`, introduce `s t hs hst`, apply
`abstract_galerkin_energy_inequality`, and simplify `1 * A = A` via `one_mul`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.abstract_galerkin_satisfies_EnergyInequality
nodes.signature:
∀ {H : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup H] [inst_1 : InnerProductSpace ℝ H] (u : ℝ → H) (D : H → ℝ)
  (B : H → H → H → ℝ),
  (∀ (w : H), B w w w = 0) →
    (∀ (t : ℝ), HasDerivAt u (deriv u t) t ∧ inner ℝ (deriv u t) (u t) + D (u t) + B (u t) (u t) (u t) = 0) →
      (∀ (a b : ℝ), IntervalIntegrable (fun τ => D (u τ)) MeasureTheory.volume a b) →
        LerayHopf.EnergyInequality
          { E := fun t => 1 / 2 * ‖u t‖ ^ 2, A := fun s t => ∫ (τ : ℝ) in s..t, D (u τ), ν := 1 }

`add_iInf`/`iInf_add` + `iSup` monotonicity; avoids the absent `le_liminf_add` import.

nodes.doc:
**General du-Bois-Reymond against Hermitian Schwartz tests.**  A locally integrable
`f : ℝ³ → ℂ` that integrates to zero against every *Hermitian* Schwartz symbol
(`G(-ξ) = conj(G ξ)`) vanishes a.e.

Same even/odd reduction as the forward direction of `mem_sigma_iff_fourier_transverse`, but
the parity assignment is mirrored: for a real smooth compactly-supported test `g`, the
even part `gE = g + g∘neg` complexifies to a *Hermitian* symbol directly, while `i·gO`
(`gO = g − g∘neg`) is Hermitian; both feed the hypothesis, and `2g = gE + gO`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.ae_zero_of_hermitianTest
nodes.signature:
∀ (f : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℂ),
  MeasureTheory.LocallyIntegrable f MeasureTheory.volume →
    (∀ (G : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
        (∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), G (-ξ) = (starRingEnd ℂ) (G ξ)) → ∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), G ξ * f ξ = 0) →
      ∀ᵐ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), f ξ = 0

measure `(volume.restrict s).prod volume`: pull `g ∘ snd` back along the measure-preserving shear
`(x, h) ↦ (x, x + h)`, then transfer to the restricted product (absolutely continuous).

nodes.doc:
**Anti-Hermitian test integral vanishing.**  If `u ∈ L2Sigma_R3`, then for every
*anti-Hermitian* Schwartz symbol `h` (`h(-ξ) = -conj(h ξ)`), the Fourier-side pairing
`∫ h(ξ) · T_u(ξ) dξ` vanishes.

This is `mem_sigma_iff_fourier_integral_zero` upgraded by `(P2)`: the available test symbols
`testSymbol φ` (`φ` real Schwartz) range over *all* anti-Hermitian Schwartz symbols
(`schwartz_antiHermitian_has_testSymbol_preimage`), so the vanishing of every `testSymbol`
pairing extends to every anti-Hermitian Schwartz pairing.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.antiHermitianTest_integral_zero

      ∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), h ξ * LerayHopf.transverseDefect✝ u ξ = 0

`inner` continuous).

        ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume),

nodes.doc:
**Aubin–Lions package on ℝ³ from the isolated time-compactness input (CLOSED).**

Produces `aubin_lions_R3`'s conclusion (`AubinLionsPackage_R3`), conditional on P3's local
spatial compactness (via `LocalRellichInput`).  The time-compactness extraction is supplied
UNCONDITIONALLY by `galerkinSpaceTimeExtraction_R3` (no separate `TimeCompactnessInput` modulus
hypothesis — it is absorbed into that single axiom).  The conclusion type is exactly
`AubinLionsPackage_R3 𝔊 F ν T u₀ galSeq` (matching the `aubin_lions_R3` binder list,
`AxiomaticClosure.lean:444–460`).

ASSEMBLY: the genuine spatial half is PROVED axiom-free (the `steklovAvg_spatial_extraction`
chain). The single irreducible LOCAL Bochner-time compactness extraction (one subsequence `φ` + a
measurable limit curve `u` with per-ball `restrictToBall R` a.e.-in-time `L²` convergence) is
supplied by `galerkinSpaceTimeExtraction_R3` (theorem, issue #44, proved; resting on the two
thinner axioms `galerkin_spacetime_precompact_R3` + `galerkin_weakLimit_R3` — local, no tightness,
no global-`L²` claim, genuinely weaker/thinner than `aubin_lions_R3`). From that
extraction this constructor assembles every field axiom-free:
* `φ`, `φ_mono`, `u` — directly from the extraction;
* `u_aestronglyMeasurable` — the extraction's measurability conjunct;
* `strong_convergence` — for each ball `R`, the extraction already delivers the per-ball
  ball-restricted differences converging to `0` a.e. in `t`, dominated by the constant
  `2‖u₀‖` on the finite window `[0,T]` (via `galerkin_norm_le_u0`); dominated convergence in `L²`
  (`tendsto_Lp_of_tendsto_ae`) gives the `eLpNorm`-in-time convergence.

This is a `noncomputable def` (not a `theorem`) because `AubinLionsPackage_R3` is a `Type`
(a data-carrying structure), not a `Prop` — mirroring the `aubin_lions_R3` axiom shape.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.aubinLionsPackage_R3_of_timeCompactness
nodes.signature:
(𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) →
  (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) →
    (ν : ℝ) →
      0 < ν →
        (T : ℝ) →
          0 < T →
            (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) →
              (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) →
                LerayHopf.LocalRellichInput → LerayHopf.AubinLionsPackage_R3 𝔊 F ν T u₀ galSeq

nodes.doc:
**Convection-form continuity along a Galerkin curve** (Schwartz-test form).  For any
Schwartz-div-free test `w`, `t ↦ F.b (gs.u t) (gs.u t) w` is continuous on forward time `Ici 0`.

Unlike `galerkin_bForm_curve_continuousOn`, this does NOT require `w` to be fixed by `𝔊.P n`; it
builds the jointly-continuous bilinear form `(u,v) ↦ b u v w` directly from `b_bound` (as a
`mkContinuous₂` CLM) and composes with the `L²`-continuous curve.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.bForm_curve_continuousOn_of_schwartz

nodes.doc: The diagonal increment of `b` splits into two one-sided differences.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.bForm_diag_increment
nodes.signature:

  F.b x x w - F.b y y w = F.b (x - y) x w + F.b y (x - y) w

nodes.doc:
**C5 (Codex-gated statement — `n`-uniformity is the load-bearing claim).** The abstract
convection form `F.b` obeys the same energy-class bound as `convIntegralSchwartz`, uniformly
over the Galerkin level `n`: for the ambient scheme `𝔊` and forms `F` there is a constant
`C_b ≥ 0` (obtained from C4's absolute constant) such that for ALL levels `n` and ALL level-`n` states `u v w : L2Sigma_R3`
(`(u:L2VF_R3) = 𝔊.P n u`, likewise `v`, `w`),
`|F.b u v w| ≤ C_b · ‖u‖^{1/2} · (V₁ u)^{1/4} · √(V₁ v) · √(V₁ w)`.

The `∃ C_b` sits OUTSIDE the quantifier over `n` and the states, so the constant is
provably `n`-independent — the property the good-sampling compactness argument needs.

Route (plan §3 C5): B10 Schwartz representations of `u, v, w`, the `F.b_galerkin` pin to
`convIntegralSchwartz`, and C4.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.bForm_galerkin_abs_le
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊},
  ∃ C_b,
    0 ≤ C_b ∧
      ∀ (n : ℕ) (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
        ↑u = (𝔊.P n) ↑u →
          ↑v = (𝔊.P n) ↑v →
            ↑w = (𝔊.P n) ↑w →
              |F.b u v w| ≤
                C_b * ‖↑u‖ ^ (1 / 2) * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑u ^ (1 / 4) * √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑v) *
                  √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑w)

nodes.doc:
**W2 nonlinear-limit convection bound (PR-4).** For any Schwartz divergence-free test
`z`, the limit-curve convection functional `t ↦ F.b (u t) (u t) z` is integrable on `[0,T]`
and its `L¹`-norm is controlled by `√(V₁ z)`, with a `z`-uniform constant `K`.

The bound descends from the Galerkin curves via `fb_tendsto_of_perball` + Fatou-in-time
(`lintegral_liminf_le'`): each approximant obeys the Ladyzhenskaya energy bound
`|b(uₙ,uₙ,z)| ≤ C_b‖uₙ‖^{1/2}(V₁ uₙ)^{3/4}√(V₁ z)` (`convIntegralSchwartz_bound_energy` +
`F.b_galerkin`), whose time integral is `n`-uniformly `≤ K·√(V₁ z)` after the crude
`‖uₙ‖^{1/2}(V₁ uₙ)^{3/4} ≤ √‖u₀‖·(1 + V₁ uₙ)` domination and `reg_bound`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.bForm_limit_convection_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n)
          (alPkg : LerayHopf.AubinLionsPackage_R3 𝔊 F ν T u₀ galSeq),
          ∃ K,
            0 ≤ K ∧
              ∀ (z : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
                LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 z →
                  MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun t => F.b (alPkg.u t) (alPkg.u t) z)
                      (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) ∧
                    ∫ (t : ℝ) in Set.Icc 0 T, ‖F.b (alPkg.u t) (alPkg.u t) z‖ ≤ K * √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑z)

nodes.doc:
**Nonlinear convection term passes to the limit under strong L² convergence.**

For a fixed Schwartz div-free test `w`, if `uₙ → u` and `vₙ → v` strongly in L²(ℝ³) with a
uniform bound, then `F.b uₙ vₙ w → F.b u v w`. This is the analytic payoff of R3-d's
`b_bound` (bilinear L²-continuity of `b` in its first two slots for Schwartz tests).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.bForm_tendsto_of_strongL2
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
  LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 w →
    ∀ (uSeq vSeq : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => ↑(uSeq n)) Filter.atTop (nhds ↑u) →
        Filter.Tendsto (fun n => ↑(vSeq n)) Filter.atTop (nhds ↑v) →
          Filter.Tendsto (fun n => F.b (uSeq n) (vSeq n) w) Filter.atTop (nhds (F.b u v w))

nodes.doc:
**BAC-CAB in the cyclic `Fin 3` convention.**  Given transversality
`∑ ξ_i wh_i = 0`, the double cross `(ξ × (ξ × ŵ))_j = -‖ξ‖² ŵ_j`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.baccab
nodes.signature:
∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3) (wh : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (j : Fin 3),
  ↑(ξ.ofLp 0) * (wh 0) ξ + ↑(ξ.ofLp 1) * (wh 1) ξ + ↑(ξ.ofLp 2) * (wh 2) ξ = 0 →
    ↑(ξ.ofLp (j + 1)) * (↑(ξ.ofLp (j + 2 + 1)) * (wh (j + 2 + 2)) ξ - ↑(ξ.ofLp (j + 2 + 2)) * (wh (j + 2 + 1)) ξ) -
        ↑(ξ.ofLp (j + 2)) * (↑(ξ.ofLp (j + 1 + 1)) * (wh (j + 1 + 2)) ξ - ↑(ξ.ofLp (j + 1 + 2)) * (wh (j + 1 + 1)) ξ) =
      -(↑(ξ.ofLp 0) ^ 2 + ↑(ξ.ofLp 1) ^ 2 + ↑(ξ.ofLp 2) ^ 2) * (wh j) ξ

nodes.doc:
**D3a.** The per-ball limits from D2 are mutually consistent: the limit on B_k agrees
a.e. on B_j (j ≤ k) with the limit on B_j (both are L² limits of the same subsequence's
restrictions, and restriction B_k → B_j is continuous). Used to assemble a single global g.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.ballLimits_are_consistent
nodes.signature:
LerayHopf.LocalRellichInput →
  ∀ (M : ℝ) (z : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
    (∀ (n : ℕ), z n ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) →
      (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (z n)) →
        (∀ (n : ℕ), ‖z n‖ ≤ M) →
          (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 (z n) ≤ M ^ 2) →
            ∀ (ψ : ℕ → ℕ),
              StrictMono ψ →
                ∀ (g : (k : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 ↑k)),
                  (∀ (k : ℕ),
                      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall (↑k) (z (ψ n))) Filter.atTop (nhds (g k))) →
                    ∃ g₀,
                      MeasureTheory.MemLp g₀ 2 MeasureTheory.volume ∧
                        ∀ (k : ℕ), ↑↑(g k) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Metric.closedBall 0 ↑k)] g₀

nodes.doc:
**Full Galerkin compactness package over the concrete scheme (issue #10).**  Assembles
the proof-carrying `GalerkinCompactnessPackageFull_R3` over `schemeOfBasis B` by feeding the
axiom-free `galSeq_R3_of_basis` into the axiom-free core builder
