nodes.doc:
**`diag_ae_subseq` — Diagonal subsequence converging a.e. in `t` for ALL ball radii `k : ℕ`.**

From refine-capable `perBall_ae_subseq`, construct by induction a Cantor tower
`φ_0, φ_1, φ_2, …` where `φ_0 = id` and `φ_{k+1} = φ_k ∘ ρ_{k+1}` with `ρ_{k+1}` from
`perBall_ae_subseq (ψ := φ_k)` at radius `k+1`. The diagonal `φ n := φ_n n` is a single
strictly-monotone subsequence converging for every ball radius k : ℕ and a.e. t.

**Proof route (for lean-prover):**
1. Base: `φ_0 = id` (StrictMono). Apply `perBall_ae_subseq id strictMono_id 0` to get `ρ_0`,
   `g_0`, and a.e.-t convergence. Set `φ_1 = ρ_0`.
2. Inductive step: given `φ_k` StrictMono converging on balls `0..k`, apply
   `perBall_ae_subseq φ_k hφ_k (k+1)` to get `ρ_{k+1}` StrictMono with a.e.-t convergence
   of `fun n => restrictToBall (k+1) ((galSeq (φ_k (ρ_{k+1} n))).u t)`. Set `φ_{k+1} = φ_k ∘ ρ_{k+1}`.
3. Diagonal: `φ n := φ_n n`. StrictMono: `φ_{n+1} (n+1) = φ_n (ρ_n (n+1)) > φ_n n = φ n`
   since `ρ_n (n+1) > n` and `φ_n` StrictMono (standard Cantor argument).
4. For fixed `k`, for `n ≥ k`, `φ n` is a value in the range of `φ_k`, so the a.e.-t
   convergence for `φ_k` (established at step k of the tower) transfers.
5. A.e. set: `⋂_{k : ℕ} S_k` is full measure by `MeasureTheory.ae_all_iff.mpr` (each `S_k`
   has full measure).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.diag_ae_subseq
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n),
          ∃ φ,
            StrictMono φ ∧
              ∀ (k : ℕ),
                ∃ g_k,
                  MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable g_k (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) ∧
                    ∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T),
                      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑((galSeq (φ n)).u t)) Filter.atTop
                        (nhds (g_k t))

nodes.doc:
**Sup→L²(B_R) transfer.**  If two L²(ℝ³) fields `u, v` agree a.e. on `B_R` with functions
whose pointwise difference is `≤ c` everywhere on `B_R`, then their ball-restrictions are within
`ballMassSqrt R · c` in `L²(B_R)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.dist_restrictToBall_le_of_ae_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ (R c : ℝ),
  0 ≤ c →
    ∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (g₁ g₂ : LerayHopf.Domain3 → EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)),
      ↑↑u =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Metric.closedBall 0 R)] g₁ →
        ↑↑v =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Metric.closedBall 0 R)] g₂ →
          (∀ x ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R, ‖g₁ x - g₂ x‖ ≤ c) →
            dist (LerayHopf.restrictToBall R u) (LerayHopf.restrictToBall R v) ≤ LerayHopf.ballMassSqrt R * c

nodes.doc:
**A3 (eLpNorm half).** The `Lp`-distance between the `toLp` classes of an `L²` curve and
a step curve is the `toReal` of the `eLpNorm` of the pointwise difference.  This is the
bridge from metric estimates in `Lp X 2 μ_T` (A4/A5/A6) back to the concrete
`eLpNorm`-of-difference form used by the axiom's conclusion.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.dist_toLp_stepCurve
nodes.signature:
∀ {X : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup X] (T : ℝ) (hT : 0 < T) (m : ℕ) (hm : 0 < m) (y : Fin m → X) {f : ℝ → X}
  (hf : MeasureTheory.MemLp f 2 (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T))),
  dist (MeasureTheory.MemLp.toLp f hf) (MeasureTheory.MemLp.toLp (LerayHopf.stepCurve T m y) ⋯) =
    (MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fun t => f t - LerayHopf.stepCurve T m y t) 2
        (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T))).toReal

nodes.doc:
Per-component Plancherel pairing: the complex cast of the real L²-inner product
`⟪(∂_j φ).toLp, u_j⟫` equals the Fourier-side pairing
`∫ conj((2π i) ξ_j 𝓕(φ̂)(ξ)) û_j(ξ) dξ`, where `φ̂ = 𝓕 (schwartzC φ)` and
`û_j = 𝓕 (L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j u)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.divComponent_eq_fourier_integral
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (j : Fin 3),
  ↑(inner ℝ
        (((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single j 1)) φ).toLp 2
          MeasureTheory.volume)
        ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) u)) =
    ∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
      (starRingEnd ℂ) (2 * ↑Real.pi * Complex.I * ↑(ξ.ofLp j) * (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ φ)) ξ) *
        ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) u)) ξ

nodes.doc: `DivFreeL2 u ↔ ∀ k, divSymbol k u = 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.divFreeL2_iff_divSymbol
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), LerayHopf.DivFreeL2 u ↔ ∀ (k : Fin 3 → ℤ), (LerayHopf.divSymbol k) u = 0

nodes.doc:
**H¹-test div-free.** The weak divergence of an `H¹_σ` element vanishes against ANY real
`L²` test `θ` (not just Schwartz): `∑ₐ ∫ (∂ₐuₐ).re·θ = 0`. Extended from `convFormH1_divFree`
(Schwartz tests) by `L²` density and continuity of the real `L²` inner product.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.divFree_L2test
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hu : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u),
  u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 →
    ∀ (θ : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)),
      ∑ a, ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), (↑↑(LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 u hu a a) x).re * ↑↑θ x = 0

nodes.doc:
From the weak-div-free hypothesis `hdiv`, the *integrated divergence* against any
Schwartz test `φ` also vanishes (this is `hdiv` after one integration by parts).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.divFree_intLeft
nodes.signature:
∀ (ψu : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  (∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
      ∑ a,
          ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
            (ψu a) x *
              ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) φ) x =
        0) →
    ∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
      ∑ a,
          ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
            ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) (ψu a)) x *
              φ x =
        0

nodes.doc:
The Leibniz pairing identity: `∑_a ∫ ψu_a (∂_a f) g + ∑_a ∫ ψu_a f (∂_a g) = 0`,
obtained by applying `hdiv` to `φ = f · g` and expanding the product derivative.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.divFree_leibniz_pair
nodes.signature:
∀ (ψu : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  (∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
      ∑ a,
          ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
            (ψu a) x *
              ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) φ) x =
        0) →
    ∀ (f g : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
      (∑ a,
            ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
              (ψu a) x *
                  ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) f) x *
                g x) +
          ∑ a,
            ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
              (ψu a) x * f x *
                ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) g) x =
        0

nodes.doc:
The divergence symbol at wavenumber `k`: a continuous ℝ-linear functional on `L2VF`.

`divSymbol k u = ∑ j : Fin 3, (k j : ℂ) * mFourierCoeff3 (L2VF_projComponentC j u) k`

Constructed as a finite sum of ℝ-linear CLMs: for each `j`, we compose
- `L2VF_projComponentC j : L2VF →L[ℝ] L2C`   (ℝ-linear projection + embedding)
- `fourierCoeffCLM k : L2C →L[ℂ] ℂ`, restricted to ℝ-scalars
- scaled by `(k j : ℂ)` via the `Module ℂ (L2VF →L[ℝ] ℂ)` instance.

`u ∈ L²_σ` iff `divSymbol k u = 0` for all `k`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.divSymbol
nodes.signature: (Fin 3 → ℤ) → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF →L[ℝ] ℂ

nodes.doc:
`divSymbol k u` unfolds to the Fourier divergence sum.

Proof: unfold `divSymbol`, use linearity of the CLM composition and `fourierCoeffCLM_apply`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.divSymbol_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (k : Fin 3 → ℤ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.divSymbol k) u = ∑ j, ↑(k j) * LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) u) k

nodes.doc:
The weak-divergence test functional against a Schwartz function `φ : 𝓢(ℝ³, ℝ)`.

  `divTestFunctional φ u = ∑ j : Fin 3, ⟪(∂_j φ).toLp 2 volume, u_j⟫`

where `u_j = L2VF_projComponent_R3 j u ∈ Lp ℝ 2 volume` is the j-th component of `u`
and `⟪g, ·⟫ = innerSL ℝ g` is the continuous real inner product functional.

This is a continuous ℝ-linear functional, being a finite sum of CLM compositions:

  `∑ j, (innerSL ℝ ((∂_j φ).toLp 2 volume)) ∘L (L2VF_projComponent_R3 j)`
nodes.name: LerayHopf.divTestFunctional
nodes.signature: SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 →L[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.doc:
The whole weak-divergence functional, complexified, as a single Fourier-side integral
against the transverse defect:
`(divTestFunctional φ u : ℂ) = ∫ conj((2π i) 𝓕(φ̂)(ξ)) · T_u(ξ) dξ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.divTestFunctional_eq_fourier_integral
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  ↑((LerayHopf.divTestFunctional φ) u) =
    ∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
      (starRingEnd ℂ) (2 * ↑Real.pi * Complex.I * (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ φ)) ξ) *
        LerayHopf.transverseDefect✝ u ξ

nodes.doc: The weak-divergence functional is the global L²-vector inner product against `gradVF φ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.divTestFunctional_eq_inner
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  (LerayHopf.divTestFunctional φ) w = inner ℝ (LerayHopf.gradVF✝ φ) w

nodes.doc:
Pointwise vanishing of the divergence of the curl:
`∑ i, ∂_i (curl ψ)_i x = 0`.  By the cyclic structure of `curlSchwartz` (A1) every term
cancels with another after applying Clairaut (`schwartz_lineDerivOp_comm`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.div_curlSchwartz_eq_zero
nodes.signature:
∀ (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  ∑ i, (LineDeriv.lineDerivOp (EuclideanSpace.single i 1) (LerayHopf.curlSchwartz ψ i)) x = 0

nodes.doc: The j-th scalar component `(∂_j φ).toLp` used throughout.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.dphiLp
nodes.signature: SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ → Fin 3 → ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)

nodes.doc:
Each scalar summand `⟪dphiLp φ j, projComp_j w⟫` equals the integral
`∫ x, (dphiLp φ j x) * (w x) j`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.dphi_inner_eq_integral
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (j : Fin 3) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  inner ℝ (LerayHopf.dphiLp✝ φ j) ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) w) =
    ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ↑↑(LerayHopf.dphiLp✝ φ j) x * (↑↑w x).ofLp j

nodes.doc: Integrability of each scalar summand `x ↦ (dphiLp φ j x) * (w x) j`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.dphi_integrable
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (j : Fin 3) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun x => ↑↑(LerayHopf.dphiLp✝ φ j) x * (↑↑w x).ofLp j) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
The `L²`-norm of the full gradient is bounded by the sum over the three coordinate
directions of the `L²`-norms of the directional derivatives:
`eLpNorm (fderiv φ) 2 ≤ ∑ᵢ eLpNorm (∂_{eᵢ}φ) 2`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.eLpNorm_fderiv_le_sum_lineDeriv
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fderiv ℝ ⇑φ) 2 MeasureTheory.volume ≤
    ∑ i,
      MeasureTheory.eLpNorm
        (fun x => ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (EuclideanSpace.single i 1)) φ) x) 2
        MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: Fresh copy of the private `eLpNorm_fderiv_le_sum_lineDeriv` (`EnergyClassConvection.lean:1174`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.eLpNorm_fderiv_le_sum_lineDeriv_C2
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fderiv ℝ ⇑φ) 2 MeasureTheory.volume ≤
    ∑ i,
      MeasureTheory.eLpNorm
        (fun x => ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (EuclideanSpace.single i 1)) φ) x) 2
        MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
Gradient → weighted-Fourier bound for Schwartz `φ`:
`eLpNorm (fderiv φ) 2 ≤ ofReal (2π · √(∫ (1+‖ξ‖²)‖𝓕φ ξ‖²))`. Combines the proved Plancherel
kernel `integral_normSq_fderiv_le` (`∫‖fderiv φ‖² ≤ (2π)²∫‖ξ‖²‖𝓕φ‖²`) with `‖ξ‖² ≤ 1+‖ξ‖²`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.eLpNorm_fderiv_le_weighted
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fderiv ℝ ⇑φ) 2 MeasureTheory.volume ≤
    ENNReal.ofReal (2 * Real.pi * √(∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), (1 + ‖ξ‖ ^ 2) * ‖(FourierTransform.fourier φ) ξ‖ ^ 2))

nodes.doc:
`eLpNorm` gradient bound for the truncation, in `ℝ≥0∞`:
`eLpNorm (fderiv (cutoff R • φ)) 2 ≤ eLpNorm (fderiv φ) 2 + ofReal (K/R) · eLpNorm φ 2`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.eLpNorm_fderiv_truncated_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (R : ℝ),
  0 < R →
    ∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (K : ℝ),
      0 ≤ K →
        (∀ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖fderiv ℝ (↑LerayHopf.cutoffBump✝) x‖ ≤ K) →
          MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fderiv ℝ fun y => LerayHopf.cutoff✝ R y • φ y) 2 MeasureTheory.volume ≤
            MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fderiv ℝ ⇑φ) 2 MeasureTheory.volume +
              ENNReal.ofReal (K / R) * MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (⇑φ) 2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
**eLpNorm raw↔clamped-average bound on `[0,T]`.**  The time-`L²` `eLpNorm` of the
ball-restricted raw↔clamped-average difference over `[0,T]` is controlled by the uniform pointwise
modulus bound `ε` times `√T`: since `restrictToBall R` is `1`-Lipschitz, the pointwise difference is
`≤ ‖u_t − clampedAvg δ T t‖ ≤ ε` (`clampedAvg_approx`), and `eLpNorm` of an `ε`-bounded function over
the finite window `[0,T]` is `≤ ε · T^{1/2}`.  This is the raw↔avg term of the C2 `ε/3` split.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.eLpNorm_raw_sub_clampedAvg_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ T ε : ℝ} (R : ℝ),
  0 < δ →
    2 * δ ≤ T →
      0 ≤ ε →
        (∀ (s s' : ℝ), s ∈ Set.Icc 0 T → s' ∈ Set.Icc 0 T → |s - s'| ≤ δ → ‖↑(gs.u s) - ↑(gs.u s')‖ ≤ ε) →
          MeasureTheory.eLpNorm
              (fun t =>
                LerayHopf.restrictToBall R ↑(gs.u t) -
                  LerayHopf.restrictToBall R (LerayHopf.clampedAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ T t))
              2 (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) ≤
            ENNReal.ofReal T ^ (ENNReal.toReal 2)⁻¹ * ENNReal.ofReal ε

nodes.doc:
**Core helper for E2.** Given the D3 good-sample data `τ` (per-cell sample times with
the integrated modulus bound `∑ᵢ ∫ ‖u(t) − u(τᵢ)‖² ≤ B`), the ball-restricted Galerkin
curve `t ↦ restrictToBall k (u t)` is `L²(0,T)`-close to the mesh-`m` step curve sampled at
`τ`, with the quantitative bound `√B`.

Route: `restrictToBall k` is `1`-Lipschitz, so pointwise the difference is dominated by
`gg t := ‖u(t) − u(τ(cell t))‖`; the `L²`-norm of `gg` is computed cell-by-cell (each cell's
integrand agrees a.e. with the continuous `‖u(·) − u(τᵢ)‖²`), giving `∫ gg² = ∑ᵢ ∫ cell ≤ B`,
whence `‖·‖_{L²} = √(∫ gg²) ≤ √B`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.eLpNorm_restrictToBall_stepCurve_diff_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (k : ℕ) (T : ℝ),
  0 < T →
    ∀ (m : ℕ),
      0 < m →
        ∀ (τ : Fin m → ℝ),
          (∀ (i : Fin m), τ i ∈ Set.Icc (↑↑i * (T / ↑m)) ((↑↑i + 1) * (T / ↑m))) →
            ∀ (w : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 ↑k)),
              (∀ (t : ℝ), w t = LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑(gs.u t)) →
                ∀ (B : ℝ),
                  ∑ i, ∫ (t : ℝ) in ↑↑i * (T / ↑m)..(↑↑i + 1) * (T / ↑m), ‖↑(gs.u t) - ↑(gs.u (τ i))‖ ^ 2 ≤ B →
                    (MeasureTheory.eLpNorm
                          (fun t =>
                            w t - LerayHopf.stepCurve T m (fun i => LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑(gs.u (τ i))) t)
                          2 (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T))).toReal ≤
                      √B

nodes.doc:
**C2.** Componentwise `L⁶` bound in terms of the Dirichlet energy: there is an absolute
coefficient `C₆ ≥ 0` (independent of `v`, `ψ`, and the component `i`) with
`eLpNorm (ψ i) 6 ≤ ENNReal.ofReal (C₆ · √(V₁ v))` for every Schwartz representative `ψ`
of `v` and every component `i`.

The `∃ C₆` sits OUTSIDE the quantifiers over `v`, `ψ`, `i`, so the constant is genuinely
absolute (Gagliardo–Nirenberg–Sobolev has no field dependence).

Route (plan §3 C2): `gns_L6_schwartz` (public GNS on `Domain3`; complex- or real-valued
variant, whichever typechecks) bounding `‖ψ_i‖₆` by `eLpNorm (fderiv ψ_i) 2`, bridged to
`∑_a ‖∂_a ψ_i‖²` (template: private `eLpNorm_fderiv_le_sum_lineDeriv`,
`EnergyClassConvection.lean:1174`; rebuilt fresh), then C1 to reach `√(V₁ v)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.eLpNorm_six_le_of_schwartzRep
nodes.signature:
∃ C₆,
  0 ≤ C₆ ∧
    ∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
      (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) v = (ψ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
        ∀ (i : Fin 3),
          MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (⇑(ψ i)) 6 MeasureTheory.volume ≤
            ENNReal.ofReal (C₆ * √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 v))

nodes.name: LerayHopf.eLpNorm_six_toReal_le_energy
nodes.signature:
∀ (C₆ : ℝ),
  0 ≤ C₆ →
    (∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
        (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) v = (ψ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
          ∀ (i : Fin 3),
            MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (⇑(ψ i)) 6 MeasureTheory.volume ≤
              ENNReal.ofReal (C₆ * √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 v))) →
      ∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
        (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) v = (ψ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
          ∀ (i : Fin 3),
            (MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (⇑(ψ i)) 6 MeasureTheory.volume).toReal ≤ C₆ * √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 v)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.eLpNorm_tendsto_of_integral_sq_tendsto
nodes.signature:
∀ (T : ℝ),
  0 < T →
    ∀ (M : ℝ) (f : ℕ → ℝ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
      (∀ (n : ℕ), MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable (f n) (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T))) →
        (∀ (n : ℕ), ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖f n t‖ ≤ M) →
          Filter.Tendsto (fun n => ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T, ‖f n t‖ ^ 2) Filter.atTop (nhds 0) →
            Filter.Tendsto (fun n => MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (f n) 2 (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)))
              Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.eLpNorm_three_component_toReal_le_energy
nodes.signature:
∀ (C₆ : ℝ),
  0 ≤ C₆ →
    (∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
        (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) v = (ψ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
          ∀ (i : Fin 3),
            MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (⇑(ψ i)) 6 MeasureTheory.volume ≤
              ENNReal.ofReal (C₆ * √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 v))) →
      ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
        (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) u = (ψ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
          ∀ (a : Fin 3),
            (MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (⇑(ψ a)) 3 MeasureTheory.volume).toReal ≤
              √C₆ * ‖u‖ ^ (1 / 2) * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 u ^ (1 / 4)

nodes.doc:
**Quantitative L²∩L⁶ interpolation at exponent 3.** For `f : Domain3 → F`,
`eLpNorm f 3 ≤ (eLpNorm f 2)^{1/2} · (eLpNorm f 6)^{1/2}`. The square route:
`(eLpNorm f 3)² = eLpNorm (‖f‖²) (3/2) ≤ eLpNorm ‖f‖ 6 · eLpNorm ‖f‖ 2` (Hölder `1/6+1/2=2/3`).

      MeasureTheory.eLpNorm f 3 MeasureTheory.volume ≤
        MeasureTheory.eLpNorm f 2 MeasureTheory.volume ^ (1 / 2) *
          MeasureTheory.eLpNorm f 6 MeasureTheory.volume ^ (1 / 2)

Fresh PUBLIC copy of the `private` `eLpNorm_three_le_interp` (`EnergyClassConvection.lean:510`);
the original is neither moved nor de-privatized.  Proof route unchanged from the original:
the square route `(eLpNorm f 3)² = eLpNorm (‖f‖²) (3/2) ≤ eLpNorm ‖f‖ 6 · eLpNorm ‖f‖ 2`
(Hölder with `1/6 + 1/2 = 2/3`).

integrable, the time-`L²` seminorm is `ENNReal.ofReal (√(∫ ‖h t‖² dμ))`. (Standard `eLpNorm`
unfolding for `p = 2`; used to feed the integral-to-`eLpNorm` step of E1.)

nodes.doc:
**A2 (MUST-PROVE).** Along ANY (local) solution `c` of `c' = G_n(c)` (here `c : ℝ → V_n` with
`HasDerivAt` at `t` into the ambient `L2VF_R3`), the energy `½‖c t‖²` has derivative
`−ν·viscousFormSq_R3 1 (c t) ≤ 0`. Local analogue of `galerkin_energy_identity`.

Proof sketch (mirror `galerkin_energy_identity`, `GalerkinODE.lean:185`, but local): rewrite
`½‖c s‖²` as `½⟪c s, c s⟫` via `real_inner_self_eq_norm_sq`; differentiate via `hc.inner ℝ hc`
giving `½(⟪c t, c'⟫ + ⟪c', c t⟫)`; with `c' = G_n (c t)`, `real_inner_comm` + A1's exact-value
form (`⟪c t, G_n (c t)⟫ = −ν·viscousFormSq_R3 1 (c t)`) collapse it to
`−ν·viscousFormSq_R3 1 (c t)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.energy_hasDerivAt_of_localSolution

∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ)
  (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)) (t : ℝ),
  HasDerivAt (fun s => ↑(c s)) (↑(LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n (c t))) t →
    HasDerivAt (fun s => 1 / 2 * ‖↑(c s)‖ ^ 2) (-ν * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑(c t)) t

nodes.doc:
**Conjunct (1): `∀t` energy inequality for the good representative.**
Kinetic term by squared-norm weak-lsc at every `t`; dissipation term by the a.e.
spatial lsc of `viscousEnn` + Fatou in time; combined against the Galerkin energy
identity via liminf superadditivity.

The hypothesis `hInt` (integrable dissipation of `v` on `[0, T]`) keeps this statement
honest — without it the real interval integral would junk-collapse to `0` in the
non-integrable regime and the inequality would hold vacuously there (Codex P2 finding).
The capstone discharges `hInt` from the energy-class conjunct (4) via a.e.-invariance.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.energy_ineq_of_representative
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n)
          (alPkg : LerayHopf.AubinLionsPackage F ν T u₀ galSeq) (v : LerayHopf.Time → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (ρ : ℕ → ℕ),
          (∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T), v t = alPkg.u t) →
            (∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T),
                Filter.Tendsto (fun k => ↑((galSeq (alPkg.φ (ρ k))).u t)) Filter.atTop (nhds ↑(alPkg.u t))) →
              (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T,
                  ∀ (z : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
                    Filter.Tendsto (fun k => inner ℝ (↑((galSeq (alPkg.φ (ρ k))).u t)) z) Filter.atTop
                      (nhds (inner ℝ (↑(v t)) z))) →
                IntervalIntegrable (fun s => LerayHopf.viscousFormSq ν ↑(v s)) MeasureTheory.volume 0 T →
                  ∀ (t : ℝ),
                    0 ≤ t →
                      t ≤ T →
                        1 / 2 * ‖↑(v t)‖ ^ 2 + ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, LerayHopf.viscousFormSq ν ↑(v s) ≤ 1 / 2 * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
**∀t energy inequality for the good representative** (private helper).

Given the good representative `v` produced by `exists_weak_representative_R3`, prove
the Leray–Hopf energy inequality `½‖v(t)‖² + ∫₀ᵗ ν·V₁(v) ≤ ½‖u₀‖²` for every
`t ∈ [0,T]`.  The proof follows the torus `energy_ineq_of_representative` template:

- **Kinetic lsc** via `normSq_le_liminf_of_inner_tendsto`: weak-∀t convergence `cₙ(t) ⇀ v(t)`
  + uniform bound `‖cₙ(t)‖ ≤ ‖u₀‖` imply `‖v(t)‖² ≤ liminf ‖cₙ(t)‖²`.
- **Viscous Fatou** (ν = 1): `viscous_pointwise_lsc` gives the a.e. ENNReal pointwise
  bound; Fatou (`lintegral_liminf_le'`) + `Monotone.map_liminf_of_continuousAt`
  give `∫₀ᵗ V₁(v) ≤ liminf_n ∫₀ᵗ V₁(cₙ)`.
- **Scaling**: `∫₀ᵗ ν·V₁(v) ≤ liminf_n ∫₀ᵗ ν·V₁(cₙ)` via real liminf commutativity.
- **Superadditivity** (`le_liminf_add`) + per-n energy identity close the bound.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.energy_ineq_of_representative_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n)
          (alPkg : LerayHopf.AubinLionsPackage_R3 𝔊 F ν T u₀ galSeq) (v : LerayHopf.Time → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
          (∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T), v t = alPkg.u t) →
            (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T,
                ∀ (z : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
                  Filter.Tendsto (fun n => inner ℝ (↑((galSeq (alPkg.φ n)).u t)) z) Filter.atTop
                    (nhds (inner ℝ (↑(v t)) z))) →
              IntervalIntegrable (fun s => LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 ν ↑(v s)) MeasureTheory.volume 0 T →
                ∀ (t : ℝ),
                  0 ≤ t →
                    t ≤ T →
                      1 / 2 * ‖↑(v t)‖ ^ 2 + ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 ν ↑(v s) ≤ 1 / 2 * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
B2.  From the density frontier `CurlSchwartzDense` and separability (B1), extract a
SINGLE ℕ-enumeration `e : ℕ → L2VF_R3` of curl fields whose finite prefix spans are
dense in `L2Sigma_R3`.

This is the separability-thinning of the (uncountable) curl family
`Set.range curlSchwartzL2` to one countable prefix-span enumeration; it is bookkeeping,
not analysis (Codex point C-D).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.exists_denseSeq_curlSchwartz

LerayHopf.CurlSchwartzDense →
  ∃ e,
    (∀ (k : ℕ), ∃ ψ, e k = LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 ψ) ∧
      LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ≤ (⨆ n, Submodule.span ℝ (Set.range fun k => e ↑k)).topologicalClosure

nodes.doc:
(P0.9c) T-AL-3 capstone [architect flags (b)+(d)]: ONE strictly monotone
extraction `φ` and ONE packaged limit function `g : ℕ → ℝ → ℝ` such that every
test pairing `t ↦ ⟪u_{φ(n)}(t), w m⟫` converges uniformly on `[0,T]` to `g m`.
The conclusion is in the reindexed single-`g` form that drops verbatim into
P0.5 `exists_weak_limit_curve`'s `hconv` with `v := fun n => (galSeq (φ n)).u`.

The body is the architect-verified wiring (not a sorry): Classical band-limit
cutoffs from `IsGalerkinTest`, P0.3 Lipschitz constants, `B m = ‖u₀‖·‖w m‖`
via P0.9b + Cauchy–Schwarz, then the T-AL-2 engine + `choose`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.exists_galerkin_modewise_extraction
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n)
          (w : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
          (∀ (m : ℕ), LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest (w m)) →
            ∃ φ,
              StrictMono φ ∧
                ∃ g,
                  ∀ (m : ℕ),
                    TendstoUniformlyOn (fun n t => inner ℝ ↑((galSeq (φ n)).u t) ↑(w m)) (g m) Filter.atTop
                      (Set.Icc 0 T)

**D1.** On a compact interval `[a, b]` (`a < b`), a continuous nonnegative integrand
has a point whose value is at or below its average: there is `τ ∈ [a, b]` with
`f τ ≤ (b − a)⁻¹ · ∫ₐᵇ f`.

Generic (no Galerkin context).  Route (plan §3 D1): min-value ≤ average, via
`IsCompact.exists_isMinOn` on `[a, b]` and monotonicity of the interval integral
(`intervalIntegral.integral_mono_on`) against the constant `f τ_min`.

    ContinuousOn f (Set.Icc a b) →
      (∀ σ ∈ Set.Icc a b, 0 ≤ f σ) → ∃ τ ∈ Set.Icc a b, f τ ≤ (b - a)⁻¹ * ∫ (σ : ℝ) in a..b, f σ

nodes.doc:
**Separability thinning (H¹ graph norm).**  From separability of the graph space, extract a
single ℕ-enumeration of Schwartz potentials `ψ` whose curl fields (i) have `L2Sigma_R3`-dense
prefix spans and (ii) are H¹-graph-dense in the whole curl family: every `curlSchwartzL2 θ` is
approximated, simultaneously in `L²` and in each weighted Fourier component, by some
`curlSchwartzL2 (ψ k)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.exists_graphDenseSeq
nodes.signature:
∃ ψ,
  LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ≤
      (⨆ n, Submodule.span ℝ (Set.range fun k => LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 (ψ ↑k))).topologicalClosure ∧
    ∀ (θ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (ρ : ℝ),
      0 < ρ →
        ∃ k,
          ‖LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 (ψ k) - LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 θ‖ < ρ ∧
            ∀ (j : Fin 3),
              ‖LerayHopf.weightedFourierComponent (LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 (ψ k)) ⋯ j -
                    LerayHopf.weightedFourierComponent (LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 θ) ⋯ j‖ <
                ρ

content is the *implication* (package ⟹ solution), which is honest and stable under

gap.note:
自然言語では「Galerkin 近似してコンパクト性で極限を取る」の一行だが、Lean では
有限次元 ODE の存在・前進評価・射影の実数値性・コンパクト性（Rellich＋時間コンパクト）・
非線形項の三重線形評価・極限移行が各々独立の補題群に展開される。名称の `axiomatic`
は歴史的接尾辞であり、本体はカーネルのみで閉じている（追加公理なし）。

corpus.proof_ja:
L1. Galerkin 近似列を構成する。射影空間 $P_n L^2_\sigma$ 上で有限次元 ODE を
Picard–Lindelöf により解き、近似解 $u_n$ を得る。
L2. エネルギー恒等式から $u_n$ の一様評価（$L^\infty_t L^2_x \cap L^2_t H^1_x$）を導く。
L3. Rellich–Kondrachov のコンパクト埋め込みと時間方向の Aubin–Lions 型議論により、
$u_n$ の部分列が $L^2_{t,x}$ で強収束することを取り出す。
L4. 非線形項の極限移行を強収束から正当化し、極限 $u$ が弱形式を満たすことを確認する。
L5. 弱下半連続性からエネルギー不等式を極限に受け継がせ、Leray–Hopf 弱解の全条件を
証明保持型構造体 `LerayHopfSolutionFull_R3` に詰める。
corpus.statement_ja:
発散ゼロな初期値 $u_0 \in L^2_\sigma(\mathbb{R}^3)$ に対し、$\mathbb{R}^3$ 上の
Navier–Stokes 方程式の Leray–Hopf 弱解が存在する。すなわち、エネルギー不等式
$$\tfrac12\|u(t)\|_{L^2}^2 + \nu\int_0^t\!\|\nabla u\|_{L^2}^2\,ds \le \tfrac12\|u_0\|_{L^2}^2$$
を満たし、任意の発散ゼロテスト関数に対して弱形式を満たす曲線 $u$ が構成できる。

nodes.doc:
**Main existence theorem on ℝ³ (axiomatic).**  Relocated from
`LerayHopf/R3/SchwartzDivFreeBasis.lean` (issue #10) so that the per-`n` Galerkin sequence is
sourced from the axiom-free `galerkinSolutionData_unconditional` over the concrete scheme
`schemeOfBasis B`, discharging `galerkin_ode_solution_R3`.

For any `u₀ ∈ L²_σ(ℝ³)`, `ν > 0`, `T > 0`, there exist a Galerkin scheme `𝔊`, an NS-forms
bundle `F`, and a Leray–Hopf solution `u` on `[0, T]`.

The witness scheme is the CONCRETE `schemeOfBasis B`, with `B` drawn from
`nonempty_schwartzGalerkinBasis` (NOT from `r3GalerkinScheme_exists`, which would discard the
basis via `Nonempty.elim` and reintroduce the need for the per-scheme ODE axiom).

The name `_axiomatic` is a legacy suffix; as of issue #4 PR-6, this result depends on **ZERO project
axioms** — it is KERNEL-ONLY (`#print axioms` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`).
Former project axioms discharged:
- `galerkin_ode_solution_R3` — here (issue #10)
- `aubin_lions_R3` — removed (issue #15): spatial half PROVED, time content swapped for the strictly
  thinner UNCONDITIONAL `galerkinSpaceTimeExtraction_R3` (now itself discharged below)
- `r3GalerkinScheme_exists` — discharged (issue #21, `curlSchwartzDense_holds`); `curlSchwartzDense_holds`
  itself discharged (issue #3/#21, Fourier curl-density route)
- `spatial_compactness_R3` — discharged (issue #2, FK chain)
- `r3_NSForms_exist` / `r3ConvectionGapOp_exists` — PROVED (issue #56) sorry-free as the theorem

nodes.doc:
**Main existence theorem (axiomatic) — rerouted through the proved solver (issue #24).**

For any `u₀ ∈ L²_σ`, `ν > 0`, `T > 0`, there exists a `Torus3NSForms` bundle `F` and a
Leray–Hopf solution `u` on `[0, T]`.

Relocated here from `LerayHopf/TorusConvectionForm.lean` (downstream of both the convection gap and
the proved torus Galerkin solver, no import cycle) so that the per-`n` Galerkin sequence is sourced
from the axiom-free `galerkinSolutionData_torus` (over the finite-dim `velocitySpan n`), discharging
`galerkin_ode_solution`.  The theorem name and statement are **byte-identical** to the original
(only the package builder swapped: `build_galerkin_package` → `build_galerkin_package_of_torus`).

The `_axiomatic` suffix is now historical — all torus project axioms have been removed:
`aubin_lions` is DISCHARGED by `torusAubinLionsPackage_of_galSeq` (issue #23, T-AL-6);
`galerkin_ode_solution`, `torusConvectionGap_exists`, and `galerkin_limit_passage` were
discharged in issues #24, #53, and PR #88 respectively; see `LerayHopf/Core.lean` for
the axiom-free layer.  The `_axiomatic` name is preserved to avoid renaming the public API.

nodes.doc:
**Existence of a normalized mollifier kernel of arbitrarily small support.**

For every `r > 0` there is a `MollifierKernel K` whose support radius is `≤ r`.  It is the
`ContDiffBump (0 : Domain3)`-mollifier with `rIn = r/3 < rOut = r/2`, normalized to unit mass by
`ContDiffBump.normed volume`.  The resulting `K.η = bump.normed volume` is `C^∞`
(`contDiff_normed`), nonnegative (`nonneg_normed`), of mass one (`integral_normed`), and supported
in `closedBall 0 (r/2)` (`tsupport_normed_eq`), so `K.supportRadius = r/2 ≤ r`.

This is the concrete approximate-identity kernel feeding `convolution_l2_tendsto_uniform`: choosing
support `< δ` (the FK uniform translation-modulus radius for the target `ε`) drives the uniform
L²-approximation rate.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.exists_normalized_mollifierKernel
nodes.signature: (r : ℝ) → 0 < r → { K // K.supportRadius ≤ r }

nodes.doc:
**A6.** Sequential extraction from a totally bounded `toLp`-range: if the curves
`f n : ℝ → X` are `L²(0,T; X)` and their `toLp` classes form a totally bounded subset of the
COMPLETE space `Lp X 2 μ_T`, then some subsequence converges in `Lp`, i.e. there are a
strictly monotone `ρ` and a limit `G : Lp X 2 μ_T` with
`eLpNorm (f (ρ j) − G) 2 μ_T → 0`.

Route (plan §3 A6): `TotallyBounded.closure` + completeness make the closure of the range
compact; `IsCompact.tendsto_subseq` extracts `ρ` and `G`; `Lp.norm_def` plus the
`coeFn`-congruences `(hf n).coeFn_toLp` / `Lp.coeFn_sub` and an `ENNReal.toReal` conversion
turn norm convergence into the stated `eLpNorm` convergence (all eLpNorms are finite).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.exists_subseq_tendsto_eLpNorm_of_totallyBounded
nodes.signature:
∀ {X : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup X] [CompleteSpace X] (T : ℝ) (f : ℕ → ℝ → X)
  (hf : ∀ (n : ℕ), MeasureTheory.MemLp (f n) 2 (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T))),
  TotallyBounded (Set.range fun n => MeasureTheory.MemLp.toLp (f n) ⋯) →
    ∃ ρ G,
      StrictMono ρ ∧
        Filter.Tendsto
          (fun j => MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fun t => f (ρ j) t - ↑↑G t) 2 (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)))
          Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

by monotonicity of balls, for every real R).

nodes.doc:
Given a doubly-indexed family `f : ℕ → ℕ → ℝ → ℝ` of curves on `[0,T]` that is
uniformly bounded (`|f m n t| ≤ B m` for all `t ∈ [0,T]`) and per-family eventually
Lipschitz (`∀ m, ∃ n₀, ∀ n ≥ n₀, |f m n t - f m n s| ≤ L m * (t - s)` for `s ≤ t`
in `[0,T]`), there exists a strictly monotone subsequence `φ : ℕ → ℕ` such that for
every `m` the sequence `n ↦ f m (φ n)` converges uniformly on `[0,T]`.

The eventual-Lipschitz form of `hlip` (cutoff `n₀ m`, not all-`n`) is load-bearing:
the Galerkin application supplies the Lipschitz estimate only for `n` past the test's
band-limit cutoff (`m ≤ n` in P0.3).  Uniform convergence along a subsequence is a
tail property, so the engine absorbs the finite prefix without loss.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.exists_uniform_subseq_of_lipschitz_family
nodes.signature:
∀ (T : ℝ),
  0 < T →
    ∀ (f : ℕ → ℕ → ℝ → ℝ) (B L : ℕ → ℝ),
      (∀ (m n : ℕ), ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, |f m n t| ≤ B m) →
        (∀ (m : ℕ),
            ∃ n₀,
              ∀ (n : ℕ),
                n₀ ≤ n → ∀ (s t : ℝ), s ∈ Set.Icc 0 T → t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T → s ≤ t → |f m n t - f m n s| ≤ L m * (t - s)) →
          ∃ φ, StrictMono φ ∧ ∀ (m : ℕ), ∃ g, TendstoUniformlyOn (fun n t => f m (φ n) t) g Filter.atTop (Set.Icc 0 T)

`M`-ball bound, and AE strong measurability.  T-AL-4 GATE (architect, 2026-07-03):

nodes.doc:
**Weak limit inside a closed submodule.**  If `xₖ ∈ K`, `‖xₖ‖ ≤ M`, and every inner-product
sequence `k ↦ ⟪xₖ, z⟫` is Cauchy, then there is `y ∈ K` with `‖y‖ ≤ M` and `⟪xₖ, z⟫ → ⟪y, z⟫`
for every `z` (Riesz representation of the limit functional; membership by the
orthogonal-projection argument).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.exists_weak_limit_in_submodule

  [K.HasOrthogonalProjection] (x : ℕ → E),
  (∀ (k : ℕ), x k ∈ K) →
    ∀ (M : ℝ),
      (∀ (k : ℕ), ‖x k‖ ≤ M) →
        (∀ (z : E), CauchySeq fun k => inner ℝ (x k) z) →
          ∃ y ∈ K, ‖y‖ ≤ M ∧ ∀ (z : E), Filter.Tendsto (fun k => inner ℝ (x k) z) Filter.atTop (nhds (inner ℝ y z))

nodes.doc:
**Master construction: the weakly-continuous representative** of the Aubin–Lions limit.

Produces a curve `v : Time → L2Sigma` and a sub-subsequence `ρ` (of the Aubin–Lions
subsequence `φ`) such that, writing `cₖ := galSeq (φ (ρ k))`:

- (i)   `v = alPkg.u` a.e. on `[0, T]`,
- (ii)  `cₖ(t) → alPkg.u(t)` strongly in `L2VF` at a.e. `t ∈ [0, T]`,
- (iii) `⟪cₖ(t), z⟫ → ⟪v(t), z⟫` for **every** `t ∈ [0, T]` and every `z : L2VF`,
- (iv)  `‖v(t)‖ ≤ ‖u₀‖` for every `t ∈ [0, T]`,
- (v)   `v 0 = u₀` (endpoint pinning via `u_initial` + `velocityProjection_n_tendsto`),
- (vi)  `t ↦ ⟪v(t), w⟫` is Lipschitz on `[0, T]` for every Galerkin test `w`.

Construction: a.e.-strong subsequence (step 2) → per-test everywhere-Cauchy via
equi-Lipschitz + density (steps 1, 3) → extension to all `z` by `L2Sigmaᗮ`-orthogonality
and Galerkin-test density → Riesz assembly inside the closed submodule `L2Sigma` (step 4).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.exists_weak_representative
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n)
          (alPkg : LerayHopf.AubinLionsPackage F ν T u₀ galSeq),
          ∃ v ρ,
            StrictMono ρ ∧
              (∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T), v t = alPkg.u t) ∧
                (∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T),
                    Filter.Tendsto (fun k => ↑((galSeq (alPkg.φ (ρ k))).u t)) Filter.atTop (nhds ↑(alPkg.u t))) ∧
                  (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T,
                      ∀ (z : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
                        Filter.Tendsto (fun k => inner ℝ (↑((galSeq (alPkg.φ (ρ k))).u t)) z) Filter.atTop
                          (nhds (inner ℝ (↑(v t)) z))) ∧
                    (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖↑(v t)‖ ≤ ‖↑u₀‖) ∧
                      v 0 = u₀ ∧
                        ∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
                          LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w →
                            ∃ L,
                              0 ≤ L ∧
                                ∀ s ∈ Set.Icc 0 T,
                                  ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, |inner ℝ ↑(v t) ↑w - inner ℝ ↑(v s) ↑w| ≤ L * |t - s|

nodes.doc:
**Master construction: the ∀t-weakly-continuous representative** of the Aubin–Lions
limit on ℝ³.

Produces `v : Time → L2Sigma_R3` such that:
- (a.e.)      `v t = alPkg.u t` for a.e. `t ∈ [0, T]`.
- (∀t weak)  `⟪(galSeq (alPkg.φ n)).u t, z⟫ → ⟪v t, z⟫` for every `t ∈ [0, T]`, `z : L2VF_R3`.
- (∀t bound) `‖v t‖ ≤ ‖u₀‖` for every `t ∈ [0, T]`.
- (v 0)       `v 0 = u₀` (endpoint pinning via `u_initial` + `𝔊.tendsto_id`).
- (Lip)       `t ↦ ⟪v t, w⟫` is Lipschitz on `[0, T]` for every Galerkin test `w`.

This is the **verbatim S5 statement** from the feasibility spike
(`LerayHopf/Scratch/Issue4LimitPassageSpike.lean`).

nodes.doc:
`F.b (·) (·) w` is jointly `L²`-continuous at a fixed Schwartz `w` — from the bilinear bound
`b_bound` (`|F.b u v w| ≤ Cb·‖u‖·‖v‖`) together with bilinearity (`b_add`, `b_smul`).  Proved by
the standard bounded-bilinear ⇒ continuous estimate on the difference
`F.b u v w − F.b u₀ v₀ w = F.b (u−u₀) v w + F.b u₀ (v−v₀) w`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fb_continuous_fixedTest
