nodes.doc:
**Step 1 (conjunct-2 atom b): the integral representation `F.b f g w = antisymmIntegral`.**
Density extension of `fb_eq_antisymmIntegral_schwartz` using joint `L²`-continuity of both sides
(`fb_continuous_fixedTest`; `antisymmIntegral_continuous`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fb_eq_antisymmIntegral
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3)
  (ψw : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) ↑w = (ψw j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
    ∀ (f g : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3), F.b f g w = LerayHopf.antisymmIntegral✝ ψw ⋯ ↑f ↑g

nodes.doc:
The Schwartz-pair agreement: for Schwartz-div-free `f', g'`, `F.b f' g' w` equals the
antisymmetric integral.  `b_galerkin` rewrites `F.b` to `convIntegralSchwartz`, then
`convIntegralSchwartz_divFree_eq` gives the IBP'd integral, which matches `antisymmIntegral`'s
inner-product integrands a.e.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fb_eq_antisymmIntegral_schwartz
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3)
  (ψw : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) ↑w = (ψw j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
    ∀ (f' g' : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
      LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 f' →
        LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 g' → F.b f' g' w = LerayHopf.antisymmIntegral✝ ψw ⋯ ↑f' ↑g'

nodes.doc:
**Per-ball convergence of F.b (atom b of WeakFormNS passage).**

For a Schwartz-div-free test `w` with witnesses `ψw`, if `uₙ → u` in L²(B_R) for every
radius R (per-ball convergence) with uniform L² bound `‖uₙ‖ ≤ M`, then
`F.b(uₙ, uₙ, w) → F.b(u, u, w)`.

**Method.** After applying `fb_eq_antisymmIntegral` and unfolding `antisymmIntegral`, each
(i, a)-term is `inner (comp_a uₙ) (mulBddR φ_{a,i} (comp_i uₙ))`.  The bilinear difference
splits into:
- TERM A `= inner (comp_a (uₙ - u)) (mulBddR φ (comp_i uₙ))`: ε/3 split via
  `schwartz_ball_tail_decay` for the `‖x‖>R` tail and per-ball for the `‖x‖≤R` ball;
- TERM B `= inner (comp_a u) (mulBddR φ (comp_i (uₙ - u)))`: self-adjointness +
  scalar ball-tail ε/3 argument (tail of FIXED element → 0, ball uses per-ball).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fb_tendsto_of_perball
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
  LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 w →
    ∀ (un : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (M : ℝ),
      0 ≤ M →
        (∀ (n : ℕ), ‖↑(un n)‖ ≤ M) →
          ‖↑u‖ ≤ M →
            (∀ (k : ℕ),
                Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑(un n)) Filter.atTop
                  (nhds (LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑u))) →
              Filter.Tendsto (fun n => F.b (un n) (un n) w) Filter.atTop (nhds (F.b u u w))

nodes.doc: `fderiv φ x (eᵢ) = (∂_{eᵢ}φ) x` for a complex Schwartz function.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fderiv_apply_single_eq_lineDeriv
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (i : Fin 3) (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  (fderiv ℝ (⇑φ) x) (EuclideanSpace.single i 1) =
    ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (EuclideanSpace.single i 1)) φ) x

nodes.doc: `fderiv` of the scaled cutoff via the chain rule.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fderiv_cutoff
nodes.signature:
∀ (R : ℝ) (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  fderiv ℝ (LerayHopf.cutoff✝ R) x = fderiv ℝ (↑LerayHopf.cutoffBump✝) ((1 / R) • x) ∘SL LerayHopf.scaleCLM✝ R

nodes.doc: Fresh copy of the private `fderiv_apply_single_eq_lineDeriv` (`EnergyClassConvection.lean:1164`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fderiv_single_eq_lineDeriv_C2
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (i : Fin 3) (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  (fderiv ℝ (⇑φ) x) (EuclideanSpace.single i 1) =
    ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (EuclideanSpace.single i 1)) φ) x

nodes.doc:
**Deliverable (Pillar E, R-global, MUST-PROVE).** A forward-global solution of the autonomous
finite-dim Galerkin ODE exists — discharging the last frontier behind `galerkin_ode_solution_R3`
over the concrete scheme `schemeOfBasis B`. **UNCONDITIONAL**: `FinDimGlobalODE` is now a
forward-time structure (`c_hasDeriv`/`ode` quantified `∀ t, 0 ≤ t →`), so the forward-global
solution G1 supplies it with no residual hypothesis.

Proof sketch: from `forwardGlobalSolution_exists` obtain `c` and its two properties; package the
four `FinDimGlobalODE` fields — `c := c`; `c_initial :=` G1's first conjunct;
`c_hasDeriv t ht :=` rewrite G1's explicit-derivative `HasDerivAt` through `HasDerivAt.deriv`
(`(hc t ht).hasDerivAt` form / `HasDerivAt.deriv`); `ode t ht :=` G1's second conjunct rewritten
through `HasDerivAt.deriv`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.finDimGlobalODE_exists
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν → ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ), Nonempty (LerayHopf.FinDimGlobalODE B F ν u₀ n)

nodes.doc:
**G1 (THE core, MUST-PROVE).** Forward-global existence: there is `c : ℝ → V_n` with
`c 0 = galerkinP B n u₀ (∈ V_n)` and `∀ t ≥ 0, HasDerivAt (↑∘c) (G_n (c t)) t`. Built by tiling
`[0,∞)` with the uniform local-existence time on the fixed a-priori ball (`solve_exists_on_step`),
gluing grid curves by uniqueness (`galerkinField_solution_agree`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.forwardGlobalSolution_exists
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ),
      ∃ c,
        ↑(c 0) = (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) ↑u₀ ∧
          ∀ (t : ℝ), 0 ≤ t → HasDerivAt (fun s => ↑(c s)) (↑(LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n (c t))) t

nodes.doc:
The finite set of wavenumbers `k : Fin 3 → ℤ` with `‖k‖_∞ ≤ n`:
the coordinate-aligned cube `[-n, n]³` in ℤ³.

`fourierBox n = {k | ∀ i, -(n : ℤ) ≤ k i ≤ n}` as a `Finset (Fin 3 → ℤ)`.
Membership is by `Fintype.mem_piFinset` + `Finset.mem_Icc`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierBox
nodes.signature: ℕ → Finset (Fin 3 → ℤ)

nodes.doc:
The fourierBox sets exhaust all of ℤ³: every wavenumber `k` lies in `fourierBox n`
for `n` large enough (namely `n = max_i |k i|`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierBox_exhausts
nodes.signature: ∀ (k : Fin 3 → ℤ), ∃ n, k ∈ LerayHopf.fourierBox n

nodes.doc: The Galerkin truncation cubes are monotone: if `m ≤ n` then `fourierBox m ⊆ fourierBox n`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierBox_monotone
nodes.signature: Monotone LerayHopf.fourierBox

nodes.doc:
The `k`-th Fourier-coefficient functional as a continuous ℂ-linear map `L2C →L[ℂ] ℂ`.

By `HilbertBasis.repr_apply_apply`, `torus3_mFourierBasis.repr f k = ⟪torus3_mFourierBasis k, f⟫`,
so this equals `mFourierCoeff3 f k`.  We define it via `innerSL` so that continuity is
automatic and the proof `fourierCoeffCLM_apply` is a one-line unfold.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierCoeffCLM
nodes.signature: (Fin 3 → ℤ) → ↥LerayHopf.L2C →L[ℂ] ℂ

nodes.doc:
`fourierCoeffCLM k f = mFourierCoeff3 f k`.

Proof: `innerSL ℂ (torus3_mFourierBasis k) f = ⟪torus3_mFourierBasis k, f⟫`
by `innerSL_apply_apply`, and `torus3_mFourierBasis.repr_apply_apply` gives
`torus3_mFourierBasis.repr f k = ⟪torus3_mFourierBasis k, f⟫`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierCoeffCLM_apply
nodes.signature: ∀ (k : Fin 3 → ℤ) (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C), (LerayHopf.fourierCoeffCLM k) f = LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f k

nodes.doc:
**Fourier of a complexified real Schwartz function is Hermitian** (local copy of the private
`CurlDensity.fourier_schwartzC_hermitian`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierH1_cxify_hermitian
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.cxifyH1✝ φ)) (-ξ) =
    (starRingEnd ℂ) ((FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.cxifyH1✝ φ)) ξ)

nodes.doc:
**Fourier of a Hermitian Schwartz function is real-valued** (local copy of the private
`CurlDensity.fourier_hermitian_real`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierH1_hermitian_real
nodes.signature:
∀ (g : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  (∀ (v : LerayHopf.Domain3), g (-v) = (starRingEnd ℂ) (g v)) →
    ∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), (starRingEnd ℂ) ((FourierTransform.fourier g) ξ) = (FourierTransform.fourier g) ξ

nodes.doc: The bounded Fourier-component CLM `𝓕 ∘ projⱼ : L²VF → L²C` (as an ℝ-CLM).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierProjCLM
nodes.signature: Fin 3 → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 →L[ℝ] ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3

nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierProjCLM_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (j : Fin 3) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  (LerayHopf.fourierProjCLM✝ j) u = FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) u)

nodes.doc:
**Continuity of the `j`-th Fourier-component curve.** `s ↦ 𝓕 (proj_j (gs.u s))` is continuous
on `Ici 0` (composition of the continuous Galerkin curve with the continuous-linear `proj_j` and
the isometry `𝓕`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierProjCurve_continuousOn
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (j : Fin 3),
  ContinuousOn (fun s => FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) ↑(gs.u s))) (Set.Ici 0)

nodes.doc:
The n-th Galerkin projection: the orthogonal projection of `L2C = L²(𝕋³; ℂ)` onto
the finite-dimensional subspace `fourierSpan n` spanned by Fourier modes with ‖k‖_∞ ≤ n.

Defined as `(fourierSpan n).starProjection : L2C →L[ℂ] L2C`, which is the composition of
`(fourierSpan n).orthogonalProjectionOnto : L2C →L[ℂ] fourierSpan n`
with `(fourierSpan n).subtypeL : fourierSpan n →L[ℂ] L2C`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierProjection_n
nodes.signature: ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2C →L[ℂ] ↥LerayHopf.L2C

nodes.doc: Self-adjointness of the scalar Fourier projection (a `starProjection`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierProjection_n_inner_symm
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (f g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C),
  inner ℂ ((LerayHopf.fourierProjection_n n) f) g = inner ℂ f ((LerayHopf.fourierProjection_n n) g)

nodes.doc:
**Bonus (finite-rank ⟹ compact).** The Galerkin projection `fourierProjection_n N` is a
compact operator on `L2C`.

Proof route for lean-prover (sorry-free, ~5 lines):
1. `fourierProjection_n N = (fourierSpan N).starProjection
     = (fourierSpan N).subtypeL ∘ (fourierSpan N).orthogonalProjectionOnto`.
2. `fourierSpan N` is `FiniteDimensional ℂ` (instance `fourierSpan_finiteDimensional`).
3. Hence `fourierSpan N` is `ProperSpace` via `RCLike.properSpace_submodule`.
4. `ProperSpace` implies `LocallyCompactSpace` (instance `locallyCompact_of_proper`).
5. `isCompactOperator_of_locallyCompactSpace_dom` applies to
   `(fourierSpan N).orthogonalProjectionOnto : L2C →L[ℂ] ↥(fourierSpan N)`.
6. `IsCompactOperator.clm_comp` with `(fourierSpan N).subtypeL` gives the result.
Key mathlib lemmas: `isCompactOperator_of_locallyCompactSpace_dom`, `IsCompactOperator.clm_comp`,
`RCLike.properSpace_submodule`, `Submodule.starProjection`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierProjection_n_isCompactOperator
nodes.signature: ∀ (N : ℕ), IsCompactOperator ⇑(LerayHopf.fourierProjection_n N)

nodes.doc:
`fourierProjection_n` is the Fourier cutoff: it preserves coefficients inside the box
and kills those outside. This is what makes it the genuine Galerkin/Fourier-truncation
projection (and is the tool for showing it preserves the divergence-free subspace).

Proof strategy for lean-prover:
The orthogonal projection `(fourierSpan n).starProjection f` onto a closed subspace `S`
is the unique element of `S` closest to `f`, and it satisfies the inner-product
characterisation `⟪g, P f⟫ = ⟪g, f⟫` for all `g ∈ S`, and `⟪g, P f⟫ = 0` for `g ∈ Sᗮ`.
Apply this with `g = torus3_mFourierBasis k` (a Hilbert basis vector):
- If `k ∈ fourierBox n`, then `torus3_mFourierBasis k ∈ fourierSpan n = S`,
  so `mFourierCoeff3 (P f) k = ⟪torus3_mFourierBasis k, P f⟫ = ⟪torus3_mFourierBasis k, f⟫
  = mFourierCoeff3 f k`.
- If `k ∉ fourierBox n`, then `torus3_mFourierBasis k ⊥ fourierSpan n` by
  orthonormality of the Hilbert basis (`HilbertBasis.orthonormal`), so
  `mFourierCoeff3 (P f) k = ⟪torus3_mFourierBasis k, P f⟫ = 0`.
Key mathlib lemmas: `Submodule.inner_orthogonalProjection_eq_of_mem`,
`Submodule.inner_orthogonalProjection_eq_zero_of_not_mem` (or the
`orthogonalProjection`/`starProjection` inner-product API),
`HilbertBasis.repr_apply_apply`, `HilbertBasis.orthonormal`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierProjection_n_mFourierCoeff
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ),
  LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.fourierProjection_n n) f) k =
    if k ∈ LerayHopf.fourierBox n then LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f k else 0

nodes.doc:
The Galerkin projections `fourierProjection_n n f` converge to `f` in `L2C` as `n → ∞`.

Proof: apply `Submodule.starProjection_tendsto_self` with:
- `U = fourierSpan` (the monotone family, by `fourierSpan_monotone`),
- `[∀ n, (fourierSpan n).HasOrthogonalProjection]` (instance, from D-27),
- `fourierSpan_iSup_dense` (the density condition, D-28).

Key mathlib lemma:
`Submodule.starProjection_tendsto_self :
  Monotone U → (∀ t, (U t).HasOrthogonalProjection) → ⊤ ≤ (⨆ t, U t).topologicalClosure →
  Filter.Tendsto (fun t => (U t).starProjection x) atTop (𝓝 x)`
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierProjection_n_tendsto
nodes.signature: ∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C), Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (LerayHopf.fourierProjection_n n) f) Filter.atTop (nhds f)

nodes.doc:
The finite-dimensional subspace of `L2C` spanned by Fourier modes with
wavenumber in `fourierBox n`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierSpan
nodes.signature: ℕ → Submodule ℂ ↥LerayHopf.L2C

nodes.doc:
`fourierSpan n` is complete: a finite-dimensional submodule of a normed space
over a complete field is complete. Uses `Submodule.complete_of_finiteDimensional`
(returns `IsComplete`) converted to `CompleteSpace` via `IsComplete.completeSpace_coe`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierSpan_completeSpace
nodes.signature: ∀ (n : ℕ), CompleteSpace ↥(LerayHopf.fourierSpan n)

nodes.doc:
`fourierSpan n` is finite-dimensional: it is the span of the finite set
`torus3_mFourierBasis '' ↑(fourierBox n)`. Uses `FiniteDimensional.span_of_finite`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierSpan_finiteDimensional
nodes.signature: ∀ (n : ℕ), FiniteDimensional ℂ ↥(LerayHopf.fourierSpan n)

nodes.doc:
`fourierSpan n` has an orthogonal projection from `L2C`.
Follows from `CompleteSpace (fourierSpan n)` via the priority-100 instance
`HasOrthogonalProjection.ofCompleteSpace`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierSpan_hasOrthogonalProjection
nodes.signature: ∀ (n : ℕ), (LerayHopf.fourierSpan n).HasOrthogonalProjection

nodes.doc:
The union of the finite Fourier span submodules is dense in `L2C = L²(𝕋³; ℂ)`:
`(⨆ n, fourierSpan n).topologicalClosure = ⊤`.

Proof outline:
1. `Set.range torus3_mFourierBasis ⊆ ⋃ n, torus3_mFourierBasis '' ↑(fourierBox n)`
   — every basis vector `torus3_mFourierBasis k` lies in `fourierSpan n` for
   `n := max_i |k i|` (by `fourierBox_exhausts`).
2. Hence `span ℂ (Set.range torus3_mFourierBasis) ≤ ⨆ n, fourierSpan n`
   (via `Submodule.span_mono` + `Submodule.iSup_span`).
3. `(⨆ n, fourierSpan n).topologicalClosure ≥ (span ℂ (Set.range torus3_mFourierBasis)).topologicalClosure = ⊤`
   (by `HilbertBasis.dense_span`).

Key mathlib lemmas:
- `HilbertBasis.dense_span` : `(span ℂ (Set.range b)).topologicalClosure = ⊤`
- `Submodule.iSup_span` : `⨆ i, span R (p i) = span R (⋃ i, p i)`
- `Submodule.topologicalClosure_mono` : monotone closure.
- `fourierBox_exhausts` for the index exhaustion.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierSpan_iSup_dense
nodes.signature: ⊤ ≤ (⨆ n, LerayHopf.fourierSpan n).topologicalClosure

nodes.doc: The Fourier span submodules are monotone: if `m ≤ n` then `fourierSpan m ≤ fourierSpan n`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourierSpan_monotone
nodes.signature: Monotone LerayHopf.fourierSpan

nodes.doc:
**A3 weighted-Fourier extraction.** For `f` in the Sobolev space, the L²-Fourier transform
`𝓕 f` is a.e. equal to `wInv • f'`, where `f'` is the Sobolev (weighted) representative,
`smulLeftCLM ℂ wPos (𝓕 f) = f'`. This is the scalar adaptation of
`RellichBall.memH1_weightedL2_integrable_R`'s a.e. extraction.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_ae_eq_wInv_smul
nodes.signature:
∀ (f f' : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3),
  (TemperedDistribution.smulLeftCLM ℂ LerayHopf.wPosC✝)
        (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution (FourierTransform.fourier f)) =
      MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f' →
    ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier f) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] fun ξ => ↑(LerayHopf.wInv✝ ξ) • ↑↑f' ξ

nodes.doc:
**Step 1 (curl Fourier multiplier).**  The Fourier transform of the `j`-th component of
the curl of a Schwartz potential is, pointwise, the `j`-th component of the cross-product
symbol `(2π i) ξ × ψ̂(ξ)`, where `ψ̂` is the Fourier transform of the underlying POTENTIAL
components `ψ k` (NOT of the curl).

This is the genuine curl Fourier multiplier identity
`𝓕(curl ψ)_j(ξ) = (2π i) (ξ × ψ̂(ξ))_j`, obtained from the symbol identity
`𝓕(∂_a f)(ξ) = 2π i ξ_a · f̂(ξ)` applied to each of the two summands of `(curlSchwartz ψ)_j`
and assembled into the cross product `crossWithIξ`.  The LHS curl component is identified via
`curlSchwartzL2_projComponent`; the RHS vector is the Fourier transform of the potential
components `potentialComponentC ψ k` — so the statement is NOT circular.

Blocker: mathlib's `SchwartzMap` Fourier API has the derivative→multiplier identity in the
shape `𝓕 (∂ ψ) = (multiplier) • 𝓕 ψ`, but threading it through `lineDerivOpCLM`, the
`Lp`-level `𝓕` on components, and the cyclic-index assembly is real (non-mechanical) work that
belongs to `lean-prover`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_curlSchwartz_eq_cross
nodes.signature:
∀ (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (j : Fin 3),
  ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier
          ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) (LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 ψ))) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume]
    fun ξ => LerayHopf.crossWithIξ ξ (fun k => ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.potentialComponentC ψ k)) ξ) j

nodes.doc:
**Stage 2 result.**  The `j`-th complex-component Fourier transform of the curl of the
regularized potential is a.e. `‖ξ‖²/(a²+‖ξ‖²) · ŵ_j`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_curl_potOf_ae
nodes.signature:
∀ (a : ℝ) (ha : 0 < a) (wh : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ)
  (hHerm : ∀ (b : Fin 3) (v : LerayHopf.Domain3), (wh b) (-v) = (starRingEnd ℂ) ((wh b) v)),
  (∀ᵐ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), ∑ i, ↑(ξ.ofLp i) * (wh i) ξ = 0) →
    ∀ (j : Fin 3),
      ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier
              ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j)
                (LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 (LerayHopf.potOf✝ a ha wh hHerm)))) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume]
        fun ξ => ↑(‖ξ‖ ^ 2 / (a ^ 2 + ‖ξ‖ ^ 2)) * (wh j) ξ

nodes.doc:
`𝓕(cxify ψ_k) = ψ̂_k`: the Fourier transform of the complexified real potential is the
regularized symbol.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_cxify_potOf
nodes.signature:
∀ (a : ℝ) (ha : 0 < a) (wh : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ)
  (hHerm : ∀ (b : Fin 3) (v : LerayHopf.Domain3), (wh b) (-v) = (starRingEnd ℂ) ((wh b) v)) (k : Fin 3),
  FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.cxifyH1✝ (LerayHopf.potOf✝ a ha wh hHerm k)) = LerayHopf.symbolHatOf✝ a ha wh k

nodes.doc:
The `j`-th component Fourier transform of the error `curl ψ_a − w` is a.e.
`-a²/(a²+‖ξ‖²) · ŵ_j`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_err_ae
nodes.signature:
∀ (a : ℝ) (ha : 0 < a) (wh : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ)
  (hHerm : ∀ (b : Fin 3) (v : LerayHopf.Domain3), (wh b) (-v) = (starRingEnd ℂ) ((wh b) v)),
  (∀ᵐ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), ∑ i, ↑(ξ.ofLp i) * (wh i) ξ = 0) →
    ∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
      (∀ (j : Fin 3),
          ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) w)) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] ⇑(wh j)) →
        ∀ (j : Fin 3),
          ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier
                  ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j)
                    (LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 (LerayHopf.potOf✝ a ha wh hHerm) - w))) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume]
            fun ξ => ↑(-(a ^ 2 / (a ^ 2 + ‖ξ‖ ^ 2))) * (wh j) ξ

nodes.doc:
**Reality of the Fourier transform of a Hermitian Schwartz function.**  If
`g : 𝓢(ℝ³, ℂ)` is Hermitian (`g(-v) = conj(g(v))` for all `v`), then `𝓕 g` is real-valued:
`conj(𝓕 g ξ) = 𝓕 g ξ`.

Pointwise integral argument (mirrors `fourier_schwartzC_hermitian`): pushing `conj` into the
integral defining `𝓕 g ξ = ∫ 𝐞(-⟪v,ξ⟫) g v` turns the unit-modulus character into its
inverse `𝐞(⟪v,ξ⟫)` and conjugates the integrand to `g(-v)` (Hermitian); the substitution
`v ↦ -v` (negation is measure preserving) restores `∫ 𝐞(-⟪v,ξ⟫) g v = 𝓕 g ξ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_hermitian_real
nodes.signature:
∀ (g : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  (∀ (v : LerayHopf.Domain3), g (-v) = (starRingEnd ℂ) (g v)) →
    ∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), (starRingEnd ℂ) ((FourierTransform.fourier g) ξ) = (FourierTransform.fourier g) ξ

nodes.doc:
**(P1) Lp-level Hermitian reflection.**  For a real `a : Lp ℝ 2`, the reflection of the
Fourier transform of its complexification equals the conjugation of that Fourier transform,
as elements of `L2C_R3`.  Density extension of `reflect_fourier_schwartzC_eq_conj` over the
real Schwartz functions (`SchwartzMap.denseRange_toLpCLM`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_ofReal_reflect_eq_conj
nodes.signature:
∀ (a : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)),
  (MeasureTheory.Lp.compMeasurePreserving Neg.neg ⋯)
      (FourierTransform.fourier ((ContinuousLinearMap.compLpL 2 MeasureTheory.volume RCLike.ofRealCLM) a)) =
    (ContinuousLinearMap.compLpL 2 MeasureTheory.volume ↑Complex.conjCLE)
      (FourierTransform.fourier ((ContinuousLinearMap.compLpL 2 MeasureTheory.volume RCLike.ofRealCLM) a))

nodes.doc:
The Fourier transform of the `k`-th potential component (as an `L²`-class) is a.e. the
regularized symbol `ψ̂_k`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_potentialComponentC_potOf
nodes.signature:
∀ (a : ℝ) (ha : 0 < a) (wh : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ)
  (hHerm : ∀ (b : Fin 3) (v : LerayHopf.Domain3), (wh b) (-v) = (starRingEnd ℂ) ((wh b) v)) (k : Fin 3),
  ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier
          (LerayHopf.potentialComponentC (LerayHopf.potOf✝ a ha wh hHerm) k)) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume]
    ⇑(LerayHopf.symbolHatOf✝ a ha wh k)

nodes.doc:
**Fourier component of a Steklov average — the L²-element commute (PROVED, no `sorry`).**
For `0 < δ` and `0 ≤ t`, the `j`-th Fourier component of the Steklov average is the time-average
of the curve's `j`-th Fourier components:

  `𝓕 (proj_j (steklovAvg gs δ t)) = δ⁻¹ • ∫_{t}^{t+δ} 𝓕 (proj_j (gs.u s)) ds`   (in `L2C_R3`).

This is the `Lp`-element identity underlying the spectral Jensen bound: the scalar `δ⁻¹` factors
out of `𝓕 ∘ proj_j` (both `ℝ`-linear), and the continuous-linear maps `proj_j` and `𝓕` commute
with the (interval) Bochner integral of the continuous curve via
`ContinuousLinearMap.integral_comp_comm` / `LinearIsometryEquiv.integral_comp_comm`. It is
`Lp`-frontier-free; the genuine missing pillar is only the subsequent *pointwise coeFn* interchange
(see `viscousFormSq_steklovAvg_le_average`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_proj_steklovAvg_eq
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ t : ℝ},
  0 < δ →
    0 ≤ t →
      ∀ (j : Fin 3),
        FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) (LerayHopf.steklovAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t)) =
          δ⁻¹ • ∫ (s : ℝ) in t..t + δ, FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) ↑(gs.u s))

nodes.doc:
Schwartz-level curl Fourier multiplier (pointwise, everywhere).  The Schwartz Fourier
transform of the complexified curl component `schwartzC (curlSchwartz ψ j)` at `ξ` equals the
`j`-th cross-product symbol on the complexified potential Fourier transforms.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_schwartzC_curl_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (j : Fin 3) (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ (LerayHopf.curlSchwartz ψ j))) ξ =
    LerayHopf.crossWithIξ ξ (fun k => (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ (ψ k))) ξ) j

nodes.doc:
**Fourier of a real function is Hermitian.**  For a real Schwartz `φ`, the Fourier
transform of its complexification satisfies `𝓕(schwartzC φ)(-ξ) = conj (𝓕(schwartzC φ)(ξ))`.

This is the conjugate symmetry of the Fourier transform of a (complexified) real function:
pushing `conj` through the integral defining `𝓕`, the unit-modulus character contributes
`conj(𝐞(-⟪v,ξ⟫)) = 𝐞(⟪v,ξ⟫) = 𝐞(-⟪v,-ξ⟫)`, while `conj` acts trivially on the real-valued
integrand `schwartzC φ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_schwartzC_hermitian
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ φ)) (-ξ) =
    (starRingEnd ℂ) ((FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ φ)) ξ)

nodes.doc:
Pointwise Fourier symbol of the complexified line derivative: for a real Schwartz `f` and
direction `m`, the Schwartz Fourier transform of `schwartzC (∂_m f)` at `ξ` is
`(2π i)(inner ℝ ξ m)·𝓕(schwartzC f)(ξ)`.

This is `fourier_lineDerivOp_eq` applied to the complexified map `schwartzC f`, using
`lineDerivOp_schwartzC` to identify `schwartzC (∂_m f) = ∂_m (schwartzC f)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_schwartzC_lineDeriv_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (f : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (m ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ (LineDeriv.lineDerivOp m f))) ξ =
    2 * ↑Real.pi * Complex.I * ↑(inner ℝ ξ m) * (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ f)) ξ

nodes.doc:
**Second-order Fourier symbol.** The Schwartz Fourier transform of the complexified
negative Laplacian is the weighted transform: `𝓕(schwartzC (-Δ f))(ξ) = (2π)²‖ξ‖² · 𝓕(schwartzC f)(ξ)`.
Proof: apply `fourier_schwartzC_lineDeriv_apply` twice per axis (the symbol of `∂ₐ∂ₐ` is
`(2π i ξₐ)² = -(2π)² ξₐ²`), sum over axes (`∑ₐ ξₐ² = ‖ξ‖²`), negate.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_schwartzC_negLap_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (f : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ (LerayHopf.negLapSchwartz✝ f))) ξ =
    ↑((2 * Real.pi) ^ 2 * ‖ξ‖ ^ 2) * (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ f)) ξ

nodes.doc: `𝓕` on `L2C_R3` distributes over subtraction.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fourier_sub_L2C
nodes.signature: ∀ (A B : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3), FourierTransform.fourier (A - B) = FourierTransform.fourier A - FourierTransform.fourier B

nodes.doc:
**DELIVERABLE.  Fréchet–Kolmogorov (Riesz) L²-precompactness criterion** — a genuine
discharge of `RellichBall.FrechetKolmogorovInput` (type copied verbatim, no weakening).

Assembles the standard mollification route: from the uniform L²-translation modulus
(`convolution_l2_tendsto_uniform`, step 1) each family member is uniformly ε-approximated by its
mollification, whose image is totally bounded by Arzelà–Ascoli
(`mollified_family_totallyBounded_L2`, step 3); the transfer lemma
(`totallyBounded_of_uniform_approx`, step 4) makes `S` totally bounded, and completeness of
`L2ballR3 R` upgrades total boundedness to precompactness
(`isCompact_iff_totallyBounded_isComplete`), giving the compact `K ⊇ S`.

**Enlarged-ball bound — RESOLVED via the criterion's global bound (Codex Gate round 3, high).**
The helper lemmas (steps 1–3) require, honestly, an ENLARGED-BALL bound
`hbdEnl : ∀ f ∈ S, ‖restrictToBall (R + r₀) (rep f)‖ ≤ C` on the kernel-support enlargement
`B_{R+r₀}` (NOT just the single-radius ball bound `‖f‖ ≤ C`, which cannot prevent arbitrarily
large low-frequency mass on the annulus `B_{R+r₀} ∖ B_R`).

`FrechetKolmogorovInput`'s strengthened type hands the prover, beyond the in-ball bound `hbd` and
the translation modulus `hmod`, a uniform GLOBAL L²-norm bound on the representatives:
* `hbddGlobal : ∀ f ∈ S, ‖rep f‖ ≤ C`.

From `hbddGlobal` the enlarged-ball bound is IMMEDIATE for any `r₀ ≥ 0`, by monotonicity of ball
mass in the radius: `‖restrictToBall (R + r₀) (rep f)‖ ≤ ‖rep f‖ ≤ C` (the same
`restrict_le_self`/`eLpNorm_mono_measure` chain as `RellichBall.admissible_family_uniform_bound`).
This is the standard Riesz full-mass control of Fréchet–Kolmogorov; it carries no Sobolev/gradient
content.  The actual Navier–Stokes call site `localRellichInput_of_frechetKolmogorov` supplies it
from its admissible family's GLOBAL `‖w‖ ≤ M` bound.

**Assembly (pure proof-body fill, no upstream blocker).**  Derive `hbdEnl` from `hbddGlobal` by
ball-mass monotonicity; feed `hbdEnl`, `hbd`, `hrep`, `hmod` into `convolution_l2_tendsto_uniform`
(step 1) to get, for each `ε`, a totally bounded mollified image approximating `S` within `ε`;
`totallyBounded_of_uniform_approx` (step 4) makes `S` totally bounded; closure of the totally
bounded `S` is compact (`isCompact_iff_totallyBounded_isComplete`, `L2ballR3 R` complete); take
`K := closure S`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.frechetKolmogorov_holds
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.FrechetKolmogorovInput

nodes.doc:
**Joint integrability for the finite-set Fubini swap (PROVED).**  On a finite-measure
measurable set `s`, the integrand `F (x, h) = K.η h · ⟪c, g (x + h)⟫` is integrable for the
product measure `(volume.restrict s).prod volume`: each `h`-slice is integrable (kernel `× L²`,
Hölder) and `x ↦ ∫ h, ‖F (x, h)‖` is bounded by the constant `‖kernelL2R K‖ · ‖c‖ · ‖g‖`,
integrable on the finite set `s`.  This absolute convergence is exactly what makes Fubini
legitimate on `s` even though the raw `ℝ³ × ℝ³` double integral is not.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.fubini_integrand_integrable
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) {s : Set LerayHopf.Domain3},
  MeasurableSet s →
    MeasureTheory.volume s ≠ ⊤ →
      ∀ (c : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)),
        MeasureTheory.Integrable (Function.uncurry fun x h => K.η h * inner ℝ c (↑↑g (x + h)))
          ((MeasureTheory.volume.restrict s).prod MeasureTheory.volume)

nodes.doc:
Further restriction of an `L²(B_k)` element to a smaller ball `B_R` (`R ≤ k`), through the
underlying a.e. function.  This is well-defined because `volume.restrict (B_R) ≤
volume.restrict (B_k)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.furtherRestrict
nodes.signature: (R k : ℝ) → R ≤ k → ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 k) → ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R)

nodes.doc: The further-restriction's underlying function agrees a.e. (on `B_R`) with the original.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.furtherRestrict_coeFn
nodes.signature:
∀ (R k : ℝ) (h : R ≤ k) (w : ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 k)),
  ↑↑(LerayHopf.furtherRestrict✝ R k h w) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Metric.closedBall 0 R)] ↑↑w

nodes.doc: Continuity of further restriction.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.furtherRestrict_continuous
nodes.signature: ∀ (R k : ℝ) (h : R ≤ k), Continuous (LerayHopf.furtherRestrict✝ R k h)

nodes.doc: Further restriction is distance-nonincreasing, hence continuous.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.furtherRestrict_dist_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (R k : ℝ) (h : R ≤ k) (w w' : ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 k)),
  dist (LerayHopf.furtherRestrict✝ R k h w) (LerayHopf.furtherRestrict✝ R k h w') ≤ dist w w'

nodes.doc: Composition: further-restricting a ball-`k` restriction gives the ball-`R` restriction.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.furtherRestrict_restrictToBall
nodes.signature:
∀ (R k : ℝ) (h : R ≤ k) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.furtherRestrict✝ R k h (LerayHopf.restrictToBall k w) = LerayHopf.restrictToBall R w

nodes.doc:
**Concrete, axiom-free Galerkin sequence (issue #10).**  For the concrete scheme
`schemeOfBasis B` and an NS-forms bundle `F`, the per-`n` Galerkin solution data is supplied
unconditionally by `galerkinSolutionData_unconditional` (which rests on the proved finite-dim
ODE solver `finDimGlobalODE_exists`, NOT on `galerkin_ode_solution_R3`).

This is the drop-in replacement for the `fun n => galerkin_ode_solution_R3 𝔊 F ν hν u₀ n`
sequence used inside `build_galerkin_package_R3`: same type
(`∀ n, GalerkinSolutionData_R3 (schemeOfBasis B) F ν u₀ n`), no axiom.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galSeq_R3_of_basis
nodes.signature:
(B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) →
  (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) →
    (ν : ℝ) →
      0 < ν →
        (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) → (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B) F ν u₀ n

nodes.doc:
**Concrete, axiom-free Galerkin sequence (issue #24).**  For a `Torus3NSForms` bundle `F`, the
per-`n` Galerkin solution data is supplied unconditionally by `galerkinSolutionData_torus` (which
rests on the proved forward-global finite-dim ODE solver `forwardGlobalSolution_exists`, NOT on
`galerkin_ode_solution`).

This is the drop-in replacement for the `fun n => galerkin_ode_solution F ν hν u₀ n` sequence used
inside `build_galerkin_package`: same type (`∀ n, GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n`), no axiom.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galSeq_of_torus
nodes.signature:
(F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) →
  (ν : ℝ) → 0 < ν → (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) → (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n

nodes.doc:
The finite (box-`n`) Galerkin convection form.

For Fourier modes `e^{2πi k·x}`, the convection structure constant is:
  `b(u, v, w) = Σᵢ Σₐ Σ_{k,l ∈ fourierBox n} û_a(k) · (2πi lₐ) · v̂ᵢ(l) · ŵᵢ(-(k+l))`
where `û_a(k) = mFourierCoeff3 (L2VF_projComponentC a u) k`.

This is the correct finite-sum approximation of the convection trilinear form `(u·∇)v·w`
on `𝕋³`.  It is well-defined (finite sum), generically nonzero (so `b = 0` is excluded by
`b_galerkin`), and equals the genuine convection form on Galerkin subspaces `Vₙ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection
nodes.signature: ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → ℝ

nodes.doc: `galerkinConvection n` is additive in the first slot.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection_add_1
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (u u' v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n (u + u') v w =
    LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v w + LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u' v w

nodes.doc: `galerkinConvection n` is additive in the second slot.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection_add_2
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (u v v' w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u (v + v') w =
    LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v w + LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v' w

nodes.doc: `galerkinConvection n` is additive in the third slot.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection_add_3
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (u v w w' : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v (w + w') =
    LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v w + LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v w'

nodes.doc:
**Antisymmetry of the Galerkin convection form over the test subspace `Vₙ`.**

For `u ∈ L²_σ` (divergence-free) and test slots `v, w ∈ Vₙ` (finite Fourier support,
`velocityProjection_n n v = v`, `velocityProjection_n n w = w`):
`galerkinConvection n u v w = -galerkinConvection n u w v`.

This is the standard Faedo–Galerkin convection antisymmetry (Temam II.§1, RRS §3.2): it is the
faithful, truthful piece of the unrestricted `Torus3NSForms.b_antisymm` field, which is
witnessed by the genuine non-truncated `∫ (u·∇)v·w` convection form and matched to this
finite-box form via `b_galerkin`.

**Why `v, w ∈ Vₙ` is required.** The box-truncated form leaves `m = -(k+l)` unrestricted, so
the raw index set `box × box` is NOT invariant under the involution `(k,l) ↦ (k,-(k+l))` that
antisymmetry needs (`neg_mem_fourierBox` covers only `k ↦ -k`).  Finite support of `u` alone
does not rescue it.  Under `hv, hw`, the coefficients `v̂_i, ŵ_i` vanish outside `box`
(`coeff_zero_outside_box`), so every nonzero summand has `l ∈ box` AND `-(k+l) ∈ box`; both
sums collapse to the `σ_k`-invariant set `C_k`, where the `l ↔ -(k+l)` reindex is a genuine
involution.  The cancellation `lₐ + (-(k+l))ₐ = -kₐ` is then killed by `∑_a kₐ û_a(k) = 0`
(`DivFreeL2 u`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection_antisymm
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) v = v →
    (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) w = w →
      LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n (↑u) v w = -LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n (↑u) w v

nodes.doc:
**Bilinear L² bound** for the finite Galerkin convection form.

For fixed `n` and `w : L2VF`, the map `(u, v) ↦ galerkinConvection n u v w` is a bilinear
form in `(u, v)` with an explicit L² bound
`|galerkinConvection n u v w| ≤ C · ‖u‖_{L²} · ‖v‖_{L²}`.

**Proof (no convergence needed — everything is a finite Finset sum):**

`galerkinConvection n u v w = (∑ i ∑ a ∑_{k ∈ box} ∑_{l ∈ box}
   û_a(k) · (2πi lₐ) · v̂_i(l) · ŵ_i(-(k+l))).re`.

Since `|z.re| ≤ ‖z‖` (`abs_re_le_norm`) and the norm of a finite sum is bounded by the sum
of norms (`norm_sum_le`), it suffices to bound each summand norm:
`‖û_a(k)·(2πi lₐ)·v̂_i(l)·ŵ_i(-(k+l))‖
   ≤ (‖projₐ‖·‖u‖)·‖2πi lₐ‖·(‖projᵢ‖·‖v‖)·‖ŵ_i(-(k+l))‖`,
using `norm_mFourierCoeff3_le` (single coefficient ≤ L² norm) and `‖proj_j x‖ ≤ ‖proj_j‖·‖x‖`
(`ContinuousLinearMap.le_opNorm`).  Summing the `‖u‖·‖v‖`-free factors over the finite box
gives the constant `C`.  The hypothesis `hw` (finite support of `w`) is not needed for the
bound — the constant uses the actual coefficients `‖ŵ_i(-(k+l))‖` directly.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) w = w →
    ∃ C, ∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), |LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v w| ≤ C * ‖u‖ * ‖v‖

nodes.doc:
**Level stability (general, symmetric form).**  If all three slots lie in `Vₘ` *and* in `Vₙ`,
then `galerkinConvection m u v w = galerkinConvection n u v w`.  Both truncations equal the
truncation at `max m n` (each is the larger box for the other), so they agree.

This is the consistency fact that makes a single total `b` pinned to `galerkinConvection` on every
`Vₙ` (the `Torus3NSForms.b_galerkin` shape, quantified over `n`) well-posed: the pinned value does
not depend on which box `n` is used, as long as it bounds the supports.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection_level_stable
nodes.signature:
∀ (m n : ℕ) (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n m) u = u →
    (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n m) v = v →
      (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) u = u →
        (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) v = v →
          LerayHopf.galerkinConvection m u v w = LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v w

nodes.doc:
**Level stability (monotone step).**  If all three slots lie in `Vₘ`
(`velocityProjection_n m · = ·`) and `m ≤ n`, then truncating at the larger box `n` gives the
same value as truncating at `m`:
`galerkinConvection n u v w = galerkinConvection m u v w`.

Proof: `fourierBox m ⊆ fourierBox n` (`fourierBox_monotone`).  In the level-`n` sum, the `k`-sum
restricts to `fourierBox m` because the leading factor `û_a(k)` vanishes for `k ∉ fourierBox m`
(`coeff_zero_outside_box m u hu`), and the `l`-sum restricts to `fourierBox m` because the factor
`v̂_i(l)` vanishes for `l ∉ fourierBox m` (`coeff_zero_outside_box m v hv`).  The remaining
`fourierBox m × fourierBox m` sum is exactly `galerkinConvection m u v w`; the `w`-coefficient is
unchanged.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection_level_step
nodes.signature:
∀ (m n : ℕ),
  m ≤ n →
    ∀ (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
      (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n m) u = u →
        (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n m) v = v →
          LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v w = LerayHopf.galerkinConvection m u v w

nodes.doc:
`galerkinConvection n` is ℝ-homogeneous in the first slot.

Proof: `mFourierCoeff3_projComponentC_smul` turns `û_a(c • u, k)` into `(c : ℂ) * û_a(u, k)`.
The scalar `(c : ℂ)` is then the outermost factor in each summand (it multiplies the
entire product since it's the first factor), so `Re[(c : ℂ) * z] = c * Re[z]` and
`Finset.mul_sum` pull `c` outside all four nested sums.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection_smul_1
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ) (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n (c • u) v w = c * LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v w

nodes.doc: `galerkinConvection n` is ℝ-homogeneous in the second slot.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection_smul_2
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ) (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u (c • v) w = c * LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v w

nodes.doc: `galerkinConvection n` is ℝ-homogeneous in the third slot.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinConvection_smul_3
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ) (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v (c • w) = c * LerayHopf.galerkinConvection n u v w

nodes.doc:
**B1.** The Galerkin solution curve is continuous (into `L²`) on forward time:
`ContinuousOn (fun t => (gs.u t : L2VF_R3)) (Ici 0)`, from the `u_hasDeriv` field.

Fresh public mirror of the `private` `galerkin_curve_continuous` in
`AubinLionsLimitPassage.lean` (plan §2 fresh-build note).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinCurve_continuousOn
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} {ν : ℝ} {u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3} {n : ℕ}
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n), ContinuousOn (fun t => ↑(gs.u t)) (Set.Ici 0)

nodes.doc:
**B8.** INTEGRATED energy identity along the Galerkin curve: for `0 ≤ a ≤ b`,
`½‖u(b)‖² − ½‖u(a)‖² = −∫ₐᵇ viscousFormSq_R3 ν (u σ) dσ`.

Named `galerkinCurve_energy_identity` (the plan's working name `galerkin_energy_identity`
is already taken by the DIFFERENTIAL-form lemma on `GalerkinODEInput` in
`GalerkinODE.lean:191`, which this integrates; content unchanged).

Route (plan §3 B8): `HasDerivAt (fun σ => ½‖u σ‖²) ⟪u'(σ), u σ⟫ σ` (inner-product calculus
on `u_hasDeriv`), the ODE field `u_ode` tested at `w := gs.u σ` (admissible by `u_inVn`),
`F.b_self_zero` to kill the convection term, then
`intervalIntegral.integral_eq_sub_of_hasDerivAt` with the continuous derivative
`σ ↦ −viscousFormSq_R3 ν (u σ)` (continuity from B5, rescaled by `ν`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinCurve_energy_identity
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} {ν : ℝ} {u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3} {n : ℕ}
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (a b : ℝ),
  0 ≤ a →
    a ≤ b →
      1 / 2 * ‖↑(gs.u b)‖ ^ 2 - 1 / 2 * ‖↑(gs.u a)‖ ^ 2 = -∫ (σ : ℝ) in a..b, LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 ν ↑(gs.u σ)

nodes.doc:
Local helper (integrand of B8; fresh `GalerkinSolutionData_R3`-level copy of the
differential-form `galerkin_energy_identity`, `GalerkinODE.lean:191`, which is stated on
`GalerkinODEInput` and sits DOWNSTREAM of this file's import set): along the Galerkin
curve, `s ↦ ½‖u(s)‖²` has forward derivative `−viscousFormSq_R3 ν (u t)` at every
`t ≥ 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinCurve_halfNormSq_hasDerivAt

nodes.doc:
**B2.** The n-uniform energy bound along the curve: `‖u(t)‖ ≤ ‖u₀‖` for all `t ≥ 0`
(from `energy_bound` + `𝔊.norm_le`).

Fresh public mirror of the inline computation in `ArzelaAscoliTime.lean`, section (H) of
`galerkin_weakLimit_R3` (plan §2 fresh-build note).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinCurve_norm_le_u0
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} {ν : ℝ} {u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3} {n : ℕ}
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (t : LerayHopf.Time), 0 ≤ t → ‖↑(gs.u t)‖ ≤ ‖↑u₀‖

nodes.doc: H¹ regularity of any vector staying in the Schwartz Galerkin subspace.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinCurve_reg_mem
nodes.signature: ∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (n : ℕ) (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), v = (𝔊.P n) v → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v

nodes.doc:
Level-`n` states are closed under subtraction: `𝔊.P n` is linear, so the difference of
two curve values stays fixed by `𝔊.P n`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinCurve_sub_inVn
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} {ν : ℝ} {u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3} {n : ℕ}
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (σ σ' : ℝ), ↑(gs.u σ - gs.u σ') = (𝔊.P n) ↑(gs.u σ - gs.u σ')

nodes.doc:
**A1 (MUST-PROVE).** `⟪v, G_n v⟫ = −ν · viscousFormSq_R3 1 v ≤ 0` for `v ∈ V_n` (the
dissipation identity at a point), from R2 + `b_self_zero` + `stokesTestPairing_R3_diag`.

Proof sketch: apply `galerkinODE_vectorField_spec B F ν n v v` (testing `G_n v` against `v`),
then `R3NSForms.b_self_zero` kills the `b`-term and `stokesTestPairing_R3_diag` turns the stokes
term into `viscousFormSq_R3 1 v`; the result `−ν·viscousFormSq_R3 1 v ≤ 0` follows from
`viscousFormSq_R3_nonneg` and `mul_nonneg hν.le`. (Use `real_inner_comm` to align slots with R2.)
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinField_inner_self_nonpos
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ),
  0 < ν → ∀ (v : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)), inner ℝ ↑v ↑(LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n v) ≤ 0

nodes.doc:
**Helper (G1, splice-agreement).** Two local solutions of the autonomous field on overlapping
closed intervals that agree at one common point agree on the whole overlap.

Proof sketch: local Lipschitz of `G_n` on the relevant ball (from C1 /
`ContDiff.lipschitzOnWith`) feeds `ODE_solution_unique_of_mem_Icc`, giving uniqueness on the
overlap, so the two pieces coincide there.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinField_solution_agree
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ)
  (α β : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)) {a b t₀ : ℝ},
  a ≤ b →
    t₀ ∈ Set.Icc a b →
      α t₀ = β t₀ →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc a b, HasDerivAt α (LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n (α t)) t) →
          (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc a b, HasDerivAt β (LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n (β t)) t) →
            ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc a b, α t = β t

nodes.doc:
**Helper (G1, uniform-`δ`).** From C1 (the field is `C¹` on `V_n`) and compactness of the
a-priori ball `closedBall (0 : V_n) R`, there is a single uniform local-existence time `δ > 0`
valid from every center in the ball: for each `x₀ ∈ closedBall 0 R` there is a local solution on
`(t₀ − δ, t₀ + δ)` starting at `x₀` at `t₀`, for every `t₀` (autonomy ⟹ time-uniform).

Proof sketch: apply `ContDiffAt.exists_forall_mem_closedBall_exists_eq_forall_mem_Ioo_hasDerivAt`
(uniform `ε` over a ball around each point) at each `x₀`, cover the compact `closedBall 0 R` by
finitely many such balls (`isCompact_closedBall`), and take `δ := min` of the finitely many `ε`s.
The field's `ContDiffAt` at every point is `galerkinODE_vectorField_contDiff …|>.contDiffAt`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinField_uniform_local_time
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (R : ℝ),
  ∃ δ,
    0 < δ ∧
      ∀ x₀ ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R,
        ∀ (t₀ : ℝ),
          ∃ α,
            α t₀ = x₀ ∧
              ∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo (t₀ - δ) (t₀ + δ), HasDerivAt α (LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n (α t)) t

nodes.doc:
**Deliverable (Pillar E).** From a Schwartz divergence-free basis `B` (concrete scheme
`schemeOfBasis B`) and the isolated finite-dim global-existence hypothesis `FinDimGlobalODE`,
construct the Galerkin ODE input.  Thin wrapper for R3, named to match the milestone
deliverable vocabulary.  This discharges R-repr (weak-form ⇄ vector-field, via the finite-dim
Riesz field R1/R2) axiom-free; the R-global gap stays isolated in `FinDimGlobalODE`.

A `noncomputable def` (not `theorem`): `GalerkinODEInput` lives in `Type`, not `Prop`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODEInput_of_basis
nodes.signature:
(B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) →
  (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) →
    (ν : ℝ) →
      0 < ν →
        (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) →
          (n : ℕ) →
            LerayHopf.FinDimGlobalODE B F ν u₀ n → LerayHopf.GalerkinODEInput (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B) F ν u₀ n

nodes.doc:
From a global curve `c : Time → V_n` solving the AUTONOMOUS field equation
`c' = G_n(c)` (the residual `FinDimGlobalODE` hypothesis), assemble the full
`GalerkinODEInput`: the curve and its initial value, subspace confinement `u_inVn`, the
ambient-derivative transport `u_hasDeriv`, and — crucially — the weak Galerkin ODE `u_ode`,
which is DERIVED from `c' = G_n(c)` via the Riesz characterization R2.  This last step is
where R-repr (weak-form ⇄ vector-field) is discharged into the weak form.

A `noncomputable def` (not `theorem`): `GalerkinODEInput` is a data-carrying structure in
`Type`, not a `Prop` (mirrors `galerkinSolutionData_R3_of_input` in `GalerkinODE.lean`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODEInput_of_globalCurve
nodes.signature:
(B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) →
  (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) →
    (ν : ℝ) →
      0 < ν →
        (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) →
          (n : ℕ) →
            LerayHopf.FinDimGlobalODE B F ν u₀ n → LerayHopf.GalerkinODEInput (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B) F ν u₀ n

nodes.doc:
**Helper (C1).** The bilinear part of the Galerkin field: the continuous-bilinear map
`V_n × V_n → V_n` representing the quadratic `b`-term `(u, u') ↦ (toDual).symm (w ↦ −b(u,u',w))`,
so that the field is `u ↦ Bil(u,u) + Lin u`. Built level by level (`bInner`/`bMid`/`bOut`) from
`F.b`'s multilinearity (`b_add_*`/`b_smul_*`) with finite-dim auto-continuity at each level, then
post-composed with the Riesz inverse CLM.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODE_bilinearPart
nodes.signature:
(B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) →
  LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B) →
    ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n) →L[ℝ] ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n) →L[ℝ] ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODE_bilinearPart_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ)
  (u u' : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)),
  ((LerayHopf.galerkinODE_bilinearPart B F ν n) u) u' = (LerayHopf.rieszSymmCLM✝ B n) (LerayHopf.bInner✝ B F n u u')

nodes.doc:
The continuous linear functional on `V_n := galerkinSpan B n` whose Riesz representative is
the Galerkin vector field: `φ w = - ν · stokesTestPairing_R3 (u, w) - F.b (σu) (σu) (σw)`.

Right-linearity in `w` comes from `stokesTestPairing_R3_add_right`/`_smul_right` (the analytic
linearity helpers, valid on span elements) and `F.b_add_3`/`F.b_smul_3` (with
`galerkinSpanToSigma` additive/homogeneous).  Continuity is automatic on the
finite-dimensional `V_n` via `LinearMap.toContinuousLinearMap`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODE_functional
nodes.signature:
(B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) →
  LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B) →
    ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n) → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n) →L[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODE_functional_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ)
  (u w : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)),
  (LerayHopf.galerkinODE_functional B F ν n u) w =
    -ν * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑w -
      F.b (LerayHopf.galerkinSpanToSigma B n u) (LerayHopf.galerkinSpanToSigma B n u)
        (LerayHopf.galerkinSpanToSigma B n w)

nodes.doc:
**Helper (C1).** The linear part of the Galerkin field: the continuous-linear map
`V_n → V_n` representing `u ↦ (toDual).symm (w ↦ −ν·stokes(u,w))`, the Riesz inverse of the
`stokesOut`-functional.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODE_linearPart
nodes.signature:
(B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) →
  LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B) →
    ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n) →L[ℝ] ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODE_linearPart_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ)
  (u : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)),
  (LerayHopf.galerkinODE_linearPart B F ν n) u = (LerayHopf.rieszSymmCLM✝ B n) (LerayHopf.stokesInner✝ B ν n u)

nodes.doc:
The finite-dim Galerkin vector field `G_n` on `V_n := galerkinSpan B n`.

It is the Riesz representative (in the finite-dim — hence complete — inner product space
`V_n`) of the continuous linear functional `galerkinODE_functional`
`w ↦ - ν · stokesTestPairing_R3 (u, w) - F.b u u w` restricted to `V_n`.  The functional is
linear in `w` (`stokes` linear in its 2nd slot — `stokesTestPairing_R3_add_right`/`_smul_right`
on span elements — and `F.b` additive + ℝ-homogeneous in its 3rd slot), so it is automatically
continuous on the finite-dim `V_n` (`LinearMap.toContinuousLinearMap`), and
`InnerProductSpace.toDual.symm` produces its representative WITHOUT needing the quantitative
`b_bound`.

Working over `schemeOfBasis B` is essential: `V_n` is finite-dimensional only for the
concrete span (P5 `galerkinSpan_finiteDimensional`), which the abstract scheme does not give.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField
nodes.signature:
(B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) →
  LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B) →
    ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n) → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)

nodes.doc:
**C1 (the enabler, MUST-PROVE).** The Galerkin field `G_n` is `C¹` (indeed smooth) on the
finite-dim `V_n`: it is `Bil(u,u) + Lin u`, a quadratic-plus-linear map of `u`. The diagonal
`u ↦ Bil(u,u)` is `ContDiff` (`Bil.contDiff.clm_apply contDiff_id`), the linear part is
`ContinuousLinearMap.contDiff`, and their sum is `ContDiff`. Finite-dim auto-continuity supplied
all continuity in `bInner`/`bMid`/`bOut`/`stokesInner`/`stokesOut`; no continuity hypothesis on
`b` is needed.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField_contDiff
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ),
  ContDiff ℝ 1 (LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n)

nodes.doc: The Galerkin field equals `Bil(u,u) + Lin u`, by Riesz injectivity (test against all `w`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField_eq_parts
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ)
  (u : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)),
  LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n u =
    ((LerayHopf.galerkinODE_bilinearPart B F ν n) u) u + (LerayHopf.galerkinODE_linearPart B F ν n) u

nodes.doc:
Defining property of the Riesz field `G_n`: testing it against any `w ∈ V_n` recovers the
functional `w ↦ - ν · stokes(u, w) - b(u, u, w)`.  This is the **R-repr** content
(weak-form ⇄ vector-field representation) made precise: it is the identity from which the
weak Galerkin ODE `u_ode` is DERIVED in R3.  Sign/`ν` conventions match `u_ode`
(`AxiomaticClosure.lean` / `GalerkinODE.lean:102`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField_spec
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ)
  (u w : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)),
  inner ℝ ↑(LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n u) ↑w =
    -ν * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑w -
      F.b (LerayHopf.galerkinSpanToSigma B n u) (LerayHopf.galerkinSpanToSigma B n u)
        (LerayHopf.galerkinSpanToSigma B n w)

nodes.doc: The n-th Galerkin projector: orthogonal projection onto `galerkinSpan B n`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinP
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis → ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 →L[ℝ] ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3

nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinP_idem
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (n : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) ((LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u) = (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u

nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinP_mem_span
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (n : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u ∈ LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n

nodes.doc:
**Nesting for the concrete projector.** A point fixed by `galerkinP B m` stays fixed by
`galerkinP B n` for `n ≥ m` (its subspace `galerkinSpan B m ⊆ galerkinSpan B n` and the
orthogonal projection fixes points of its subspace). Discharges `R3GalerkinScheme.mono_range`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinP_mono_range
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (m n : ℕ),
  m ≤ n → ∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), (LerayHopf.galerkinP B m) w = w → (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) w = w

nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinP_norm_le
nodes.signature: ∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (n : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), ‖(LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u‖ ≤ ‖u‖

nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinP_preserves_sigma
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (n : ℕ),
  ∀ u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3, (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3

nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinP_range_schwartz
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (n : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  ∃ ψ,
    ∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) ((LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u) = (ψ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinP_tendsto_id
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis),
  ∀ u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3, Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u) Filter.atTop (nhds u)

nodes.doc:
**Supporting lemma (P5).** From a total Schwartz divergence-free basis, the orthogonal
projections onto its finite prefix spans form a Galerkin approximation scheme on
`L²_σ(ℝ³)`: they are non-expansive, idempotent, preserve the divergence-free subspace,
have component-wise Schwartz range, and converge strongly to the identity on
`L²_σ(ℝ³)`.  This is the axiom-free constructive content of `r3GalerkinScheme_exists`
(modulo the bundled density hypothesis `B.dense_span`), packaged field-by-field; the
headline deliverable `nonempty_r3GalerkinScheme_of_basis` (D7') assembles these into an
actual `R3GalerkinScheme`.

The `tendsto_id` conjunct is the Σ-restricted form, which (after the soundness fix in
`AxiomaticClosure.lean`) now matches the `R3GalerkinScheme.tendsto_id` field exactly: a
divergence-free basis is total only in `L2Sigma_R3`, so convergence is claimed precisely
where it is true.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinScheme_properties_of_basis
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis),
  (∀ (n : ℕ), ∀ u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3, (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) ∧
    (∀ u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3, Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u) Filter.atTop (nhds u)) ∧
      (∀ (n : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), ‖(LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u‖ ≤ ‖u‖) ∧
        (∀ (n : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
            (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) ((LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u) = (LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u) ∧
          ∀ (n : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
            ∃ ψ,
              ∀ (j : Fin 3),
                (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) ((LerayHopf.galerkinP B n) u) = (ψ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
**Deliverable.** From the isolated global-existence/representation input, assemble the
full `GalerkinSolutionData_R3`: the curve, its initial value, subspace confinement,
differentiability, the weak ODE, H¹ regularity, and the uniform energy + dissipation
bounds — the last three DERIVED axiom-free from the ODE via `R3NSForms.b_self_zero`.  This is
the axiom-free analytic content of `galerkin_ode_solution_R3`, modulo the bundled input.

The first five fields are passed straight from the input (they are what an existence theorem
supplies); `reg_mem`/`energy_bound`/`reg_bound` are the proved payoff (M0/E2/E3).  Field
types match `GalerkinSolutionData_R3` byte-for-byte (the structure is NOT altered).

This is a `noncomputable def` (not `theorem`): `GalerkinSolutionData_R3` is a data-carrying
structure in `Type`, not a `Prop`.

nodes.doc:
**Bochner-time / space-time compactness extraction on ℝ³ (the isolated Aubin–Lions-time
pillar) — stated UNCONDITIONALLY and LOCALLY for the bounded Galerkin sequence.**  From the proved
spatial precompactness at sample times (`steklovAvg_spatial_extraction`, fed by P3 via `B`) together
with the intrinsic uniform-in-`n` time equicontinuity of the Galerkin curves (which the curves
genuinely possess via their `W^{1,p}(0,T;X)` time-derivative estimate, but which mathlib cannot
mechanize), the Galerkin curves admit a SINGLE strictly-monotone subsequence `φ` and a limit curve
`u : Time → L2Sigma_R3` such that, **for every ball radius `R`**, the ball restrictions
`restrictToBall R ((galSeq (φ n)).u t)` converge to `restrictToBall R (u t)` at a.e. time `t`, with
`u` time-measurable.

**LOCAL, not global (the load-bearing correction).**  The convergence is asserted ONLY per ball,
through `restrictToBall R`, NOT in the full `L2VF_R3` norm.  On ℝ³ the available compactness is local:
`spatial_compactness_R3` / the Fréchet–Kolmogorov–Rellich chain is ball-restricted with NO tightness,
so the Galerkin sequence may lose mass at spatial infinity and local precompactness does NOT yield
global `L²` a.e.-in-time convergence.  A global statement would therefore be OVER-STRONG (stronger
than the removed `aubin_lions_R3`, and not generally true); the per-ball form is the honest content
and is exactly what the package needs (its `strong_convergence` field is itself per-ball, over
`L²(0,T; L²(B_R))`).

This is the genuine Aubin–Lions *time* content, LOCALIZED: the Arzelà–Ascoli diagonalization
(countable dense times × `δ`-mesh → 0 × balls) that upgrades pointwise-in-time LOCAL spatial
precompactness + time equicontinuity into a single subsequence with per-ball a.e.-in-time `L²`
convergence.  Mathlib lacks the Bochner-valued Fréchet–Kolmogorov / Aubin–Lions compactness theorem
in `L²(0,T;X)`, so this single extraction step is isolated as an axiom.  It is STRICTLY THINNER /
WEAKER than the former `aubin_lions_R3`: the spatial half is now genuinely PROVED (the
`steklovAvg_spatial_extraction` chain, axiom-free), and this axiom carries ONLY the LOCAL
time-compactness extraction — no spatial compactness, no tightness, no global-`L²` claim.

The axiom is UNCONDITIONAL: it does NOT take a `TimeCompactnessInput`/modulus hypothesis.  The
Galerkin curves' time-equicontinuity is a TRUE standalone consequence of their proved uniform bounds
(`galerkin_norm_le_u0`) and ODE structure; the single irreducible fact mathlib cannot supply is the
Bochner-time-LOCAL compactness *extraction* itself, which this axiom names directly.  By stating it
unconditionally we absorb the modulus, so the time layer rests on exactly THIS ONE axiom — the
redundant `timeCompactnessInput_R3` axiom from the prior revision of this PR (which only ever fed
this extraction) is dropped.

Once supplied, the whole `AubinLionsPackage_R3` is assembled axiom-free from this conclusion:
`u_aestronglyMeasurable` is the second conjunct; `strong_convergence` follows for each ball `R`
directly from the per-ball a.e. convergence + dominated convergence in `L²` (the constant dominator
`‖u₀‖` on the finite window `[0,T]`, via `galerkin_norm_le_u0`).  NON-VACUOUS: the conclusion pins
the ball restrictions of `u` to the per-ball a.e.-`L²`-limits of the given subsequence (not a free
choice).  Temam III.2.1; Lemarié-Rieusset §6 (Aubin–Lions, local).

**Issue #44 note:** This was formerly `axiom galerkinSpaceTimeExtraction_R3`.  It is now a
PROVED `theorem`, proved via `u_lim_aestronglyMeasurable` from the two sound thinner axioms
`galerkin_spacetime_precompact_R3` (refine-capable local Aubin–Lions–Simon spacetime precompactness)
and `galerkin_weakLimit_R3` (Banach–Alaoglu + div-free weak limit in `L2Sigma_R3`), plus the
proved Arzelà–Ascoli-in-time chain (`perBall_ae_subseq`, `diag_ae_subseq`) in
`ArzelaAscoliTime.lean`.  Signature is BYTE-IDENTICAL.  No sorry in proof body.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinSpaceTimeExtraction_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n),
          LerayHopf.LocalRellichInput →
            ∃ φ u,
              StrictMono φ ∧
                MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable (fun t => ↑(u t)) (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) ∧
                  ∀ (R : ℝ),
                    ∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T),
                      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall R ↑((galSeq (φ n)).u t)) Filter.atTop
                        (nhds (LerayHopf.restrictToBall R ↑(u t)))

A span element has **complex Schwartz** component representatives: for `w ∈ V_n`, each
complex component `L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j w` is the `L²`-class of a (complex-valued)
Schwartz map.  This is the complexification of `galerkinP_range_schwartz` (real components ⇒
real Schwartz), post-composed with the real-to-complex embedding `RCLike.ofRealCLM`.

nodes.doc:
For a span element `w ∈ V_n`, the pointwise coercion of the `L²`-Fourier transform of its
`j`-th complex component is a.e. equal to a genuine **Schwartz** function (the classical
Schwartz Fourier transform of the component's Schwartz representative).  This is the bridge
from the a.e.-only `Lp.fourierTransformₗᵢ` coercion to the classical Schwartz `fourierIntegral`,
via `SchwartzMap.toLp_fourier_eq`.

  ∃ S, ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) ↑w)) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] ⇑S

The prefix span lies in the divergence-free subspace: every generator `B.e k` is
divergence-free (`B.e_mem_sigma`) and `L2Sigma_R3` is a submodule, so the span is contained
in it.  This is the `Submodule` inequality underlying the `V_n ↪ L2Sigma_R3` embedding.

nodes.signature: ∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis), Monotone (LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B)

nodes.doc:
**B10 (plumbing).** Every Galerkin state has componentwise Schwartz representatives:
`∃ ψ : Fin 3 → 𝓢(Domain3, ℝ), ∀ j, projⱼ (u t) = (ψ j).toLp 2 volume`.

From `𝔊.range_schwartz n` applied at `𝔊.P n (gs.u t)`, transported through `u_inVn`
(`(gs.u t : L2VF_R3) = 𝔊.P n (gs.u t)`).  Feeds the `F.b_galerkin` pin and the
GNS/trilinear chain (C2–C5, PR-2).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinState_schwartzRep

nodes.doc:
**`galerkinTestSpan_subset_H1Sigma` (torus #53).**

Every Galerkin test `w : L2Sigma` (i.e., `IsGalerkinTest w`, so `w ∈ Vₙ` for some `n`)
lies in `H1SigmaTorus`.

**Proof sketch (for PR-2):** By `IsGalerkinTest`, get `n` with `velocityProjection_n n w = w`.
Then `coeff_zero_outside_box` gives `mFourierCoeff3 (L2VF_projComponentC j w) k = 0` for
`k ∉ fourierBox n`.  The weighted H¹ sum `∑_k weight(k) * ‖ŵ_j(k)‖²` reduces to a
finite sum over `fourierBox n`, which is summable.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkinTestSpan_subset_H1Sigma
nodes.signature: ∀ {w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma}, LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w → w ∈ LerayHopf.H1SigmaTorus

nodes.doc:
**C6.** For a fixed level-`n` test `w`, the diagonal convection integrand along the
Galerkin curve, `σ ↦ F.b (gs.u σ) (gs.u σ) w`, is continuous on forward time `Ici 0`.

Route (plan §3 C6): multilinearity (`b_add_*`) splits the increment into two terms each
controlled by C5 with one slot `= gs.u σ − gs.u σ₀` (still level-`n`), and B6
(H¹-continuity of the Galerkin curve, `galerkin_curve_H1_continuousOn`) drives them to `0`.
Primary (strong) form stated per task; the plan's weaker `IntervalIntegrable` fallback
(`galerkin_bForm_intervalIntegrable`) remains available if the fractional-power ε–δ fights.
This is the interval-integrability input consumed by B9.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_bForm_curve_continuousOn

nodes.doc:
**D2.** Per-cell sampling error on the mesh cell `[a, a+δ]` (`0 < δ`, `0 ≤ a`) with a
good sample `τ ∈ [a, a+δ]` satisfying the good-sample Dirichlet bound
`V₁ (u τ) ≤ 2·δ⁻¹·ν⁻¹·E₀` (`E₀ = ½‖u₀‖²`).  The cell `L²`-error integral splits as

`‖u t − u τ‖² = (‖u t‖² − ‖u τ‖²) − 2⟪u t − u τ, u τ⟫`,

whose energy part is controlled by the integrated energy identity (B8) and whose pairing
part is controlled by the pairing FTC (B9) with the fixed test `w := u τ`, the stokes
Cauchy–Schwarz bound (B7), and the energy-class trilinear bound (C5).  Concretely:

`∫ t in a..(a+δ), ‖u t − u τ‖²
   ≤ 2·δ·(∫ σ in a..(a+δ), V_ν (u σ))
     + 2·δ·√(2·δ⁻¹·ν⁻¹·E₀) · ∫ σ in a..(a+δ),
         (ν·√(V₁ (u σ)) + C_b·‖u₀‖^{1/2}·(V₁ (u σ))^{3/4})`.

The C5 constant `C_b ≥ 0` and its bound `hC_b` are taken as EXPLICIT hypotheses (rather
than obtained internally from `bForm_galerkin_abs_le`) so that D2 — and the master bound
D3 built from it — stays visibly `n`-uniform: `C_b` cannot depend on the level `n`.

Consumes B2, B5, B7, B8, B9, C5.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_cell_error_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ) (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (C_b : ℝ),
      0 ≤ C_b →
        (∀ (n : ℕ) (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
            ↑u = (𝔊.P n) ↑u →
              ↑v = (𝔊.P n) ↑v →
                ↑w = (𝔊.P n) ↑w →
                  |F.b u v w| ≤
                    C_b * ‖↑u‖ ^ (1 / 2) * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑u ^ (1 / 4) *
                        √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑v) *
                      √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑w)) →
          ∀ (a δ : ℝ),
            0 ≤ a →
              0 < δ →
                ∀ τ ∈ Set.Icc a (a + δ),
                  LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑(gs.u τ) ≤ 2 * δ⁻¹ * ν⁻¹ * (1 / 2 * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2) →
                    ∫ (t : ℝ) in a..a + δ, ‖↑(gs.u t) - ↑(gs.u τ)‖ ^ 2 ≤
                      (2 * δ * ∫ (σ : ℝ) in a..a + δ, LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 ν ↑(gs.u σ)) +
                        2 * δ * √(2 * δ⁻¹ * ν⁻¹ * (1 / 2 * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2)) *
                          ∫ (σ : ℝ) in a..a + δ,
                            ν * √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑(gs.u σ)) +
                              C_b * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ (1 / 2) * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑(gs.u σ) ^ (3 / 4)

nodes.doc:
**B6.** H¹-continuity of the Galerkin curve: the Dirichlet energy of the increment
vanishes as `s → s₀` within forward time,
`V₁ (u s − u s₀) → 0` as `s → s₀` in `Ici 0`.

Route: B3 + B4 turn `V₁ (u s − u s₀)` into `∑ j, ‖wFC (u s) j − wFC (u s₀) j‖²`
(memH1 of the difference via `memH1VF_R3_add`/`memH1VF_R3_smul`), which tends to `0` by
the `viscous_curve_continuous` field.  This is the H¹-continuity consumed by C6 (PR-2).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_curve_H1_continuousOn
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} {ν : ℝ} {u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3} {n : ℕ}
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (s₀ : ℝ),
  0 ≤ s₀ →
    Filter.Tendsto (fun s => LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 (↑(gs.u s) - ↑(gs.u s₀))) (nhdsWithin s₀ (Set.Ici 0)) (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
The Galerkin velocity curve `t ↦ ((galSeq n).u t : L2VF_R3)` is continuous on **forward**
time `Set.Ici 0` (it is even differentiable there by `u_hasDeriv`).

Forward-only: `u_hasDeriv` now guarantees differentiability only for `t ≥ 0` (the curve is a
physical Galerkin solution, confined only on forward time).  Everything downstream uses the
curve only on `[0,T] ⊆ Ici 0`, so forward continuity suffices.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_curve_continuous
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n), ContinuousOn (fun s => ↑(gs.u s)) (Set.Ici 0)

nodes.doc: Continuity of the Galerkin curve on `[0, ∞)` (from `u_hasDeriv`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_curve_continuousOn
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n), ContinuousOn (fun s => ↑(gs.u s)) (Set.Ici 0)

nodes.doc:
**Raw-curve time-equicontinuity, uniform in `n` (the Arzelà–Ascoli input).**  The
`TimeCompactnessInput` modulus is stated directly on the raw Galerkin curves, so it already gives
uniform-in-`n` L² equicontinuity of `t ↦ (galSeq n).u t` on `[0,T]`: for every `ε > 0` there is
`δ > 0` such that for all `n` and all `s,t ∈ [0,T]` with `|s − t| < δ`,
`‖(galSeq n).u s − (galSeq n).u t‖ < ε`.  This is a thin restatement of
`Htime.uniform_time_modulus`, isolated as the named Arzelà–Ascoli equicontinuity hypothesis the C2
assembly consumes (the spatial precompactness at sample times being supplied separately by the
Steklov averages + `steklovAvg_spatial_extraction`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_curves_equicontinuous
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν T : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3)
  (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n),
  LerayHopf.TimeCompactnessInput 𝔊 F ν T u₀ galSeq →
    ∀ (ε : ℝ),
      0 < ε →
        ∃ δ,
          0 < δ ∧
            ∀ (n : ℕ) (s t : ℝ),
              s ∈ Set.Icc 0 T → t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T → |s - t| < δ → ‖↑((galSeq n).u s) - ↑((galSeq n).u t)‖ < ε

nodes.doc: Uniform energy bound: `½‖u(t)‖² ≤ ½‖𝔊.P n u₀‖²` for `t ≥ 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_energy_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ) (I : LerayHopf.GalerkinODEInput 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (t : LerayHopf.Time),
      0 ≤ t → 1 / 2 * ‖↑(I.u t)‖ ^ 2 ≤ 1 / 2 * ‖(𝔊.P n) ↑u₀‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
Energy identity at forward times `t ≥ 0`: `½ d/dt ‖u(t)‖² = −ν · viscousFormSq_R3 1 (u t)`
along the ODE.  Equivalently `HasDerivAt (fun s => ½‖u s‖²) (−ν·viscousFormSq_R3 1 (u t)) t`.

Forward-only: it consumes `u_hasDeriv`/`u_ode`, both now restricted to `0 ≤ t`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_energy_identity
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (I : LerayHopf.GalerkinODEInput 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (t : LerayHopf.Time),
  0 ≤ t → HasDerivAt (fun s => 1 / 2 * ‖↑(I.u s)‖ ^ 2) (-ν * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑(I.u t)) t

nodes.doc:
**The proved limit-passage existential for ℝ³** (replaces axiom `galerkin_limit_passage_R3`).

Produces a **good representative** `u : Time → L2Sigma_R3` of the Aubin–Lions limit
`alPkg.u` satisfying the five Leray–Hopf conjuncts:

1. **(a.e.-link)** `u t = alPkg.u t` for a.e. `t ∈ [0, T]`.
2. **(WeakFormNS)** `WeakFormNS ν T (r3Evolution 𝔊 F) u` — the weak Navier–Stokes
   equation holds for all admissible test data.
3. **(Energy inequality)** `½‖u(t)‖² + ∫₀ᵗ ν·V₁(u) ≤ ½‖u₀‖²` for all `t ∈ [0, T]`.
4. **(Initial trace)** `u(t) → u₀` strongly in L²_σ(ℝ³) as `t → 0⁺`.
5. **(Energy class)** a.e. `memH1VF_R3 (u t)` + integrable `viscousFormSq_R3 ν (u t)`.

The hypothesis `htest : R3TestApproxH1 𝔊` is the H¹-graph test-approximation property
proved for the concrete Schwartz Galerkin basis in `nonempty_schwartzGalerkinBasis_H1`
(PR-3 / `GalerkinBasisH1.lean`).  It enters through `weakFormNS_limit_passage` (PR-4)
to close the nonlinear limit.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_limit_passage_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n)
          (alPkg : LerayHopf.AubinLionsPackage_R3 𝔊 F ν T u₀ galSeq),
          LerayHopf.R3TestApproxH1 𝔊 →
            ∃ u,
              (∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T), u t = alPkg.u t) ∧
                LerayHopf.WeakFormNS ν T (LerayHopf.r3Evolution 𝔊 F) u ∧
                  (∀ (t : ℝ),
                      0 ≤ t →
                        t ≤ T →
                          1 / 2 * ‖↑(u t)‖ ^ 2 + ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 ν ↑(u s) ≤
                            1 / 2 * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2) ∧
                    Filter.Tendsto (fun t => ↑(u t)) (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ici 0)) (nhds ↑u₀) ∧
                      (∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T),
                          LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 ↑(u t)) ∧
                        IntervalIntegrable (fun s => LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 ν ↑(u s)) MeasureTheory.volume 0 T

nodes.doc:
**Galerkin-side uniform energy bound (provable core of E1).** For every `n` and every
`t ≥ 0`, the Galerkin state is L²-bounded by the initial datum: `‖(galSeq n).u t‖ ≤ ‖u₀‖`.

Proof: `energy_bound` gives `½‖uₙ t‖² ≤ ½‖𝔊.P n u₀‖²`; `𝔊.norm_le` gives
`‖𝔊.P n u₀‖ ≤ ‖u₀‖`; combine. This is the side of E1 that needs no time-measurability.

  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {t : ℝ}, 0 ≤ t → ‖↑(gs.u t)‖ ≤ ‖↑u₀‖
