nodes.doc:
For a band-limited test `w` (IsGalerkinTest), the Galerkin ODE holds against `w`
for all Galerkin levels `n ≥ n₀` and all forward times `t ≥ 0`.

**Proof:** `IsGalerkinTest w` gives `n₀` with `Pₙ₀ w = w`.  By
`velocityProjection_n_eq_of_le`, `Pₙ w = w` for all `n ≥ n₀`.  The `u_ode` field of
`GalerkinSolutionData` then fires directly.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_ode_fires_on_test
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma)
  (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w →
    ∃ n₀,
      ∀ (n : ℕ),
        n₀ ≤ n →
          ∀ (t : LerayHopf.Time),
            0 ≤ t →
              inner ℝ (deriv (fun s => ↑((galSeq n).u s)) t) ↑w + ν * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing ↑((galSeq n).u t) ↑w +
                  F.b ((galSeq n).u t) ((galSeq n).u t) w =
                0

nodes.doc:
**B9 (Codex-gated statement; deferred from PR-1).** The weak-form pairing FTC along the
Galerkin curve: for a level-`n` test `w` (`(w:L2VF_R3) = 𝔊.P n w`) and `0 ≤ a ≤ b`,
`⟪u(b) − u(a), w⟫ = ∫ σ in a..b, (−ν · stokes(u σ, w) − b(u σ, u σ, w))`.

Route (plan §3 B9): scalar FTC on `g σ := ⟪u σ, w⟫` whose derivative is given by the ODE
field; interval-integrability of the RHS from continuity — the stokes term via
`viscous_curve_continuous` + `stokesTestPairing_eq_sum_inner_wFC`, and the `b` term via C6
(`galerkin_bForm_curve_continuousOn`).  Named `galerkin_pairing_FTC` per the plan's B9
note authorizing the move to File C.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_pairing_FTC
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} {ν : ℝ} {u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3} {n : ℕ}
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
  ↑w = (𝔊.P n) ↑w →
    ∀ (a b : ℝ),
      0 ≤ a →
        a ≤ b →
          inner ℝ (↑(gs.u b) - ↑(gs.u a)) ↑w =
            ∫ (σ : ℝ) in a..b, -ν * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑(gs.u σ) ↑w - F.b (gs.u σ) (gs.u σ) w

nodes.doc:
Uniform (n-independent, `T`-independent) dissipation bound:
`∫₀ᵀ viscousFormSq_R3 ν (u t) dt ≤ ½‖u₀‖²`.  Since `viscousFormSq_R3` already carries the
`ν` factor (`= ν · ‖∇u‖²`), this RHS is `ν`-independent.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_reg_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ) (I : LerayHopf.GalerkinODEInput 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (T : ℝ),
      0 < T → ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T, LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 ν ↑(I.u t) ≤ 1 / 2 * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
**D3 (MASTER; Codex-gated statement).** The `n`-uniform integrated sampling-error
bound.  There is a constant `C_mod ≥ 0` — a function of `‖u₀‖, ν, T, C_b` ONLY, standing
OUTSIDE every quantifier over the Galerkin level `n`, the Galerkin data `gs`, and the mesh
count `m` — such that for every level `n`, every Galerkin solution `gs` at that level, and
every mesh count `m > 0`, with `δ := T/m`, there exist sample times `τ : Fin m → ℝ` with

* `τ i ∈ [i·δ, (i+1)·δ]` (each sample lives in its cell),
* `V₁ (u (τ i)) ≤ 2·δ⁻¹·ν⁻¹·E₀` (the good-sample Dirichlet bound, `E₀ = ½‖u₀‖²`), and
* `∑ i, ∫ t in (i·δ)..((i+1)·δ), ‖u t − u (τ i)‖² ≤ C_mod · √δ`.

The conclusion is an INTEGRATED (summed-over-cells) modulus — no pointwise-in-time strong
modulus is asserted (plan §1 guardrail-4).  The critical structural claims are that the
existential `∃ C_mod` sits OUTSIDE `∀ n`, `∀ gs`, and `∀ m` (so `C_mod` is `n`- and
`m`-independent), and that the mesh is the FIXED uniform mesh of size `m`.  The C5 constant
`C_b ≥ 0` and its bound `hC_b` are taken as EXPLICIT hypotheses so that this `n`-uniformity
is structurally evident (`C_mod` is built from `C_b`, `‖u₀‖`, `ν`, `T` alone).

Route (plan §3 D3): D1 per cell (choice per `(n,i)` is classical), D2 per cell, sum with
`intervalIntegral.sum_integral_adjacent_intervals`, then time-Hölder
(`∫₀ᵀ √V₁ ≤ √T·√(∫V₁)`, `∫₀ᵀ V₁^{3/4} ≤ T^{1/4}·(∫V₁)^{3/4}`) with `reg_bound`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_sampling_error_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} (ν T : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    0 < T →
      ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (C_b : ℝ),
        0 ≤ C_b →
          (∀ (n : ℕ) (u v w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
              ↑u = (𝔊.P n) ↑u →
                ↑v = (𝔊.P n) ↑v →
                  ↑w = (𝔊.P n) ↑w →
                    |F.b u v w| ≤
                      C_b * ‖↑u‖ ^ (1 / 2) * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑u ^ (1 / 4) *
                          √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑v) *
                        √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑w)) →
            ∃ C_mod,
              0 ≤ C_mod ∧
                ∀ (n : ℕ) (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (m : ℕ),
                  0 < m →
                    ∃ τ,
                      (∀ (i : Fin m), τ i ∈ Set.Icc (↑↑i * (T / ↑m)) ((↑↑i + 1) * (T / ↑m))) ∧
                        (∀ (i : Fin m),
                            LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑(gs.u (τ i)) ≤ 2 * (T / ↑m)⁻¹ * ν⁻¹ * (1 / 2 * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2)) ∧
                          ∑ i, ∫ (t : ℝ) in ↑↑i * (T / ↑m)..(↑↑i + 1) * (T / ↑m), ‖↑(gs.u t) - ↑(gs.u (τ i))‖ ^ 2 ≤
                            C_mod * √(T / ↑m)

nodes.doc:
**`galerkin_spacetime_precompact_R3` — LOCAL Aubin–Lions–Simon spacetime precompactness on
ℝ³ (REFINE-CAPABLE).**  [issue #46 PR-4: converted from `axiom` to `theorem`, discharged by
`galerkin_spacetime_precompact_of_goodSampling` (File E, `SpacetimePrecompact.lean`).]

For every input subsequence `ψ : ℕ → ℕ` (strictly monotone) and every ball radius `k : ℕ`,
there is a FURTHER strictly-monotone `ρ : ℕ → ℕ` and a measurable limit curve
`g_k : ℝ → L2ballR3 k` such that the Bochner L²-in-time norm of the difference
`restrictToBall k ((galSeq (ψ (ρ n))).u t) - g_k t` converges to `0` as `n → ∞`.

**Why refine-capable?** The Cantor-diagonal construction in `diag_ae_subseq` builds the tower
`φ_0 = ρ_0`, `φ_{k+1} = φ_k ∘ ρ_{k+1}` inductively, where at each step we extract a FURTHER
subsequence of the CURRENT one. This requires applying the compactness result to the composition
`galSeq ∘ φ_k` — but `galSeq n : GalerkinSolutionData_R3 … n` has the level `n` baked into
its dependent type, making reindexing `n ↦ galSeq (φ_k n)` type-incorrect as a new `galSeq`.
The refine-capable form avoids reindexing by keeping `galSeq` fixed and instead taking the
current subsequence `ψ = φ_k` as input, returning a further `ρ` such that `φ_k ∘ ρ` is the
next level.

**Soundness:** Any subsequence of a sequence satisfying the hypotheses of the Aubin–Lions–Simon
theorem also satisfies the same hypotheses (the energy bound `galerkin_norm_le_u0` and
`reg_bound` are uniform in `n`, so they hold uniformly along any `ψ`). Hence the conclusion
holds for every input `ψ`.

**Mathematical content:** Aubin–Lions–Simon compactness theorem in `L²(0,T; L²(B_k))`.
SOUND/LOCAL: no tightness, no pointwise-in-time equicontinuity, no global-L² claim.
Mathlib lacks the Bochner-valued Aubin–Lions/Fréchet–Kolmogorov theorem in L²(0,T;X); the
concrete route (step-curve total-boundedness via the `n`-uniform integrated sampling modulus +
Rellich ball-compactness) is assembled sorry-free in `SpacetimePrecompact.lean`.

**Proof:** delegated to `galerkin_spacetime_precompact_of_goodSampling` (E2), which has the
BYTE-IDENTICAL statement.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_spacetime_precompact_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (ψ : ℕ → ℕ),
          StrictMono ψ →
            ∀ (k : ℕ),
              ∃ ρ g_k,
                StrictMono ρ ∧
                  MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable g_k (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) ∧
                    Filter.Tendsto
                      (fun n =>
                        MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fun t => LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑((galSeq (ψ (ρ n))).u t) - g_k t) 2
                          (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)))
                      Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
**E2.** LOCAL Aubin–Lions–Simon spacetime precompactness on ℝ³ (refine-capable form).

For every input subsequence `ψ` (strictly monotone) and every ball radius `k : ℕ`, there is
a further strictly-monotone `ρ` and a measurable limit curve `g_k : ℝ → L2ballR3 k` such
that the Bochner `L²`-in-time norm of `restrictToBall k ((galSeq (ψ (ρ n))).u t) - g_k t`
tends to `0`.

The statement is **binder-for-binder identical** to the axiom
`galerkin_spacetime_precompact_R3` in `ArzelaAscoliTime.lean` — only the name differs.  This
is the theorem that will discharge that axiom (E3).

Route (plan §3 E2): fix `ψ, k`; the family `t ↦ restrictToBall k ((galSeq (ψ n)).u t)` is
`MemLp` by E1; its `toLp` range is totally bounded (D3's `n`-uniform integrated sampling
modulus places each sample slice in the compact `LocalRellichInput.ballCompact`, A4 gives a
compact step-curve set, A5 transfers total-boundedness); A6 extracts the convergent
subsequence.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_spacetime_precompact_of_goodSampling
nodes.signature:

        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (ψ : ℕ → ℕ),
          StrictMono ψ →
            ∀ (k : ℕ),
              ∃ ρ g_k,
                StrictMono ρ ∧
                  MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable g_k (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) ∧
                    Filter.Tendsto
                      (fun n =>
                        MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fun t => LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑((galSeq (ψ (ρ n))).u t) - g_k t) 2
                          (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)))
                      Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
For `n ≥ n₀`, the WeakFormNS integrand for `galSeq n` integrates to 0 on `[0, T]`.

**Proof:** ODE at each `t ≥ 0` (after projection promotion) × ψ, integrated.
IBP eliminates the time-derivative term using `ψ(0) = ψ(T) = 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_weakFormNS_zero
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n) (ψ : ℝ → ℝ),
          HasCompactSupport ψ →
            tsupport ψ ⊆ Set.Ioo 0 T →
              ContDiff ℝ 1 ψ →
                ∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
                  LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w →
                    ∀ (n₀ : ℕ),
                      (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n₀) ↑w = ↑w →
                        ∀ (n : ℕ),
                          n₀ ≤ n →
                            ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T,
                                -inner ℝ ↑((galSeq n).u t) ↑w * deriv ψ t +
                                  ψ t *
                                    (ν * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing ↑((galSeq n).u t) ↑w +
                                      F.b ((galSeq n).u t) ((galSeq n).u t) w) =
                              0

nodes.doc:
**Per-level WeakFormNS identity.**  For a Galerkin level `n` and a test `w` fixed by `𝔊.P n`,
the level-`n` approximant integrand integrates to `0` over `[0,T]`.

Time-IBP via the product-rule FTC on `h(t) := ⟪uₙ t, w⟫ ψ(t)`: the integrand equals `-h'(t)` (using
the ODE field `u_ode` to rewrite `ν stokes + b = -⟪uₙ'(t), w⟫`), and the boundary terms vanish since
`tsupport ψ ⊆ Ioo 0 T`.  Mirrors the T³ `galerkin_weakFormNS_zero`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_weakFormNS_zero_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} {ν : ℝ} {u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3} {n : ℕ} (T : ℝ),
  0 < T →
    ∀ (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
      ↑w = (𝔊.P n) ↑w →
        ∀ (ψ : LerayHopf.Time → ℝ),
          tsupport ψ ⊆ Set.Ioo 0 T →
            ContDiff ℝ 1 ψ →
              ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T,
                  -inner ℝ ↑(gs.u t) ↑w * deriv ψ t +
                    ψ t * (ν * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑(gs.u t) ↑w + F.b (gs.u t) (gs.u t) w) =
                0

nodes.doc:
**`galerkin_weakLimit_R3` — Measurable weak limit in `L2Sigma_R3` from per-ball a.e.-t
convergence.**  [#47: converted from `axiom` to `theorem`]

Given a strictly-monotone subsequence `φ` such that for every `k : ℕ` and for a.e. `t ∈ [0,T]`
the ball-k-restricted Galerkin states converge in `L2ballR3 k`, there exists a measurable limit
curve `u : Time → L2Sigma_R3` such that:
- `u` is `AEStronglyMeasurable` on `[0,T]`, and
- for every `R : ℝ`, for a.e. `t ∈ [0,T]`, `restrictToBall R ((galSeq (φ n)).u t) → restrictToBall R (u t)`.

**Proof (#47 PR-A — sorry-free):** Uses a STRONG ball-exhaustion + Mazur route:
Cauchy diagonal over the liftG-extended per-ball limits (sections A–K), computes
the strong limit via `exists_stronglyMeasurable_limit_of_tendsto_ae` (section L),
confirms membership in `L2Sigma_R3` via `weakLimit_mem_L2Sigma_R3` (WL-5, Mazur),
and establishes per-R a.e.-t convergence by norm monotonicity (sections M–O).
This route does NOT use `L2VF_R3_weakSeqCompact_closedBall` (that axiom is DELETED).

**SAME signature as the former axiom:** all downstream consumers (`u_lim_aestronglyMeasurable`,
`AubinLionsLimitPassage`, the capstone) continue to see this by the SAME name and type.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.galerkin_weakLimit_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3)
  (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (φ : ℕ → ℕ),
  StrictMono φ →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        (∀ (k : ℕ),
            ∃ g_k,
              MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable g_k (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) ∧
                ∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T),
                  Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑((galSeq (φ n)).u t)) Filter.atTop
                    (nhds (g_k t))) →
          ∃ u,
            MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable (fun t => ↑(u t)) (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) ∧
              ∀ (R : ℝ),
                ∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T),
                  Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall R ↑((galSeq (φ n)).u t)) Filter.atTop
                    (nhds (LerayHopf.restrictToBall R ↑(u t)))

nodes.doc:
**A2 `gns_L6_cc1_R3` [must-prove].**
The Gagliardo–Nirenberg–Sobolev inequality for `C¹` compactly-supported functions on ℝ³
with values in a finite-dimensional normed real vector space `F`.

`eLpNorm u 6 volume ≤ C * eLpNorm (fderiv ℝ u) 2 volume`

where `C := SNormLESNormFDerivOfEqConst F (volume : Measure Domain3) 2` is the
mathlib GNS constant (independent of `u`, depending only on `F` and the measure on ℝ³).

**Proof plan (for prover pass):** Direct application of
`MeasureTheory.eLpNorm_le_eLpNorm_fderiv_of_eq` at:
- `E := Domain3` (`FiniteDimensional ℝ`, `finrank ℝ Domain3 = 3`)
- `p := (2 : NNReal)`, `p' := (6 : NNReal)` in the NNReal signature
- `hp' : (6 : ℝ)⁻¹ = (2 : ℝ)⁻¹ - (3 : ℝ)⁻¹` (= `1/6 = 1/2 - 1/3`, by `norm_num`)
- `hp : 1 ≤ (2 : NNReal)` (by `norm_num`)
- `hn : 0 < finrank ℝ Domain3` (= `0 < 3`)

The μ in `eLpNorm_le_eLpNorm_fderiv_of_eq` is any `IsAddHaarMeasure`, satisfied by
`volume : Measure Domain3` via `measureSpaceOfInnerProductSpace`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gns_L6_cc1_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ {F : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup F] [inst_1 : NormedSpace ℝ F] [inst_2 : FiniteDimensional ℝ F]
  {u : LerayHopf.Domain3 → F},
  ContDiff ℝ 1 u →
    HasCompactSupport u →
      MeasureTheory.eLpNorm u 6 MeasureTheory.volume ≤
        ↑(MeasureTheory.SNormLESNormFDerivOfEqConst F MeasureTheory.volume 2) *
          MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fderiv ℝ u) 2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
**A3 `gns_L6_of_memH1_R3` [PROVED sorry-free].**
If a complex-valued `L²(ℝ³; ℂ)` function `f` lies in the Sobolev space `H^{1,2}(ℝ³; ℂ)`
(i.e. `TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (f : 𝓢'(Domain3, ℂ))` via the
`Lp.instCoeDep` coercion in `TemperedDistribution.lean:178`), then `f ∈ L⁶(ℝ³; ℂ)`.

Conclusion: `MemLp (⇑f) 6 (volume : Measure Domain3)`, where `⇑f : Domain3 → ℂ` is the
a.e. representative of `f : L2C_R3 = Lp ℂ 2 (volume : Measure Domain3)`.

**Proof.**
1. Extract the Sobolev representative `f' ∈ L²` with `smulLeftCLM (1+‖·‖²)^(1/2) (𝓕 f) = f'`,
   giving `⇑(𝓕 f) =ᵐ wInv • f'` (`fourier_ae_eq_wInv_smul`).
2. Take a Schwartz sequence `ηₙ.toLp 2 → f'` (`denseRange_toLpCLM`) and set the Schwartz
   approximants `φₙ := 𝓕⁻¹(smulLeftCLM wInv ηₙ)`, so `𝓕 φₙ ξ = wInv ξ • ηₙ ξ` and
   `∫(1+‖ξ‖²)‖𝓕φₙ‖² = ‖ηₙ.toLp‖²`.
3. `gns_L6_schwartz` + `eLpNorm_fderiv_le_weighted` give `eLpNorm φₙ 6 ≤ C·2π·‖ηₙ.toLp‖`,
   uniformly `≤ C·2π·M` with `M := ‖f'‖+1` (since `‖ηₙ.toLp‖ → ‖f'‖`).
4. `φₙ.toLp → f` in L² (Fourier isometry + the bounded multiplier `mulBdd wInv`); extract an
   a.e.-convergent subsequence and apply Fatou (`Lp.eLpNorm_le_of_ae_tendsto`) to get
   `eLpNorm ⇑f 6 ≤ C·2π·M < ∞`, hence `MemLp (⇑f) 6`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gns_L6_of_memH1_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3),
  TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) →
    MeasureTheory.MemLp (↑↑f) 6 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
**GNS for Schwartz functions** (no compact support):
`eLpNorm φ 6 ≤ C · eLpNorm (fderiv φ) 2`. Exported (non-`private`) so the energy-class
convection development can obtain a uniform `L⁶` bound on `H¹` Schwartz approximants.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gns_L6_schwartz
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (⇑φ) 6 MeasureTheory.volume ≤
    ↑(MeasureTheory.SNormLESNormFDerivOfEqConst ℂ MeasureTheory.volume 2) *
      MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fderiv ℝ ⇑φ) 2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
**B2 helper `gradComp_of_memH1`** — the spectral L² weak-derivative representative.

For `u : L2VF_R3` with `memH1VF_R3 u`, the j-th Sobolev component satisfies
`MemSobolev 1 2 (L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j u)`. Applying `MemSobolev.lineDerivOp` at
`m = EuclideanSpace.single a 1` yields `MemSobolev 0 2 (∂_{eₐ} (L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j u))`,
and `memSobolev_zero_iff` gives an `L²` representative.

`gradComp_of_memH1 u hu a j` is the L²(ℝ³; ℂ) representative of the weak
directional derivative `∂_{eₐ}` of the j-th component of `u`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1
nodes.signature: (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u → Fin 3 → Fin 3 → ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3

nodes.doc: The weak gradient component is additive in the field: `gradComp(v+v') = gradComp v + gradComp v'`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1_add
nodes.signature:
∀ (v v' : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hv : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v) (hv' : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v') (a i : Fin 3),
  LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 (v + v') ⋯ a i =
    LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 v hv a i + LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 v' hv' a i

nodes.doc: The weak gradient component is ℝ-homogeneous in the field: `gradComp(c•v) = c • gradComp v`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1_smul
nodes.signature:
∀ (c : ℝ) (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hv : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v) (a i : Fin 3),
  LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 (c • v) ⋯ a i = ↑c • LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 v hv a i

nodes.doc:
The defining spectral identity of `gradComp_of_memH1`: as a tempered distribution it is the
line derivative `∂_{eₐ}` of the j-th complex component of `u`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1_spec
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hu : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u) (a j : Fin 3),
  LineDeriv.lineDerivOp (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)
      (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) u)) =
    MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution (LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 u hu a j)

nodes.doc:
For a Schwartz-class field component, the weak gradient `gradComp_of_memH1` equals the
complexified classical line derivative `(cxify (∂ₐ ψ)).toLp 2`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradComp_schwartz_eq
nodes.signature:
∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hv : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v) (ψ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (a i : Fin 3),
  (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 i) v = ψ.toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume →
    LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 v hv a i =
      (LerayHopf.cxify✝
            ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) ψ)).toLp
        2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
**B2 `gradComponent_weakDeriv` [must-prove].** The spectral weak-derivative L²
representative satisfies the integration-by-parts identity: for any Schwartz test function
`φ : 𝓢(Domain3, ℝ)`,

  `∫ (L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j u x).re * (∂ₐ φ)(x) dx =
    -∫ (gradComp_of_memH1 u hu a j x).re * φ(x) dx`

This is the weak (distributional) definition of `∂ₐ`, transported from the Schwartz
tempered-distribution IBP through the Lp coercion.

**Proof route (for prover):** unfold `gradComp_of_memH1`, use
`TemperedDistribution.memSobolev_zero_iff.mp (hu j).lineDerivOp` to get the representative;
the IBP identity follows from the definition of the distributional derivative as the
adjoint of `∂ₐ` under the Fourier pairing, combined with the `Lp.toTemperedDistributionCLM`
coercion and the classical Schwartz IBP
`SchwartzMap.integral_mul_lineDerivOp_right_eq_neg_left` on the L² level.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradComponent_weakDeriv
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hu : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u) (a j : Fin 3) (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
      (↑↑((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) u) x).re *
        ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) φ) x =
    -∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), (↑↑(LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 u hu a j) x).re * φ x

nodes.doc:
**`gradPairingSummable` (torus #53).**

For `u : L2VF`, `v : L2VF` with `memH1VF v` (H¹ on the MIDDLE / gradient slot),
and `w : L2Sigma` a Galerkin test, the Parseval convection summand

  `(k, l) ↦ (û_a(k) · (2πi lₐ) · v̂_i(l) · ŵ_i(-(k+l))).re`

is summable over `(Fin 3 → ℤ) × (Fin 3 → ℤ)` for each fixed `(i, a) : Fin 3 × Fin 3`.

**Why the H¹ condition is on `v` (NOT symmetric):** The weight `(2πi lₐ)` is the
Fourier multiplier for `∂_a v`.  Writing `α(k) = |û_a(k)|`, `β(l) = |lₐ| · |v̂_i(l)|`,
`γ(m) = |ŵ_i(m)|`, the double sum is a convolution-diagonal bound.  Since `w` is a
Galerkin test (finite support), `γ` is finitely supported ⟹ anti-diagonal collapse
gives summability from `α ∈ ℓ²`, `β ∈ ℓ²` (the `ℓ²·ℓ²·ℓ¹_finite` product).  Three
arbitrary L² slots do NOT suffice.

**Corresponds to:** `convSummand_summable_of_h1_test` in the PR-0 spike.

Proof: `Complex.reCLM`-image of `convSummand_summable` (the complex-summable workhorse).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradPairingSummable
nodes.signature:
∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF v →
    ∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
      LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w →
        ∀ (i a : Fin 3),
          Summable fun kl =>
            (LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC a) u) kl.1 *
                (2 * ↑Real.pi * Complex.I * ↑(kl.2 a) *
                  (LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC i) v) kl.2 *
                    LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC i) ↑w) (-(kl.1 + kl.2))))).re

nodes.doc: A.e. pointwise additivity of the real part of the weak gradient under field addition.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradRe_add_ae
nodes.signature:
∀ (v v' : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hv : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v) (hv' : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v') (a i : Fin 3),
  (fun x => (↑↑(LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 (v + v') ⋯ a i) x).re) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] fun x =>
    (↑↑(LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 v hv a i) x).re + (↑↑(LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 v' hv' a i) x).re

nodes.doc: A.e. pointwise homogeneity of the real part of the weak gradient under field smul.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradRe_smul_ae
nodes.signature:
∀ (c : ℝ) (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hv : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v) (a i : Fin 3),
  (fun x => (↑↑(LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 (c • v) ⋯ a i) x).re) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] fun x =>
    c * (↑↑(LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 v hv a i) x).re

nodes.doc:
The **gradient vector field** of a Schwartz test `φ`, as an element of `L2VF_R3`:
`gradVF φ x = ∑ j, (∂_j φ)(x) • e_j = ∇φ(x)`.  Built by lifting each scalar component
`(∂_j φ).toLp` along `toSpanSingleton ℝ (e_j)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradVF
nodes.signature: SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3

nodes.doc: Pointwise (a.e.) value of `gradVF φ`: `gradVF φ x = ∑ j, (∂_j φ)(x) • e_j`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.gradVF_coeFn
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  ↑↑(LerayHopf.gradVF✝ φ) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] fun x => ∑ j, ↑↑(LerayHopf.dphiLp✝ φ j) x • EuclideanSpace.single j 1

nodes.doc:
The graph embedding of a Schwartz potential: its curl field together with the vector of
weighted Fourier components (√W · 𝓕(curl)ⱼ).  The product-space distance between two such
points controls BOTH the `L²` error and the viscous seminorm error `viscousFormSq_R3 1`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.graphEmbed
nodes.signature: (Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 × (Fin 3 → ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3)

nodes.doc:
The squared H¹ energy of a velocity field `u ∈ L2VF`, defined componentwise via
Fourier coefficients:

  `h1EnergySq u = ∑ j : Fin 3, ∑' k : Fin 3 → ℤ, (1 + ∑ i, (k i : ℝ)²) * ‖ûⱼ(k)‖²`

where `ûⱼ(k) = mFourierCoeff3 (L2VF_projComponentC j u) k`.

This is the natural H¹(𝕋³; ℝ³) energy: the `(1 + ∑ᵢ kᵢ²)` weight is the standard
Sobolev weight at order 1.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.h1EnergySq
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → ℝ

nodes.doc:
Each single-component H¹ Fourier sum is dominated by the full `h1EnergySq`:
the energy is a sum of nonneg per-component sums.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.h1EnergySq_component_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (j : Fin 3) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  ∑' (k : Fin 3 → ℤ), (1 + ∑ i, ↑(k i) ^ 2) * ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) w) k‖ ^ 2 ≤
    LerayHopf.h1EnergySq w

nodes.doc:
(b′) Integrability side condition to consume `reg_bound` soundly: for a Galerkin
curve (band-limited at level `n`), `t ↦ h1EnergySq (u_n t)` is continuous on `[0,∞)`
(finite `fourierBox` sum of squared moduli of continuous coefficient functions).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.h1EnergySq_continuousOn_galerkin
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (n : ℕ) (D : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n),
  ContinuousOn (fun t => LerayHopf.h1EnergySq ↑(D.u t)) (Set.Ici 0)

nodes.doc: The H¹ energy is nonneg: each summand is a product of nonneg factors.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.h1EnergySq_nonneg
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), 0 ≤ LerayHopf.h1EnergySq u

nodes.doc:
**H¹·H¹ weak Leibniz.** For `f, g : L2C_R3` in `H^{1,2}` with weak `eₐ`-derivatives
`fG, gG` (i.e. `∂ₐ(f:𝓢') = (fG:𝓢')`, `∂ₐ(g:𝓢') = (gG:𝓢')`), and any real Schwartz `φ`:

  `∫ (f.re)·(g.re)·(∂ₐφ) = -∫ ((fG.re)·(g.re) + (f.re)·(gG.re))·φ`.

Proved by approximating `f, g, fG, gG` by Schwartz sequences (`reSchwartz_approx`), applying
the classical Schwartz Leibniz IBP (`schwartzLeibniz2`), and passing to the `L²` limit
(`tendsto_integral_mul_mul`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.h1Leibniz2
nodes.signature:
∀ (f g fG gG : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3) (a : Fin 3),
  TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) →
    TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution g) →
      LineDeriv.lineDerivOp (EuclideanSpace.single a 1) (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) =
          MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution fG →
        LineDeriv.lineDerivOp (EuclideanSpace.single a 1) (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution g) =
            MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution gG →
          ∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
            ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
                (↑↑f x).re * (↑↑g x).re *
                  ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) φ) x =
              -∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ((↑↑fG x).re * (↑↑g x).re + (↑↑f x).re * (↑↑gG x).re) * φ x

nodes.doc:
**H¹-test weak Leibniz.** Same as `h1Leibniz2`, but the test factor is an `H¹` function `vt`
(with weak `eₐ`-derivative `vtG`) rather than a Schwartz function. The un-differentiated test is
paired in `L³` (its multi-direction Schwartz approximants converge in `L³` via
`reSchwartz_L3_approx`); the differentiated test in `L²`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.h1Leibniz2_H1test
nodes.signature:
∀ (f g fG gG vt vtG : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3) (a : Fin 3),
  TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) →
    TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution g) →
      LineDeriv.lineDerivOp (EuclideanSpace.single a 1) (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) =
          MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution fG →
        LineDeriv.lineDerivOp (EuclideanSpace.single a 1) (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution g) =
            MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution gG →
          TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution vt) →
            LineDeriv.lineDerivOp (EuclideanSpace.single a 1) (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution vt) =
                MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution vtG →
              MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => (↑↑f x).re) 6 MeasureTheory.volume →
                MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => (↑↑f x).re) 3 MeasureTheory.volume →
                  MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => (↑↑g x).re) 6 MeasureTheory.volume →
                    ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), (↑↑f x).re * (↑↑g x).re * (↑↑vtG x).re =
                      -∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ((↑↑fG x).re * (↑↑g x).re + (↑↑f x).re * (↑↑gG x).re) * (↑↑vt x).re

nodes.doc:
**A4 `h1Sigma_dense_in_L2Sigma` [must-prove].**
The `H¹` divergence-free velocity fields (those satisfying `memH1VF_R3`) are **dense** in
`L2Sigma_R3`.

For every `u ∈ L2Sigma_R3`, there is a sequence `s : ℕ → L2Sigma_R3` with
`∀ n, memH1VF_R3 (s n : L2VF_R3)` and `Filter.Tendsto s atTop (nhds u)`.

**Proof plan (for prover pass):**
1. Every Schwartz function is in all Sobolev spaces: `SchwartzMap.memSobolev` gives
   `MemSobolev 1 2 (φ.toLp 2 volume : 𝓢'(...))` for any `φ : 𝓢(Domain3, ℂ)`.
2. Hence `IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 v ⟹ memH1VF_R3 (v : L2VF_R3)` (each component
   is a Schwartz `L²`-class, hence H¹ via `SchwartzMap.memSobolev`).
3. `schwartzDivFree_dense_of_curlDense curlSchwartzDense_holds` (proved in `ConvectionForm.lean:594`
   and `CurlDensityCapstone.lean`) gives density of `IsSchwartzDivFree_R3` in `L2Sigma_R3`.
4. Combine (2)+(3): H¹_σ ⊇ Schwartz_σ which is dense → H¹_σ is dense.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.h1Sigma_dense_in_L2Sigma
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3), ∃ s, (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 ↑(s n)) ∧ Filter.Tendsto s Filter.atTop (nhds u)

nodes.doc:
The canonical measure on `Torus3`: the product Haar (probability) measure
`Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin 3 => AddCircle.haarAddCircle)`.

This is the measure under which `UnitAddTorus.mFourierBasis` is a Hilbert basis, so
**all** torus L² spaces in this project (`L2VF`, `L2C`) use it.  It coincides with the
global `volume : Measure Torus3` propositionally (see `volume_torus3_eq_haarTorus3`).

viscous lower Lebesgue integral: `⨆ k, ofReal ‖mulBdd_k (𝓕 projⱼ w)‖² = viscousLintegrand_j j w`.

nodes.doc:
**Vector Parseval bridge.**  The (complexified) real inner product of two velocity fields
equals the integral of the three-component sum of Hermitian products of their complex component
Fourier transforms:
`⟪u, w⟫ = ∫ ∑_j conj(û_j(ξ)) · ŵ_j(ξ) dξ`, where `û_j = 𝓕 (L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j u)`.

Assembles `inner_L2VF_eq_sum_component` (vector → component inners), `complexInner_compLpL_ofReal`
(real → complex component inner), `Lp.inner_fourier_eq` (Plancherel per component), and the
finite-sum/integral swap.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.inner_L2VF_eq_integral_sum_fourier
nodes.signature:
∀ (a b : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  ↑(inner ℝ a b) =
    ∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
      ∑ j,
        (starRingEnd ℂ) (↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) a)) ξ) *
          ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) b)) ξ

nodes.doc:
**Fixed-test-vector weak convergence at a.e. `t`.** From per-ball a.e.-`t` convergence
`restrictToBall R (uₙ t) → restrictToBall R (u t)` (all `R`) plus the uniform `L²` bounds
`‖uₙ t‖ ≤ M`, `‖u t‖ ≤ M`, the inner products against any fixed `e` converge:
`⟪e, uₙ t⟫ → ⟪e, u t⟫`.  Ball/tail ε/3 split: the ball part converges (`restrictToBall` strong),
the tails are bounded by `‖tail e‖ · M → 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.inner_tendsto_of_perball
nodes.signature:
∀ (e : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (un : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (M : ℝ),
  0 ≤ M →
    (∀ (n : ℕ), ‖un n‖ ≤ M) →
      ‖u‖ ≤ M →
        (∀ (k : ℕ),
            Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall (↑k) (un n)) Filter.atTop
              (nhds (LerayHopf.restrictToBall (↑k) u))) →
          Filter.Tendsto (fun n => inner ℝ e (un n)) Filter.atTop (nhds (inner ℝ e u))

nodes.doc:
**A1 [scaffold].** The Hölder triple `(6, 3, 2)` in `ENNReal`:
`6⁻¹ + 3⁻¹ = 2⁻¹` in `ℝ≥0∞`, i.e. `1/6 + 1/3 = 1/2`.

This is one of two pairwise instances needed to prove integrability of a triple product
`u · ∂v · w` with `u ∈ L⁶`, `∂v ∈ L²`, `w ∈ L³` (where `1/6 + 1/2 + 1/3 = 1`):
- **This instance** `HolderTriple 6 3 2`: combine `u ∈ L⁶` and `w ∈ L³` to get
  `u·w ∈ L²` (since `1/6 + 1/3 = 1/2`).
- Then use `HolderConjugate 2 2` (already in mathlib as `instTwoTwo`) to combine
  `u·w ∈ L²` and `∂v ∈ L²` to get `u·∂v·w ∈ L¹`.

NOTE: The plan's description `(6,2,3)` with `1/6+1/2+1/3=1` referred to the TRIPLE
of exponents appearing in Hölder's inequality for three factors, NOT to an `ENNReal.HolderTriple`
instance (which is a PAIRWISE predicate: `p⁻¹ + q⁻¹ = r⁻¹`). The correct pairwise
instance for the B3b chain is `HolderTriple 6 3 2` (`1/6 + 1/3 = 1/2`).

Used by B3b (`convFormH1_integrable`) in `EnergyClassConvection.lean`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.instHolderTriple_6_3_2
nodes.signature: ENNReal.HolderTriple 6 3 2

B1.  `L2VF_R3 = L²(ℝ³; ℝ³)` is a `SeparableSpace`.

`volume` on `Domain3` is a separable measure and `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)` is
second-countable, so `Lp.SecondCountableTopology` makes `L2VF_R3` second-countable,
hence separable.

second-countability, each `L2C_R3` factor via `instSecondCountable_L2C_R3`).

nodes.doc:
`mollifyRep K g` is **integrable on every finite-measure set** (continuous and globally
bounded by `‖kernelL2R K‖·‖g‖`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integrableOn_mollifyRep
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) {s : Set LerayHopf.Domain3},
  MeasureTheory.volume s ≠ ⊤ → MeasureTheory.IntegrableOn (LerayHopf.mollifyRep K g) s MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
The per-component integrand of `divComponent_eq_fourier_integral` is integrable: it is the
pointwise inner product of two `L²` Fourier transforms.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integrable_divComponent
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (j : Fin 3),
  MeasureTheory.Integrable
    (fun ξ =>
      (starRingEnd ℂ) (2 * ↑Real.pi * Complex.I * ↑(ξ.ofLp j) * (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ φ)) ξ) *
        ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) u)) ξ)
    MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
The kernel-weighted translation family `h ↦ K.η h • translate_L2VF h g` is Bochner
integrable as an `L2VF_R3`-valued map: it is continuous (so strongly measurable) and supported in
`tsupport K.η ⊆ closedBall 0 K.supportRadius` (compact), with `‖K.η h • translate_L2VF h g‖
= |K.η h| · ‖g‖` bounded.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integrable_kernel_smul_translate
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun h => K.η h • LerayHopf.translate_L2VF h g) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
The inner integrand `y ↦ K.η (x − y) • g y` of `mollifyRep K g x` is Bochner integrable:
its norm `|K.η (x − y)| · ‖g y‖` is the product of two `L²` functions (Hölder, `p = q = 2`),
hence `L¹`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integrable_kernel_translate_smul
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun y => K.η (x - y) • ↑↑g y) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
The weight `‖ξ‖²‖ŵ_j ξ‖²` is integrable (`ŵ_j` Schwartz): write
`‖ξ‖²‖ŵ_j ξ‖² = ∑_a ‖(ξ_a • ŵ_j) ξ‖²` with each `ξ_a • ŵ_j` Schwartz.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integrable_normsq_weighted
nodes.signature: ∀ (f : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ξ => ‖ξ‖ ^ 2 * ‖f ξ‖ ^ 2) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
`(1 + ‖ξ‖²)·‖g ξ‖²` is integrable for a Schwartz `g` (Schwartz decay dominates the quadratic
weight): dominated by `C·((1+‖ξ‖²)·‖g ξ‖)` where `C := ‖g‖_∞`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integrable_one_add_normSq_schwartz
nodes.signature:
∀ (g : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ξ => (1 + ‖ξ‖ ^ 2) * ‖g ξ‖ ^ 2) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: The squared norm of a Schwartz map is integrable.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integrable_sq_norm_schwartz
nodes.signature: ∀ (f : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ), MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ξ => ‖f ξ‖ ^ 2) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
An a.e.-strongly-measurable curve bounded by `C` on `[0,T]` has integrable squared
norm on the finite interval measure.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integrable_sq_of_aesm_bounded
nodes.signature:
∀ {β : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup β] (T C : ℝ) (g : ℝ → β),
  MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable g (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) →
    (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖g t‖ ≤ C) →
      MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun t => ‖g t‖ ^ 2) (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T))

nodes.doc:
**T0b integrability input.** For `w ∈ H¹(ℝ³)`, the spectral viscous integrand
`(2π)² ‖ξ‖² ‖(𝓕 cⱼ) ξ‖²` (with `cⱼ = L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j w`) is Lebesgue-integrable.

This is the ONE genuinely missing analytic fact: it is the statement that the H¹-membership
`memH1VF_R3 w` (defined as `TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2` on each complex component)
implies the *concrete* weighted-L² integrability `∫ (1+‖ξ‖²) ‖(𝓕 cⱼ) ξ‖² < ∞` of the
**L²-Fourier transform** `𝓕 cⱼ = Lp.fourierTransformₗᵢ cⱼ`.

**OPEN — isolated frontier (see TODO):** `MemSobolev 1 2 (cⱼ : 𝓢')` unfolds to
`∃ f' : Lp ℂ 2, besselPotential E ℂ 1 (cⱼ : 𝓢') = f'`, equivalently (mathlib
`memSobolev_iff_exists_smulLeftCLM_fourier`) `∃ f', smulLeftCLM (fun ξ ↦ (1+‖ξ‖²)^(1/2)) (𝓕 (cⱼ : 𝓢')) = f'`.
To turn this into the concrete integrability one must (i) bridge `𝓕 (cⱼ : 𝓢')` to the
L²-Fourier `(𝓕 cⱼ : Lp)` via `Lp.fourier_toTemperedDistribution_eq`, and (ii) extract the
a.e. pointwise identity `(1+‖ξ‖²)^(1/2) · (𝓕 cⱼ) ξ = f' ξ` from
`smulLeftCLM weight ((𝓕 cⱼ : Lp) : 𝓢') = (f' : 𝓢')`.  Step (ii) needs an a.e. characterization
of `smulLeftCLM` applied to an `Lp`-coerced distribution for an **unbounded** multiplier
`weight = (1+‖ξ‖²)^(1/2)`; mathlib's `Lp.toTemperedDistribution_smul_eq` only covers
`MemLp`-bounded multipliers (and `weight ∉ Lᵖ`), so this a.e.-extraction-for-unbounded-weights
is the missing piece.  Once it lands, `‖ξ‖² ≤ 1 + ‖ξ‖²` gives the claim by domination.
Everything else in T0b is fully proved and consumes only this lemma.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integrable_viscous_integrand_of_memH1
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w →
    ∀ (j : Fin 3),
      MeasureTheory.Integrable
        (fun ξ =>
          (2 * Real.pi) ^ 2 * ‖ξ‖ ^ 2 * ‖↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) w)) ξ‖ ^ 2)
        MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
The integral `∫ a·b·h` over `ℝ³` for real `L²` elements `a, b` and bounded `h` equals the
real `L²` inner product `⟪a, mulRBdd h b⟫`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integral_mul_mul_eq_inner
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hh : MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  (a b : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)),
  ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ↑↑a x * ↑↑b x * h x = inner ℝ a (LerayHopf.mulRBdd✝ h hh b)

nodes.doc:
**A3 kernel.** Integrated gradient–Fourier bound for Schwartz `φ`:
`∫ ‖fderiv ℝ φ x‖² ≤ (2π)² ∫ ‖ξ‖² ‖𝓕 φ ξ‖²`.  Combine the pointwise HS bound
(`opNorm_le_sqrt_sum_sq`, squared) with the summed per-direction Plancherel
(`normSq_lineDeriv_toLp`) over the standard basis (`∑ᵢ ⟨ξ,eᵢ⟩² = ‖ξ‖²`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integral_normSq_fderiv_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖fderiv ℝ (⇑φ) x‖ ^ 2 ≤
    ∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), (2 * Real.pi) ^ 2 * ‖ξ‖ ^ 2 * ‖(FourierTransform.fourier φ) ξ‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
For a real Schwartz `ψ` and an essentially bounded `h`, the integral of `ψ·ψ'·h` (Schwartz
values) equals the same integral with the `L²`-classes of `ψ, ψ'` (a.e. equal to `ψ, ψ'`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integral_schwartz_eq_toLp
nodes.signature:
∀ (ψ ψ' : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ),
  ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ψ x * ψ' x * h x =
    ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ↑↑(ψ.toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) x * ↑↑(ψ'.toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) x * h x

nodes.doc:
(P0.10) Step D — finite-dim strong part.  Weak convergence at every `t ∈ [0,T]`
plus uniform ball bounds give strong convergence of the level-`N` projections in
`L²(0,T)`: coordinates against the finite-dimensional `velocitySpan N` converge
pointwise (projection self-adjointness), `‖P_N(v n t − u t)‖ ≤ 2M` dominates, DCT.
Conclusion shape = P0.8's `hint` input (interval integral over `0..T`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integral_sq_proj_tendsto_zero_of_weak
nodes.signature:
∀ (T : ℝ),
  0 < T →
    ∀ (M : ℝ) (N : ℕ) (v : ℕ → ℝ → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (u : ℝ → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
      (∀ (n : ℕ), ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖↑(v n t)‖ ≤ M) →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖↑(u t)‖ ≤ M) →
          (∀ (n : ℕ),
              MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable (fun t => ↑(v n t)) (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T))) →
            MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable (fun t => ↑(u t)) (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) →
              (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T,
                  ∀ (z : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
                    Filter.Tendsto (fun n => inner ℝ ↑(v n t) ↑z) Filter.atTop (nhds (inner ℝ ↑(u t) ↑z))) →
                Filter.Tendsto
                  (fun n =>
                    ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T,
                      ‖(LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n N) ↑(v n t) - (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n N) ↑(u t)‖ ^ 2)
                  Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
(P0.16) Step F core — strong `L²(0,T; L²)` convergence of the extracted Galerkin
subsequence to the limit curve, from the Step-D projected convergence + the two
T-AL-5 tail bounds via the Pythagoras split and an ε-squeeze over the level `N`.
Conclusion shape = P0.8's `hint` input verbatim.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integral_sq_sub_tendsto_zero_of_galSeq
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n) (φ : ℕ → ℕ)
          (u : LerayHopf.Time → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
          (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖↑(u t)‖ ≤ ‖↑u₀‖) →
            MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable (fun t => ↑(u t)) (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) →
              (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T,
                  ∀ (z : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
                    Filter.Tendsto (fun n => inner ℝ ↑((galSeq (φ n)).u t) ↑z) Filter.atTop
                      (nhds (inner ℝ ↑(u t) ↑z))) →
                (∀ (N : ℕ),
                    Filter.Tendsto
                      (fun n =>
                        ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T,
                          ‖(LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n N) ↑((galSeq (φ n)).u t) -
                                (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n N) ↑(u t)‖ ^
                            2)
                      Filter.atTop (nhds 0)) →
                  Filter.Tendsto (fun n => ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T, ‖↑((galSeq (φ n)).u t) - ↑(u t)‖ ^ 2) Filter.atTop
                    (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
(P0.14) Step E, Galerkin side — the n-UNIFORM tail integral bound.  Pointwise
P0.6b (fed by `reg_mem : ∀ t, memH1VF (u_n t)`), interval-integral monotonicity
(integrands continuous: `galerkin_u_continuousOn` + CLM for the LHS, P0.6b′ for the
majorant), then the `reg_bound` field divided by `1 + N²`.  No `0 < ν` binder: the
chain runs entirely through `reg_bound`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integral_tail_sq_galerkin_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (n : ℕ) (D : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n)
  (T : ℝ),
  0 < T →
    ∀ (N : ℕ),
      ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T, ‖↑(D.u t) - (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n N) ↑(D.u t)‖ ^ 2 ≤
        (T * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2 + ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2 / (2 * ν)) / (1 + ↑N ^ 2)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.integral_tail_sq_limit_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n) (φ : ℕ → ℕ)
          (u : LerayHopf.Time → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
          (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖↑(u t)‖ ≤ ‖↑u₀‖) →
            MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable (fun t => ↑(u t)) (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) →
              (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T,
                  ∀ (z : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
                    Filter.Tendsto (fun n => inner ℝ ↑((galSeq (φ n)).u t) ↑z) Filter.atTop
                      (nhds (inner ℝ ↑(u t) ↑z))) →
                ∀ (N : ℕ),
                  ∫ (t : ℝ) in 0..T, ‖↑(u t) - (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n N) ↑(u t)‖ ^ 2 ≤
                    (T * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2 + ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2 / (2 * ν)) / (1 + ↑N ^ 2)

nodes.doc: Integrability of the `(i,a)`-summand integrand. (Shared by B1 and Tier A.)
nodes.name: LerayHopf.integrand_integrable'
nodes.signature:
∀ (ψu ψv ψw : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (i a : Fin 3),
  MeasureTheory.Integrable
    (fun x =>
      (ψu a) x * ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) (ψv i)) x *
        (ψw i) x)
    MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
**Time-Hölder helper.** For a continuous nonnegative integrand `h` on `[0, T]`
(`0 < T`) and an exponent `0 < r < 1`, the integral of `hʳ` is controlled by a power of the
length times a power of the integral:
`∫₀ᵀ hʳ ≤ T^(1-r) · (∫₀ᵀ h)ʳ`.

This is Hölder's inequality against the constant `1` with conjugate exponents
`p = (1-r)⁻¹`, `q = r⁻¹` (so `1/p = 1-r`, `1/q = r`).  Specialized at `r = 1/2` it gives the
`√`-Cauchy–Schwarz bound, and at `r = 3/4` the `^{3/4}`-Hölder bound consumed by D3.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.intervalIntegral_rpow_le_of_nonneg
nodes.signature:
∀ {h : ℝ → ℝ} {T : ℝ},
  0 < T →
    ContinuousOn h (Set.Icc 0 T) →
      (∀ σ ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, 0 ≤ h σ) →
        ∀ {r : ℝ}, 0 < r → r < 1 → ∫ (σ : ℝ) in 0..T, h σ ^ r ≤ T ^ (1 - r) * (∫ (σ : ℝ) in 0..T, h σ) ^ r

nodes.doc:
**A4.** For a compact `K ⊆ X`, the set of `toLp` classes of all mesh-`m` step curves
with values in `K` is COMPACT in `Lp X 2 μ_T`.

Route (plan §3 A4): the assembly map `y ↦ (stepCurve_memLp …).toLp` is Lipschitz from
`Fin m → X` with the sup metric (`‖stepCurve y − stepCurve y'‖_{L²} ≤ √T · max_i ‖y i − y' i‖`),
and `{y | ∀ i, y i ∈ K}` is compact (`IsCompact.pi` on `Set.pi Set.univ (fun _ => K)`), so the
image is compact by `IsCompact.image`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.isCompact_stepCurve_toLp
nodes.signature:
∀ {X : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup X] (T : ℝ) (hT : 0 < T) (m : ℕ) (hm : 0 < m) {K : Set X},
  IsCompact K →
    IsCompact ((fun y => MeasureTheory.MemLp.toLp (LerayHopf.stepCurve T m y) ⋯) '' {y | ∀ (i : Fin m), y i ∈ K})

nodes.doc:
The volume of the closed ball `B_R ⊆ ℝ³` is finite (it is compact in a proper space), so the
restricted measure `volume.restrict B_R` is a finite measure: needed to invoke
`Lp.norm_le_of_ae_bound` for the sup→L² transfer.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.isFiniteMeasure_volume_restrict_closedBall
nodes.signature: ∀ (R : ℝ), MeasureTheory.IsFiniteMeasure (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Metric.closedBall 0 R))

nodes.doc: `IsSchwartzDivFree_R3` is closed under subtraction (add + `(-1)`-smul).
