∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
  LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 u → LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 v → LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 (u - v)

nodes.doc:
**H1.** The sum of two `IsSchwartzDivFree_R3` fields is again `IsSchwartzDivFree_R3`.

Proof: if `u` has Schwartz witnesses `ψu` and `v` has witnesses `ψv`, then
`u + v` has witnesses `(fun j => ψu j + ψv j)`, because `toLp` is additive a.e.
and `L2VF_projComponent_R3` is linear.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.isSchwartzDivFree_add
nodes.signature:
∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
  LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 u → LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 v → LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 (u + v)

nodes.doc:
**H3.** A finite ℝ-linear combination of `IsSchwartzDivFree_R3` fields is
`IsSchwartzDivFree_R3`.

Follows by induction from H1 and H2 (scalar multiplication preserves the class, and the
class is closed under addition).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.isSchwartzDivFree_linearCombination
nodes.signature:
∀ {ι : Type u_1} (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → ℝ) (v : ι → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
  (∀ i ∈ s, LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 (v i)) → LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 (∑ i ∈ s, f i • v i)

nodes.doc:
**H2.** A scalar multiple of an `IsSchwartzDivFree_R3` field is again `IsSchwartzDivFree_R3`.

Proof: if `u` has witnesses `ψu` then `c • u` has witnesses `(fun j => c • ψu j)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.isSchwartzDivFree_smul
nodes.signature: ∀ (c : ℝ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3), LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 u → LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 (c • u)

nodes.doc:
The scalar kernel `K.η`, coerced to a scalar L¹-class.  It is `MemLp 1` because it is
continuous (`K.smooth.continuous`) with compact support (`K.hasCompactSupport`).  This is the mass
factor `‖η‖₁` in Young's convolution inequality `‖η ⋆ g‖₂ ≤ ‖η‖₁ · ‖g‖₂`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.kernelL1R
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.MollifierKernel → ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 1 MeasureTheory.volume)

nodes.doc:
The scalar kernel `K.η`, coerced to a scalar L²-class.  It is `MemLp` because it is continuous
(`K.smooth.continuous`) with compact support (`K.hasCompactSupport`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.kernelL2R
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.MollifierKernel → ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)

nodes.doc:
**Helper 1 — the kernel's L²-translation modulus vanishes.**

For a `MollifierKernel K`, the map `h ↦ ‖translate_L2R h (kernelL2R K) − kernelL2R K‖` tends to `0`
as `h → 0` in `Domain3`.  `K.η` is smooth with compact support, hence uniformly continuous and
dominated by a fixed compactly supported `L²` envelope, so dominated convergence gives L²-continuity
of translation of the kernel.  This is the *uniform* (g-independent) modulus that drives the
equicontinuity of the mollified family.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.kernel_translate_L2_tendsto
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel),
  Filter.Tendsto (fun h => ‖LerayHopf.translate_L2R h (LerayHopf.kernelL2R K) - LerayHopf.kernelL2R K‖) (nhds 0)
    (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
**Abstract a.e.-`t` norm-lsc transfer (local copy of `Bochner.TimeSobolev`'s
`kineticEnergy_lsc_transfer`).** Inlined here because `AubinLionsLimitPassage` does not import
`Bochner.TimeSobolev` (and adding an import is a `lean-coder`-owned change). Same statement and
proof: from `L²(μ)`-convergence of an a.e.-strongly-measurable sequence `f` to an
a.e.-strongly-measurable limit `g`, with a uniform a.e. pointwise bound `‖f n t‖ ≤ M`, the limit
inherits the bound at `μ`-a.e. `t`.

The isolated measurability pillar `hg : AEStronglyMeasurable g μ` is mandatory (Lane-D
statement-gate fix; without it the statement is FALSE via a Vitali-set counterexample — see the
`Bochner.TimeSobolev` docstring). Route: L²-convergence ⇒ convergence in measure
(`tendstoInMeasure_of_tendsto_eLpNorm`, which itself requires `hg`) ⇒ a.e.-convergent subsequence
(`TendstoInMeasure.exists_seq_tendsto_ae`) ⇒ pass the bound through the a.e. limit.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.kineticEnergyLscTransfer
nodes.signature:
∀ {β : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup β] {μ : MeasureTheory.Measure ℝ} {f : ℕ → ℝ → β} {g : ℝ → β} {M : ℝ},
  (∀ (n : ℕ), MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable (f n) μ) →
    MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable g μ →
      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (fun t => f n t - g t) 2 μ) Filter.atTop (nhds 0) →
        (∀ (n : ℕ), ∀ᵐ (t : ℝ) ∂μ, ‖f n t‖ ≤ M) → ∀ᵐ (t : ℝ) ∂μ, ‖g t‖ ≤ M

nodes.doc:
Lower-semicontinuity of the kinetic energy under the (local strong) L² limit, combined
with the uniform Galerkin energy bound, gives `½‖u t‖² ≤ ½‖u₀‖²` at the limit, A.E. IN TIME.

Honesty (no-smuggle, Codex Gate-1): the conclusion is A.E.-in-`t` over `[0,T]`, NOT `∀ t`.
`AubinLionsPackage_R3` supplies LOCAL space-time integral convergence, and (as of #14-C) also
`u_aestronglyMeasurable` (time-measurability of `t ↦ u t`). The measurability field enables
the a.e.-`t` norm-lsc transfer (E1 lean-prover target, #14-P). A `∀ t` bound would still
smuggle the GOOD-REPRESENTATIVE (pointwise-in-time) content — strong time-measurability is NOT
a pointwise representative. The a.e.-in-time bound is the honest derivable form; promoting it
to `∀ t` requires the good-representative frontier (weak-time-continuity / trace), which this
package deliberately does not carry.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.kineticEnergy_lsc_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν T : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3)
  (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n)
  (alPkg : LerayHopf.AubinLionsPackage_R3 𝔊 F ν T u₀ galSeq),
  ∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T), 1 / 2 * ‖↑(alPkg.u t)‖ ^ 2 ≤ 1 / 2 * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc: **L² error in Fourier form.**
nodes.name: LerayHopf.l2_err_eq
nodes.signature:
∀ (a : ℝ) (ha : 0 < a) (wh : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ)
  (hHerm : ∀ (b : Fin 3) (v : LerayHopf.Domain3), (wh b) (-v) = (starRingEnd ℂ) ((wh b) v)),
  (∀ᵐ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), ∑ i, ↑(ξ.ofLp i) * (wh i) ξ = 0) →
    ∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
      (∀ (j : Fin 3),
          ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) w)) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] ⇑(wh j)) →
        ‖LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 (LerayHopf.potOf✝ a ha wh hHerm) - w‖ ^ 2 =
          ∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), ∑ j, (a ^ 2 / (a ^ 2 + ‖ξ‖ ^ 2)) ^ 2 * ‖(wh j) ξ‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
**Isolated analytic core of Step 4 (longitudinal ⊥ transverse).**  If `u` is weakly
divergence-free (`u ∈ L2Sigma_R3`, so `û` is transverse a.e. by the forward direction of
`mem_sigma_iff_fourier_transverse`) and `w` is orthogonal to every curl
(`w ∈ (span (range curlSchwartzL2))ᗮ`, which forces `ŵ` to be longitudinal a.e. — orthogonal
to the plane `ξ^⊥` swept out by the curl symbols, `cross_iξ_spans_transverse`), then `u ⊥ w`.

Pointwise on the Fourier side `⟪û(ξ), ŵ(ξ)⟫ = 0` (transverse ⊥ longitudinal,
`transverse_longitudinal_cancel`), and the vector Parseval bridge
`inner_L2VF_eq_integral_sum_fourier` lifts this to `⟪u, w⟫ = 0`.  Transversality of `û` is the
forward direction of `mem_sigma_iff_fourier_transverse`; longitudinality of `ŵ` is
`orthogonalCurl_longitudinal_ae`.  PROVED, `sorry`-free.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.l2sigma_inner_orthogonalCurl_eq_zero
nodes.signature: ∀ u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3, ∀ w ∈ (Submodule.span ℝ (Set.range LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2))ᗮ, inner ℝ u w = 0

nodes.doc:
**Step 4 (density transfer).**  The full deliverable, stated as the closure containment.
Given a transverse field `u ∈ L2Sigma_R3` (Step 2), the fiberwise spanning (Steps 1+3) lets us
approximate `û` in L² by curl symbols `𝓕(curl ψ)`; transferring back through Plancherel places
`u` in the L²-closure of the span of curls.

Route (refined; no "measurable selection" needed).  The clean proof is the orthogonal-complement
density criterion, all pieces of which ARE in pinned mathlib:
`Submodule.orthogonal_orthogonal_eq_closure` reduces the goal to: every `v` orthogonal to all
`curlSchwartzL2 ψ` and lying in `L2Sigma_R3` is `0`.  By Parseval (`Lp.inner_fourier_eq`) plus the
curl symbol `iξ×ψ̂` (Step 1) and the fiberwise spanning (Step 3), `v ⊥ curls` forces `v̂` a.e.
*longitudinal* (parallel to `ξ`); `v ∈ L2Sigma_R3` forces `v̂` a.e. *transverse* (Step 2 forward);
the two meet only at `v̂ = 0`, whence `v = 0` by the L² Fourier isometry `Lp.fourierTransformₗᵢ`.

Status: PROVED, `sorry`-free.  The orthogonal-complement reduction below uses
`Submodule.orthogonal_orthogonal_eq_closure` and `Submodule.mem_orthogonal'` to reduce the goal to
the analytic core `l2sigma_inner_orthogonalCurl_eq_zero` (every `w ⊥ all curls` is orthogonal to
every `u ∈ L2Sigma_R3`), which is itself proved.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.l2sigma_le_closure_span_curl
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ≤ (Submodule.span ℝ (Set.range LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2)).topologicalClosure

nodes.doc:
The full-lattice involution `(k, l) ↦ (k, -(k + l))` on the product index set, used for
the antisymmetry reindex.  Unlike the box-truncated `galerkinConvection_antisymm` (which needs
`v, w ∈ Vₙ` because `box × box` is not `σ`-invariant), the **full** lattice IS invariant, so the
reindex is an unconditional `Equiv`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.latticeInvol
nodes.signature: (Fin 3 → ℤ) × (Fin 3 → ℤ) ≃ (Fin 3 → ℤ) × (Fin 3 → ℤ)

nodes.doc:
The Leray projection Π_div : L2VF →L[ℝ] L2Sigma.

The orthogonal projection of `L²(𝕋³;ℝ³)` onto the closed divergence-free subspace
`L2Sigma = L²_σ(𝕋³)`.  Its range is exactly `L2Sigma` and its kernel is
`L2Sigma`ᗮ (the orthogonal complement of `L2Sigma` in `L2VF`).

Note: the Helmholtz decomposition identifies `L2Sigma`ᗮ with the space of L²
gradients (i.e. `{∇φ : φ ∈ H¹(𝕋³)}`), but this identification is NOT formalized
here and should be regarded as future work.

Defined as `L2Sigma.orthogonalProjectionOnto`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lerayProjection
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2VF →L[ℝ] ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma

nodes.doc:
The Leray projection Π_σ : L2VF_R3 →L[ℝ] L2Sigma_R3.

The orthogonal projection of L²(ℝ³; ℝ³) onto the closed weakly-divergence-free subspace
`L2Sigma_R3 = L²_σ(ℝ³)`.  Its range is exactly `L2Sigma_R3` and its kernel is
`L2Sigma_R3`ᗮ (the space of L² gradients, by the Helmholtz decomposition — not
formalized here).

Defined as `L2Sigma_R3.orthogonalProjectionOnto`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lerayProjection_R3
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 →L[ℝ] ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3

nodes.doc:
The Leray projection fixes divergence-free fields: if `u ∈ L²_σ` then
`L2Sigma.starProjection u = u`. Uses `Submodule.starProjection_eq_self_iff`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lerayProjection_fixes_divFree
nodes.signature: ∀ u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma, LerayHopf.L2Sigma.starProjection u = u

nodes.doc:
The Leray projection (as L2VF → L2VF map) is idempotent: applying it twice
equals applying it once. Uses `Submodule.isIdempotentElem_starProjection`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lerayProjection_idempotent
nodes.signature: IsIdempotentElem LerayHopf.L2Sigma.starProjection

nodes.doc:
The Leray projection is self-adjoint (symmetric as a linear map on L2VF):
`inner (𝕜 := ℝ) (L2Sigma.starProjection u) v = inner (𝕜 := ℝ) u (L2Sigma.starProjection v)`.
Uses `Submodule.inner_starProjection_left_eq_right`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lerayProjection_isSymmetric
nodes.signature:
∀ (u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  inner ℝ (LerayHopf.L2Sigma.starProjection u) v = inner ℝ u (LerayHopf.L2Sigma.starProjection v)

nodes.doc:
The Leray projection has operator norm ≤ 1: `‖L2Sigma.starProjection‖ ≤ 1`.
Uses `Submodule.starProjection_norm_le`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lerayProjection_norm_le
nodes.signature: ‖LerayHopf.L2Sigma.starProjection‖ ≤ 1

nodes.doc:
The range of the Leray projection (as the L2VF → L2VF map) equals L²_σ.
Uses `Submodule.range_starProjection`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lerayProjection_range
nodes.signature: (↑LerayHopf.L2Sigma.starProjection).range = LerayHopf.L2Sigma

nodes.doc:
**A.e. finiteness of the approximant-`liminf` viscous form.** From `reg_bound`
(`∫₀ᵀ viscousFormSq_R3 ν (uₙ) ≤ ½‖u₀‖²`, `n`-uniform) and Fatou-in-time, the `liminf` of the
approximants' viscous forms is finite at a.e. `t`.  No `H¹`/measurability of the LIMIT is used —
only the continuous (hence measurable) approximant curves.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.liminf_viscousFormSq_lt_top_ae
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n)
          (alPkg : LerayHopf.AubinLionsPackage_R3 𝔊 F ν T u₀ galSeq),
          ∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T),
            Filter.liminf (fun n => ENNReal.ofReal (LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 ↑((galSeq (alPkg.φ n)).u t)))
                Filter.atTop <
              ⊤

nodes.doc:
Complexification commutes with the line derivative: `∂_{m}(cxify φ) = cxify (∂_{m} φ)`.
Both sides agree pointwise: `lineDeriv` commutes with the `ℝ`-linear map `ofRealCLM`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lineDerivOp_cxify
nodes.signature:
∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3) (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  (LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) m) (LerayHopf.cxify✝ φ) =
    LerayHopf.cxify✝ ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) m) φ)

nodes.doc: Classical Leibniz rule for the directional derivative of a Schwartz product.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lineDerivOp_prodS
nodes.signature:
∀ (f g : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (v x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) v) (LerayHopf.prodS✝ f g)) x =
    ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) v) f) x * g x +
      f x * ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) v) g) x

nodes.doc: The directional derivative of the real part is the real part of the directional derivative.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lineDerivOp_reS
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (m x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) m) (LerayHopf.reS✝ φ)) x =
    (((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) m) φ) x).re

nodes.doc: Line derivative commutes with complexification: `∂_m (schwartzC f) = schwartzC (∂_m f)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lineDerivOp_schwartzC
nodes.signature:
∀ (f : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (m : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  LineDeriv.lineDerivOp m (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ f) = LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ (LineDeriv.lineDerivOp m f)

nodes.doc: Leibniz rule for the directional derivative `∂_{v}` on a Schwartz product.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lineDerivOp_schwartzMul
nodes.signature:
∀ (f g : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (v x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) v) (LerayHopf.schwartzMul✝ f g)) x =
    ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) v) f) x * g x +
      f x * ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) v) g) x

nodes.doc: A bounded multiplier `∂ₐψwᵢ` is `MemLp ⊤` (it is a Schwartz function, hence `L^∞`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lineDerivOp_schwartz_memLp_top
nodes.signature:
∀ (ψ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (a : Fin 3),
  MeasureTheory.MemLp
    (fun x => ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) ψ) x) ⊤
    MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
Line-derivative commutes with the complexification `postcompCLM ofRealCLM`:
`(∂_m (ψi ∘ ↑)) x = ↑((∂_m ψi) x)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lineDeriv_postcomp_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (ψi : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (m x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  (LineDeriv.lineDerivOp m ((SchwartzMap.postcompCLM Complex.ofRealCLM) ψi)) x = ↑((LineDeriv.lineDerivOp m ψi) x)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.lineDeriv_toLp_norm_le_sqrt_viscous_energy
nodes.signature:
∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) v = (ψ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
    ∀ (i a : Fin 3),
      ‖((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) (ψ i)).toLp 2
            MeasureTheory.volume‖ ≤
        √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 v)

nodes.doc:
**LOCAL spatial compactness on ℝ³, from the isolated local-Rellich input.**

Reproduces the exact conclusion of `spatial_compactness_R3` (AxiomaticClosure.lean:378–389)
axiom-free, conditional only on `LocalRellichInput` (the unconditional local compact
embedding H¹(B_R) ↪↪ L²(B_R), which mathlib lacks).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.localCompactness_R3_of_ballCompact
nodes.signature:
LerayHopf.LocalRellichInput →
  ∀ (M : ℝ) (z : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
    (∀ (n : ℕ), z n ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) →
      (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (z n)) →
        (∀ (n : ℕ), ‖z n‖ ≤ M) →
          (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 (z n) ≤ M ^ 2) →
            ∃ ψ g,
              StrictMono ψ ∧
                g ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ∧
                  ∀ (R : ℝ),
                    Filter.Tendsto
                      (fun n => ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3) in Metric.closedBall 0 R, ‖↑↑(z (ψ n)) x - ↑↑g x‖ ^ 2)
                      Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
**T2 (DELIVERABLE).  LOCAL Rellich input on ℝ³, from the abstract Fréchet–Kolmogorov
criterion.**

Constructs a `LocalRellichInput` (per-ball precompactness of the H¹-bounded div-free family)
from `FrechetKolmogorovInput`.  The Navier–Stokes-specific content (gradient bound ⇒ uniform
translation modulus, via Plancherel on `viscousFormSq_R3`) is ASSEMBLED axiom-free here, and
its Plancherel core T0b is fully proved, so this deliverable is `sorry`-free; only the
domain-agnostic FK precompactness criterion is assumed as a hypothesis.  The conclusion
reproduces `LocalRellichInput` verbatim (no weakening; Hard rule 3).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.localRellichInput_of_frechetKolmogorov
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.FrechetKolmogorovInput → LerayHopf.LocalRellichInput

nodes.doc:
**Local integrability of the weighted Fourier transform.** For `g : L2C_R3`, the pointwise
product `ξ ↦ besselWeightC ξ • g ξ` (an unbounded-multiplier product) is locally integrable:
on each ball the continuous weight is bounded and `g ∈ L²` is integrable.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.locallyIntegrable_besselWeight_smul
nodes.signature:
∀ (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3),
  MeasureTheory.LocallyIntegrable (fun ξ => LerayHopf.besselWeightC✝ ξ • ↑↑g ξ) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
The pointwise product `ξ ↦ besselWeightC_R ξ • g ξ` (unbounded multiplier) is locally
integrable: on each ball the continuous weight is bounded and `g ∈ L²` is integrable.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.locallyIntegrable_besselWeight_smul_R
nodes.signature:
∀ (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3),
  MeasureTheory.LocallyIntegrable (fun ξ => LerayHopf.besselWeightC_R✝ ξ • ↑↑g ξ) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
The transverse defect `T_u` is locally integrable: each `û_j ∈ L²` is integrable on every
compact set, the weight `ξ ↦ ξ_j` is continuous, and the finite sum stays locally integrable.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.locallyIntegrable_transverseDefect
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), MeasureTheory.LocallyIntegrable (LerayHopf.transverseDefect✝ u) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.name: LerayHopf.locallyIntegrable_wPosC_smul
nodes.signature: ∀ (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3), MeasureTheory.LocallyIntegrable (fun ξ => LerayHopf.wPosC✝ ξ • ↑↑g ξ) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
From a local-lifespan lower bound `C / N(t)² ≤ T - t` (with `C > 0` and `N` positive
on `(-∞, T)`), the blow-up quantity satisfies the lower bound `N(t) ≥ √(C/(T-t))`,
equivalently `1 / N(t) ≤ √((T-t)/C)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.lower_bound_from_inverse_square_lifespan

nodes.doc:
The k-th Fourier coefficient of a ℂ-valued L²(𝕋³) function, as the `mFourierBasis`
representation coefficient.

`mFourierCoeff3 f k = torus3_mFourierBasis.repr f k`, where `repr` is the isometric
isomorphism from `L2C` to `ℓ²(ℤ³, ℂ)` given by the Hilbert basis.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2C → (Fin 3 → ℤ) → ℂ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3_add
nodes.signature:
∀ (f g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ),
  LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 (f + g) k = LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f k + LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 g k

nodes.doc: Conjugation reflects Fourier coefficients: `(conj f)^(k) = conj (f^(-k))`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3_conjL2C
nodes.signature:
∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ),
  LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 (LerayHopf.conjL2C✝ f) k = (starRingEnd ℂ) (LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f (-k))

nodes.doc:
The Fourier coefficient as a concrete integral against `haarTorus3`
(definitional transport of `UnitAddTorus.mFourierBasis_repr`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3_integral
nodes.signature:
∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ),
  LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f k = ∫ (t : LerayHopf.Torus3), (UnitAddTorus.mFourier (-k)) t • ↑↑f t ∂LerayHopf.haarTorus3

nodes.doc: Helper: the norm of a Fourier coefficient of `f + g` is bounded by the sum of norms.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3_norm_add_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (f g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ),
  ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 (f + g) k‖ ≤ ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f k‖ + ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 g k‖

nodes.doc: Helper: `‖mFourierCoeff3 (c • f) k‖ = |c| * ‖mFourierCoeff3 f k‖`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3_norm_smul
nodes.signature:
∀ (c : ℝ) (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ),
  ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 (c • f) k‖ = |c| * ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f k‖

nodes.doc: Additivity: `û_j(u + u', k) = û_j(u, k) + û_j(u', k)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3_projComponentC_add
nodes.signature:
∀ (j : Fin 3) (u u' : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ),
  LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) (u + u')) k =
    LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) u) k +
      LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) u') k

nodes.doc: ℝ-homogeneity: `û_j(c • u, k) = (c : ℂ) * û_j(u, k)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3_projComponentC_smul
nodes.signature:
∀ (j : Fin 3) (c : ℝ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ),
  LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) (c • u)) k =
    ↑c * LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) u) k

nodes.doc:
Helper: `mFourierCoeff3 (c • f) k = (c : ℂ) * mFourierCoeff3 f k`.

Follows the same `rw` chain as `TorusConvectionForm.lean` (lines 88–93).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3_smul_eq
nodes.signature: ∀ (c : ℝ) (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ), LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 (c • f) k = ↑c * LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f k

nodes.doc:
**Brick-1 helper.** The bounded Fourier multiplier `mLD m ξ := 2π·⟨ξ,m⟩·wInv ξ` for the
weak directional derivative in direction `m`. Its modulus is bounded by `2π‖m‖`
(`|2π⟨ξ,m⟩|·wInv ξ ≤ 2π‖m‖` since `|⟨ξ,m⟩| ≤ ‖ξ‖‖m‖` and `‖ξ‖·wInv ξ ≤ 1`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mLD
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.Domain3 → LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ

nodes.doc:
A velocity field `u ∈ L2VF` lies in `H¹_σ`: it is divergence-free (`u ∈ L2Sigma`)
and has finite H¹ energy component-wise (`memH1VF u`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1Sigma
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → Prop

nodes.doc:
Membership in H¹(𝕋³; ℂ): a function `f ∈ L²(𝕋³; ℂ)` lies in the order-1 Sobolev space
iff its Fourier coefficients are square-summable against the weight `1 + ‖k‖²`:
`∑_k (1 + ∑_i (k_i)²) · ‖f̂(k)‖² < ∞`.

Scaffold (M2): mathlib has no torus Sobolev API (`TemperedDistribution.memSobolev` is ℝⁿ only),
so this is built from the Fourier characterization. The H¹-inner-product Hilbert structure and
the compact embedding H¹ ↪ L² (Rellich) are deferred to M5–M6.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1Torus
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2C → Prop

nodes.doc: The zero function is in H¹(𝕋³; ℂ), confirming the predicate is non-vacuous.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1Torus_zero
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.memH1Torus 0

nodes.doc:
A velocity field `u ∈ L2VF` has finite H¹ energy component-wise:
each scalar component `L2VF_projComponentC j u` lies in `H¹(𝕋³; ℂ)` (i.e., the
H¹-weight Fourier sum is summable).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → Prop

nodes.doc:
A velocity field `u ∈ L²(ℝ³; ℝ³)` has **H¹ regularity** if each complex-valued component
`L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j u : L2C_R3 = Lp ℂ 2 volume` lies in the Sobolev space `H^{1,2}(ℝ³; ℂ)`.

We use the **complex-valued** component projection `L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j` (rather than the
real-valued `L2VF_projComponent_R3 j`) because:
- `L2C_R3 = Lp ℂ 2 volume` carries a `CoeDep` instance to `𝓢'(Domain3, ℂ)`;
- `MemSobolev` requires `[NormedSpace ℂ F]`, which holds for `F = ℂ` but requires an extra
  restriction-of-scalars instance for `F = ℝ`.

The coercion `(f : 𝓢'(Domain3, ℂ))` for `f : L2C_R3` is via `Lp.instCoeDep`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 → Prop

nodes.doc:
**B1a.** If `u, v ∈ L2VF_R3` both satisfy `memH1VF_R3`, then so does `u + v`.

Proof: `L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j` is ℝ-linear, so the j-th component of `u + v` equals
the sum of the j-th components of `u` and `v`. Then `MemSobolev.add` closes the goal.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3_add
nodes.signature: ∀ {u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3}, LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (u + v)

nodes.doc: Every curl field `curlSchwartzL2 θ` is `H¹` (its components are Schwartz, A3).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3_curl
nodes.signature: ∀ (θ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ), LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2 θ)

nodes.doc:
A field with Schwartz component representatives is `H¹`.  Local copy of the private
`memH1VF_R3_of_schwartz`/`memH1VF_R3_of_isSchwartzDivFree`, stated on the raw component
witness (avoids the `L2Sigma_R3` subtype).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3_of_components
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  (∃ φ, ∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) u = (φ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
    LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u

nodes.doc:
**A4 helper.** Every `IsSchwartzDivFree_R3` field has `H¹` regularity (`memH1VF_R3`).

Each real component is `(ψ j).toLp 2 volume` for a Schwartz `ψ j : 𝓢(Domain3, ℝ)`; the
complex component projection `L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j` then equals `toLp` of the postcomposed
Schwartz function `(ψ j).postcompCLM RCLike.ofRealCLM : 𝓢(Domain3, ℂ)`, and
`memSobolev_of_eq_schwartz_toLp` finishes.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3_of_isSchwartzDivFree
nodes.signature: ∀ {v : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3}, LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 v → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 ↑v

nodes.doc:
H¹ regularity of any level-`n` state (its range is Schwartz).  Local copy of
`galerkinCurve_reg_mem` (`GalerkinODE.lean`, DOWNSTREAM of this file's imports); proof route
identical (B10-style Schwartz reps + `SchwartzMap.memSobolev`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3_of_projFixed
nodes.signature: ∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (n : ℕ) (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), v = (𝔊.P n) v → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v

nodes.doc:
**B1b.** If `u ∈ L2VF_R3` satisfies `memH1VF_R3 u`, then so does `c • u` for any `c : ℝ`.

Proof: `L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j (c • u) = c • L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j u` (ℝ-linearity).
The Lp coercion `(c • f : Lp ℂ 2 μ) = (c : ℂ) • f` as tempered distributions
(since `c` acts via real scalar multiplication, which equals complex multiplication by `c`).
Then `MemSobolev.smul (c : ℂ)` closes the goal.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3_smul
nodes.signature: ∀ {u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3} (c : ℝ), LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (c • u)

nodes.doc: `H¹` is closed under subtraction (via `memH1VF_R3_add` + `memH1VF_R3_smul (-1)`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3_sub
nodes.signature: ∀ {u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3}, LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (u - v)

nodes.doc:
`memH1VF_R3` is closed under subtraction (add + `(-1)`-smul; public mirror of the
`private` `GalerkinBasisH1.memH1VF_R3_sub`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3_sub_local
nodes.signature: ∀ {u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3}, LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (u - v)

nodes.doc:
**B1c.** `memH1VF_R3 (0 : L2VF_R3)`.

Proof: the j-th component of `0` is `0`, and `memSobolev_fun_zero` gives `MemSobolev 1 2 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3_zero
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 0

nodes.doc:
`memH1VF` is closed under addition: if `u` and `v` are H¹, so is `u + v`.

Key: `‖(f̂+ĝ)(k)‖² ≤ 2(‖f̂(k)‖² + ‖ĝ(k)‖²)` (parallelogram / Cauchy–Schwarz), so
the weighted sum for `u + v` is dominated term-wise by the H¹ sums for `u` and `v`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_add
nodes.signature: ∀ {u v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF}, LerayHopf.memH1VF u → LerayHopf.memH1VF v → LerayHopf.memH1VF (u + v)

nodes.doc: A Galerkin test is H¹: finite Fourier support ⟹ every Sobolev norm finite.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_of_galerkinTest
nodes.signature: ∀ {w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma}, LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w → LerayHopf.memH1VF ↑w

nodes.doc: If `viscousEnn ν u < ⊤` (and `ν > 0`), then `u ∈ H¹` component-wise.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_of_viscousEnn_lt_top
nodes.signature: ∀ (ν : ℝ), 0 < ν → ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), LerayHopf.viscousEnn ν u < ⊤ → LerayHopf.memH1VF u

nodes.doc:
`memH1VF` is closed under real scalar multiplication: if `u` is H¹ and `c : ℝ`,
then `c • u` is H¹.

Key: `‖mFourierCoeff3 (c • f) k‖ = |c| · ‖mFourierCoeff3 f k‖` (by linearity + norm_smul),
so the weighted sum for `c • u` equals `c²` times the weighted sum for `u`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_smul
nodes.signature: ∀ (c : ℝ) {u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF}, LerayHopf.memH1VF u → LerayHopf.memH1VF (c • u)

nodes.doc:
The zero velocity field satisfies `memH1VF`: each component is 0, so the
weighted Fourier sum is identically 0 (summable).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1VF_zero
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.memH1VF 0

nodes.doc:
**Helper for `RellichBall.integrable_viscous_integrand_of_memH1`.**

For `w ∈ H¹(ℝ³)` (i.e. `memH1VF_R3 w`, defined as `TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2` on each
complex component `cⱼ = L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j w`), the L²-Fourier transform `𝓕 cⱼ` is
square-integrable against the genuine `H¹` weight `1 + ‖ξ‖²`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1_weightedL2_integrable
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w →
    ∀ (j : Fin 3),
      MeasureTheory.Integrable
        (fun ξ => (1 + ‖ξ‖ ^ 2) * ‖↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) w)) ξ‖ ^ 2)
        MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
**Local H¹ ⇒ Bessel-weighted-L² integrability** (self-contained replica of
`FrechetKolmogorov.memH1_weightedL2_integrable`, which cannot be imported here).  For
`w ∈ H¹(ℝ³)`, the L²-Fourier transform `𝓕 cⱼ` is square-integrable against the genuine `H¹`
weight `1 + ‖ξ‖²`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memH1_weightedL2_integrable_R
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w →
    ∀ (j : Fin 3),
      MeasureTheory.Integrable
        (fun ξ => (1 + ‖ξ‖ ^ 2) * ‖↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) w)) ξ‖ ^ 2)
        MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: `mollifyRep K g`, as an a.e. function, lies in `L²`: it agrees a.e. with `convL2 K g`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memLp_mollifyRep
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  MeasureTheory.MemLp (LerayHopf.mollifyRep K g) 2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
`mulBdd m hm hC g` is the `L²`-class of `ξ ↦ (m ξ : ℂ) • g ξ`, for a bounded
a.e.-strongly-measurable real multiplier `m` (bound `C`).  Well-defined because the bounded
multiplier keeps `g ∈ L²` inside `L²` (Hölder `∞ · 2 ⊆ 2`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memLp_mulBdd
nodes.signature:
∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ),
  MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume →
    ∀ (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3), MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ) • ↑↑g ξ) 2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: The real part of the j-th complex component of `u ∈ H¹_σ` lies in `L⁶` (via A3 / GNS).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memLp_six_componentRe
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u →
    ∀ (j : Fin 3),
      MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => (↑↑((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) u) x).re) 6 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
For `w ∈ H¹`, the weighted Fourier component `√W • 𝓕(projⱼ w)` lies in `L²`: its squared norm
is the (finite) viscous spectral integrand.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memLp_sqrtWeight_smul_fourier
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w →
    ∀ (j : Fin 3),
      MeasureTheory.MemLp
        (fun ξ =>
          ↑(LerayHopf.sqrtViscousWeight ξ) • ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) w)) ξ)
        2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: The real part of the j-th complex component of `u ∈ H¹_σ` lies in `L³` (interpolation).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memLp_three_componentRe
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u →
    ∀ (j : Fin 3),
      MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => (↑↑((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) u) x).re) 3 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: The complexified line-derivative multiplier is bounded in `L^∞`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memLp_top_ofReal_mLD
nodes.signature: ∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3), MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(LerayHopf.mLD✝ m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.name: LerayHopf.memLp_top_ofReal_wInv
nodes.signature: MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(LerayHopf.wInv✝ ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: The ℂ-coerced truncated weight is in `L^∞`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memLp_top_sqrtViscousWeightTrunc
nodes.signature: ∀ (k : ℕ), MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(LerayHopf.sqrtViscousWeightTrunc k ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: The real part of the j-th complex component of `u ∈ L²` lies in `L²`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memLp_two_componentRe
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (j : Fin 3),
  MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => (↑↑((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) u) x).re) 2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: The real part of the weak gradient component `gradComp_of_memH1` lies in `L²`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memLp_two_gradRe
nodes.signature:
∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hv : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 v) (a i : Fin 3),
  MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun x => (↑↑(LerayHopf.gradComp_of_memH1 v hv a i) x).re) 2 MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc:
**A4 helper.** If a complex `L²`-class `g : L2C_R3` is the `toLp` of a Schwartz function
`φ : 𝓢(Domain3, ℂ)`, then it lies in the Sobolev space `H^{1,2}` as a tempered distribution.

This is the "Schwartz ⊂ H¹" bridge: `((φ.toLp 2 volume) : 𝓢') = (φ : 𝓢')` by
`Lp.toTemperedDistribution_toLp_eq`, and every Schwartz function is in every Sobolev space by
`SchwartzMap.memSobolev`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memSobolev_of_eq_schwartz_toLp
nodes.signature:
∀ (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3) (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  g = φ.toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume → TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution g)

nodes.doc:
**H¹ membership from finite weighted-Fourier integral.**
If for each component `j : Fin 3` the lower Lebesgue integral
`∫⁻ ξ, (2π)²‖ξ‖² ‖𝓕(projⱼ w) ξ‖² dξ` is finite, then `w` lies in `H¹(ℝ³; ℝ³)`,
i.e. `memH1VF_R3 w`.

**Proof plan (for prover pass):**
For each `j`, finiteness of the lintegral gives `MemLp (fun ξ => wPosC ξ • (𝓕(projⱼ w) ξ)) 2`:
- `‖wPosC ξ‖² · ‖𝓕(projⱼ w) ξ‖² = (1+‖ξ‖²) · ‖𝓕(projⱼ w) ξ‖²`
  `≤ ‖𝓕(projⱼ w) ξ‖² + (1/(2π)²) · (2π)²‖ξ‖²‖𝓕(projⱼ w) ξ‖²`,
  so `∫⁻ ‖wPosC ξ • (𝓕(projⱼ w) ξ)‖² < ∞` by the hypothesis plus `𝓕(projⱼ w) ∈ L²`.
- `MemLp ... 2` gives via `ae_eq_of_integral_contDiff_smul_eq` + `locallyIntegrable_wPosC_smul`
  that `smulLeftCLM ℂ wPosC (𝓕 (projⱼ w)) = f'` for some `f' ∈ L²` (the `wPosC_eq_smulLeftCLM_weight`
  reconciliation as in `gns_L6_of_memH1_R3`).
- Apply `memSobolev_iff_exists_smulLeftCLM_fourier.mpr` to conclude `MemSobolev 1 2 (projⱼ w)`.
- Universally quantify over `j` to obtain `memH1VF_R3 w`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.memSobolev_of_finite_weightedFourier_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  (∀ (j : Fin 3),
      ∫⁻ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
          ENNReal.ofReal
            ((2 * Real.pi) ^ 2 * ‖ξ‖ ^ 2 *
              ‖↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) w)) ξ‖ ^ 2) <
        ⊤) →
    LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w

nodes.doc:
Membership characterisation for `H1SigmaTorus`:
`u ∈ H1SigmaTorus ↔ u ∈ L2Sigma ∧ memH1VF (u : L2VF)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mem_H1SigmaTorus_iff
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma), u ∈ LerayHopf.H1SigmaTorus ↔ LerayHopf.memH1Sigma ↑u

nodes.doc: Membership in `L2Sigma` is equivalent to `DivFreeL2`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mem_L2Sigma_iff
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma ↔ LerayHopf.DivFreeL2 u

nodes.doc:
Membership in `L2Sigma_R3` is exactly: every Schwartz weak-divergence test integral
vanishes.  Unfolds `L2Sigma_R3 = ⨅ φ, ker (divTestFunctional φ)` and feeds in the Fourier
form `divTestFunctional_eq_fourier_integral`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mem_sigma_iff_fourier_integral_zero
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ↔
    ∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
      ∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
          (starRingEnd ℂ) (2 * ↑Real.pi * Complex.I * (FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ φ)) ξ) *
            LerayHopf.transverseDefect✝ u ξ =
        0

nodes.doc:
**Step 2 (spectral div-free characterization).**  A field `u ∈ L2VF_R3` is weakly
divergence-free (`u ∈ L2Sigma_R3`) iff its Fourier transform is a.e. transverse:
`∑ j, ξ_j û_j(ξ) = 0` for a.e. `ξ`.

This is the Fourier transcription of `div u = 0`: pairing with `∂_j φ` and applying Plancherel
turns the weak-divergence functional `divTestFunctional` into the pairing of `∑_j ξ_j û_j(ξ)`
against the (dense set of) Schwartz `φ̂`.

Blocker: requires Plancherel for the `divTestFunctional` pairing plus the
fundamental lemma of the calculus of variations on the Fourier side (a transverse symbol that
pairs to zero against all Schwartz `φ̂` vanishes a.e.).  Genuine analysis; lean-prover target
on `FourierL2` (`normSq_eq_integral_normSq_C`, `fourierComponentC_ae_schwartz`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mem_sigma_iff_fourier_transverse
nodes.signature:
∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ↔
    (fun ξ =>
        LerayHopf.IsTransverseAt (fun j => ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) u)) ξ)
          ξ) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume]
      fun x => True

nodes.doc:
Embedding helper: an element of `V_n := galerkinSpan B n`, viewed as an `L2VF_R3`
vector, is divergence-free, i.e. lies in `L2Sigma_R3`.  Used to coerce `V_n` elements into
the domain `L2Sigma_R3` of the convection form `F.b`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mem_sigma_of_mem_galerkinSpan
nodes.signature: ∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (n : ℕ) (v : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)), ↑v ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3

nodes.doc: The `j`-th component coercion is the same data; helper to keep the `L2Sigma` membership.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mem_sigma_of_mem_velocitySpan
nodes.signature: ∀ (n : ℕ) (v : ↥(LerayHopf.velocitySpan n)), ↑v ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma

nodes.doc:
**Reverse direction (a.e. transverse ⇒ weakly divergence-free).**  If the transverse
defect `T_u` vanishes a.e., then every Schwartz weak-divergence test integral vanishes, so
`u ∈ L2Sigma_R3`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mem_sigma_of_transverse_ae
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), (∀ᵐ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), LerayHopf.transverseDefect✝ u ξ = 0) → u ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3

nodes.doc: Membership in `velocitySpan n`: is the projection of some divergence-free field.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mem_velocitySpan_iff
nodes.signature:
∀ (n : ℕ) (x : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  x ∈ LerayHopf.velocitySpan n ↔ ∃ y ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma, (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) y = x

nodes.doc:
**Bridge (fixed points ⊆ range).** Any divergence-free `x` fixed by the projector lies in
`velocitySpan n`.  The `L2Sigma` hypothesis is exactly satisfied by the Galerkin test functions
in `u_ode` (typed `w : L2Sigma` with `velocityProjection_n n w = w`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mem_velocitySpan_of_fixed
nodes.signature: ∀ (n : ℕ), ∀ x ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma, (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n) x = x → x ∈ LerayHopf.velocitySpan n

nodes.doc:
**FK step 2 (equicontinuity).**  The CONCRETE mollified family is uniformly equicontinuous on
its explicit representative.

For a fixed smooth compactly supported kernel `K`, the modulus of continuity of `η ⋆ g` is
controlled by `‖g‖₂` times the (kernel-only) modulus of `η`
(`‖(η ⋆ g)(x) − (η ⋆ g)(y)‖ ≤ ‖g‖₂ · ‖τ_{x−y} η − η‖₂`), uniformly over the L²-bounded family —
because `K.η`, being smooth with compact support, is uniformly continuous with an L²-modulus
independent of `g`.  Stated for `mollifyRep K (rep f)`, NOT for an arbitrary operator.

**Norm-correctness (Codex Gate round 3 → resolved round 4).**  Restricting attention to
`x, y ∈ B_R` (the region the ball-restriction keeps) and bounding the difference quotient by the
kernel modulus localizes the mass factor `‖g‖₂` to the kernel-reach `B_{R + K.supportRadius}`:
the relevant inequality is
`‖(η⋆g)(x)−(η⋆g)(y)‖ ≤ ‖restrictToBall (R+K.supportRadius) g‖ · ‖τ_{x−y}K.η − K.η‖₂` for
`x, y ∈ B_R`.  Hence the local hypothesis is the ENLARGED-BALL bound `hbdEnl` on `B_{R+r}`, which
the call site supplies from `FrechetKolmogorovInput`'s GLOBAL bound `hbddGlobal : ‖rep f‖ ≤ C`
by ball-mass monotonicity (no upstream gap).  The equicontinuity conclusion is stated for
`x, y ∈ B_R` only.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mollified_family_equicontinuous
nodes.signature:
∀ (R C : ℝ) (S : Set ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R)) (rep : ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R) → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3)
  (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel),
  (∀ f ∈ S, ‖LerayHopf.restrictToBall (R + K.supportRadius) (rep f)‖ ≤ C) →
    ∀ ε > 0,
      ∃ δ > 0,
        ∀ f ∈ S,
          ∀ (x y : LerayHopf.Domain3),
            x ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R →
              y ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R →
                ‖x - y‖ < δ → ‖LerayHopf.mollifyRep K (rep f) x - LerayHopf.mollifyRep K (rep f) y‖ < ε

nodes.doc:
**FK step 3.**  Arzelà–Ascoli: a uniformly bounded, uniformly equicontinuous family of
continuous functions on the compact ball `B_R` is totally bounded in `C(B_R)`, hence (via the
continuous embedding `C(B_R) ↪ L²(B_R)` on a finite-measure ball) totally bounded in `L²(B_R)`.

Consumes `mollified_family_uniformly_bounded` (step 2a) and `mollified_family_equicontinuous`
(step 2b) for the CONCRETE kernel `K`; produces the total boundedness used by
`convolution_l2_tendsto_uniform`.  The L²-classes `ρf f` whose representative is
`mollifyRep K (rep f)` are supplied (with the a.e. agreement hypothesis `hρf`) so the result
lands in `L²(B_R)` rather than `C(B_R)`.

**Norm-correctness (Codex Gate round 3 → resolved round 4).**  Both steps 2a/2b it consumes are
stated on `B_R` only and rest on the ENLARGED-BALL bound
`‖restrictToBall (R+K.supportRadius) (rep f)‖ ≤ C`, which the call site derives from
`FrechetKolmogorovInput`'s GLOBAL bound `hbddGlobal : ‖rep f‖ ≤ C` by ball-mass monotonicity.
Arzelà–Ascoli runs on the COMPACT ball `B_R`, where
the ball-restriction `restrictToBall R (ρf f)` lives, so the enlarged-ball sup/equicontinuity bounds
on `B_R` are exactly enough.  This lemma threads the same enlarged-ball bound `hbdEnl`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mollified_family_totallyBounded_L2
nodes.signature:
∀ (R C : ℝ) (S : Set ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R)) (rep : ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R) → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3)
  (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (ρf : ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R) → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  (∀ f ∈ S, ↑↑(ρf f) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] LerayHopf.mollifyRep K (rep f)) →
    (∀ f ∈ S, ‖LerayHopf.restrictToBall (R + K.supportRadius) (rep f)‖ ≤ C) →
      TotallyBounded ((fun f => LerayHopf.restrictToBall R (ρf f)) '' S)

nodes.doc:
**FK step 2 (equiboundedness).**  The CONCRETE mollified family has a uniform sup bound on
its explicit representative.

For a fixed smooth compactly supported kernel `K`, convolving an L²-bounded family with `K.η`
gives a family with a common `L^∞`-bound via Young's inequality
`‖η ⋆ g‖_∞ ≤ ‖η‖₂ · ‖g‖₂` (the kernel is in `L²` because it is continuous with compact support).
The bound is stated for the genuine representative `mollifyRep K (rep f)`, NOT for the pointwise
evaluation of an arbitrary `Lp`-class.

**Norm-correctness (Codex Gate round 3 → resolved round 4).**  Young's inequality bounds
`‖η ⋆ g‖_∞` by `‖η‖₂‖g‖₂`.  The OUTPUT bound is needed only on `x ∈ B_R` (the region the
ball-restriction of `mollifyRep` keeps), and there `mollifyRep K (rep f) x = ∫ y, η(x−y)·rep f(y)`
only sees `rep f` over the kernel-reach `B_{R + K.supportRadius}` (since `η` is supported in
`closedBall 0 K.supportRadius`).  Hence the local hypothesis is the ENLARGED-BALL bound
`hbdEnl : ∀ f ∈ S, ‖restrictToBall (R + K.supportRadius) (rep f)‖ ≤ C`
on the kernel-support enlargement `B_{R+r}` — exactly what Young needs on `B_R`.  This bound is
supplied at the call site directly from `FrechetKolmogorovInput`'s GLOBAL bound
`hbddGlobal : ‖rep f‖ ≤ C` by ball-mass monotonicity (`‖restrictToBall (R+r) g‖ ≤ ‖g‖`), so
there is no longer any upstream gap: the criterion's own strengthened data furnishes it.  The
output bound is stated for `x ∈ B_R` only.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mollified_family_uniformly_bounded
nodes.signature:
∀ (R C : ℝ) (S : Set ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R)) (rep : ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R) → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3)
  (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel),
  (∀ f ∈ S, ‖LerayHopf.restrictToBall (R + K.supportRadius) (rep f)‖ ≤ C) →
    ∃ B, ∀ f ∈ S, ∀ x ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R, ‖LerayHopf.mollifyRep K (rep f) x‖ ≤ B

nodes.doc:
**Explicit continuous representative of the mollified field.**

`mollifyRep K f x = ∫ y, K.η (x − y) • (f y : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)) ∂volume` is the
genuine pointwise-defined vector-valued convolution `η ⋆ f`.  Because `K.η` is smooth and
compactly supported, this integral converges for every `x` and defines a smooth (in particular
continuous) function `Domain3 → EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)` — a *faithful* representative of the
mollified L²-class, on which pointwise sup and equicontinuity bounds are legitimate.

This is the concrete operator the family lemmas below are stated for, replacing the unsound
arbitrary `ρ : L2VF_R3 → L2VF_R3`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mollifyRep
nodes.signature: LerayHopf.MollifierKernel → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 → LerayHopf.Domain3 → EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)

nodes.doc:
Global Cauchy–Schwarz / Young sup bound for the pointwise convolution, valid at EVERY `x`
(no ball localization): `‖mollifyRep K g x‖ ≤ ‖kernelL2R K‖ · ‖g‖`.  Same Cauchy–Schwarz as
`mollifyRep_sup_le` but with the trivial enlarged ball `B = univ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mollifyRep_apply_bound
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  ‖LerayHopf.mollifyRep K g x‖ ≤ ‖LerayHopf.kernelL2R K‖ * ‖g‖

nodes.doc:
**Shared primitive.**  The `L²`-class of `mollifyRep K g` equals the `Lp`-valued Bochner
integral `convL2 K g = ∫ h, K.η h • τ_h g`.  Proved (no sorry) from the isolated coeFn identity
`convL2_coeFn_ae` by `Lp.ext`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mollifyRep_eq_lp_integral
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3)
  (hmem : MeasureTheory.MemLp (LerayHopf.mollifyRep K g) 2 MeasureTheory.volume),
  MeasureTheory.MemLp.toLp (LerayHopf.mollifyRep K g) hmem =
    ∫ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3), K.η h • LerayHopf.translate_L2VF h g

nodes.doc:
**RHS scalar reduction (PROVED).**  For any constant `c` and finite-measure measurable `s`,
the inner product of `c` with the set-integral of the pointwise convolution `mollifyRep K g`
over `s` equals the iterated scalar integral
`∫ x in s, ∫ h, K.η h · ⟪c, g (x + h)⟫`.  Uses `integral_inner` on the inner `∫ y` integral,
the translation substitution `y ↦ x + h`, and `K.even`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mollifyRep_setIntegral_inner
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) {s : Set LerayHopf.Domain3},
  MeasurableSet s →
    MeasureTheory.volume s ≠ ⊤ →
      ∀ (c : EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)),
        inner ℝ c (∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3) in s, LerayHopf.mollifyRep K g x) =
          ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3) in s, ∫ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3), K.η h * inner ℝ c (↑↑g (x + h))

nodes.doc:
**Helper 3 — convolution-difference estimate (modulus of the mollified field).**

For `x, y ∈ closedBall 0 R`, the increment of the mollified field is bounded by the enlarged-ball
mass of `f` times the kernel's own L²-translation modulus at the shift `x − y`:

  `‖mollifyRep K f x − mollifyRep K f y‖`
      `≤ ‖restrictToBall (R + K.supportRadius) f‖ · ‖translate_L2R (x − y) (kernelL2R K) − kernelL2R K‖`.

After the change of variables `z = x − y'` the difference is `∫ (K.η(x−·) − K.η(y−·)) • f`, and
Cauchy–Schwarz factors it as (kernel translation-modulus)·(enlarged-ball mass of `f`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mollifyRep_sub_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (R : ℝ) {x y : LerayHopf.Domain3},
  x ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R →
    y ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R →
      ‖LerayHopf.mollifyRep K f x - LerayHopf.mollifyRep K f y‖ ≤
        ‖LerayHopf.restrictToBall (R + K.supportRadius) f‖ *
          ‖LerayHopf.translate_L2R (x - y) (LerayHopf.kernelL2R K) - LerayHopf.kernelL2R K‖

nodes.doc:
**Helper 2 — pointwise sup bound for the mollified field (Cauchy–Schwarz / Young).**

For `x ∈ closedBall 0 R`, the convolution value `mollifyRep K f x = ∫ y, K.η (x−y) • f y` is bounded
by `‖kernelL2R K‖ · ‖restrictToBall (R + K.supportRadius) f‖`.  Cauchy–Schwarz on the integrand
`K.η(x−y) • f y` gives `‖kernel‖₂ · ‖f‖₂`, and because `K.η` is supported in
`closedBall 0 K.supportRadius` the integral only sees `f` over `y ∈ B_{R + K.supportRadius}`
(kernel reach from `B_R`), localizing the mass factor to the enlarged ball.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mollifyRep_sup_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (R : ℝ) {x : LerayHopf.Domain3},
  x ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R →
    ‖LerayHopf.mollifyRep K f x‖ ≤ ‖LerayHopf.kernelL2R K‖ * ‖LerayHopf.restrictToBall (R + K.supportRadius) f‖

nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBdd
nodes.signature:
(m : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) →
  MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume → ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3 → ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3

nodes.doc: The bounded truncated-weight multiplier `mulBdd m` (for `|m| ≤ C`) as an ℝ-CLM on `L²C`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBddCLM
nodes.signature:
(m : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) →
  MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume →
    {C : ℝ} → 0 ≤ C → (∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), |m ξ| ≤ C) → ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3 →L[ℝ] ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3

nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBddCLM_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hmem : MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume) {C : ℝ} (hC : 0 ≤ C)
  (hmle : ∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), |m ξ| ≤ C) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3),
  (LerayHopf.mulBddCLM✝ m hmem hC hmle) g = LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem g

nodes.doc:
Multiplication of a real `L²` element by an essentially bounded real function, landing in
`L²` (local builder; only its `coeFn` and an `L²`-Lipschitz bound are needed).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBddR
nodes.signature:
(h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) →
  MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume →
    ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume) → ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBddR_coeFn
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hh : MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  (a : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)),
  ↑↑(LerayHopf.mulBddR✝ h hh a) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] fun x => h x * ↑↑a x

nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBddR_continuous
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hh : MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume),
  Continuous fun a => LerayHopf.mulBddR✝ h hh a

nodes.doc:
**Key convergence lemma.** Schwartz φ, component `j`, sequence `fn : ℕ → L2VF_R3`
with uniform bound `‖fn n‖ ≤ M` and per-ball convergence `restrictToBall k (fn n) → 0`
for every `k : ℕ`. Then `‖mulBddR (⇑φs) hφ (L2VF_projComponent_R3 j (fn n))‖ → 0`.

Proof sketch: split the norm-sq into ball and tail integrals, bound each by ε/2.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBddR_projComp_norm_tendsto_CF
nodes.signature:
∀ (φs : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (hφ : MeasureTheory.MemLp ⇑φs ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume) (j : Fin 3)
  (fn : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (M : ℝ),
  0 ≤ M →
    (∀ (n : ℕ), ‖fn n‖ ≤ M) →
      (∀ (k : ℕ), Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall (↑k) (fn n)) Filter.atTop (nhds 0)) →
        Filter.Tendsto (fun n => ‖LerayHopf.mulBddR✝ (⇑φs) hφ ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) (fn n))‖)
          Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
Self-adjointness of real pointwise multiplication: `⟨f, h·g⟩ = ⟨h·f, g⟩`
in `Lp ℝ 2 volume`.

Both sides equal `∫ x, (↑f x) * h x * (↑g x) ∂μ` via `MeasureTheory.L2.inner_def`,
`mulBddR_coeFn`, and the identity `inner ℝ a b = a * b` for real scalars.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBddR_self_adjoint
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hh : MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  (f g : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)),
  inner ℝ f (LerayHopf.mulBddR✝ h hh g) = inner ℝ (LerayHopf.mulBddR✝ h hh f) g

nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBddR_sub
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hh : MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  (a a' : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)),
  LerayHopf.mulBddR✝ h hh a' - LerayHopf.mulBddR✝ h hh a = LerayHopf.mulBddR✝ h hh (a' - a)

nodes.doc:
The truncated multiplier norm² (a real, always-finite quantity) lower-bounds the `j`-th
viscous lower Lebesgue integral: `ofReal ‖mulBdd (min(√W,k)) (𝓕 projⱼ w)‖² ≤ viscousLintegrand_j`.
No `H¹` hypothesis — the truncated integrand is `≤` the full weight pointwise and both are
nonneg, so `lintegral_mono` closes it.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBddTrunc_integrable
nodes.signature:
∀ (k : ℕ) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3),
  MeasureTheory.Integrable (fun ξ => LerayHopf.sqrtViscousWeightTrunc k ξ ^ 2 * ‖↑↑g ξ‖ ^ 2) MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: **Additivity of the bounded multiplier.**
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBdd_add
nodes.signature:
∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hmem : MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  (g h : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3), LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem (g + h) = LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem g + LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem h

nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBdd_coeFn
nodes.signature:
∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hmem : MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3), ↑↑(LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem g) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] fun ξ => ↑(m ξ) • ↑↑g ξ

nodes.doc:
**Bounded-multiplier Bochner commute (interval integral).**  For a curve `G` that is
interval-integrable on `[a,b]`, the bounded multiplier commutes with the Bochner integral:
`mulBdd m (∫_a^b G s ds) = ∫_a^b mulBdd m (G s) ds`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBdd_intervalIntegral_comm
nodes.signature:
∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hmem : MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  {G : ℝ → ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3} {a b : ℝ},
  IntervalIntegrable G MeasureTheory.volume a b →
    IntervalIntegrable (fun s => LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem (G s)) MeasureTheory.volume a b →
      LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem (∫ (s : ℝ) in a..b, G s) = ∫ (s : ℝ) in a..b, LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem (G s)

nodes.doc: **ℝ-homogeneity of the bounded multiplier.**
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBdd_smul
nodes.signature:
∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hmem : MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume) (r : ℝ)
  (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3), LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem (r • g) = r • LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem g

nodes.doc:
**Per-truncation weighted Jensen bound.** For a bounded multiplier `m` (`MemLp ⊤`) and a curve
`G` continuous on `[t,t+δ]`, the squared weighted norm of the Steklov-averaged Fourier component is
bounded by the time-average of the squared weighted norms:

  `‖mulBdd m (δ⁻¹ • ∫_{t}^{t+δ} G s)‖² ≤ δ⁻¹ · ∫_{t}^{t+δ} ‖mulBdd m (G s)‖²`.

This is the Hilbert-space Bochner–Jensen template `norm_integral_sq_le_length_mul_integral_normSq`
applied to the continuous curve `s ↦ mulBdd m (G s)` (continuity via `continuous_mulBdd`), combined
with the bounded-multiplier Bochner commute `mulBdd_intervalIntegral_comm` — entirely
`Lp`-frontier-free (no pointwise coeFn interchange).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBdd_steklov_normSq_le_average
nodes.signature:
∀ {m : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ} (hmem : MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume) {C : ℝ},
  0 ≤ C →
    (∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), |m ξ| ≤ C) →
      ∀ {G : ℝ → ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3} {δ t : ℝ},
        0 < δ →
          0 ≤ t →
            ContinuousOn G (Set.Ici 0) →
              ‖LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem (δ⁻¹ • ∫ (s : ℝ) in t..t + δ, G s)‖ ^ 2 ≤
                δ⁻¹ * ∫ (s : ℝ) in t..t + δ, ‖LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem (G s)‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc: **Subtractivity of the bounded multiplier.**
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulBdd_sub
nodes.signature:
∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hmem : MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun ξ => ↑(m ξ)) ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  (g h : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3), LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem (g - h) = LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem g - LerayHopf.mulBdd m hmem h

nodes.doc:
Multiplication of a real `L²` element by an essentially bounded real function, landing in
`L²`. Built directly (not as a CLM) — we only need its `coeFn` and an `L²`-Lipschitz bound.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulRBdd
nodes.signature:
(h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) →
  MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume →
    ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume) → ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulRBdd_coeFn
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hh : MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  (a : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)),
  ↑↑(LerayHopf.mulRBdd✝ h hh a) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] fun x => h x * ↑↑a x

nodes.doc: `mulRBdd` is additive (used to express the difference of two multiplier images).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.mulRBdd_sub
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hh : MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  (a b : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)),
  LerayHopf.mulRBdd✝ h hh a - LerayHopf.mulRBdd✝ h hh b = LerayHopf.mulRBdd✝ h hh (a - b)

nodes.doc:
The scalar **negative Laplacian** of a real Schwartz map, `-Δ f = -∑ₐ ∂ₐ∂ₐ f`, as a Schwartz
map (so it carries a canonical `L²`-class `(negLapSchwartz f).toLp`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.negLapSchwartz
nodes.signature: SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ

nodes.doc:
The involution `σ_k : l ↦ -(k+l)` on the symmetric index set `C_k` used in the antisymmetry
reindex.  On `C_k := (fourierBox n).filter (fun l => -(k+l) ∈ fourierBox n)` it is a
self-inverse bijection (an involution): `σ_k (σ_k l) = l` and `σ_k` maps `C_k` to itself.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.neg_add_involutive

nodes.doc: The truncation cube is symmetric under `k ↦ -k`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.neg_mem_fourierBox
nodes.signature: ∀ {n : ℕ} {k : Fin 3 → ℤ}, -k ∈ LerayHopf.fourierBox n ↔ k ∈ LerayHopf.fourierBox n

nodes.doc:
**Deliverable (P5).** From a total Schwartz divergence-free basis `B`, the orthogonal
projections onto its finite prefix spans assemble into an actual `R3GalerkinScheme`.  This
is the axiom-free constructive witness of the `r3GalerkinScheme_exists` axiom: the only
input is the bundled density hypothesis `B.dense_span` (a Helmholtz/Weyl-lemma fact carried
as a hypothesis, not axiomatized).

D5 (`galerkinP_tendsto_id`) now matches the weakened `R3GalerkinScheme.tendsto_id` field
shape `∀ u, u ∈ L2Sigma_R3 → Tendsto …` exactly, so the assembly is direct.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.nonempty_r3GalerkinScheme_of_basis
nodes.signature: ∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis), Nonempty LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme

nodes.doc:
**C2 (discharged via the proved density, issue #21 / #3).**  Unconditional
inhabitation of the Schwartz divergence-free basis.

Previously this theorem lived in `SchwartzDivFreeBasis.lean` and consumed the axiom
`curlSchwartzDense_holds`; it is now relocated here, below the proved route, so the
body is identical but the dependency on the axiom is replaced by a dependency on the
proved theorem `curlSchwartzDense_holds` (above).

The name is preserved so every downstream consumer (`GalerkinODECapstone.lean`) is
unchanged.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.nonempty_schwartzGalerkinBasis
nodes.signature: Nonempty LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis

nodes.doc:
There exists a `SchwartzGalerkinBasis` whose induced Galerkin scheme satisfies
the H¹(graph-norm) test-approximation property `R3TestApproxH1`.

This is the strengthened counterpart of `nonempty_schwartzGalerkinBasis`
(`CurlDensityCapstone.lean`): the basis produced here witnesses not only L²-density of
its Galerkin span (the existing `SchwartzGalerkinBasis.dense_span` field) but also
H¹(graph-norm) approximation of every Schwartz divergence-free test by Galerkin tests of
the induced scheme.

The proof discharges `R3TestApproxH1` for the concrete `schemeOfBasis B` via
`curl_approx_H1` (Fourier low-cut kernel) and the separability of `L2VF_R3`.
The capstone chain (`GalerkinODECapstone.lean`, PR-6) will use the subtype witness
`⟨B, htest⟩` to thread `htest : R3TestApproxH1 (schemeOfBasis B)` into the assembly.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.nonempty_schwartzGalerkinBasis_H1
nodes.signature: Nonempty { B // LerayHopf.R3TestApproxH1 (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B) }

nodes.doc: `‖w‖² = ∫ ‖w x‖² ∂vol` for `w : L2VF_R3`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.normSq_VF_eq_integral_CF
nodes.signature: ∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), ‖w‖ ^ 2 = ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖↑↑w x‖ ^ 2

nodes.name: LerayHopf.normSq_besselWeightC
nodes.signature: ∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖LerayHopf.besselWeightC✝ ξ‖ ^ 2 = 1 + ‖ξ‖ ^ 2

nodes.signature: ∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖LerayHopf.besselWeightC_R✝ ξ‖ ^ 2 = 1 + ‖ξ‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
L²-norm-squared as an integral of the pointwise squared norm, for an element of an
`L²` space over a real inner-product target.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.normSq_eq_integral_normSq
nodes.signature:
∀ {μ : MeasureTheory.Measure LerayHopf.Domain3} (h : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)) 2 μ)),
  ‖h‖ ^ 2 = ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖↑↑h x‖ ^ 2 ∂μ

nodes.doc:
L²-norm-squared as an integral of the pointwise squared norm (local copy; P3's is
`private`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.normSq_eq_integral_normSq'
nodes.signature:
∀ {μ : MeasureTheory.Measure LerayHopf.Domain3} (h : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)) 2 μ)),
  ‖h‖ ^ 2 = ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖↑↑h x‖ ^ 2 ∂μ

nodes.doc:
L²-norm-squared as an integral of the pointwise squared norm, for an element of `L²(ℝ³;ℝ³)`.
(Local copy of the private `SpatialCompactness.normSq_eq_integral_normSq`.)
nodes.name: LerayHopf.normSq_eq_integral_normSq_VF
nodes.signature: ∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), ‖f‖ ^ 2 = ∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖↑↑f ξ‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
**Squared-norm weak lower semicontinuity** (inner-product form): if `⟪xₖ, y⟫ → ‖y‖²` and
`‖xₖ‖ ≤ M`, then `‖y‖² ≤ liminf ‖xₖ‖²`.  Trick: square the inner products
(`⟪xₖ, y⟫² ≤ ‖xₖ‖² ‖y‖²`) and cancel `‖y‖²` at the liminf level.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.normSq_le_liminf_of_inner_tendsto
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : InnerProductSpace ℝ E] (y : E) (x : ℕ → E) (M : ℝ),
  (∀ (k : ℕ), ‖x k‖ ≤ M) →
    Filter.Tendsto (fun k => inner ℝ (x k) y) Filter.atTop (nhds (‖y‖ ^ 2)) →
      ‖y‖ ^ 2 ≤ Filter.liminf (fun k => ‖x k‖ ^ 2) Filter.atTop

nodes.doc:
**Squared-norm weak-lsc (`toWeakSpace` form).** From `gₙ ⇀ g` and `‖gₙ‖ ≤ M`,
`‖g‖² ≤ liminf ‖gₙ‖²`.  Squaring `‖g‖ ≤ liminf ‖gₙ‖` via the continuous monotone `Real.sqrt`
(`‖gₙ‖ = √(‖gₙ‖²)`, so `liminf ‖gₙ‖ = √(liminf ‖gₙ‖²)`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.normSq_le_liminf_of_weak
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E] (g : E) (gn : ℕ → E)
  (M : ℝ),
  (∀ (n : ℕ), ‖gn n‖ ≤ M) →
    Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (toWeakSpace ℝ E) (gn n)) Filter.atTop (nhds ((toWeakSpace ℝ E) g)) →
      ‖g‖ ^ 2 ≤ Filter.liminf (fun n => ‖gn n‖ ^ 2) Filter.atTop

nodes.doc:
**A3 kernel.** Per-direction Schwartz Plancherel: for `φ : 𝓢(ℝ³, ℂ)` and a direction `m`,
`‖(∂_{m}φ).toLp 2‖² = ∫ (2π)² ⟨ξ,m⟩² ‖𝓕 φ ξ‖²`. Uses `fourier_lineDerivOp_eq`
(`𝓕(∂_{m}φ) = 2πi⟨·,m⟩ 𝓕 φ`) + Plancherel (`norm_fourier_toL2_eq`) + `normSq_toLp_two`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.normSq_lineDeriv_toLp
nodes.signature:

the pointwise squared norm: `‖g.toLp 2‖² = ∫ ‖g ξ‖²`. (Via `SchwartzMap.inner_toL2_toL2_eq`.)

nodes.doc:
**T0b.** L²-translation modulus controlled by the spectral gradient form (Plancherel):
`‖τ_h w − w‖²_{L²(ℝ³)} ≤ ‖h‖² · viscousFormSq_R3 1 w`.

This is the Navier–Stokes-specific core, now CLOSED via the shared Fourier–L² foundation
(`FourierL2.lean`, F5/F7/F8/F9).  The proof: (a) decompose the ℝ³-valued L²-norm into the
sum over the three complex components `cⱼ = L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j w`
(`componentC_translate_ae` + the Euclidean norm decomposition); (b) Plancherel
`‖τ_h^C cⱼ − cⱼ‖ = ‖𝓕(τ_h^C cⱼ) − 𝓕 cⱼ‖` (`Lp.norm_fourier_eq`); (c) the Fourier-modulation
Plancherel core `FourierL2.normSq_sub_eq_integral_phase_sub` (F8); (d) the pointwise phase
estimate `FourierL2.normSq_phaseFun_sub_one_le` (F9) integrated up to recognize
`FourierL2.viscousFormSq_R3_eq_integral_normSq_fourier` (F7).

Everything downstream (T0c, T1b, T2/deliverable) consumes ONLY this statement, so closing it
discharges the whole pillar modulo the `FrechetKolmogorovInput` hypothesis.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.normSq_translate_sub_le_viscousFormSq
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w → ‖LerayHopf.translate_L2VF h w - w‖ ^ 2 ≤ ‖h‖ ^ 2 * LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 w

nodes.doc: The `O(1/R)` gradient bound: `‖fderiv (cutoff R) x‖ ≤ K / R`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_fderiv_cutoff_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (R : ℝ),
  0 < R →
    ∀ (K : ℝ),
      (∀ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖fderiv ℝ (↑LerayHopf.cutoffBump✝) x‖ ≤ K) →
        ∀ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖fderiv ℝ (LerayHopf.cutoff✝ R) x‖ ≤ K / R

nodes.doc:
Pointwise gradient bound for the truncation:
`‖fderiv (cutoff R • φ) x‖ ≤ ‖fderiv φ x‖ + (K/R)·‖φ x‖`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_fderiv_truncated_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (R : ℝ),
  0 < R →
    ∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (K : ℝ),
      (∀ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ‖fderiv ℝ (↑LerayHopf.cutoffBump✝) x‖ ≤ K) →
        ∀ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
          ‖fderiv ℝ (fun y => LerayHopf.cutoff✝ R y • φ y) x‖ ≤ ‖fderiv ℝ (⇑φ) x‖ + K / R * ‖φ x‖

nodes.doc:
**Hilbert-space Jensen for a Bochner interval average (squared-norm form).** For a curve
`f : ℝ → H` into a complete real inner-product space, continuous on the window `[a,b]` (`a ≤ b`),
the squared norm of the Bochner interval integral is bounded by the window length times the
integral of the squared norm:

  `‖∫_a^b f s ds‖² ≤ (b − a) · ∫_a^b ‖f s‖² ds`.

This is the analytic core of the Steklov/Jensen averaging estimate: dividing by `(b−a)²` turns
it into `‖⨍_a^b f‖² ≤ ⨍_a^b ‖f‖²` (Jensen for the convex map `‖·‖²` under the normalized window
measure). It is `Lp`-frontier-free — the proof is the triangle inequality
`‖∫ f‖ ≤ ∫ ‖f‖` (`intervalIntegral.norm_integral_le_integral_norm`) followed by the scalar
Cauchy–Schwarz / Hölder `(∫_a^b ‖f‖·1)² ≤ (∫_a^b ‖f‖²)(∫_a^b 1²)`
(`MeasureTheory.integral_mul_norm_le_Lp_mul_Lq`, `p = q = 2`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_integral_sq_le_length_mul_integral_normSq
nodes.signature:
∀ {H : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup H] [inst_1 : InnerProductSpace ℝ H] [CompleteSpace H] {f : ℝ → H} {a b : ℝ},
  a ≤ b → ContinuousOn f (Set.uIcc a b) → ‖∫ (s : ℝ) in a..b, f s‖ ^ 2 ≤ (b - a) * ∫ (s : ℝ) in a..b, ‖f s‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc: The `L¹`-mass of the kernel is `1` (`K.nonneg` ⇒ `|η| = η`, `K.mass_one`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_kernelL1R
nodes.signature: ∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel), ‖LerayHopf.kernelL1R K‖ = 1

nodes.doc: `‖K.η h • translate_L2VF h g‖ = K.η h · ‖g‖` (using `K.nonneg` so `|K.η h| = K.η h`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_kernel_smul_translate
nodes.signature:
∀ (K : LerayHopf.MollifierKernel) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (h : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  ‖K.η h • LerayHopf.translate_L2VF h g‖ = K.η h * ‖g‖

nodes.doc:
**Norm lower-semicontinuity from a single inner-product convergence.** On a real
inner-product space, if `‖gₙ‖ ≤ M` and `⟪g, gₙ⟫ → ‖g‖²`, then `‖g‖ ≤ liminf ‖gₙ‖`.

This is the inner-product-trick form of weak-lsc of the norm: `‖g‖² = lim ⟪g, gₙ⟫ ≤ ‖g‖ · liminf ‖gₙ‖`
(Cauchy–Schwarz + `Monotone.map_liminf_of_continuousAt` to commute the nonneg scalar `‖g‖` with
`liminf`), then divide by `‖g‖`.  No Mazur, no subsequence extraction.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_le_liminf_of_inner_tendsto
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : InnerProductSpace ℝ E] (g : E) (gn : ℕ → E) (M : ℝ),
  (∀ (n : ℕ), ‖gn n‖ ≤ M) →
    Filter.Tendsto (fun n => inner ℝ g (gn n)) Filter.atTop (nhds (‖g‖ ^ 2)) →
      ‖g‖ ≤ Filter.liminf (fun n => ‖gn n‖) Filter.atTop

nodes.doc:
**Norm weak-lsc (`toWeakSpace` form).** If `gₙ ⇀ g` weakly in `WeakSpace ℝ E` and `‖gₙ‖ ≤ M`,
then `‖g‖ ≤ liminf ‖gₙ‖`.  (Extract the inner-product convergence `⟪g, gₙ⟫ → ‖g‖²` from the weak
limit — eval against the Riesz dual of `g` — then apply `norm_le_liminf_of_inner_tendsto`.)
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_le_liminf_of_weak
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [CompleteSpace E] (g : E) (gn : ℕ → E)
  (M : ℝ),
  (∀ (n : ℕ), ‖gn n‖ ≤ M) →
    Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (toWeakSpace ℝ E) (gn n)) Filter.atTop (nhds ((toWeakSpace ℝ E) g)) →
      ‖g‖ ≤ Filter.liminf (fun n => ‖gn n‖) Filter.atTop

nodes.doc:
**A3 (MUST-PROVE).** Any forward local solution on `[0, T]` stays in the ball
`‖c t‖ ≤ ‖c 0‖`.

Proof sketch: from A2, `t ↦ ½‖c t‖²` has nonpositive derivative `−ν·viscousFormSq_R3 1 (c t)`
on `Icc 0 T` (`viscousFormSq_R3_nonneg`, `mul_nonneg hν.le`), hence is `AntitoneOn (Icc 0 T)`
(`Convex`/`MeanValue` antitone-from-nonpositive-derivative); so `½‖c t‖² ≤ ½‖c 0‖²` for
`t ∈ Icc 0 T`, giving `‖c t‖ ≤ ‖c 0‖` after clearing the `½` and taking square roots
(`sq_le_sq'`/`abs_le_abs` + `norm_nonneg`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_le_of_forwardSolution
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (c : ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)) {T : ℝ},
      0 ≤ T →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, HasDerivAt (fun s => ↑(c s)) (↑(LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n (c t))) t) →
          ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖↑(c t)‖ ≤ ‖↑(c 0)‖

nodes.doc:
A single Fourier coefficient is bounded by the `L²` norm (Bessel/`lp` bound):
`‖û(k)‖ ≤ ‖f‖`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_mFourierCoeff3_le
nodes.signature: ∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C) (k : Fin 3 → ℤ), ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f k‖ ≤ ‖f‖

nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_mulBddR_le

∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hh : MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)

  ‖LerayHopf.mulBddR✝ h hh a‖ ≤ (MeasureTheory.eLpNorm h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume).toReal * ‖a‖

nodes.doc:
For an `H¹` field `w`, the truncated multiplier norm² is `≤` the (genuinely-integrable) `j`-th
viscous integrand: `‖mulBdd (min(√W,k)) (𝓕 projⱼ w)‖² ≤ viscousIntegrand_j j w`.  (The full weight
integrand is integrable because `w ∈ H¹` — `integrable_viscous_integrand_of_memH1`.)
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_mulBddTrunc_sq_le_integrand_of_memH1
nodes.signature:
∀ (k : ℕ) (j : Fin 3) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w →
    ‖LerayHopf.mulBdd (LerayHopf.sqrtViscousWeightTrunc k) ⋯
            (FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) w))‖ ^
        2 ≤
      LerayHopf.viscousIntegrand_j✝ j w

nodes.doc:
**Operator bound for the bounded multiplier:** `‖mulBdd m g‖ ≤ C ‖g‖` whenever `|m| ≤ C`
everywhere (the sup bound on `m` dominates the operator).

nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_mulBdd_sq

nodes.doc:
Ball-mass monotonicity in the radius: restricting to a smaller ball does not increase the
`L²`-norm (`closedBall 0 a ⊆ closedBall 0 b` for `a ≤ b`, so the restricted measures are nested
and `eLpNorm_mono_measure` applies).  Mirrors `SpatialCompactness.norm_restrictToBall_le`
(which is the `b = ∞`/global case) at finite radii.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_restrictToBall_mono
nodes.signature: ∀ {a b : ℝ}, a ≤ b → ∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), ‖LerayHopf.restrictToBall a w‖ ≤ ‖LerayHopf.restrictToBall b w‖

nodes.doc:

bounded by the global difference.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_restrictToBall_sub_le

nodes.signature: ∀ (R : ℝ), 0 < R → ‖LerayHopf.scaleCLM✝ R‖ ≤ 1 / R

nodes.signature: ∀ (R : ℝ) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), ‖LerayHopf.tailVF'✝ R w‖ ≤ ‖w‖

nodes.doc:
**T0c.** Uniform translation modulus for the admissible family: from `0 ≤ M` and
`viscousFormSq_R3 1 w ≤ M²`, `‖τ_h w − w‖ ≤ ‖h‖·M`.  Supplies the modulus hypothesis of
`FrechetKolmogorovInput`.

The sign hypothesis `hM : 0 ≤ M` is REQUIRED: the squared bound `viscousFormSq_R3 1 w ≤ M²`
alone does not control the sign of `M` (e.g. `M = -1`, `w = 0` would give the false
`0 ≤ -‖h‖`).  Taking square roots of the T0b estimate only yields `‖h‖·|M|`; the conclusion
`‖h‖·M` is correct precisely when `M ≥ 0`.  Downstream (`localRellichInput_of_frechetKolmogorov`)
`0 ≤ M` is discharged from `‖w‖ ≤ M` on any admissible witness.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_translate_sub_le_of_viscousBound
nodes.signature:
∀ (M : ℝ) (h : LerayHopf.Domain3) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w → 0 ≤ M → LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 w ≤ M ^ 2 → ‖LerayHopf.translate_L2VF h w - w‖ ≤ ‖h‖ * M

nodes.doc: The Fourier–Galerkin projection is norm-nonincreasing (Pythagoras).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_velocityProjection_le
nodes.signature: ∀ (N : ℕ) (x : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), ‖(LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n N) x‖ ≤ ‖x‖

nodes.doc: Its squared `L²`-norm is the viscous spectral integrand `∫ W ‖𝓕(projⱼ w)‖²`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.norm_weightedFourierComponent_sq
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hw : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w) (j : Fin 3),
  ‖LerayHopf.weightedFourierComponent w hw j‖ ^ 2 =
    ∫ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
      (2 * Real.pi) ^ 2 * ‖ξ‖ ^ 2 * ‖↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) w)) ξ‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc: On fixed points of `conjL2C`, re-embedding the real part is the identity.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.ofRealLp_reLp_eq_self
nodes.signature:
∀ {h : ↥LerayHopf.L2C},
  LerayHopf.conjL2C✝ h = h →
    (ContinuousLinearMap.compLpL 2 LerayHopf.haarTorus3 RCLike.ofRealCLM)
        ((ContinuousLinearMap.compLpL 2 LerayHopf.haarTorus3 RCLike.reCLM) h) =
      h

nodes.name: LerayHopf.ofReal_norm_mulBddTrunc_sq_le_lintegrand
nodes.signature:
∀ (k : ℕ) (j : Fin 3) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  ENNReal.ofReal
      (‖LerayHopf.mulBdd (LerayHopf.sqrtViscousWeightTrunc k) ⋯
            (FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) w))‖ ^
        2) ≤
    LerayHopf.viscousLintegrand_j✝ j w

nodes.doc:
(P0.13) ENNReal bridge for the tail identity: valid for EVERY `v : L2VF` (the mode
tail is a sub-family of the always-summable Parseval family; no `memH1VF` needed).
Connects the real integrals of P0.14/P0.15 to P0.6c's ENNReal mode sums.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.ofReal_tail_sq_eq_tailEnn
nodes.signature:
∀ (N : ℕ) (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  ENNReal.ofReal (‖v - (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n N) v‖ ^ 2) =
    ∑ j,
      ∑' (k : { k // k ∉ LerayHopf.fourierBox N }),
        ENNReal.ofReal (‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) v) ↑k‖ ^ 2)

nodes.doc:
The real viscous form is dominated by the honest ENNReal sum (equality on the
summable set; the junk-`0` collapse only helps the inequality).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.ofReal_viscousFormSq_le
nodes.signature: ∀ (ν : ℝ), 0 ≤ ν → ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), ENNReal.ofReal (LerayHopf.viscousFormSq ν u) ≤ LerayHopf.viscousEnn ν u

nodes.doc:
`ofReal (∫ f) ≤ ∫⁻ ofReal f` for the (nonneg) viscous integrand — no integrability needed
(if non-integrable the Bochner integral is `0`).

nodes.doc:
**A3 kernel.** Operator norm of a real-linear functional `L : Domain3 →L[ℝ] ℂ` is bounded
by the Euclidean norm `√(∑ᵢ ‖L (b i)‖²)` of its gradient covector, for any orthonormal basis `b`.
This is the Hilbert–Schmidt bound `‖L‖_op ≤ ‖L‖_HS`; the reverse inequality is false for a
ℂ-valued (rank-2) codomain, so only `≤` holds — which is the direction the GNS upper bound needs.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.opNorm_le_sqrt_sum_sq
nodes.signature:
∀ {ι : Type u_1} [inst : Fintype ι] (b : OrthonormalBasis ι ℝ LerayHopf.Domain3) (L : LerayHopf.Domain3 →L[ℝ] ℂ),
  ‖L‖ ≤ √(∑ i, ‖L (b i)‖ ^ 2)

nodes.doc:
**Longitudinality of `ŵ` from orthogonality to all curls.**  If `w` is orthogonal to every
curl `curlSchwartzL2 ψ`, then its complex Fourier transform `ŵ` is longitudinal a.e.: the cross
field `ŵ × ξ` vanishes, here the `k`-th component
`ŵ_{k+1}(ξ) ξ_{k+2} − ŵ_{k+2}(ξ) ξ_{k+1} = 0` for a.e. `ξ`.

Proof: pairing `⟪curlSchwartzL2 ψ, w⟫ = 0` (Parseval bridge + curl symbol `fourier_curlSchwartz_eq_cross`
+ `conj_cross_sum_eq` triple-product re-grouping) gives, for a single-component potential
`ψ`, `∫ conj(𝓕(schwartzC φ)) · (ŵ × ξ)_k = 0` for every real `φ`.  By the
Hermitian-test du-Bois-Reymond lemma `ae_zero_of_hermitianTest` (whose Hermitian tests are realised
through `schwartz_hermitian_has_fourier_preimage`), `(ŵ × ξ)_k = 0` a.e.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.orthogonalCurl_longitudinal_ae
nodes.signature:
∀ w ∈ (Submodule.span ℝ (Set.range LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2))ᗮ,
  ∀ (k : Fin 3),
    ∀ᵐ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
      ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 (k + 1)) w)) ξ * ↑(ξ.ofLp (k + 2)) -
          ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 (k + 2)) w)) ξ * ↑(ξ.ofLp (k + 1)) =
        0

nodes.doc:
**`perBall_ae_subseq` — From L²-in-time Bochner convergence (refine-capable),
extract a FURTHER subsequence converging a.e. in `t` in the `L2ballR3 k` norm.**

Given an input subsequence `ψ`, applies refine-capable axiom A to get `ρ` (further
subsequence of `ψ`) with eLpNorm → 0 along `ψ ∘ ρ`, then converts to a.e.-t convergence
via `tendstoInMeasure_of_tendsto_eLpNorm` + `TendstoInMeasure.exists_seq_tendsto_ae`.

**Proof route (for lean-prover):**
1. Apply refine-capable axiom A with input `ψ` at radius `k` to get `ρ₀` and `g_k` with
   `eLpNorm (fun t => restrictToBall k ((galSeq (ψ (ρ₀ n))).u t) - g_k t) 2 μ → 0`.
2. Each `fun t => restrictToBall k ((galSeq (ψ (ρ₀ n))).u t) - g_k t` is AESM on `[0,T]`
   (Galerkin curve continuous on Ici 0 via `u_hasDeriv`, composed with continuous
   `restrictToBall`, minus AESM `g_k`).
3. Apply `MeasureTheory.tendstoInMeasure_of_tendsto_eLpNorm` (p=2, hp_ne_zero=by norm_num)
   to get `TendstoInMeasure μ (fun n t => restrictToBall k ((galSeq (ψ (ρ₀ n))).u t)) atTop g_k`.
4. Apply `TendstoInMeasure.exists_seq_tendsto_ae` to get FURTHER `σ : ℕ → ℕ` StrictMono
   with a.e.-t convergence of `ψ ∘ ρ₀ ∘ σ`.
5. Set the output `ρ := ρ₀ ∘ σ` (StrictMono by composition); conclusion uses `ψ (ρ n) = ψ (ρ₀ (σ n))`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.perBall_ae_subseq
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (ψ : ℕ → ℕ),
          StrictMono ψ →
            ∀ (k : ℕ),
              ∃ ρ g_k,
                StrictMono ρ ∧
                  MeasureTheory.AEStronglyMeasurable g_k (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) ∧
                    ∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T),
                      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑((galSeq (ψ (ρ n))).u t)) Filter.atTop
                        (nhds (g_k t))

The scalar test curve `t ↦ ⟪uₙ(t), w⟫` has derivative
`-(ν·stokesTestPairing(uₙ t, w) + b(uₙ t, uₙ t, w))` at forward times, for tests fixed at
level `n` (from `u_hasDeriv` + `u_ode`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.perTest_hasDerivAt
nodes.signature:

  (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
