nodes.doc:
**Per-test uniform Lipschitz bound** (uniform in the Galerkin level `n ≥ n₀`): for a
test `w` fixed at level `n₀`, all Galerkin scalar curves at levels `n ≥ n₀` are
`L(w)`-Lipschitz on `[0, ∞)`, with `L(w) = ν·Cs(w)·‖u₀‖ + Cb(w)·‖u₀‖²` independent of `n`.
This is the torus form of R3-spike obligation 2; the level-promotion is
`velocityProjection_n_eq_of_le` (the R3 nesting blocker is absent here).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.perTest_lipschitz
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma)
      (n₀ : ℕ),
      (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n₀) ↑w = ↑w →
        ∃ L,
          0 ≤ L ∧
            ∀ (n : ℕ),
              n₀ ≤ n →
                ∀ s ∈ Set.Ici 0,
                  ∀ t ∈ Set.Ici 0, |inner ℝ ↑((galSeq n).u t) ↑w - inner ℝ ↑((galSeq n).u s) ↑w| ≤ L * |t - s|

nodes.doc:
**Per-Galerkin-test equi-Lipschitz bound** on ℝ³.  For a Galerkin test `w` at level `n₀`,
and any Galerkin level `n ≥ n₀`, the scalar pairing curve
`t ↦ ⟪uₙ(t), w⟫` is Lipschitz on `[0,∞)` with constant
`L(w) := ν·Cs(w)·‖u₀‖ + Cb(w)·‖u₀‖²`,
where `Cs(w)` comes from `stokesTestPairing_abs_le` via `range_schwartz` + negLap,
and `Cb(w)` from `F.b_bound`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.perTest_lipschitz_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n)
      (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
      LerayHopf.IsSchwartzDivFree_R3 w →
        ∀ (n₀ : ℕ),
          (𝔊.P n₀) ↑w = ↑w →
            ∃ L,
              0 ≤ L ∧
                ∀ (n : ℕ),
                  n₀ ≤ n →
                    ∀ s ∈ Set.Ici 0,
                      ∀ t ∈ Set.Ici 0, |inner ℝ ↑((galSeq n).u t) ↑w - inner ℝ ↑((galSeq n).u s) ↑w| ≤ L * |t - s|

nodes.doc:
The complex `L2C_R3` class of a real Schwartz POTENTIAL component `ψ k`: its `Lp ℝ`
class embedded into `Lp ℂ` through the real-to-complex coercion `RCLike.ofRealCLM`, lifted
to the `Lp` level by `compLpL`.

This is the SAME real→complex coercion used by `L2VF_projComponentC_R3`
(`Domain.lean`), so `potentialComponentC ψ k` is `L2VF_projComponentC_R3`-shaped: it is the
complex L²-class whose a.e. value is `ξ ↦ (ψ k ξ : ℂ)`.  It is the input whose Fourier
transform the curl multiplier acts on — the FOURIER TRANSFORM OF THE POTENTIAL, not of the
curl.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.potentialComponentC

The ℝ³ weak-convection operator-gap exists — THEOREM (formerly `axiom r3ConvectionGapOp_exists`,
removed in issue #56). Re-exports the proved `ConvectionExtension.r3ConvectionGapOp_holds` under the
original public name (Hard Rule #2: no rename). Being a theorem, it does NOT appear in `#print axioms`,
so the capstone stays at 2 project axioms.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.r3ConvectionGapOp_exists
nodes.signature: ∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme), Nonempty (LerayHopf.ConvectionGapOp 𝔊)

nodes.doc:
Build a `DissipativeEvolution` from an `R3GalerkinScheme` and an `R3NSForms`.

`H := L2Sigma_R3`, with the `L2Sigma_R3`-subspace instances inherited from `L2VF_R3`.
The regularity functional is `viscousFormSq_R3 1 ∘ (↑)` (matching the design doc:
`reg := viscousFormSq_R3 1`), the viscous form is the concrete `stokesTestPairing_R3`,
the convection form is `F.b`, and the test predicate is the **canonical**
`IsSchwartzDivFree_R3` (Schwartz divergence-free test class — scheme-independent).

Using `IsSchwartzDivFree_R3` here (rather than `IsGalerkinTest_R3 𝔊`) ensures that
`WeakFormNS … (r3Evolution 𝔊 F) u` is tested against the full Schwartz div-free class,
which is the correct formulation for the whole-space Leray–Hopf weak equation.

This construction is **sorry-free**: `DissipativeEvolution` carries no Galerkin or
compactness fields.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.r3Evolution
nodes.signature: (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) → LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊 → LerayHopf.DissipativeEvolution

nodes.doc:
**Discharged former axiom AX-G (relocated from SchwartzDivFreeBasis.lean, issue #3).**

A Galerkin approximation-projection family on `L²_σ(ℝ³)` exists.  No longer an axiom
(was discharged in issue #21 by routing through `curlSchwartzDense_holds`); now
additionally axiom-free because `curlSchwartzDense_holds` is itself a proved theorem.

Body is unchanged: assembled from `nonempty_r3GalerkinScheme_of_basis` applied to a
basis `B` from `nonempty_schwartzGalerkinBasis`.  The name is preserved so every
downstream consumer is unchanged.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.r3GalerkinScheme_exists
nodes.signature: Nonempty LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme

nodes.doc:
The ℝ³ NS convection form exists — THEOREM (was axiom `r3ConvectionGapOp_exists`, issue #56),
now proved from `r3ConvectionGapOp_holds` (the sorry-free determined-form construction in
`ConvectionExtension.lean`) + proved density, via the sorry-free `R3NSForms_of_gap`.

Route: obtain `g : ConvectionGapOp 𝔊` from the proved theorem `r3ConvectionGapOp_holds`;
supply proved density (`convectionGap_schwartz_dense curlSchwartzDense_holds`) as
`schwartz_dense`; assemble a full `ConvectionGap 𝔊`; apply `R3NSForms_of_gap`.

The conclusion `Nonempty (R3NSForms 𝔊)` is IDENTICAL to what `r3_NSForms_exist` asserted —
no statement weakening.  The operator core is now PROVED sorry-free in this file
(`r3ConvectionGapOp_holds`, C11); no project axiom remains for the convection operator.
Mirrors the torus `torus3_NSForms_exists` (issue #22).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.r3_NSForms_exists
nodes.signature: ∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme), Nonempty (LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊)

nodes.doc:
**L³ approximation of the un-differentiated `H¹` test factor.**
For `h : L2C_R3` in `H^{1,2}` and direction `eₐ` with weak `eₐ`-derivative `(hG : 𝓢')`, there is
a real Schwartz sequence `ψₙ` (the real parts of the multi-direction Brick-1 sequence) that
converges to `reLp h` in `L²`, whose `eₐ`-derivatives converge to `reLp hGval` in `L²`, **and**
which converges to `h.re` in `L³`. The `L³` convergence uses the uniform `L⁶` (GNS) bound on the
full gradient together with the `L²∩L⁶ ↪ L³` interpolation.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.reSchwartz_L3_approx
nodes.signature:
∀ (h hGval : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3) (a : Fin 3),
  TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution h) →
    LineDeriv.lineDerivOp (EuclideanSpace.single a 1) (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution h) =
        MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution hGval →
      ∃ ψ,
        Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (ψ n).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) Filter.atTop (nhds (LerayHopf.reLp✝ h)) ∧
          Filter.Tendsto
              (fun n =>
                ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) (ψ n)).toLp
                  2 MeasureTheory.volume)
              Filter.atTop (nhds (LerayHopf.reLp✝ hGval)) ∧
            ∃ Φ,
              (↑↑Φ =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] fun x => (↑↑h x).re) ∧
                Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (ψ n).toLp 3 MeasureTheory.volume) Filter.atTop (nhds Φ)

nodes.doc:
**Per-component Schwartz approximation with real-part gradient convergence.**
For `f : L2C_R3` in `H^{1,2}` and direction `eₐ` whose weak derivative distribution equals
`(g : 𝓢')`, there is a real Schwartz sequence `ψₙ` with `(ψₙ).toLp → reLp f` and
`(∂ₐψₙ).toLp → reLp g` in `Lp ℝ 2`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.reSchwartz_approx
nodes.signature:
∀ (f g : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3) (a : Fin 3),
  TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) →
    LineDeriv.lineDerivOp (EuclideanSpace.single a 1) (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) =
        MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution g →
      ∃ ψ,
        Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (ψ n).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) Filter.atTop (nhds (LerayHopf.reLp✝ f)) ∧
          Filter.Tendsto
            (fun n =>
              ((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) (ψ n)).toLp 2
                MeasureTheory.volume)
            Filter.atTop (nhds (LerayHopf.reLp✝ g))

nodes.doc:
Taking the real part undoes the complex embedding of a component
(cf. `velocityProjection_n_tendsto`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.re_compLpL_projComponentC
nodes.signature:
∀ (j : Fin 3) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (ContinuousLinearMap.compLpL 2 LerayHopf.haarTorus3 RCLike.reCLM) ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) w) =
    (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent j) w

nodes.doc:
Base case of (P1): the reflected Fourier transform of a complexified real Schwartz rep
equals the conjugated one, as `Lp` elements.  Uses the Schwartz-level Hermitian identity
`fourier_schwartzC_hermitian` lifted through `toLp`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.reflect_fourier_schwartzC_eq_conj
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  (MeasureTheory.Lp.compMeasurePreserving Neg.neg ⋯)
      (FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ φ).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume)) =
    (ContinuousLinearMap.compLpL 2 MeasureTheory.volume ↑Complex.conjCLE)

nodes.doc:
**Rellich compactness for L²_σ.**

Any sequence `u : ℕ → L2VF` of divergence-free velocity fields bounded in H¹
has a subsequence converging in `L2VF`.

More precisely: if each `u n ∈ L2Sigma`, each component satisfies `memH1VF (u n)`,
and `h1EnergySq (u n) ≤ M²` for all `n`, then there exist a strictly monotone
extraction `φ : ℕ → ℕ` and a limit `g ∈ L2Sigma` such that
`u (φ n) → g` in `L2VF`.

Proof strategy (prover target):
- Apply `rellich_seq_compact` componentwise (j = 0, 1, 2) to extract convergent
  subsequences in `L2C`, using `hH1` (summability) and `hbound` (energy bound).
- Combine via diagonal extraction to get a single subsequence converging in all
  components.
- Reconstruct the limit in `L2VF` from the componentwise limits.
- Use `velocityProjection_n_preserves_L2Sigma` / `mem_L2Sigma_iff` to verify
  the limit is divergence-free.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.rellich_L2Sigma
nodes.signature:
∀ (M : ℝ) (u : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (∀ (n : ℕ), u n ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma) →
    (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.memH1VF (u n)) →
      (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.h1EnergySq (u n) ≤ M ^ 2) →
        ∃ φ g, StrictMono φ ∧ g ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma ∧ Filter.Tendsto (fun n => u (φ n)) Filter.atTop (nhds g)

gap.note:
自然言語の「Rellich でコンパクト」に対し、Lean では Fréchet–Kolmogorov の
平行移動連続性と裾評価を明示的に組む必要がある。

corpus.proof_ja:
L1. Fréchet–Kolmogorov の全有界性判定を用いる。一様 $H^1$ 有界性から、平行移動の
連続性評価
$$\|f(\cdot+h)-f(\cdot)\|_{L^2} \le |h|\,\|\nabla f\|_{L^2}$$
が点列に一様に成り立つ。
L2. 遠方の裾（大きな球の外側の $L^2$ 質量）が一様に小さいことを、重み付き評価から示す。
L3. L1・L2 より点列は $L^2$ で全有界。前コンパクト性から $L^2$ 収束部分列を取り出す。
corpus.statement_ja:
$H^1$ ノルムが一様有界な $L^2(\mathbb{R}^3)$ の点列は、$L^2$ で強収束する部分列をもつ
（Rellich–Kondrachov のコンパクト埋め込み $H^1 \hookrightarrow L^2$ の点列版）。

nodes.doc:
**Rellich (sequential form).** Any sequence bounded in `H¹(𝕋³)` has an `L²(𝕋³)`-convergent
subsequence.  This is the form used in the Aubin–Lions / Galerkin limit-passage argument.

Proof strategy for lean-prover:
1. Let `S := {f : L2C | memH1Torus f ∧ ∑' k, (1 + ∑ i, (k i : ℝ)^2) * ‖f̂(k)‖² ≤ M²}`.
2. `S` is totally bounded: `H1_ball_totallyBounded M`.
3. `closure S` is totally bounded: `TotallyBounded.closure`.
4. `closure S` is closed, hence complete in the `CompleteSpace L2C`:
   `IsClosed.isComplete (isClosed_closure)`.
5. `closure S` is compact:
   `TotallyBounded.isCompact_of_isComplete (TotallyBounded.closure _) (isClosed_closure.isComplete)`.
6. Each `u n ∈ S ⊆ closure S` (by `subset_closure`).
7. Apply `IsCompact.tendsto_subseq` (exact mathlib name, in `Mathlib.Topology.Sequences`):
   given `hs : IsCompact (closure S)` and `hx : ∀ n, u n ∈ closure S`, it returns
   `∃ a ∈ closure S, ∃ φ : ℕ → ℕ, StrictMono φ ∧ Tendsto (u ∘ φ) atTop (𝓝 a)`.
   Destructure to get `φ`, `g`, and the `Tendsto` (the `Tendsto (u ∘ φ)` goal matches
   `Filter.Tendsto (fun n => u (φ n))` by `Function.comp`).

Key mathlib lemmas (grep-verified to exist):
- `H1_ball_totallyBounded`              (this file, L5)
- `TotallyBounded.closure`              (`Mathlib.Topology.UniformSpace.Cauchy`, line 560)
- `IsClosed.isComplete`                 (`Mathlib.Topology.UniformSpace.Cauchy`, line 447)
- `isClosed_closure`                    (`Mathlib.Topology.Closure`)
- `TotallyBounded.isCompact_of_isComplete` (`Mathlib.Topology.UniformSpace.Cauchy`, line 745)
- `subset_closure`                      (`Mathlib.Topology.Closure`)
- `IsCompact.tendsto_subseq`            (`Mathlib.Topology.Sequences`, line 298)
nodes.name: LerayHopf.rellich_seq_compact
nodes.signature:
∀ (M : ℝ) (u : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2C),
  (∀ (n : ℕ),
      LerayHopf.memH1Torus (u n) ∧
        ∑' (k : Fin 3 → ℤ), (1 + ∑ i, ↑(k i) ^ 2) * ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 (u n) k‖ ^ 2 ≤ M ^ 2) →
    ∃ φ g, StrictMono φ ∧ Filter.Tendsto (fun n => u (φ n)) Filter.atTop (nhds g)

**D0b.** Restriction of an L²(ℝ³) velocity field to the ball `B_R`, as an element of
`L2ballR3 R`.

Goes through the underlying a.e.-function: `MemLp.restrict (Lp.memLp w)` gives
`MemLp (w : Domain3 → _) 2 (volume.restrict (closedBall 0 R))`, and `MemLp.toLp` packages it.
(Restriction is not measure-preserving, so `Lp.compMeasurePreserving` is not applicable —
G1.)

nodes.doc:
**E1.** The ball-restricted Galerkin solution curve `t ↦ restrictToBall k (u t)` is
`MemLp 2` on the compact interval `[0, T]` (with the restricted Lebesgue measure).

Route (plan §3 E1): `t ↦ (gs.u t : L2VF_R3)` is continuous on `Ici 0` (B1
`galerkinCurve_continuousOn`), and `restrictToBall k` is 1-Lipschitz, so the composition is
continuous on `[0, T]`; a continuous curve into a normed space is `MemLp 2` on a finite
interval.

          MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun t => LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑(gs.u t)) 2
            (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T))

nodes.doc:
The concrete Galerkin scheme attached to a Schwartz divergence-free basis `B`.

This is the record built inside P5's `nonempty_r3GalerkinScheme_of_basis`
(`GalerkinScheme.lean:338`), extracted as a `def` so that its projector is *definitionally*
`galerkinP B`: in particular `(schemeOfBasis B).P n = galerkinP B n` holds by `rfl`, hence
`range ((schemeOfBasis B).P n)` has the explicit finite basis of `galerkinSpan B n` (P5
`galerkinSpan_finiteDimensional`). This finite-dimensionality is exactly what the abstract
`R3GalerkinScheme` does not expose, and is what makes the Riesz construction (R1/R2) and the
whole milestone feasible (see the header, §1.4).

nodes.doc:
**(P2) Schwartz Hermitian preimage extraction (must-prove — item 11 in the plan).**

If `h : 𝓢(ℝ³, ℂ)` is anti-Hermitian in the sense `h(-ξ) = -conj(h(ξ))` (i.e. `h` is an
anti-Hermitian Schwartz symbol, exactly the kind produced by `testSymbol φ` for real `φ`),
then there exists `φ : 𝓢(ℝ³, ℝ)` such that `testSymbol φ = h`.

**Constructibility sketch** (no Mathlib PR needed — all primitives present):
- Let `g = (2πi)⁻¹ • h` (Hermitian: `g(-ξ) = conj(g(ξ))`).
- Let `Ψ : 𝓢(ℝ³, ℂ)` be the Schwartz inverse Fourier transform `𝓕⁻ g`
  (`FourierTransform.fourierCLE.symm` applied to `g`).
- Since `g` is Hermitian, `Ψ` is real-valued: use `fourier_ofReal_reflect_eq_conj` (proved,
  P1 above) to establish that `𝓕⁻ g` is real-valued, so `Im Ψ = 0`.
- Extract the real Schwartz function `φ := Ψ.postcompCLM reCLM`
  (via `SchwartzMap.postcompCLM (RCLike.reCLM : ℂ →L[ℝ] ℝ)`, which gives `φ ξ = Re(Ψ ξ)`).
- Verify `testSymbol φ = h`:
  `testSymbol φ ξ = conj((2πi) · 𝓕(schwartzC φ)(ξ))`.
  Since `Ψ` is real-valued, `schwartzC φ = schwartzC (Re Ψ) = Ψ` (as Schwartz maps).
  Then `𝓕(Ψ) = 𝓕(𝓕⁻ g) = g` by `FourierInvPair`, and `conj((2πi)·g) = h` by definition of `g`.

Key Mathlib decls: `SchwartzMap.postcompCLM`, `Complex.conjCLE`, `RCLike.reCLM`,
`FourierTransform.fourierCLE` (symm), `FourierInvPair`,
`fourier_ofReal_reflect_eq_conj` (item 9, proved above).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.schwartz_antiHermitian_has_testSymbol_preimage
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  (∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), h (-ξ) = -(starRingEnd ℂ) (h ξ)) → ∃ φ, LerayHopf.testSymbol✝ φ = ⇑h

nodes.doc:
**Brick-1 (per-direction).** For `f : L2C_R3` in `H^{1,2}` and a fixed direction `m`, there
is a Schwartz sequence converging in L² to `f` whose `m`-directional derivatives converge in L²
to the weak derivative `∂_m (f : 𝓢')`. Derived from the `m`-uniform engine
`schwartz_h1_gradConv_aux`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.schwartz_h1_gradConv
nodes.signature:
∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3) (m : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) →
    ∃ g,
      ∃ (_ :
        LineDeriv.lineDerivOp m (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) =
          MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution g),
        ∃ φ,
          Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (φ n).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) Filter.atTop (nhds f) ∧
            Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (LineDeriv.lineDerivOp m (φ n)).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) Filter.atTop (nhds g)

nodes.doc:
**Shared engine for Brick-1 (`schwartz_h1_gradConv` and its multi-direction export).**
The `φₙ = 𝓕⁻¹(smulLeftCLM wInv ηₙ)` construction is `m`-independent, so a SINGLE Schwartz
sequence gives value-L² convergence to `f` together with, for EVERY direction `m`, L²
convergence of `(∂_{m} φₙ).toLp` to the weak derivative `∂_m (f : 𝓢')`. Both public
exports peel off the appropriate piece.

∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3),
  TemperedDistribution.MemSobolev 1 2 (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) →
    ∃ φ,
      Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (φ n).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) Filter.atTop (nhds f) ∧
        ∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3),
          ∃ g,
            ∃ (_ :
              LineDeriv.lineDerivOp m (MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution f) =
                MeasureTheory.Lp.toTemperedDistribution g),
              Filter.Tendsto (fun n => (LineDeriv.lineDerivOp m (φ n)).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) Filter.atTop
                (nhds g)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.schwartz_h1_gradConv_multi
nodes.signature:
∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C_R3),

    ∃ φ,

        ∀ (m : LerayHopf.Domain3),

nodes.doc:
**Hermitian Schwartz preimage under `𝓕 ∘ schwartzC`.**  Every Hermitian Schwartz symbol
`G` (`G(-ξ) = conj(G ξ)`) is `𝓕 (schwartzC φ)` for some *real* Schwartz `φ`.  Same construction
as `schwartz_antiHermitian_has_testSymbol_preimage` (P2), minus the `testSymbol` wrapper:
`Φ = 𝓕⁻ G` is real-valued (`fourier_hermitian_real`), `φ = Re ∘ Φ`, and
`𝓕 (schwartzC φ) = 𝓕 (𝓕⁻ G) = G`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.schwartz_hermitian_has_fourier_preimage
nodes.signature:
∀ (G : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  (∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3), G (-ξ) = (starRingEnd ℂ) (G ξ)) →
    ∃ φ, FourierTransform.fourier (LerayHopf.schwartzC✝ φ) = G

nodes.doc:
Clairaut for Schwartz functions: mixed second line derivatives commute pointwise.
`(∂_u ∂_v f) x = (∂_v ∂_u f) x`.  Proved by reducing the Schwartz line derivative to
`fderiv` (`lineDerivOp_apply_eq_fderiv`), pushing the inner evaluation through `fderiv`
(`fderiv_clm_apply`), and invoking symmetry of the second Fréchet derivative of the smooth
function `f` (`ContDiffAt.isSymmSndFDerivAt`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.schwartz_lineDerivOp_comm
nodes.signature:
∀ (f : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (u v x : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  (LineDeriv.lineDerivOp u (LineDeriv.lineDerivOp v f)) x = (LineDeriv.lineDerivOp v (LineDeriv.lineDerivOp u f)) x

      (MeasureTheory.eLpNorm (⇑h) 6 MeasureTheory.volume).toReal

nodes.doc:
**D0c.** The ball set-integral of the squared difference equals the squared
L²(B_R)-distance of the two restrictions. This is the bridge between the conclusion's
set-integral shape and the metric in which `IsCompact.tendsto_subseq` produces convergence.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.setIntegral_normSq_eq_dist_sq_restrictToBall

    dist (LerayHopf.restrictToBall R u) (LerayHopf.restrictToBall R v) ^ 2

Local copy of `ContDiffAt.exists_forall_mem_closedBall_exists_eq_forall_mem_Ioo_hasDerivAt`

nodes.doc:
Tiling induction: a forward solution exists on `[0, k·δ]` for every `k`, started at `x₀`,
provided `δ` is the uniform local time on `closedBall 0 R` and `R = ‖x₀‖`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.solve_exists_on_step
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms (LerayHopf.schemeOfBasis B)) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (n : ℕ) (x₀ : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)) {δ : ℝ},
      0 < δ →
        (∀ y ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 ‖x₀‖,
            ∀ (t₀ : ℝ),
              ∃ α,
                α t₀ = y ∧
                  ∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo (t₀ - δ) (t₀ + δ), HasDerivAt α (LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n (α t)) t) →
          ∀ (k : ℕ),
            ∃ c,
              c 0 = x₀ ∧
                ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 (↑k * (δ / 2)), HasDerivAt c (LerayHopf.galerkinODE_vectorField B F ν n (c t)) t

nodes.doc: Two-sided uniqueness on `Icc a b` with interior initial time `t₀`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.solve_ode_unique_Icc
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : NormedSpace ℝ E] {v : ℝ → E → E} {s : ℝ → Set E} {K : NNReal}
  {f' g' : ℝ → E} {a b t₀ : ℝ},
  (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo a b, LipschitzOnWith K (v t) (s t)) →
    t₀ ∈ Set.Ioo a b →
      ContinuousOn f' (Set.Icc a b) →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo a b, HasDerivAt f' (v t (f' t)) t) →
          (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo a b, f' t ∈ s t) →
            ContinuousOn g' (Set.Icc a b) →
              (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo a b, HasDerivAt g' (v t (g' t)) t) →
                (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioo a b, g' t ∈ s t) → f' t₀ = g' t₀ → Set.EqOn f' g' (Set.Icc a b)

nodes.doc: Backward uniqueness on `Icc a b` with `b` the initial time (time-reversed).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.solve_ode_unique_left
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : NormedSpace ℝ E] {v : ℝ → E → E} {s : ℝ → Set E} {K : NNReal}
  {f' g' : ℝ → E} {a b : ℝ},
  (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioc a b, LipschitzOnWith K (v t) (s t)) →
    ContinuousOn f' (Set.Icc a b) →
      (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioc a b, HasDerivWithinAt f' (v t (f' t)) (Set.Iic t) t) →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioc a b, f' t ∈ s t) →
          ContinuousOn g' (Set.Icc a b) →
            (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioc a b, HasDerivWithinAt g' (v t (g' t)) (Set.Iic t) t) →
              (∀ t ∈ Set.Ioc a b, g' t ∈ s t) → f' b = g' b → Set.EqOn f' g' (Set.Icc a b)

nodes.doc: Forward uniqueness on `Icc a b` with `a` the initial time.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.solve_ode_unique_right
nodes.signature:
∀ {E : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup E] [inst_1 : NormedSpace ℝ E] {v : ℝ → E → E} {s : ℝ → Set E} {K : NNReal}
  {f' g' : ℝ → E} {a b : ℝ},
  (∀ t ∈ Set.Ico a b, LipschitzOnWith K (v t) (s t)) →
    ContinuousOn f' (Set.Icc a b) →
      (∀ t ∈ Set.Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt f' (v t (f' t)) (Set.Ici t) t) →
        (∀ t ∈ Set.Ico a b, f' t ∈ s t) →
          ContinuousOn g' (Set.Icc a b) →
            (∀ t ∈ Set.Ico a b, HasDerivWithinAt g' (v t (g' t)) (Set.Ici t) t) →
              (∀ t ∈ Set.Ico a b, g' t ∈ s t) → f' a = g' a → Set.EqOn f' g' (Set.Icc a b)

nodes.signature:

  LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑(w + w') =
    LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑w + LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑w'

nodes.doc: Local copy: per-component integrand of `stokesTestPairing_R3` is integrable on span elements.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.solve_stokes_integrand_integrable
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (n : ℕ) (u w : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)) (j : Fin 3),
  MeasureTheory.Integrable
    (fun ξ =>
      (2 * Real.pi) ^ 2 * ‖ξ‖ ^ 2 *
        (↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) ↑u)) ξ *
            (starRingEnd ℂ) (↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) ↑w)) ξ)).re)
    MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.signature: Submodule.span ℝ (Set.range LerayHopf.curlSchwartzL2) ≤ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3

nodes.doc:
The `spatial` hypothesis required by the Aubin–Lions combination is exactly P3's
`localCompactness_R3_of_ballCompact`. Supplying P3's isolated input discharges it.

This type is the `aubin_lions_R3` `spatial` binder verbatim (`AxiomaticClosure.lean:449–459`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.spatialInput_R3_of_localRellich
nodes.signature:
LerayHopf.LocalRellichInput →
  ∀ (M : ℝ) (z : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
    (∀ (n : ℕ), z n ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) →
      (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (z n)) →
        (∀ (n : ℕ), ‖z n‖ ≤ M) →
          (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 (z n) ≤ M ^ 2) →
            ∃ ψ g,
              StrictMono ψ ∧
                g ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ∧
                  ∀ (R : ℝ),
                    Filter.Tendsto
                      (fun n => ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3) in Metric.closedBall 0 R, ‖↑↑(z (ψ n)) x - ↑↑g x‖ ^ 2)
                      Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
**AX-SC (now a theorem):** LOCAL spatial compactness on ℝ³ — the ℝ³ replacement for
`rellich_L2Sigma`.

Rellich's theorem FAILS globally on ℝ³, but the LOCAL version holds: given uniform L²
and H¹ bounds, there is a subsequence `z (ψ n)` and a limit `g ∈ L²_σ(ℝ³)` such that
`z (ψ n)` converges to `g` in L² on every ball `{x | ‖x‖ ≤ R}`.

No tightness hypothesis is needed: local Rellich `H¹(B_R) ↪↪ L²(B_R)` (compact embedding)
is unconditional; a diagonal argument over growing balls extracts the subsequence.
This is the genuine Leray 1934 construction.

**DISCHARGED (issue #2).** Formerly the `axiom spatial_compactness_R3`, this is now PROVED
(no new axiom; kernel axioms only) by composing the sorry-free Fréchet–Kolmogorov chain:
`localCompactness_R3_of_ballCompact (localRellichInput_of_frechetKolmogorov frechetKolmogorov_holds)`.
The statement is byte-identical to the former axiom; consumers (`aubin_lions_R3` spatial
slot, `build_galerkin_package_R3_of_galSeq`) are unchanged.  Verified via `#print axioms`: the chain
depends only on `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` (no `sorryAx`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.spatial_compactness_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ (M : ℝ) (z : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  (∀ (n : ℕ), z n ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) →
    (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (z n)) →
      (∀ (n : ℕ), ‖z n‖ ≤ M) →
        (∀ (n : ℕ), LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 (z n) ≤ M ^ 2) →
          ∃ ψ g,
            StrictMono ψ ∧
              g ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ∧
                ∀ (R : ℝ),
                  Filter.Tendsto
                    (fun n => ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3) in Metric.closedBall 0 R, ‖↑↑(z (ψ n)) x - ↑↑g x‖ ^ 2)
                    Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

`1 + V₁`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.sqrt_le_one_add_self_R3

nodes.doc:
**Steklov interval-average of a Galerkin curve.** For mesh `δ` and base time `t`,
`steklovAvg gs δ t := δ⁻¹ • ∫_{t}^{t+δ} (gs.u s : L2VF_R3) ds` (Bochner interval integral
in `L2VF_R3`). For `δ > 0` this is the average of the curve over `[t, t+δ]`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvg
nodes.signature:
(𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) →
  (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) →
    (ν : ℝ) →
      (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) → (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n → ℝ → ℝ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3

nodes.doc:
**Backward Steklov interval-average** over `[t−δ, t]`: `δ⁻¹ • ∫_{t−δ}^{t} (gs.u s) ds`.
This is the boundary-strip companion of `steklovAvg`: for `t` near `T` the forward window
`[t, t+δ]` exits `[0,T]`, but the backward window `[t−δ, t]` stays inside `[0,T]` whenever
`δ ≤ t ≤ T`, so the time modulus controls it.  Definitionally `steklovAvgBack δ t = steklovAvg δ (t−δ)`
(the forward average based at `t−δ`), which lets every forward lemma transfer by a base-point shift.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvgBack
nodes.signature:
(𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) →
  (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) →
    (ν : ℝ) →
      (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) → (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n → ℝ → ℝ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3

nodes.doc:
**Backward Steklov average approximates the original curve at the right endpoint.** If, on the
backward window `[t−δ, t]`, the curve varies from its value at `t` by less than `ε`, then
`‖(gs.u t) − steklovAvgBack δ t‖ ≤ ε`.  Boundary-strip companion of `steklovAvg_approx`; the window
`[t−δ,t]` stays in `[0,T]` for `δ ≤ t ≤ T`, so the time modulus supplies `hmod`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvgBack_approx
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ t ε : ℝ},
  0 < δ →
    δ ≤ t →
      (∀ s ∈ Set.uIoc (t - δ) t, ‖↑(gs.u t) - ↑(gs.u s)‖ ≤ ε) →
        ‖↑(gs.u t) - LerayHopf.steklovAvgBack✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t‖ ≤ ε

nodes.doc: The backward average is the forward average based at `t − δ`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvgBack_eq
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (δ t : ℝ),
  LerayHopf.steklovAvgBack✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t = LerayHopf.steklovAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ (t - δ)

nodes.doc: H¹ regularity of the **backward** Steklov average (window `[t−δ,t] ⊆ Ici 0` when `δ ≤ t`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvgBack_memH1
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ t : ℝ},
  0 < δ → δ ≤ t → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (LerayHopf.steklovAvgBack✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t)

nodes.doc: L² uniform bound on the **backward** Steklov average (window `[t−δ,t] ⊆ Ici 0` when `δ ≤ t`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvgBack_norm_le_u0
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ t : ℝ},
  0 < δ → δ ≤ t → ‖LerayHopf.steklovAvgBack✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t‖ ≤ ‖↑u₀‖

nodes.doc:
**Steklov average approximates the original curve.** If, on the window
`[t, t+δ]`, the curve varies by less than `ε` in L², then
`‖(gs.u t) − steklovAvg δ t‖ ≤ ε`.  This is the key step-3 estimate of the Steklov route.

The window-variation hypothesis `hmod` is the *intended consumer* of
`Htime.uniform_time_modulus`, but ONLY when the whole window stays in `[0,T]`: that modulus
controls pairs with both times in `[0,T]`, so it supplies `hmod` uniformly in `n,t` exactly for
`t, t+δ ∈ [0,T]` (i.e. `t ≤ T-δ`). For `t` in the boundary strip `(T-δ, T]` the forward window
exits `[0,T]` and the modulus does NOT supply `hmod`; that strip needs separate handling (see the
C2 route TODO). This lemma itself only requires `hmod` on `uIoc t (t+δ)` and is agnostic to its
source.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvg_approx
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ t ε : ℝ},
  0 < δ →
    0 ≤ t →
      (∀ s ∈ Set.uIoc t (t + δ), ‖↑(gs.u t) - ↑(gs.u s)‖ ≤ ε) →
        ‖↑(gs.u t) - LerayHopf.steklovAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t‖ ≤ ε

nodes.doc:
**The Steklov average stays in the Galerkin subspace** `Vₙ = range (𝔊.P n)`.  Because every
sample `gs.u s` is a fixed point of `𝔊.P n` (`u_inVn`) and `𝔊.P n` (a CLM) commutes with the
Bochner integral.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvg_inVn
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ t : ℝ},
  0 < δ → 0 ≤ t → LerayHopf.steklovAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t = (𝔊.P n) (LerayHopf.steklovAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t)

nodes.doc:
**H¹ regularity of the Steklov average.**  The averaged state is in `H¹`, since it stays in the
Schwartz Galerkin subspace `Vₙ` (`steklovAvg_inVn`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvg_memH1
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ t : ℝ},
  0 < δ → 0 ≤ t → LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 (LerayHopf.steklovAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t)

nodes.doc:
**The Steklov average is divergence-free** (`∈ L2Sigma_R3`).  The integrand `s ↦ gs.u s` is
valued in the closed subspace `L2Sigma_R3`, which therefore contains the (scaled) Bochner integral:
`steklovAvg = (L2Sigma_R3).subtypeL (δ⁻¹ • ∫_s gs.u s)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvg_mem_sigma
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ t : ℝ},
  0 < δ → 0 ≤ t → LerayHopf.steklovAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3

nodes.doc:
**L² Jensen bound on the Steklov average (squared-norm form).** For `0 < δ` and `0 ≤ t`,
the squared L² norm of the Steklov average is bounded by the average of the squared norms over
the forward window:

  `‖steklovAvg gs δ t‖² ≤ δ⁻¹ · ∫_{t}^{t+δ} ‖(gs.u s)‖² ds`.

This is the kinetic (`H = L²`) instance of the Hilbert-space Bochner-average Jensen lemma
`norm_integral_sq_le_length_mul_integral_normSq`, applied to the continuous Galerkin curve on
the window `[t, t+δ] ⊆ Ici 0`. It is `Lp`-frontier-free.

This is the template the still-open viscous/H¹ Jensen bound instantiates at the Fourier level
(modulo the pointwise `Lp`-valued Bochner-integral coeFn interchange — the genuine missing
mathlib pillar, isolated in this repo as `convL2_coeFn_ae`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvg_normSq_le_average
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ t : ℝ},
  0 < δ → 0 ≤ t → ‖LerayHopf.steklovAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t‖ ^ 2 ≤ δ⁻¹ * ∫ (s : ℝ) in t..t + δ, ‖↑(gs.u s)‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
**L² uniform bound on the Steklov average.** For `0 < δ` and `0 ≤ t`, the averaged
state is bounded by `‖u₀‖`, uniformly in `n` and `t`.  This is the average of a curve all
of whose values satisfy `‖(gs.u s)‖ ≤ ‖u₀‖` (via `galerkin_norm_le_u0`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvg_norm_le_u0
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ) (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (n : ℕ)
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) {δ t : ℝ},
  0 < δ → 0 ≤ t → ‖LerayHopf.steklovAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ n gs δ t‖ ≤ ‖↑u₀‖

nodes.doc:
**Single-`(δ, t₀)` spatial extraction for the Steklov averages.**  For a fixed mesh `δ > 0`
and base time `t₀` with `[t₀, t₀+δ] ⊆ [0,T]`, P3 (`spatialInput_R3_of_localRellich B`) applied to the
sequence `n ↦ steklovAvg (galSeq n) δ t₀` extracts a strictly-monotone subsequence `ψ` and a
ball-restricted spatial limit `g ∈ L2Sigma_R3`.  The required uniform `L²`+`H¹` bounds are exactly
`steklovAvg_norm_le_u0`, `steklovAvg_mem_sigma`, `steklovAvg_memH1`, and the proved gate-derived
`viscousFormSq_steklovAvg_uniform_bound` — all `n`-uniform on the fixed window.

This is the atomic extraction the `δ`-mesh diagonalization iterates.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.steklovAvg_spatial_extraction
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n),
          LerayHopf.LocalRellichInput →
            ∀ {δ t₀ : ℝ},
              0 < δ →
                0 ≤ t₀ →
                  t₀ + δ ≤ T →
                    ∃ ψ g,
                      StrictMono ψ ∧
                        g ∈ LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3 ∧
                          ∀ (R : ℝ),
                            Filter.Tendsto
                              (fun n =>
                                ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3) in Metric.closedBall 0 R,
                                  ‖↑↑(LerayHopf.steklovAvg✝ 𝔊 F ν u₀ (ψ n) (galSeq (ψ n)) δ t₀) x - ↑↑g x‖ ^ 2)
                              Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
**A1.** The piecewise-constant **step curve** on `[0, T]` with mesh `δ = T/m` and
values `y : Fin m → X`: on the `i`-th cell `[i·T/m, (i+1)·T/m)` it takes the value `y i`
(the last cell absorbs `t = T` and everything beyond via the `min · (m-1)` clamp; for
`t < 0` the floor clamps to the cell `0`).  For the degenerate mesh `m = 0` the curve is
constantly `0` (there is no data to sample; all consumers assume `0 < m`).

Concretely, the cell index is `min ⌊t·m/T⌋₊ (m − 1)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stepCurve
nodes.signature: {X : Type u_1} → [NormedAddCommGroup X] → ℝ → (m : ℕ) → (Fin m → X) → ℝ → X

nodes.doc: Local helper: evaluation of the step curve for a positive mesh count.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stepCurve_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ {X : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup X] {T : ℝ} {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) (y : Fin m → X) (t : ℝ),
  LerayHopf.stepCurve T m y t = y ⟨min ⌊t * ↑m / T⌋₊ (m - 1), ⋯⟩

nodes.doc:
Local helper (fresh copy of the `private` `stepCurve_apply` in
`LerayHopf.Bochner.StepFunctionCompactness`): evaluation of the step curve for a positive
mesh count.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stepCurve_eval
nodes.signature:
∀ {X : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup X] {T : ℝ} {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m) (y : Fin m → X) (t : ℝ),
  LerayHopf.stepCurve T m y t = y ⟨min ⌊t * ↑m / T⌋₊ (m - 1), ⋯⟩

nodes.doc:
**A2.** A step curve is in `L²(0,T; X)`: it takes finitely many values on the finite
measure space `volume.restrict (Icc 0 T)`, hence is (strongly measurable and) `MemLp` at
exponent `2`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stepCurve_memLp
nodes.signature:
∀ {X : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup X] (T : ℝ),
  0 < T →
    ∀ (m : ℕ),
      0 < m →
        ∀ (y : Fin m → X),
          MeasureTheory.MemLp (LerayHopf.stepCurve T m y) 2 (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T))

nodes.doc:
Local helper: the step curve is strongly measurable — it is the composition of the
(discrete-target, hence strongly measurable) sampling map `ℕ → X` with the measurable
cell-index map `t ↦ ⌊t·m/T⌋₊`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stepCurve_stronglyMeasurable
nodes.signature:
∀ {X : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup X] (T : ℝ) {m : ℕ},
  0 < m → ∀ (y : Fin m → X), MeasureTheory.StronglyMeasurable (LerayHopf.stepCurve T m y)

nodes.doc:
**A3 (membership half).** The difference of an `L²(0,T; X)` curve and a step curve is
again in `L²(0,T; X)` (both terms are `MemLp`, and `MemLp` is closed under subtraction).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stepCurve_sub_memLp
nodes.signature:
∀ {X : Type u_1} [inst : NormedAddCommGroup X] (T : ℝ),
  0 < T →
    ∀ (m : ℕ),
      0 < m →
        ∀ (y : Fin m → X) {f : ℝ → X},
          MeasureTheory.MemLp f 2 (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T)) →
            MeasureTheory.MemLp (fun t => f t - LerayHopf.stepCurve T m y t) 2
              (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T))

nodes.doc:
Inner functional: `w ↦ -ν·stokes(σu, σw)`, linear in `w` by the stokes right-slot
linearity (`solve_stokes_add_right`/`_smul_right`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesInner
nodes.signature:
(B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) →
  ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n) → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n) →L[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.doc: Left-additivity of `−ν·stokes(·, w)` on span elements, via right-additivity + symmetry.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesInner_add
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (u u' w : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)),
  LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑(u + u') ↑w =
    LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑w + LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u' ↑w

nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesInner_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (u w : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)),
  (LerayHopf.stokesInner✝ B ν n u) w = -ν * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑w

nodes.doc: Left-homogeneity of `−ν·stokes(·, w)` on span elements, via right-homogeneity + symmetry.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesInner_smul
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (c : ℝ) (u w : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)),
  LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑(c • u) ↑w = c * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑w

nodes.doc: Outer map: `u ↦ stokesInner u`, linear in `u` by left-linearity of stokes.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesOut
nodes.signature:
(B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) →
  ℝ → (n : ℕ) → ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n) →L[ℝ] ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n) →L[ℝ] ℝ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesOut_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (ν : ℝ) (n : ℕ) (u : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)),
  (LerayHopf.stokesOut✝ B ν n) u = LerayHopf.stokesInner✝ B ν n u

nodes.doc:
The **concrete viscous (Stokes) test-slot pairing** defined via Fourier coefficients:

  `stokesTestPairing u w = ∑ j, ∑' k, (2π)² (∑ᵢ kᵢ²) · Re[ûⱼ(k) · conj(ŵⱼ(k))]`

This is the Fourier-series representation of `∫_{𝕋³} ∇u : ∇w` (using the `e^{2πi k·x}`
convention where `∂_xᵢ e^{2πi k·x}` contributes `(2π kᵢ)²`).  The diagonal recovers
`viscousFormSq 1 u` (since `Re[ûⱼ(k)·conj(ûⱼ(k))] = |ûⱼ(k)|²`).

This is a **concrete definition** — NOT axiomatized — so it is sound even for `u ∉ H¹`
(the tsum is simply 0 off the summable set, a mathlib convention).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF → ℝ

nodes.doc:
The **viscous (Stokes) test-slot pairing** on `L²(ℝ³; ℝ³)`, defined via the Fourier
integral formula (the ℝ³ analogue of the T³ `stokesTestPairing`):

  `stokesTestPairing_R3 u w = ∑ j, ∫ ξ, (2π)² ‖ξ‖² · Re[(𝓕 uⱼ ξ) · conj(𝓕 wⱼ ξ)] dξ`

where `uⱼ = L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j u : L2C_R3` and `𝓕` is applied via the
`MeasureTheory.Lp.instFourierTransform` typeclass instance
(`Lp.fourierTransformₗᵢ Domain3 ℂ`).

The pointwise value `(𝓕 f) ξ` uses the `Lp.instCoeFun` a.e. representative coercion.
The integral is the Bochner integral on `Domain3` with the Lebesgue `volume` measure;
on non-integrable integrands it returns 0 (the standard mathlib convention).

This definition is **sorry-free**: no Fourier-multiplier operator or `smulLeftCLM`
apparatus is needed.  The diagonal recovers `viscousFormSq_R3 1 u`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3
nodes.signature: ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 → ℝ

nodes.doc:
Right-additivity of `stokesTestPairing_R3` on span elements: the bare Fourier-integral
pairing is additive in its second argument when both arguments lie in `V_n`.  This uses
additivity of the component CLM and of the `L²`-Fourier transform (`map_add`), `Lp.coeFn_add`
to push it through the a.e. coercion, and the integrability of each component integrand
(`stokesTestPairing_R3_integrand_integrable`) to split the Bochner integral via `integral_add`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3_add_right
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (n : ℕ) (u w w' : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)),
  LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑(w + w') =
    LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑w + LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑w'

nodes.doc:
On the diagonal the viscous pairing is the dissipation:
`stokesTestPairing_R3 u u = viscousFormSq_R3 1 u`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3_diag
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 u u = LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 u

nodes.doc:
Local helper (fresh copy of `stokesTestPairing_R3_diag`, `GalerkinODE.lean:175`, which
sits DOWNSTREAM of this file's import set): on the diagonal the viscous pairing is the
dissipation, `stokesTestPairing_R3 u u = viscousFormSq_R3 1 u`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3_diag_local
nodes.signature: ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 u u = LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 u

nodes.doc:
**Viscous Plancherel–Laplacian reformulation (conjunct-2 atom (a)).**  For a Schwartz
divergence-free test `w` (witness `ψ`), the viscous test pairing is a finite sum of real
`L²`-inner products of the components of `u` against the FIXED negative-Laplacian Schwartz fields:
`stokesTestPairing_R3 u w = ∑ⱼ ⟪uⱼ, (-Δ ψⱼ).toLp⟫`.

Each summand `u ↦ ⟪L2VF_projComponent_R3 j u, (-Δ ψⱼ).toLp⟫` is `L²`-WEAK-continuous in `u`
(`L2VF_projComponent_R3 j` is a CLM, then the inner against a fixed element is weak-continuous),
so this exhibits `stokesTestPairing_R3 (·) w` as a weakly-continuous functional — the form the
nonlinear-free viscous limit passage `B(uₙ,w) → B(u,w)` consumes.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3_eq_sum_inner_negLap
nodes.signature:
∀ (u w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) w = (ψ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
    LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 u w =
      ∑ j,
        inner ℝ ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) u) ((LerayHopf.negLapSchwartz✝ (ψ j)).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume)

nodes.doc:
The per-component integrand of `stokesTestPairing_R3` is **integrable** when both arguments
are span elements: their Fourier components are a.e. Schwartz, so the integrand is dominated by
`C · (‖ξ‖² · ‖S_w ξ‖)` (`S_u` bounded, `S_w` rapidly decaying), which is integrable by
`SchwartzMap.integrable_pow_mul`.  This is the crux that licenses `integral_add`/
`integral_const_mul` and hence the right-linearity of `stokesTestPairing_R3` on `V_n`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3_integrand_integrable
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (n : ℕ) (u w : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)) (j : Fin 3),
  MeasureTheory.Integrable
    (fun ξ =>
      (2 * Real.pi) ^ 2 * ‖ξ‖ ^ 2 *
        (↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) ↑u)) ξ *
            (starRingEnd ℂ) (↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) ↑w)) ξ)).re)
    MeasureTheory.volume

nodes.doc: Right-homogeneity of `stokesTestPairing_R3` on span elements.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3_smul_right
nodes.signature:
∀ (B : LerayHopf.SchwartzGalerkinBasis) (n : ℕ) (u w : ↥(LerayHopf.galerkinSpan B n)) (c : ℝ),
  LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑(c • w) = c * LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑u ↑w

nodes.doc:
Right-slot subtraction-linearity of the viscous pairing on `H¹` fields, via the
weighted-Fourier representation (`stokesTestPairing_eq_sum_inner_wFC` +
`weightedFourierComponent_sub`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3_sub_right
nodes.signature:
∀ (u a b : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u →
    LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 a →
      LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 b →
        LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 u (a - b) =
          LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 u a - LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 u b

nodes.doc:
Stokes is symmetric on `V_n` span elements: the integrand `Re[(𝓕 uⱼ)·conj(𝓕 wⱼ)]` equals
`Re[(𝓕 wⱼ)·conj(𝓕 uⱼ)]` pointwise, so the whole pairing is symmetric (no integrability needed).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3_symm
nodes.signature: ∀ (u w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 u w = LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 w u

nodes.doc:
**B7 (Codex-gated statement).** Weighted-Fourier Cauchy–Schwarz for the stokes pairing:
for H¹ fields `u, w`,
`|stokesTestPairing_R3 u w| ≤ √(V₁ u) · √(V₁ w)` where `V₁ := viscousFormSq_R3 1`.

Route (plan §3 B7): componentwise Cauchy–Schwarz on the weighted L² integrands via
`stokesTestPairing_eq_sum_inner_wFC` (integrability from the public
`integrable_viscous_integrand_of_memH1`, `RellichBall.lean:400`), then the finite-sum
Cauchy–Schwarz `∑ aⱼbⱼ ≤ √(∑aⱼ²)·√(∑bⱼ²)` and B4 to reassemble `V₁`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_abs_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (u w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u →
    LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w →
      |LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 u w| ≤ √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 u) * √(LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 w)

nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_bound_of_galerkinTest
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w → ∃ C, ∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), |LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing u ↑w| ≤ C * ‖u‖

nodes.doc: `stokesTestPairing v w` as a finite `fourierBox n₀` sum, for band-limited `w`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_eq_boxSum
nodes.signature:
∀ (n₀ : ℕ) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n₀) w = w →
    ∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
      LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing v w =
        ∑ j,
          ∑ k ∈ LerayHopf.fourierBox n₀,
            ((2 * Real.pi) ^ 2 * ∑ i, ↑(k i) ^ 2) *
              (LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) v) k *
                  (starRingEnd ℂ) (LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) w) k)).re

nodes.doc:
**Helper (for B7 here and B9 in PR-2).** Weight-split bilinear expansion of the stokes
pairing: `stokesTestPairing_R3 u w = ∑ j, Re ⟪wFC w j, wFC u j⟫_ℂ`.

Unfolding: `stokesTestPairing_R3 u w = ∑ j ∫ (2π)²‖ξ‖² · Re[𝓕uⱼ · conj(𝓕wⱼ)]`
(`Regularity.lean:123`), and with the mathlib `L²(ℂ)` inner product
(conjugate-linear in the FIRST slot) `⟪wFC w j, wFC u j⟫ = ∫ conj(√W·𝓕wⱼ) · (√W·𝓕uⱼ)
= ∫ W · conj(𝓕wⱼ) · 𝓕uⱼ`, whose real part matches the stokes integrand.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_eq_sum_inner_wFC
nodes.signature:
∀ (u w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (hu : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 u) (hw : LerayHopf.memH1VF_R3 w),
  LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 u w =
    ∑ j, (inner ℂ (LerayHopf.weightedFourierComponent w hw j) (LerayHopf.weightedFourierComponent u hu j)).re

nodes.doc:
**Viscous-form L²-bound at a band-limited test `w`:** `|stokesTestPairing v w| ≤ C · ‖v‖`.
The constant is a finite `fourierBox n₀` sum of Fourier-coefficient operator norms.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokes_abs_le
nodes.signature:
∀ (n₀ : ℕ) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n₀) w = w →
    ∃ C, 0 ≤ C ∧ ∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), |LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing v w| ≤ C * ‖v‖

nodes.doc: **Viscous-form L²-bound at a band-limited test `w`:** `|stokesTestPairing v w| ≤ C · ‖v‖`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokes_abs_le'
nodes.signature:
∀ (n₀ : ℕ) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n₀) w = w →
    ∃ C, 0 ≤ C ∧ ∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), |LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing v w| ≤ C * ‖v‖

nodes.doc: `stokesTestPairing v w` as a finite `fourierBox n₀` sum, for band-limited `w`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokes_boxSum
nodes.signature:
∀ (n₀ : ℕ) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n n₀) w = w →
    ∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
      LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing v w =
        ∑ j,
          ∑ k ∈ LerayHopf.fourierBox n₀,
            ((2 * Real.pi) ^ 2 * ∑ i, ↑(k i) ^ 2) *
              (LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) v) k *
                  (starRingEnd ℂ) (LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) w) k)).re

nodes.doc:
**Stokes-pairing continuity along a Galerkin curve.**  For a Galerkin test `w` with a Schwartz
witness `ψw`, `t ↦ stokesTestPairing_R3 (gs.u t) w` is continuous on forward time `Ici 0`.

The negative-Laplacian reformulation `stokesTestPairing_R3_eq_sum_inner_negLap` exhibits the pairing
as a finite sum of `L²`-inner products of the (CLM) component projections of the curve against FIXED
Schwartz elements, so continuity is `(component CLM ∘ curve).inner const`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.stokes_curve_continuousOn_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ {𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme} {F : LerayHopf.R3NSForms 𝔊} {ν : ℝ} {u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3} {n : ℕ}
  (gs : LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3)
  (ψw : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) ↑w = (ψw j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
    ContinuousOn (fun s => LerayHopf.stokesTestPairing_R3 ↑(gs.u s) ↑w) (Set.Ici 0)

nodes.doc:
**Conjunct (3): strong initial trace.**  The weakly-continuous representative attains
`u₀` strongly at `0⁺`: weak trace (above) + the uniform bound `‖v(t)‖ ≤ ‖u₀‖` + the
norm-expansion `‖v(t) − u₀‖² = ‖v(t)‖² − 2⟪v(t), u₀⟫ + ‖u₀‖² ≤ 2‖u₀‖² − 2⟪v(t), u₀⟫ → 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.strong_trace_of_props
nodes.signature:
∀ (T : ℝ),
  0 < T →
    ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (v : LerayHopf.Time → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
      (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖↑(v t)‖ ≤ ‖↑u₀‖) →
        v 0 = u₀ →
          (∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
              LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest w →
                ∃ L,
                  0 ≤ L ∧ ∀ s ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, |inner ℝ ↑(v t) ↑w - inner ℝ ↑(v s) ↑w| ≤ L * |t - s|) →
            Filter.Tendsto (fun t => ↑(v t)) (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ici 0)) (nhds ↑u₀)

nodes.doc:
**Conjunct (4): strong initial trace** on ℝ³.

Port of `TorusTraceEnergy.lean:935` (`strong_trace_of_props`). The strategy is identical:
`weak_trace_inner_R3` gives `⟪v(t), u₀⟫ → ‖u₀‖²`; together with the uniform bound `‖v(t)‖ ≤ ‖u₀‖`
and the norm expansion `‖v(t) − u₀‖² = ‖v(t)‖² − 2⟪v(t), u₀⟫ + ‖u₀‖² ≤ 2‖u₀‖² − 2⟪v(t), u₀⟫ → 0`,
this yields strong `L²_σ(ℝ³)` convergence.  Density at `u₀` for `hlip` is `𝔊.tendsto_id`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.strong_trace_of_props_R3
nodes.signature:
∀ (𝔊 : LerayHopf.R3GalerkinScheme) (T : ℝ),
  0 < T →
    ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (v : LerayHopf.Time → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
      (∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ‖↑(v t)‖ ≤ ‖↑u₀‖) →
        v 0 = u₀ →
          (∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3),
              LerayHopf.IsGalerkinTest_R3 𝔊 w →
                ∃ L,
                  0 ≤ L ∧ ∀ s ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, ∀ t ∈ Set.Icc 0 T, |inner ℝ ↑(v t) ↑w - inner ℝ ↑(v s) ↑w| ≤ L * |t - s|) →
            Filter.Tendsto (fun t => ↑(v t)) (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ici 0)) (nhds ↑u₀)

nodes.doc:
**C1 (mathlib-gap risk R1).** The Dirichlet energy at `ν = 1` equals the total squared
`L²`-mass of the component partial derivatives of the Schwartz representatives:
`∑_i ∑_a ‖∂_a (ψ i)‖²_{L²} = viscousFormSq_R3 1 v` for any `v : L2VF_R3` whose components
are represented by `ψ` (as delivered by `galerkinState_schwartzRep`, B10).

Route (plan §3 C1): derivative-Fourier Plancherel `‖∂_a ψ_i‖²_{L²} = ∫ (2π)² ξ_a² ‖𝓕ψ_i‖²`
per component (template: private `normSq_lineDeriv_toLp`, `SobolevEmbedding.lean:243`;
rebuilt fresh), summed over `a` using `∑_a ξ_a² = ‖ξ‖²`, then over `i` to reassemble
`viscousFormSq_R3 1 v`.  The plan permits the `≤` form downstream; the equality is stated
here (prover may downgrade to `≤` if the equality fights — record it if so).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.sum_gradSq_eq_viscousFormSq_of_schwartzRep
nodes.signature:
∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (ψ : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ),
  (∀ (j : Fin 3), (LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent_R3 j) v = (ψ j).toLp 2 MeasureTheory.volume) →
    ∑ i,
        ∑ a,
          ‖((LineDeriv.lineDerivOpCLM ℝ (SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (EuclideanSpace.single a 1)) (ψ i)).toLp 2
                MeasureTheory.volume‖ ^
            2 =
      LerayHopf.viscousFormSq_R3 1 v

nodes.doc:
The componentwise reassembly recovers a vector field
(cf. `velocityProjection_n_tendsto`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.sum_inject_projComponent
nodes.signature: ∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), ∑ j, (LerayHopf.L2VF_injectComponent j) ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponent j) w) = w

nodes.doc: Superadditivity of `liminf` over a `Finset` (in `ENNReal`): `∑ liminf ≤ liminf ∑`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.sum_liminf_le_liminf_sum
nodes.signature:
∀ {α : Type u_1} (s : Finset α) (f : α → ℕ → ENNReal),
  ∑ a ∈ s, Filter.liminf (fun n => f a n) Filter.atTop ≤ Filter.liminf (fun n => ∑ a ∈ s, f a n) Filter.atTop

nodes.doc: For nonnegative families, `∑_i x_i y_i ≤ (∑_i x_i)(∑_i y_i)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.sum_mul_le_mul_sum
nodes.signature:
∀ {ι : Type u_1} (s : Finset ι) (x y : ι → ℝ),
  (∀ i ∈ s, 0 ≤ x i) → (∀ i ∈ s, 0 ≤ y i) → ∑ i ∈ s, x i * y i ≤ (∑ i ∈ s, x i) * ∑ i ∈ s, y i

nodes.doc:
The squared Fourier coefficients of any `f : L2C` are summable (from `lp` membership).

Local copy of `summable_norm_mFourierCoeff3_sq` (which lives in the downstream
`TorusGalerkinODESolve.lean`); proved here directly from `Memℓp (repr f) 2`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.summable_coeff_sq
nodes.signature: ∀ (f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C), Summable fun k => ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f k‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc:
The H¹-gradient-weighted squared coefficients are summable for an H¹ component:
`∑_l (∑ᵢ (lᵢ)²) · ‖v̂(l)‖² < ∞`.  Dominated by the full H¹ weight `(1 + ∑ᵢ(lᵢ)²)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.summable_grad_weight_sq
nodes.signature:
∀ {f : ↥LerayHopf.L2C},
  LerayHopf.memH1Torus f → Summable fun l => (∑ i, ↑(l i) ^ 2) * ‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 f l‖ ^ 2

nodes.doc: The regularized potential symbol `ψ̂_k = symbolMul · (ξ × ŵ)_k` as a Schwartz map.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.symbolHatOf
nodes.signature: (a : ℝ) → 0 < a → (Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) → Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.symbolHatOf_apply
nodes.signature:
∀ (a : ℝ) (ha : 0 < a) (wh : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ) (k : Fin 3) (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  (LerayHopf.symbolHatOf✝ a ha wh k) ξ = LerayHopf.symbolMul✝ a ξ * (LerayHopf.crossHatOf✝ wh k) ξ

nodes.doc: The potential symbol is Hermitian: `ψ̂_k(-ξ) = conj(ψ̂_k ξ)`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.symbolHatOf_hermitian
nodes.signature:
∀ (a : ℝ) (ha : 0 < a) (wh : Fin 3 → SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℂ),
  (∀ (b : Fin 3) (v : LerayHopf.Domain3), (wh b) (-v) = (starRingEnd ℂ) ((wh b) v)) →
    ∀ (k : Fin 3) (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
      (LerayHopf.symbolHatOf✝ a ha wh k) (-ξ) = (starRingEnd ℂ) ((LerayHopf.symbolHatOf✝ a ha wh k) ξ)

nodes.doc: The complex Schwartz-symbol multiplier `ξ ↦ -(2π i)⁻¹ (a² + ‖ξ‖²)⁻¹`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.symbolMul
nodes.signature: ℝ → LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℂ

nodes.name: LerayHopf.tailEnn_lsc_of_weak
nodes.signature:
∀ (N : ℕ) (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF) (vk : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (∀ (y : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), Filter.Tendsto (fun i => inner ℝ (vk i) y) Filter.atTop (nhds (inner ℝ v y))) →
    ∑ j,
        ∑' (k : { k // k ∉ LerayHopf.fourierBox N }),
          ENNReal.ofReal (‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) v) ↑k‖ ^ 2) ≤
      Filter.liminf
        (fun i =>
          ∑ j,
            ∑' (k : { k // k ∉ LerayHopf.fourierBox N }),
              ENNReal.ofReal (‖LerayHopf.mFourierCoeff3 ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC j) (vk i)) ↑k‖ ^ 2))
        Filter.atTop

nodes.doc: Restriction of an L²(ℝ³) field to the tail `B_Rᶜ`, as an element of `L2tailR3 R`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tailVF
nodes.signature: (R : ℝ) → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 → ↥(LerayHopf.L2tailR3✝ R)

nodes.signature:
(R : ℝ) →
  ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3 →
    ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp (EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)) 2 (MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Metric.closedBall 0 R)ᶜ))

nodes.doc:
(b) H¹ domination of the tail, for H¹ fields (the Galerkin curves: `reg_mem`).
The `memH1VF` hypothesis supplies the summability that keeps `h1EnergySq`'s `tsum`
honest (no junk-0 collapse); the LIMIT curve does NOT get this lemma — it goes through
the ENNReal Fatou route (c).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tail_sq_le_h1EnergySq_div
nodes.signature:
∀ (N : ℕ) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  LerayHopf.memH1VF u → ‖u - (LerayHopf.velocityProjection_n N) u‖ ^ 2 ≤ LerayHopf.h1EnergySq u / (1 + ↑N ^ 2)

nodes.doc:
(P0.12) Weak-convergence upgrade `L2Sigma` tests → all `L2VF` tests, via the Leray
projection: for div-free `v n, u`, testing against `y : L2VF` equals testing against
`lerayProjection y ∈ L2Sigma`.  Makes P0.6c's premise reachable from the T-AL-4
capstone's weak-convergence conjunct.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tendsto_inner_L2VF_of_tendsto_inner_L2Sigma
nodes.signature:
∀ (v : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma),
  (∀ (z : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma), Filter.Tendsto (fun n => inner ℝ ↑(v n) ↑z) Filter.atTop (nhds (inner ℝ ↑u ↑z))) →
    ∀ (y : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), Filter.Tendsto (fun n => inner ℝ (↑(v n)) y) Filter.atTop (nhds (inner ℝ (↑u) y))

nodes.doc:
**Core limit lemma.** If `aₙ → a` and `bₙ → b` in `L²(ℝ³;ℝ)` and `h` is essentially bounded,
then `∫ aₙ·bₙ·h → ∫ a·b·h`. (Real `L²` inner product is jointly continuous.)
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tendsto_integral_mul_mul
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ),
  MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume →
    ∀ {a b : ℕ → ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)} {a₀ b₀ : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)},
      Filter.Tendsto a Filter.atTop (nhds a₀) →
        Filter.Tendsto b Filter.atTop (nhds b₀) →
          Filter.Tendsto (fun n => ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ↑↑(a n) x * ↑↑(b n) x * h x) Filter.atTop
            (nhds (∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ↑↑a₀ x * ↑↑b₀ x * h x))

nodes.doc:
The real `L^p × L^q → ℝ` pairing `(c, k) ↦ ∫ c·k` (for Hölder-conjugate `p q`) is continuous
in `k`; hence `kₙ → k₀` in `Lp q` gives `∫ c·kₙ → ∫ c·k₀`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tendsto_integral_pairing
nodes.signature:
∀ {p q : ENNReal} [p.HolderConjugate q] [Fact (1 ≤ p)] [inst : Fact (1 ≤ q)]
  (c : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ p MeasureTheory.volume)) {k : ℕ → ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ q MeasureTheory.volume)}
  {k₀ : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ q MeasureTheory.volume)},
  Filter.Tendsto k Filter.atTop (nhds k₀) →
    Filter.Tendsto (fun n => ∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ↑↑c x * ↑↑(k n) x) Filter.atTop
      (nhds (∫ (x : LerayHopf.Domain3), ↑↑c x * ↑↑k₀ x))

nodes.doc: The bounded multiplier sends an `L²`-convergent sequence to an `L²`-convergent sequence.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tendsto_mulRBdd
nodes.signature:
∀ (h : LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℝ) (hh : MeasureTheory.MemLp h ⊤ MeasureTheory.volume)
  {a : ℕ → ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)} {a₀ : ↥(MeasureTheory.Lp ℝ 2 MeasureTheory.volume)},
  Filter.Tendsto a Filter.atTop (nhds a₀) →
    Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.mulRBdd✝ h hh (a n)) Filter.atTop (nhds (LerayHopf.mulRBdd✝ h hh a₀))

nodes.doc:
**Ball exhaustion of the L²(ℝ³) norm.** The squared L²(B_k)-norm of `restrictToBall k w`
increases to `‖w‖²` as the integer radius `k → ∞` (the balls exhaust `ℝ³`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tendsto_normSq_restrictToBall
nodes.signature:
∀ (w : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  Filter.Tendsto (fun k => ‖LerayHopf.restrictToBall (↑k) w‖ ^ 2) Filter.atTop (nhds (‖w‖ ^ 2))

nodes.doc: **Tail-vanishing** of a fixed L²VF field: `‖tailVF' k v‖ → 0` as `k → ∞` (ball exhaustion).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tendsto_norm_tailVF'_zero
nodes.signature: ∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), Filter.Tendsto (fun k => ‖LerayHopf.tailVF'✝ (↑k) v‖) Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
**Tail-vanishing** of a fixed L² field: `‖tailVF R v‖ → 0` as `R → ∞`.

Proved via dominated/monotone convergence: `eLpNorm v 2 (B_Rᶜ-restricted)`'s square is the
set-lintegral over `B_Rᶜ` of `‖v ·‖ₑ²`, which decreases to the lintegral over `⋂_R B_Rᶜ = ∅`,
hence to `0`.  We obtain it from the ball-exhaustion `tendsto_setLIntegral_closedBall`: the
ball part increases to the full (finite) integral, so the complement part tends to `0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tendsto_norm_tailVF_zero
nodes.signature: ∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3), Filter.Tendsto (fun k => ‖LerayHopf.tailVF✝ (↑k) v‖) Filter.atTop (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
**Weak convergence propagates through continuous ℝ-linear functionals.**  If
`⟪vk i, y⟫ → ⟪v, y⟫` for every test `y`, then `L (vk i) → L v` for every
`L : L2VF →L[ℝ] ℝ` (Riesz representation of `L`).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tendsto_real_functional_of_weak
nodes.signature:
∀ (v : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF) (vk : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF),
  (∀ (y : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF), Filter.Tendsto (fun i => inner ℝ (vk i) y) Filter.atTop (nhds (inner ℝ v y))) →
    ∀ (L : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF →L[ℝ] ℝ), Filter.Tendsto (fun i => L (vk i)) Filter.atTop (nhds (L v))

nodes.doc:
If a global L² function `g` agrees a.e. (on `B_k`) with a ball-`k` limit `gk`, and the
ball-`k` restrictions of a sequence converge to `gk`, then for every real `R ≤ k` the
ball-`R` restrictions converge to `restrictToBall R g`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tendsto_restrictToBall_of_ballLimit
nodes.signature:
∀ (z : ℕ → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (g : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3) (R k : ℝ),
  R ≤ k →
    ∀ (gk : ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 k)),
      ↑↑gk =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Metric.closedBall 0 k)] ↑↑g →
        Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall k (z n)) Filter.atTop (nhds gk) →
          Filter.Tendsto (fun n => LerayHopf.restrictToBall R (z n)) Filter.atTop (nhds (LerayHopf.restrictToBall R g))

nodes.doc: If `f → 0` and `p > 0`, then `f ^ p → 0` (real rpow, base approaching `0` from nonneg).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.tendsto_rpow_of_tendsto_zero
nodes.signature:
∀ {α : Type u_1} {l : Filter α} {f : α → ℝ} {p : ℝ},
  0 < p → Filter.Tendsto f l (nhds 0) → Filter.Tendsto (fun x => f x ^ p) l (nhds 0)

nodes.doc:
**Ball exhaustion for the Lebesgue integral.** The set-lintegral of a function over the
closed ball `B_k` increases to the full-space lintegral as `k → ∞`, because the balls
exhaust `ℝ³`.

nodes.doc:
The Fourier test symbol attached to a real Schwartz `φ` in the weak-divergence pairing:
`testSymbol φ ξ = conj((2π i)·𝓕(schwartzC φ)(ξ))`.  As `φ` ranges over all real Schwartz
functions this is exactly the available family of test symbols paired against the transverse
defect in `divTestFunctional_eq_fourier_integral` / `mem_sigma_iff_fourier_integral_zero`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.testSymbol
nodes.signature: SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ → LerayHopf.Domain3 → ℂ

nodes.doc:
**Anti-Hermitian symmetry of the available test symbols.**  For a real Schwartz `φ`, the
test symbol `testSymbol φ` is anti-Hermitian: `testSymbol φ (-ξ) = -conj(testSymbol φ ξ)`.

This is the structural constraint behind the Hermitian wall in the forward direction: every
test symbol obtainable from a *real* `φ` is anti-Hermitian, so the all-`g` hypothesis of
mathlib's `ae_eq_zero_of_integral_contDiff_smul_eq_zero` cannot be met by a single `φ`; an
even/odd reduction over this symmetry is required.  Proof: `fourier_schwartzC_hermitian`
gives `φ̂(-ξ) = conj(φ̂(ξ))`, and `conj((2πi)·) = -2πi·conj(·)` flips the sign of the
factor `2πi`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.testSymbol_antiHermitian
nodes.signature:
∀ (φ : SchwartzMap LerayHopf.Domain3 ℝ) (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  LerayHopf.testSymbol✝ φ (-ξ) = -(starRingEnd ℂ) (LerayHopf.testSymbol✝ φ ξ)

Build a `DissipativeEvolution` from a `Torus3NSForms`.

`H := L2Sigma`, with the `L2Sigma`-subspace instances.  The regularity functional is
`h1EnergySq ∘ (↑)`, viscous form is the concrete `stokesTestPairing`, convection form is `F.b`.

This construction is **sorry-free**.  The weak formulation tests against the Fourier/Galerkin
class `IsGalerkinTest`; density and fixed-test continuity support extension arguments, but the
current theorem statement itself is Galerkin-test based rather than quantified over all smooth
divergence-free tests.

The ℤ³-indexed Fourier Hilbert basis for L²(𝕋³; ℂ).

This is `UnitAddTorus.mFourierBasis` instantiated at `d = Fin 3`.  The definition typechecks
without sorry because `L2C` uses `haarTorus3 = Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin 3 => AddCircle.haarAddCircle)`,
which is definitionally equal to the measure used by `mFourierBasis` in `AddCircleMulti`
(that file's local instance sets `volume = haarAddCircle` on `UnitAddCircle`, so

Given the full Galerkin-solution sequence and Aubin–Lions compactness package on 𝕋³,
the raw limit `alPkg.u` satisfies, for a.e. `t ∈ [0, T]`, `memH1VF (alPkg.u t : L2VF)`,
and the dissipation `viscousFormSq ν (alPkg.u s : L2VF)` is integrable on `[0, T]`.

This is the hypothesis `h4` of `torus_galerkin_limit_passage_of_energyClass`, which
plugging in here closes the `galerkin_limit_passage` removal.

**Galerkin energy identity (forward-only):** for `t ≥ 0`,
`½‖uₙ(t)‖² + ∫₀ᵗ viscousFormSq ν (uₙ(s)) ds = ½‖Pₙ u₀‖²`.

Derivation: `u_ode` tested against `w := uₙ(t)` itself (admissible by `u_inVn`),
`b(u,u,u) = 0` (`b_self_zero`), the Stokes diagonal (`stokesTestPairing_diag`), and FTC-2.

  0 ≤ t →
    1 / 2 * ‖↑(gs.u t)‖ ^ 2 + ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, LerayHopf.viscousFormSq ν ↑(gs.u s) =

**Torus limit passage — the good-representative existential**, matching the conclusion
of the project axiom `galerkin_limit_passage` byte-for-byte, PROVED from the trace+energy
pillar, GIVEN the energy-class conjunct (4) for the raw Aubin–Lions limit `alPkg.u`
(a.e. `memH1VF` + integrable dissipation on `[0, T]`; supplied by the separate
viscous-limit development).  Conjuncts (0)/(1)/(3) are produced by the weakly-continuous
representative construction of this file; conjunct (2) transfers from
`torus_weakFormNS_of_strongConvergence` through the a.e.-equality (the WeakFormNS
integrals only see a.e. values); conjunct (4) transfers from `h4` the same way.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.torus_galerkin_limit_passage_of_energyClass
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n)
          (alPkg : LerayHopf.AubinLionsPackage F ν T u₀ galSeq),
          (∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T), LerayHopf.memH1VF ↑(alPkg.u t)) ∧
              IntervalIntegrable (fun s => LerayHopf.viscousFormSq ν ↑(alPkg.u s)) MeasureTheory.volume 0 T →
            ∃ u,
              (∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T), u t = alPkg.u t) ∧
                LerayHopf.WeakFormNS ν T (LerayHopf.torus3Evolution F) u ∧
                  (∀ (t : ℝ),
                      0 ≤ t →
                        t ≤ T →
                          1 / 2 * ‖↑(u t)‖ ^ 2 + ∫ (s : ℝ) in 0..t, LerayHopf.viscousFormSq ν ↑(u s) ≤
                            1 / 2 * ‖↑u₀‖ ^ 2) ∧
                    Filter.Tendsto (fun t => ↑(u t)) (nhdsWithin 0 (Set.Ici 0)) (nhds ↑u₀) ∧
                      (∀ᵐ (t : LerayHopf.Time) ∂MeasureTheory.volume.restrict (Set.Icc 0 T), LerayHopf.memH1VF ↑(u t)) ∧
                        IntervalIntegrable (fun s => LerayHopf.viscousFormSq ν ↑(u s)) MeasureTheory.volume 0 T

non-expansiveness of the projection).  Torus analogue of R3's `galerkin_norm_le_u0`.

nodes.doc:
**WeakFormNS limit passage on 𝕋³ (conjunct 2).**

The Aubin–Lions limit curve `alPkg.u` satisfies the distributional Navier–Stokes weak
equation `WeakFormNS ν T (torus3Evolution F) alPkg.u`.

**Density-free (key 𝕋³ advantage):** Every WeakFormNS test `w` satisfies
`IsGalerkinTest w = ∃ n₀, Pₙ₀ w = w`, so the Galerkin ODE fires directly for all
`n ≥ n₀` — no density / test-approximation step is needed.

**Limit passage:** per-approximant WeakFormNS = 0 (IBP + ODE); the linear/viscous/
convection errors are killed by the eLpNorm strong convergence
`alPkg.strong_convergence` via Cauchy–Schwarz on `[0,T]`, using
`alPkg.u_aestronglyMeasurable` for the dominators.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.torus_weakFormNS_of_strongConvergence
nodes.signature:
∀ (F : LerayHopf.Torus3NSForms) (ν : ℝ),
  0 < ν →
    ∀ (T : ℝ),
      0 < T →
        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData F ν u₀ n)
          (alPkg : LerayHopf.AubinLionsPackage F ν T u₀ galSeq),
          LerayHopf.WeakFormNS ν T (LerayHopf.torus3Evolution F) alPkg.u

nodes.doc:
**Abstract Arzelà–Ascoli + sup→L² transfer.**  Let `Φ f` be a representative function for the
L²(B_R)-class `restrictToBall R (ρf f)` (`hΦae`).  If the family `{Φ f : f ∈ S}` is uniformly
bounded (`hB`) and uniformly equicontinuous (`hequi`) on the compact ball `B_R`, then the image
`{restrictToBall R (ρf f) : f ∈ S}` is totally bounded in `L²(B_R)`.

The proof is the classical Arzelà–Ascoli net construction: a finite `δ`-net of centers in the
compact `B_R` (with `δ` from equicontinuity), a finite value-net of the bounded range, classify
each `f` by the tuple of nearest value-net points at the centers, choose one representative per
class; equicontinuity plus the value-net make functions in the same class uniformly close on
`B_R`, and `dist_restrictToBall_le_of_ae_bound` (finite-measure sup→L² bound) turns that into
L²(B_R)-closeness.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.totallyBounded_image_of_equicont_bdd
nodes.signature:
∀ (R : ℝ) (S : Set ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R)) (ρf : ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R) → ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3)
  (Φ : ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 R) → LerayHopf.Domain3 → EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin 3)),
  (∀ f ∈ S, ↑↑(ρf f) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume] Φ f) →
    ∀ (B : ℝ),
      (∀ f ∈ S, ∀ x ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R, ‖Φ f x‖ ≤ B) →
        (∀ ε > 0,
            ∃ δ > 0,
              ∀ f ∈ S,
                ∀ (x y : LerayHopf.Domain3),
                  x ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R → y ∈ Metric.closedBall 0 R → ‖x - y‖ < δ → ‖Φ f x - Φ f y‖ < ε) →
          TotallyBounded ((fun f => LerayHopf.restrictToBall R (ρf f)) '' S)

nodes.doc:
**FK step 4.**  Total-boundedness transfer.  In a metric space, a set `S` that is uniformly
ε-approximable (for every `ε > 0`) by a totally bounded set is itself totally bounded.

This is the abstract glue between step 1 (uniform mollification approximation) and step 3
(total boundedness of the mollified family): an ε-net of the approximant, fattened by ε, is a
`2ε`-net of `S`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.totallyBounded_of_uniform_approx
nodes.signature:
∀ {α : Type u_1} [inst : PseudoMetricSpace α] (S : Set α),
  (∀ ε > 0, ∃ T, TotallyBounded T ∧ ∀ s ∈ S, ∃ t ∈ T, dist s t < ε) → TotallyBounded S

nodes.doc:
**A5.** Total-boundedness transfer: in a pseudometric space, a set `S` that is, for
EVERY `ε > 0`, uniformly ε-approximable by a totally bounded set is itself totally bounded
(an ε-net of the approximant, fattened by ε, is a 2ε-net of `S`).

FRESH mathlib-only copy of the public `totallyBounded_of_uniform_approx`
(`LerayHopf/R3/FrechetKolmogorov.lean:1806`), duplicated by design so this file stays free
of R3 imports (plan §2 fresh-build note; A5 decision recorded in the file header).
nodes.name: LerayHopf.totallyBounded_of_uniform_approx'
nodes.signature:
∀ {α : Type u_2} [inst : PseudoMetricSpace α] (S : Set α),
  (∀ ε > 0, ∃ A, TotallyBounded A ∧ ∀ s ∈ S, ∃ a ∈ A, dist s a < ε) → TotallyBounded S

nodes.doc:
**STEP 1 of E2.**  The `toLp` classes of the ball-restricted Galerkin curves form a
totally bounded subset of `Lp (L2ballR3 k) 2 μ_T`.

`f` is kept abstract (with the pointwise identity `hfeq` linking it to the ball-restricted
curve) so that the matching against the core step-curve bound is syntactic — this keeps the
elaboration budget small.  Route: given `ε`, choose a fine mesh `m` (D3's uniform modulus
coefficient `C_mod` fixes `m` so `√(C_mod·√(T/m)) < ε`); D3's per-cell samples place each slice
in the compact `LocalRellichInput.ballCompact Mδ k` (B2 for the `L²` bound, D3 for the `V₁`
bound); the mesh-`m` step-curve set is compact (A4), hence totally bounded, and each curve is
within `ε` of its step curve (core helper); A5 transfers total-boundedness.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.totallyBounded_restrictToBall_toLp_range

        ∀ (u₀ : ↥LerayHopf.L2Sigma_R3) (galSeq : (n : ℕ) → LerayHopf.GalerkinSolutionData_R3 𝔊 F ν u₀ n) (ψ : ℕ → ℕ)
          (k : ℕ) (f : ℕ → ℝ → ↥(LerayHopf.L2ballR3 ↑k))

          (∀ (j : ℕ) (t : ℝ), f j t = LerayHopf.restrictToBall ↑k ↑((galSeq (ψ j)).u t)) →
            TotallyBounded (Set.range fun j => MeasureTheory.MemLp.toLp (f j) ⋯)

will be characterised by `T_u = 0` a.e.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.transverseDefect

∀ (u : ↥LerayHopf.L2VF_R3),
  ((fun ξ =>
        LerayHopf.IsTransverseAt (fun j => ↑↑(FourierTransform.fourier ((LerayHopf.L2VF_projComponentC_R3 j) u)) ξ)
          ξ) =ᵐ[MeasureTheory.volume]

nodes.doc:
**Pointwise transverse ⊥ longitudinal cancellation.**  At a fixed `ξ ≠ 0`, if `û` is
transverse (`∑_j ξ_j û_j = 0`) and `ŵ` is longitudinal (`ŵ × ξ = 0`, written componentwise
`ŵ_{k+1} ξ_{k+2} − ŵ_{k+2} ξ_{k+1} = 0`), then the Hermitian pairing `∑_j conj(û_j) ŵ_j = 0`.

Pure ℂ³ linear algebra: `ŵ × ξ = 0 ⟹ ŵ ∥ ξ`, so `ξ_i · ∑_j conj(û_j) ŵ_j = ŵ_i · ∑_j conj(û_j) ξ_j`
and the right factor is `conj(∑_j ξ_j û_j) = 0`; pick `i` with `ξ_i ≠ 0`.
nodes.name: LerayHopf.transverse_longitudinal_cancel
nodes.signature:
∀ (ξ : LerayHopf.Domain3),
  ξ ≠ 0 →
    ∀ (û ŵ : Fin 3 → ℂ),
      ∑ j, ↑(ξ.ofLp j) * û j = 0 →
        (∀ (k : Fin 3), ŵ (k + 1) * ↑(ξ.ofLp (k + 2)) - ŵ (k + 2) * ↑(ξ.ofLp (k + 1)) = 0) →
          ∑ j, (starRingEnd ℂ) (û j) * ŵ j = 0
